<div class="t13ne-poolhand" mode="jsvg"><div class="t13ne-cardwrap-jsvg" id="cardrewritebox0" data-mode="jsvg" data-wyrd="1" data-yarn="1"><img src="https://www.cjmoseley.dev.cc/wp-content/plugins/t13ne/images/cards-svg/ad.svg" class="t13ne-svgcard"><div class="t13ne-svgpips"><div class="t13ne-textpips">14<!-- write suit--><span class="t13ne-symbol"><span class="t13ne-redcard">♦</span></span><details class="t13ne-suit"><summary></summary><p class="t13ne-name"><strong>Suit: </strong>Diamonds <span class="t13ne-redcard">♦</span></p><p><strong>Ordeal Trump: </strong><span class="t13ne-trump">Preparation</span></p><p class="t13ne-desc"><strong>Description: </strong>Diamonds represent the finer things of life, they are bright and sparkling. In the Tarot, Diamonds are replaced with coins, which represent  not just Wealth, but elemental Earth as well.</p><p class="t13ne-tarot"><strong>Tarot Meaning: </strong>Travel, Money, Business, Contracts, Deals, Voyages.</p><p class="t13ne-otext"><strong>Ordeal Text: </strong>Ordeal Types: Travel (e.g. Chase, Race) / Creativity (e.g. Smithing, Painting). Diamonds act as Trump for the following Action Types: Art, Building, Charming, Crafting, Driving, Emoting, Forgiving, Keeping Your Cool, Listening, Meditation, Motion, Prayer, Preparation, Research, Sprinting and Working in general. Any type of action can be prepared with a Diamond.</p><p class="t13ne-parry"><strong>Parry: </strong><strong>Seize the initative:</strong> The cards of the original attack (less the matched card) are immediately added to the Defender’s Ordeal Pool. They may then play as many cards as their Descendant/Annex normally plays including the Parry card (1-4) usually into one of their Style Reserve, and then immediately discard any cards over Pool Limit.</p><p class="t13ne-soak"><strong>Soak: </strong><strong>Ready:</strong> The matched Attack card is immediately Prepared by the Defender (as they wish). The Soak card is discarded.</p></details></div></div><div class="t13ne-svg-facet"><div>Awe, Craft, Orthodox, Virtue, Zeal</div></div><div class="t13ne-wound"><div><strong class="t13ne-woundname">Carnage</strong><details><summary></summary><strong>Carnage</strong><p class="t13ne-base-pips">Base Pips to summon: 21</p><p class="t13ne-min-pips">Minimum Pips Required: 14</p><p class="t13ne-desc">The Target should be dead, all exploded or turned to stone, or something. Sometimes though, they keep on going... for a while. And just occasionally, they get better.</p><details class="t13ne-roll"><summary>Rules</summary>Create a "Dead/Destroyed" Flaw Hitch, or increase the Boon of any Flaw Hitch by 1 Boon per Miasma Boon treated as Yarn Limit Duration outside of Ordeals) until healed. May also alter the target’s Incarna Facet choice (turning to stone, etc). Alternatively Decrease Character Scale by 1 or remove a Character's access to the Wounded Alternate.</details><details class="t13ne-ordeal"><summary>Ordeal Rules</summary>Immediately Treat Personality / Master Descendant as Wounded. At the end of the Round either create a new "Dead / Destroyed" Flaw Hitch (Half the Incarna Boon + Miasma Score) or increase a Flaw Hitch by Miasma Score. Alternatively Decrease Scale by 1, or alter Incarna / Persona / Core choices. If the Hitch Boon rises above Incarna Boon the Wound increases to Carnage+</details><details class="t13ne-psych"><summary>Psych Wounds</summary><details class="t13ne-negative"><summary>Negative Psych</summary><p class="t13ne-normal"><strong>Normal</strong>Can create a Flaw Hitch of any Type, as the Ref decides. Consciousness is always lost until the wound is Stabilised. Note may remove a Character's access to the Wounded Alternate.</p><p class="t13ne-extreme"><strong>Extreme</strong>Creates Maximised Flaw Hitches of any type but favours... Fear, Madness, Vague (Amnesia), Paranoia, Loss of Sense (e.g. Hysterical Blindness) or Taboo Hitches. Consciousness is always lost until Healed and may remove a Character's access to the Wounded Alternate. Can create a new Split Alternate.</p></details><details class="t13ne-positive"><summary>Positive Psych</summary><p class="t13ne-normal"><strong>Normal</strong>May Gain a new Power Annex / Descendant. Can Create a Flaw Hitch of any type, Consciousness is always lost until Stabilised, and access to the Alternate may be lost.</p><p class="t13ne-extreme"><strong>Extreme</strong> May create or Resolve Flaw Hitches of any type but favours... Duty, Dependance, Madness, Vague (Amnesia), Paranoia, Loss of Sense (e.g. Hysterical Blindness) or Taboo Hitches. Consciousness is always lost until Healed. Either creates a new Alternate, Intergrates an Alternate one Step toward Coalesce, or Gain a new Power Annex / Descendant.</p></details></details></details></div></div><div class="t13ne-tarot-svg"><details><summary class="t13ne-wyrdtarot"><strong> Wyrd Tarot: </strong> Success</summary><p class="t13ne-normal"><strong> Normal: </strong>+2 Success Levels while atop Target's pile.</p><p class="t13ne-crossed"><strong> Crossed: </strong>-2 Success Levels while atop the Target's pile</p></details></div><div class="t13ne-yarn-fancy">
			<details><summary><strong>Yarn</strong> <br>The Ambition</summary>
			<span class="t13ne-description">The Card of new Ambitions, sudden monetary gains, new business opportunities, new treasures or rumours of treasures and money being made. There may be conflict and collisions, new obstacles in the way or a Rival's ambitions may block progress.</span>
			<span class="t13ne-significator">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-significator"><strong>Significator</strong></summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-plot"><strong>Plot: </strong> This Plot is known as the "Rags-To-Riches" Plot. It seeks Characters of poor circumstance, with the more Hitches the better, and grants them power and success. However, once they are raised in riches and power, the Plot will pick a new Pauper to Hook. Ambition Plots always try to Trigger Hitches of Hooked Characters, but grant doubled Gains to those who have Hitches triggered.</aside>
					<aside class="t13ne-char"><strong>Character: </strong> The Ambition significator will make a Character who is defined by their Ambitions. The Character should set an Ambition. This should be a difficult to achieve ambition, such as "Become the Greatest Living Swordsman". This gives the Character a Gain (Yin) Sway when they speak about this ambition or their current goals, Gain (Yang) Sway when they choose an option or take an action that hastens this ambition. They will also Gain Chi if their ambition is thwarted by another's actions. Additionally, when directly working towards their Ambition (such as training or challenging other swordsmen) they may add +1 Success Level.</aside>
					<aside class="t13ne-scene"><strong>Scene: </strong> This Plot is known as the "Rags-To-Riches" Plot. It seeks Characters of poor circumstance, with the more Hitches the better, and grants them power and success. However, once they are raised in riches and power, the Plot will pick a new Pauper to Hook. Ambition Plots always try to Trigger Hitches of Hooked Characters, but grant doubled Gains to those who have Hitches triggered.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-scene">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-scene-type">
						<strong>Scene: </strong> Hook
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">Hooks hook a character into a Conflict. A Hook offers a Character (or Group) a reason to get involved in the Story. This lets the Plot use the Conflict Facet Boon/Value of the Character to help build their Conflict Annex.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-conflict-embodiment">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-conflict-embodiment">
						<strong>Conflict Embodiment: </strong> External
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">One side of the Conflict (usually the Pressed side) is embodied within the Characters (sometimes an NPC), the Dominant side is embodied as something external to all the Characters. Embodiments might include Tones, Locations, Descendants, Quests, Ordeals/Tests and suitable Obstacles.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>

			<span class="t13ne-facet-embodiment">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-facet-embodiment">
						<strong>Facet Embodiment: </strong> Monster
					</summary>
					<section class="t13ne-description">The Conflict Embodies one Facet as a Character Monster Facet.This Monster might be a Dragon, an Orc Horde, or just a human Villain. Monsters can be great fun, but are rarely subtle. Where possible when using Monster embodiments, the Plot may want to keep the exact nature of the Monster a secret for as long as it can.</section>
				</details>
			</span>
			 <div class="t13ne-archetypes"><details><summary><strong>Archetype Character: </strong>Enchanter</summary> <details class="t13ne-Persona"><summary><strong>Persona: </strong><span class="t13ne-output">Idol</span>
      </summary>
      <details class="t13ne-motivation"><summary><strong>Motivation: </strong></summary><span class="t13ne-description">Idols want to be respected, honoured, worshipped and adored for who they are, or what they do.</span></details>
       <details class="t13ne-avoid"><summary><strong>Avoid: </strong></summary><span class="t13ne-description">Idols don't like to be alone, or ignored.</span></details>
       <details class="t13ne-gain-chi"><summary><strong>Gain Chi: </strong></summary><span class="t13ne-description"><span>Gain Chi when you speak, or draw attention to yourself.</span><p>Ordeal gain Chi when you play a ♦ to attack a target.</p></span></details></details>   <details class="t13ne-core"><summary><strong>Core: </strong><span class="t13ne-output">Prevailer</span></summary><span class="t13ne-description">Ignore a Hitch when triggered by paying Sway equal to the half the normal reward.</span></details><details class="t13ne-hitch"><summary><strong>Hitch: </strong><span class="t13ne-output">Doomed</span></summary><span class="t13ne-description">Cursed, star-crossed, destined, fated, or just inevitably dying. This is a flexible Hitch that can cover a lot of scenarios when the gods just seem to have it in for you.</span></details><p class="t13ne-description">Enchanters or Enchantresses are magic users that use Charms and Enchantments to get what they want. If magic is off then they will use hypnotic or psychological tricks to charm, manipulate and seduce.</p></details></div>
			 <span class="t13ne-hook">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-hook">
						<strong>Hook: </strong> Opportunity
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">The Story hooks Characters by offering them an opportunity to Gain something they want or need to fulfil an ambition.</aside>
					<details class="t13ne-hook-aspect">
						<summary><strong>Hook Aspect: </strong>Geometry</summary>
						<section class="t13ne-description">The Hook targets the Character's Geometry. For example: In order to Hook a Circle Geometry simply offer to fulfil an ambition.</section>
					</details>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-obstacle-fancy"><details class="t13ne-obstacle"><summary><strong>Obstacle:</strong>Required</summary><p class="t13ne-description">The way is blocked by some required descendant, or business that must be completed before proceeding. It usually involves a payment of some sort being made.</p></details></span>
			<span class="t13ne-gain">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-gain">
						<strong>Gain </strong> 
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">Ultimate Power (A Super-Skill Descendant / An Artefact)</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-fray">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-fray">
						<strong>Fray: </strong> Quest
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">The Fray is a Quest, usually based on the Facets of the Conflict, for something the PC requires or desires.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-snag">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-snag">
						<strong>Snag: </strong> Falling Behind
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">The Character's Rival or Opposition makes a Gain (or just advances to the next Stage of a larger Plot's Ordeal). You may Play another Card as a Gain, or use this card's <em>Ultimate Power</em>.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-sweeping">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-sweeping">
						<strong>Sweeping: </strong> Win
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">The Pressed side has a Win, this may be a battle against a weak or weakened foe, or they might catch a lucky break, or lottery win. The Win doesn't have to be big, just enough to keep hope alive will do, but a Gain is in order. Perhaps even the Resolution of a Hitch.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
		 <details class="t13ne-revelation-fancy"><summary><strong>Revelation</strong></summary><details class="t13ne-about"><summary><strong>About: </strong> Pressed </summary><p class="t13ne-description">The information is about the Pressed Conflict Embodiment, which is often a PC in a game, and is usually the antagonist in a Story.</p></details><details class="t13ne-info"><summary><strong>Info: </strong> Ambition </summary><p class="t13ne-description">Information that is about the ambitions of the Embodiment.</p></details><details class="t13ne-vector"><summary><strong>Vector: </strong> Informant </summary><p class="t13ne-description">Someone tells you it clearly, but privately. They probably want to keep their identity secret, or certainly protected, but maybe not, perhaps they wish to be paid, or need something else from the Character.</p></details><details class="t13ne-alternate"><summary><strong>Alternate: </strong> Incarna </summary><p class="t13ne-description">The information is about the Incarna of someone or something. If the Incarna is specified it is usually because it is special. So probably not Flesh and Blood...</p></details><details class="t13ne-detail"><summary><strong>Detail: </strong> Functional </summary><p class="t13ne-description">A functional detail is information about a function of the Narrative Element being revealed. It might include the fact that a particular car make cannot make certain tyre tracks, or that Rumble mountain isn't actually a volcano, but has a huge machine built within it.</p></details></details>
		 <details class="t13ne-ordeal-fancy"><summary><strong>Ordeal</strong></summary><p class="t13ne-ordeal-type"><strong>Ordeal-Type </strong> — Solitary Ordeal</p><p class="t13ne-stages"><strong>Stages </strong> — 6</p><p class="t13ne-ordeal-spread"><strong>Ordeal-Spread </strong> — Map-Geography</p><p class="t13ne-stakes"><strong>Stakes </strong> — Blended Low-Medium-High-Extreme</p><p class="t13ne-test"><strong>Test </strong> — Art (Awe)</p><p class="t13ne-obstacles"><strong>Obstacles </strong> — 3 Obstacles in every Stage</p><p class="t13ne-suggested-action"><strong>Suggested-Action </strong> — Intimidate</p><details class="t13ne-stage"><summary><strong>Stage: </strong> Speed Stunt </summary><p class="t13ne-description">Something needs to be done at speed, maybe its a skid, shooting rapids, speed typing or speed reading.</p></details><details class="t13ne-motional"><summary><strong>Motional: </strong> Dangerous Vehicular Dash </summary><p class="t13ne-description">The Ordeal is meant to be done with haste and speed. Get somewhere or do something as quickly as possible, through a dangerous area.</p></details><details class="t13ne-fight"><summary><strong>Fight: </strong> Ambush </summary><p class="t13ne-description">Ambushed by a group of weaker foes (often Chorus or Vex Extras), with numbers greater then those they attack, usually equivalent weapons and armour to the PCs, they may attempt to retreat after the third Phase.</p></details></details>
		 </details>
			</div>
				<div class="t13ne-card-lea"><details class="t13ne-lea"><summary><strong>Lea</strong></summary><p class="t13ne-colour"><strong>Path Colour —</strong> Brown/Blue Omnivore</p><p class="t13ne-bulmas"><strong>Allowed Bulmäs —</strong> Broccen, Vermis, Labyrinthine, All-Walkers</p><p class="t13ne-animal-category"><strong>Animal Category —</strong> All-path</p><p class="t13ne-animal-example"><strong>Example Animal —</strong> All-Walkers</p><p class="t13ne-notes"><strong>Notes —</strong> The Paths of the All-Walkers can allow Bulmäs to do extraordinary things, like walking an individual’s life-path, so that all of their personal history is known to them. All-Walkers can assume even the forms and souls of other people by using these paths, without directly eating of the Soul. All-Walkers can even Doom-Weave or Yarn-Tell and play other cards from their Hand as Wyrd Tarot cards or Yarn cards, once they have played an Ace on themselves.</p></details></div></div><div class="t13ne-cardwrap-jsvg" id="cardrewritebox1" data-mode="jsvg" data-wyrd="1" data-yarn="1"><img src="https://www.cjmoseley.dev.cc/wp-content/plugins/t13ne/images/cards-svg/2D.svg" class="t13ne-svgcard"><div class="t13ne-svgpips"><div class="t13ne-textpips">2<!-- write suit--><span class="t13ne-symbol"><span class="t13ne-redcard">♦</span></span><details class="t13ne-suit"><summary></summary><p class="t13ne-name"><strong>Suit: </strong>Diamonds <span class="t13ne-redcard">♦</span></p><p><strong>Ordeal Trump: </strong><span class="t13ne-trump">Preparation</span></p><p class="t13ne-desc"><strong>Description: </strong>Diamonds represent the finer things of life, they are bright and sparkling. In the Tarot, Diamonds are replaced with coins, which represent  not just Wealth, but elemental Earth as well.</p><p class="t13ne-tarot"><strong>Tarot Meaning: </strong>Travel, Money, Business, Contracts, Deals, Voyages.</p><p class="t13ne-otext"><strong>Ordeal Text: </strong>Ordeal Types: Travel (e.g. Chase, Race) / Creativity (e.g. Smithing, Painting). Diamonds act as Trump for the following Action Types: Art, Building, Charming, Crafting, Driving, Emoting, Forgiving, Keeping Your Cool, Listening, Meditation, Motion, Prayer, Preparation, Research, Sprinting and Working in general. Any type of action can be prepared with a Diamond.</p><p class="t13ne-parry"><strong>Parry: </strong><strong>Seize the initative:</strong> The cards of the original attack (less the matched card) are immediately added to the Defender’s Ordeal Pool. They may then play as many cards as their Descendant/Annex normally plays including the Parry card (1-4) usually into one of their Style Reserve, and then immediately discard any cards over Pool Limit.</p><p class="t13ne-soak"><strong>Soak: </strong><strong>Ready:</strong> The matched Attack card is immediately Prepared by the Defender (as they wish). The Soak card is discarded.</p></details></div></div><div class="t13ne-svg-facet"><div>Awe</div></div><div class="t13ne-wound"><div><strong class="t13ne-woundname">Distract</strong><details><summary></summary><strong>Distract</strong><p class="t13ne-base-pips">Base Pips to summon: 2</p><p class="t13ne-min-pips">Minimum Pips Required: 2</p><p class="t13ne-desc">The Target is distracted by the attack.</p><details class="t13ne-roll"><summary>Rules</summary><strong>-6</strong> Score on next Roll the Character makes and then Discard.</details><details class="t13ne-ordeal"><summary>Ordeal Rules</summary>Lose a Success Level <ul><li>Draw -1 Card on next Draw and then Discard.</li><li> Or <strong>-3</strong> Pips to the next Card played and Discard.</li><li>Or <strong>+3</strong> Pips to the next Attack on the Character, then Discard.</li></ul></details><details class="t13ne-psych"><summary>Psych Wounds</summary><details class="t13ne-negative"><summary>Negative Psych</summary><p class="t13ne-normal"><strong>Normal</strong>Mild Emotional influence (tears in eyes, heightened heart rate, etc) until Stabilized / Discarded.</p><p class="t13ne-extreme"><strong>Extreme</strong>Must scream, swear, or otherwise express the emotion until Stabilized or Discarded.</p></details><details class="t13ne-positive"><summary>Positive Psych</summary><p class="t13ne-normal"><strong>Normal</strong>Mild Emotional Influence (Smile, Pupil Dilation, Eyebrow twitches, etc) until Stabilised / Discarded.</p><p class="t13ne-extreme"><strong>Extreme</strong>Must speak, laugh, gasp, moan, tick, or otherwise express the emotion until Stabilised or Discarded.</p></details></details></details></div></div><div class="t13ne-tarot-svg"><details><summary class="t13ne-wyrdtarot"><strong> Wyrd Tarot: </strong> Foe</summary><p class="t13ne-normal"><strong> Normal: </strong>Player and target are "foes"</p><p class="t13ne-crossed"><strong> Crossed: </strong>Player and target are "friendly".</p></details></div><div class="t13ne-yarn-fancy">
			<details><summary><strong>Yarn</strong> <br>The Wraith</summary>
			<span class="t13ne-description">Whether an expression of the supernatural or simply the psychological forces at play. The card is about People's Emotions and especially Fears. These can be as simple as a fear of missing out, or falling behind others, or expressions of deeper phobias. It is a card of small gains and intense, but limited resistance.</span>
			<span class="t13ne-significator">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-significator"><strong>Significator</strong></summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-plot"><strong>Plot: </strong> This Plot is known as a "Ghost Story" it may be Horror Genre, or may be a Morality Tale. It will involve either the Supernatural (in the form of a Ghost or Haunting) or some sort of Psychological Terror (the Scare). Wraith Plots will always try to pile on the Psych attacks adding +1 Success Level to any Negative Psyche Attack.</aside>
					<aside class="t13ne-char"><strong>Character: </strong> The Wraith Significator indicates a Character that is haunted by their past. They may see actual ghosts or spirits, but more normally they are plagued by thoughts, feelings, dreams and memories that they would like to forget. They Gain Yin when they mope, brood or act depressed, Gain Yang when they engage with situations or people that remind them of their past. Gain Chi when they tell others their story. Additionally they gain +1 Success Level with any situation that relates to their past.</aside>
					<aside class="t13ne-scene"><strong>Scene: </strong> This Plot is known as a "Ghost Story" it may be Horror Genre, or may be a Morality Tale. It will involve either the Supernatural (in the form of a Ghost or Haunting) or some sort of Psychological Terror (the Scare). Wraith Plots will always try to pile on the Psych attacks adding +1 Success Level to any Negative Psyche Attack.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-scene">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-scene-type">
						<strong>Scene: </strong> Test
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">Some Scenes are nothing but a single Test, often with a lot of discussion around them. Tests are usually a single dice roll, like a Facet Test, and usually a Test of one of the Conflict Facets. A Ref should have some idea what the Difficulty is, what will occur on a pass or a fail, and what a Stalemate would look like. Occasionally Tests may be a single roll against an Opponent (such as the Villain). You can find example Tests in the Ordeal details for a card.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-conflict-embodiment">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-conflict-embodiment">
						<strong>Conflict Embodiment: </strong> External
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">One side of the Conflict (usually the Pressed side) is embodied within the Characters (sometimes an NPC), the Dominant side is embodied as something external to all the Characters. Embodiments might include Tones, Locations, Descendants, Quests, Ordeals/Tests and suitable Obstacles.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>

			<span class="t13ne-facet-embodiment">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-facet-embodiment">
						<strong>Facet Embodiment: </strong> Proficiency
					</summary>
					<section class="t13ne-description">The Conflict Embodies one Facet as a single Proficiency from that Facet. Characters or Descendants that have the Proficiency may be Hooked to that side. This is a very subtle Embodiment when handled gently.</section>
				</details>
			</span>
			 <div class="t13ne-archetypes"><details><summary><strong>Archetype Character: </strong>Necromancer</summary> <details class="t13ne-Persona"><summary><strong>Persona: </strong><span class="t13ne-output">Fiend</span>
      </summary>
      <details class="t13ne-motivation"><summary><strong>Motivation: </strong></summary><span class="t13ne-description">The Fiend is motivated by selfishness, hatred, corruption, and the temptations of amoral living. They are well represented amongst criminal elements, and often seem to delight in simply breaking rules and laws.</span></details>
       <details class="t13ne-avoid"><summary><strong>Avoid: </strong></summary><span class="t13ne-description">The Fiend sees no reason to follow the rules of the rest of society, and will often deliberately break moral codes simply because they are moral codes.</span></details>
       <details class="t13ne-gain-chi"><summary><strong>Gain Chi: </strong></summary><span class="t13ne-description"><span>Gain Chi when you break a rule or law (including etiquette and unwritten rules), act “evil”, rude, or self-important, or succumb to temptations. </span><p>Ordeal gain Chi when you discard a low card to keep a higher one.</p></span></details></details>   <details class="t13ne-core"><summary><strong>Core: </strong><span class="t13ne-output">Doctor</span></summary><span class="t13ne-description">The character may choose one Incarna, when using or trying to affect that Incarna add one level of success.</span></details><details class="t13ne-hitch"><summary><strong>Hitch: </strong><span class="t13ne-output">Paranoia</span></summary><span class="t13ne-description">Too much knowledge can be a bad thing. Knowing that a conspiracy exists can make you see its agents everywhere; no matter how crazy that may make you look to others. For a spy, Paranoia is a perfectly normal response to living amongst the enemy. Most people however are not spies, are not persecuted by shadowy pseudo-governmental organizations, even if they believe they are.</span></details><p class="t13ne-description">Necromancers are a spiritual or mage type who deal exclusively with the dead and Undead.</p></details></div>
			 <span class="t13ne-hook">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-hook">
						<strong>Hook: </strong> Banshee's Wail
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">The Story opens with a warning, usually the warning is a Plot Revelation, but it may traditionally be a warning of a death in the family. The point is to make the PC's a little more afraid.</aside>
					<details class="t13ne-hook-aspect">
						<summary><strong>Hook Aspect: </strong>Geometry</summary>
						<section class="t13ne-description">The Hook targets the Character's Geometry. For example: In order to Hook a Circle Geometry simply offer to fulfil an ambition.</section>
					</details>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-obstacle-fancy"><details class="t13ne-obstacle"><summary><strong>Obstacle:</strong>Wraith</summary><p class="t13ne-description">The way is blocked by a jump-scare, fright, terror or horror of some sort, usually as a 1-3 card Psych attack that affects all present in the Stage simultaneously, and then resets.</p></details></span>
			<span class="t13ne-gain">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-gain">
						<strong>Gain </strong> 
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">A single coin, suitable for paying a ferryman (Yin/Yang Gain).</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-fray">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-fray">
						<strong>Fray: </strong> Insubstantial
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">The Fray is an insubstantial, or trifling matter, but failure may have profound consequences later. An example may be a single question asked by a co-worker, it seems unimportant at the time, but is actually part of an human resources investigation. Usually a Test on a single Facet (A,C,O,Q,V,Z) (although an appropriate Annex might help). The difficulty is usually quite low (between 1-15). Facet Tests are meant to be quick, easy checks, a simple roll. Failures may involve negative Psyche effects.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-snag">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-snag">
						<strong>Snag: </strong> Haunting
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">The Character may see an actual ghost, or may be reminded of a previous situation, drawing comparisons with the current Fray. The Haunting Snag usually has a magical or psychological effect that may often create new Hitches.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-sweeping">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-sweeping">
						<strong>Sweeping: </strong> Boo!
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">A jump scare that isn't harmful to the Characters, like having someone tap on a the window / knock on the door during a tense moment, or having a dog suddenly bark out of sight. Can act to ease Tension, or ratchet up suspense. A Boo! can also be enough to Resolve a Fear Hitch, under the right circumstances.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
		 <details class="t13ne-revelation-fancy"><summary><strong>Revelation</strong></summary><details class="t13ne-about"><summary><strong>About: </strong> Pressed </summary><p class="t13ne-description">The information is about the Pressed Conflict Embodiment, which is often a PC in a game, and is usually the antagonist in a Story.</p></details><details class="t13ne-info"><summary><strong>Info: </strong> Hint </summary><p class="t13ne-description">Not necessarily information, but a pointer in the right direction. There is a lot of crossover with the Clue Wyrd Tarot Card here. Hints are generally a little more direct while a Clue must be worked out or pieced together.</p></details><details class="t13ne-vector"><summary><strong>Vector: </strong> Source </summary><p class="t13ne-description">A Whistle-blower or some credible source for the information is found (like an old article), or a turncoat from the enemy.</p></details><details class="t13ne-alternate"><summary><strong>Alternate: </strong> Hitch </summary><p class="t13ne-description">The information reveals a Hitch. Often this revealed Hitch is added to a Lore.</p></details><details class="t13ne-detail"><summary><strong>Detail: </strong> Specific </summary><p class="t13ne-description">A specific detail is a tiny, but important details. It might include knowledge that a particular Annex or Descendant has a particular Umbral or Nimbed, or that a Character uses a specific Descendant, Proficiency, etc. Such specificity usually has a reason behind it, that may also be revealed.</p></details></details>
		 <details class="t13ne-ordeal-fancy"><summary><strong>Ordeal</strong></summary><p class="t13ne-ordeal-type"><strong>Ordeal-Type </strong> — Solitary Ordeal</p><p class="t13ne-stages"><strong>Stages </strong> — 1</p><p class="t13ne-ordeal-spread"><strong>Ordeal-Spread </strong> — Railroad</p><p class="t13ne-stakes"><strong>Stakes </strong> — Low</p><p class="t13ne-test"><strong>Test </strong> — Cool (Awe)</p><p class="t13ne-obstacles"><strong>Obstacles </strong> — Usually none</p><p class="t13ne-suggested-action"><strong>Suggested-Action </strong> — Walk</p><details class="t13ne-stage"><summary><strong>Stage: </strong> Short Straight </summary><p class="t13ne-description">A short, straight path, street or road, simple preparation or research</p></details><details class="t13ne-motional"><summary><strong>Motional: </strong> Ground Vehicle Dash </summary><p class="t13ne-description">The Ordeal is meant to be done quickly, along the ground in, or on, a vehicle.</p></details><details class="t13ne-fight"><summary><strong>Fight: </strong> Vex Fright </summary><p class="t13ne-description">An aggressive, solitary Vex attacks, usually with a Duck Psych Attack, or a Toxic sting, then it will retreat after the first Phase, if it survives.</p></details></details>
		 </details>
			</div>
				<div class="t13ne-card-lea"><details class="t13ne-lea"><summary><strong>Lea</strong></summary><p class="t13ne-colour"><strong>Path Colour —</strong> Brown/Blue Omnivore</p><p class="t13ne-bulmas"><strong>Allowed Bulmäs —</strong> Broccen, Vermis, Labyrinthine, All-Walkers</p><p class="t13ne-animal-category"><strong>Animal Category —</strong> Microfauna</p><p class="t13ne-animal-example"><strong>Example Animal —</strong> Plankton/Zooplankton / Tardigrade</p><p class="t13ne-notes"><strong>Notes —</strong> Microscopic animal and bacterial paths, often overlooked.</p></details></div></div><div class="t13ne-cardwrap-jsvg" id="cardrewritebox2" data-mode="jsvg" data-wyrd="1" data-yarn="1"><img src="https://www.cjmoseley.dev.cc/wp-content/plugins/t13ne/images/cards-svg/3D.svg" class="t13ne-svgcard"><div class="t13ne-svgpips"><div class="t13ne-textpips">3<!-- write suit--><span class="t13ne-symbol"><span class="t13ne-redcard">♦</span></span><details class="t13ne-suit"><summary></summary><p class="t13ne-name"><strong>Suit: </strong>Diamonds <span class="t13ne-redcard">♦</span></p><p><strong>Ordeal Trump: </strong><span class="t13ne-trump">Preparation</span></p><p class="t13ne-desc"><strong>Description: </strong>Diamonds represent the finer things of life, they are bright and sparkling. In the Tarot, Diamonds are replaced with coins, which represent  not just Wealth, but elemental Earth as well.</p><p class="t13ne-tarot"><strong>Tarot Meaning: </strong>Travel, Money, Business, Contracts, Deals, Voyages.</p><p class="t13ne-otext"><strong>Ordeal Text: </strong>Ordeal Types: Travel (e.g. Chase, Race) / Creativity (e.g. Smithing, Painting). Diamonds act as Trump for the following Action Types: Art, Building, Charming, Crafting, Driving, Emoting, Forgiving, Keeping Your Cool, Listening, Meditation, Motion, Prayer, Preparation, Research, Sprinting and Working in general. Any type of action can be prepared with a Diamond.</p><p class="t13ne-parry"><strong>Parry: </strong><strong>Seize the initative:</strong> The cards of the original attack (less the matched card) are immediately added to the Defender’s Ordeal Pool. They may then play as many cards as their Descendant/Annex normally plays including the Parry card (1-4) usually into one of their Style Reserve, and then immediately discard any cards over Pool Limit.</p><p class="t13ne-soak"><strong>Soak: </strong><strong>Ready:</strong> The matched Attack card is immediately Prepared by the Defender (as they wish). The Soak card is discarded.</p></details></div></div><div class="t13ne-svg-facet"><div>Craft</div></div><div class="t13ne-wound"><div><strong class="t13ne-woundname">Distract</strong><details><summary></summary><strong>Distract</strong><p class="t13ne-base-pips">Base Pips to summon: 2</p><p class="t13ne-min-pips">Minimum Pips Required: 2</p><p class="t13ne-desc">The Target is distracted by the attack.</p><details class="t13ne-roll"><summary>Rules</summary><strong>-6</strong> Score on next Roll the Character makes and then Discard.</details><details class="t13ne-ordeal"><summary>Ordeal Rules</summary>Lose a Success Level <ul><li>Draw -1 Card on next Draw and then Discard.</li><li> Or <strong>-3</strong> Pips to the next Card played and Discard.</li><li>Or <strong>+3</strong> Pips to the next Attack on the Character, then Discard.</li></ul></details><details class="t13ne-psych"><summary>Psych Wounds</summary><details class="t13ne-negative"><summary>Negative Psych</summary><p class="t13ne-normal"><strong>Normal</strong>Mild Emotional influence (tears in eyes, heightened heart rate, etc) until Stabilized / Discarded.</p><p class="t13ne-extreme"><strong>Extreme</strong>Must scream, swear, or otherwise express the emotion until Stabilized or Discarded.</p></details><details class="t13ne-positive"><summary>Positive Psych</summary><p class="t13ne-normal"><strong>Normal</strong>Mild Emotional Influence (Smile, Pupil Dilation, Eyebrow twitches, etc) until Stabilised / Discarded.</p><p class="t13ne-extreme"><strong>Extreme</strong>Must speak, laugh, gasp, moan, tick, or otherwise express the emotion until Stabilised or Discarded.</p></details></details></details></div></div><div class="t13ne-tarot-svg"><details><summary class="t13ne-wyrdtarot"><strong> Wyrd Tarot: </strong> Proficiency</summary><p class="t13ne-normal"><strong> Normal: </strong>Player gains an additional, applicable Proficiency (May fill a slot).</p><p class="t13ne-crossed"><strong> Crossed: </strong>-1 Prof Die on Target's next roll.</p></details></div><div class="t13ne-yarn-fancy">
			<details><summary><strong>Yarn</strong> <br>The Grindstone</summary>
			<span class="t13ne-description">The Grindstone is the card of hard work, wearing down opposition, or training yourself to a finely sharpened point. It is a card of determined hard-won success for little reward or bitter, grinding defeat.</span>
			<span class="t13ne-significator">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-significator"><strong>Significator</strong></summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-plot"><strong>Plot: </strong> The Grindstone Plot is one that seeks to grind down the Characters that it Hooks, it tries to exhaust and wear them down, not so that they may be destroyed, but be improved, sharpened and refined. It knocks a Character down so that they may build themselves up again. All Tests and Ordeals the Plot creates will have twice the Difficulty, or increased Stakes.</aside>
					<aside class="t13ne-char"><strong>Character: </strong> The Grindstone Significator indicates a  Character who works hard for what they have. They Gain Yin by doing any routine task, such as washing, cleaning, maintenance, etc. They Gain Yang by working up a sweat, doing hard work or exercise. They Gain Chi whenever they receive any payment for their work, goods or services. Grindstone Characters additionally gain a +1 Success Level whenever they are faced with gruelling working conditions or circumstances.</aside>
					<aside class="t13ne-scene"><strong>Scene: </strong> The Grindstone Plot is one that seeks to grind down the Characters that it Hooks, it tries to exhaust and wear them down, not so that they may be destroyed, but be improved, sharpened and refined. It knocks a Character down so that they may build themselves up again. All Tests and Ordeals the Plot creates will have twice the Difficulty, or increased Stakes.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-scene">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-scene-type">
						<strong>Scene: </strong> Ordeal
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">Ordeals test Characters in a complex way (more than a single dice roll or a Facet Test). Ordeals can range from Low Stakes Ordeals (like doing the Crossword), to High Stakes Ordeals, such as solving a crossword puzzle, while exchanging gunfire with enemies, during a car chase. Action generally, and Fights in particular, involve an Ordeal. Ordeals are usually created by playing a number of cards to define everything about the Ordeal (from the Type, Stakes, Number of Obstacles you should draw and add, to a suggested action that could be taken, or a description of a Stage).</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-conflict-embodiment">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-conflict-embodiment">
						<strong>Conflict Embodiment: </strong> External
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">One side of the Conflict (usually the Pressed side) is embodied within the Characters (sometimes an NPC), the Dominant side is embodied as something external to all the Characters. Embodiments might include Tones, Locations, Descendants, Quests, Ordeals/Tests and suitable Obstacles.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>

			<span class="t13ne-facet-embodiment">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-facet-embodiment">
						<strong>Facet Embodiment: </strong> Tone
					</summary>
					<section class="t13ne-description">The Conflict Embodies one Facet as the Tone of the Scene, Act or Story. This can be an easy way to embody some Facets, but can be restrictive and may not suit your style. Use with care.</section>
				</details>
			</span>
			 <div class="t13ne-archetypes"><details><summary><strong>Archetype Character: </strong>Tradesman</summary> <details class="t13ne-Persona"><summary><strong>Persona: </strong><span class="t13ne-output">Smith</span>
      </summary>
      <details class="t13ne-motivation"><summary><strong>Motivation: </strong></summary><span class="t13ne-description">Smiths are driven to create things (usually Descendants, although not always). They may sell the Descendants for a profit, but aren't interested in that side of things as much as creating the best Descendants (or otherwise) possible, be they weapons, armour, buildings, spaceships, or cakes.</span></details>
       <details class="t13ne-avoid"><summary><strong>Avoid: </strong></summary><span class="t13ne-description">Smiths tend to avoid waste, any unused materials are recycled into a new project.</span></details>
       <details class="t13ne-gain-chi"><summary><strong>Gain Chi: </strong></summary><span class="t13ne-description"><span>Gain Chi when you spend (Craft Boon as Yarn Limit Duration) time with your creations, complete an Ordeal Stage in a Creation Ordeal, or simply create something.</span><p>Ordeal gain Chi when you use a Descendant you have created in Ordeal.</p></span></details></details>   <details class="t13ne-core"><summary><strong>Core: </strong><span class="t13ne-output">Worker</span></summary><span class="t13ne-description">If the character takes their time at some task they commonly do, they will automatically completely succeed (during an Ordeal add 2 Success Levels to each draw) for a cost of one Chi.</span></details><details class="t13ne-hitch"><summary><strong>Hitch: </strong><span class="t13ne-output">Defensive</span></summary><span class="t13ne-description">Some people are quick to strike back when they feel threatened. It can become a big issue for them, bordering on Paranoia, where they feel they must respond to criticism, or become hyper-vigilant, or even hyper-apologetic.</span></details><p class="t13ne-description">The Tradesman, be they Carpenter, Plumber, Builder, Electrician, or jobbing Programmer.</p></details></div>
			 <span class="t13ne-hook">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-hook">
						<strong>Hook: </strong> Working Start
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">The Conflict engages with a Character's current task. This is best expressed in TV by the cold start or In Media Res, that involves the Character's day-to-day job.</aside>
					<details class="t13ne-hook-aspect">
						<summary><strong>Hook Aspect: </strong>Geometry</summary>
						<section class="t13ne-description">The Hook targets the Character's Geometry. For example: In order to Hook a Circle Geometry simply offer to fulfil an ambition.</section>
					</details>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-obstacle-fancy"><details class="t13ne-obstacle"><summary><strong>Obstacle:</strong>Hard Work</summary><p class="t13ne-description">The way is blocked by debris or stacked items that must be moved out of the way by a proper application of elbow grease.</p></details></span>
			<span class="t13ne-gain">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-gain">
						<strong>Gain </strong> 
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">Wage (Normal Chi Gain or payment, may be daily, weekly, monthly or yearly)</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-fray">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-fray">
						<strong>Fray: </strong> Working
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">The Test is like a Facet Test (A,C,O,Q,V,Z), but the Difficulty is a little higher (Diff 16+). These Tests are simple, and intended to just check some aspect of the Character, they represent a normal day at work, usually.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-snag">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-snag">
						<strong>Snag: </strong> Drudgery
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">Boredom is the enemy in this Snag. Whatever the Fray, the Snag is either the Fray or the aftermath is tedious. The Snag will inevitably have a negative Psychological effect, which can leave people despondent, dismayed and even suicidal.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-sweeping">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-sweeping">
						<strong>Sweeping: </strong> Wages
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">A Wage (or bonus) is a single Cash (Chi) Gain, Wages are a Skill Treasure Descendant</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
		 <details class="t13ne-revelation-fancy"><summary><strong>Revelation</strong></summary><details class="t13ne-about"><summary><strong>About: </strong> Pressed </summary><p class="t13ne-description">The information is about the Pressed Conflict Embodiment, which is often a PC in a game, and is usually the antagonist in a Story.</p></details><details class="t13ne-info"><summary><strong>Info: </strong> Aspects </summary><p class="t13ne-description">Information about Aspects of the situation or element, perhaps an exploitable weakness, or a warning about a strength.</p></details><details class="t13ne-vector"><summary><strong>Vector: </strong> Details </summary><p class="t13ne-description">The information is hidden as an easily overlooked detail. Like an addressed, but empty envelope, or a circled phone number in the phone book. Such Details sometimes require a "Spot Hidden" Key (or a "Knowledge" Orthodox or Heresy, as appropriate) Test to reveal them. Maybe it won't be noticed until later.</p></details><details class="t13ne-alternate"><summary><strong>Alternate: </strong> Skills </summary><p class="t13ne-description">The information reveals some knowledge of the Skills available to the Narrative Element. This may simply be a Lore Skill.</p></details><details class="t13ne-detail"><summary><strong>Detail: </strong> Weakness </summary><p class="t13ne-description">A weakness detail may be a Hitch, or some Umbral condition, or just knowledge of a low Facet Boon.</p></details></details>
		 <details class="t13ne-ordeal-fancy"><summary><strong>Ordeal</strong></summary><p class="t13ne-ordeal-type"><strong>Ordeal-Type </strong> — Solitary Ordeal</p><p class="t13ne-stages"><strong>Stages </strong> — 1</p><p class="t13ne-ordeal-spread"><strong>Ordeal-Spread </strong> — Railroad</p><p class="t13ne-stakes"><strong>Stakes </strong> — Medium</p><p class="t13ne-test"><strong>Test </strong> — Crafting (Craft)</p><p class="t13ne-obstacles"><strong>Obstacles </strong> — Usually none</p><p class="t13ne-suggested-action"><strong>Suggested-Action </strong> — Ride</p><details class="t13ne-stage"><summary><strong>Stage: </strong> Sharp corner </summary><p class="t13ne-description">A sharp corner, change of direction, preparation or research</p></details><details class="t13ne-motional"><summary><strong>Motional: </strong> Aerial Vehicle Dash </summary><p class="t13ne-description">The Ordeal is meant to involve travel quickly in an aerial vehicle, or somewhere high.</p></details><details class="t13ne-fight"><summary><strong>Fight: </strong> Stand-off </summary><p class="t13ne-description">Two or more forces that are evenly matched in size and power can form a Stand-off.</p></details></details>
		 </details>
			</div>
				<div class="t13ne-card-lea"><details class="t13ne-lea"><summary><strong>Lea</strong></summary><p class="t13ne-colour"><strong>Path Colour —</strong> Brown/Blue Omnivore</p><p class="t13ne-bulmas"><strong>Allowed Bulmäs —</strong> Broccen, Vermis, Labyrinthine, All-Walkers</p><p class="t13ne-animal-category"><strong>Animal Category —</strong> Meiofauna</p><p class="t13ne-animal-example"><strong>Example Animal —</strong> Small Nematode worms/shrimps</p><p class="t13ne-notes"><strong>Notes —</strong> A lot of parasite paths at this level</p></details></div></div><div class="t13ne-cardwrap-jsvg" id="cardrewritebox3" data-mode="jsvg" data-wyrd="1" data-yarn="1"><img src="https://www.cjmoseley.dev.cc/wp-content/plugins/t13ne/images/cards-svg/4D.svg" class="t13ne-svgcard"><div class="t13ne-svgpips"><div class="t13ne-textpips">4<!-- write suit--><span class="t13ne-symbol"><span class="t13ne-redcard">♦</span></span><details class="t13ne-suit"><summary></summary><p class="t13ne-name"><strong>Suit: </strong>Diamonds <span class="t13ne-redcard">♦</span></p><p><strong>Ordeal Trump: </strong><span class="t13ne-trump">Preparation</span></p><p class="t13ne-desc"><strong>Description: </strong>Diamonds represent the finer things of life, they are bright and sparkling. In the Tarot, Diamonds are replaced with coins, which represent  not just Wealth, but elemental Earth as well.</p><p class="t13ne-tarot"><strong>Tarot Meaning: </strong>Travel, Money, Business, Contracts, Deals, Voyages.</p><p class="t13ne-otext"><strong>Ordeal Text: </strong>Ordeal Types: Travel (e.g. Chase, Race) / Creativity (e.g. Smithing, Painting). Diamonds act as Trump for the following Action Types: Art, Building, Charming, Crafting, Driving, Emoting, Forgiving, Keeping Your Cool, Listening, Meditation, Motion, Prayer, Preparation, Research, Sprinting and Working in general. Any type of action can be prepared with a Diamond.</p><p class="t13ne-parry"><strong>Parry: </strong><strong>Seize the initative:</strong> The cards of the original attack (less the matched card) are immediately added to the Defender’s Ordeal Pool. They may then play as many cards as their Descendant/Annex normally plays including the Parry card (1-4) usually into one of their Style Reserve, and then immediately discard any cards over Pool Limit.</p><p class="t13ne-soak"><strong>Soak: </strong><strong>Ready:</strong> The matched Attack card is immediately Prepared by the Defender (as they wish). The Soak card is discarded.</p></details></div></div><div class="t13ne-svg-facet"><div>Orthodox</div></div><div class="t13ne-wound"><div><strong class="t13ne-woundname">Distract</strong><details><summary></summary><strong>Distract</strong><p class="t13ne-base-pips">Base Pips to summon: 2</p><p class="t13ne-min-pips">Minimum Pips Required: 2</p><p class="t13ne-desc">The Target is distracted by the attack.</p><details class="t13ne-roll"><summary>Rules</summary><strong>-6</strong> Score on next Roll the Character makes and then Discard.</details><details class="t13ne-ordeal"><summary>Ordeal Rules</summary>Lose a Success Level <ul><li>Draw -1 Card on next Draw and then Discard.</li><li> Or <strong>-3</strong> Pips to the next Card played and Discard.</li><li>Or <strong>+3</strong> Pips to the next Attack on the Character, then Discard.</li></ul></details><details class="t13ne-psych"><summary>Psych Wounds</summary><details class="t13ne-negative"><summary>Negative Psych</summary><p class="t13ne-normal"><strong>Normal</strong>Mild Emotional influence (tears in eyes, heightened heart rate, etc) until Stabilized / Discarded.</p><p class="t13ne-extreme"><strong>Extreme</strong>Must scream, swear, or otherwise express the emotion until Stabilized or Discarded.</p></details><details class="t13ne-positive"><summary>Positive Psych</summary><p class="t13ne-normal"><strong>Normal</strong>Mild Emotional Influence (Smile, Pupil Dilation, Eyebrow twitches, etc) until Stabilised / Discarded.</p><p class="t13ne-extreme"><strong>Extreme</strong>Must speak, laugh, gasp, moan, tick, or otherwise express the emotion until Stabilised or Discarded.</p></details></details></details></div></div><div class="t13ne-tarot-svg"><details><summary class="t13ne-wyrdtarot"><strong> Wyrd Tarot: </strong> Discourage</summary><p class="t13ne-normal"><strong> Normal: </strong>-1 Success Level on any Psychology Test</p><p class="t13ne-crossed"><strong> Crossed: </strong>+1 Success Level on any Psychology Test</p></details></div><div class="t13ne-yarn-fancy">
			<details><summary><strong>Yarn</strong> <br>The Book</summary>
			<span class="t13ne-description">The Book is the card of knowledge, authority and responsibility. Books are information in permanent form, frozen thoughts, and as such are technology, language and magic. Books are granted authority, and define monotheistic religions, for example, but they may sometimes contain mistakes (in translation, spelling, or printing), or the author's opinions presented as facts, that can lead one astray. By the Book Characters act with this same occasionally flawed authority.</span>
			<span class="t13ne-significator">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-significator"><strong>Significator</strong></summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-plot"><strong>Plot: </strong> The Book Plot indicates a Plot that is reworking some other work. It may be a retelling of an older story, or a retelling of history, re-imagined in a new place or time. The new Plot will always have differences to the original (often completely inverting the Conflict or Final Resolution), but is fundamentally the same thing. Book Plots often rely heavily on Revelations and Characterizations in their Conflict, and have no hard limits on Extras for this reason.</aside>
					<aside class="t13ne-char"><strong>Character: </strong> The Book Significator indicates a Character who does things by the Book. They Gain Yin when they remind others of the rules, and point out rule-breaking, Gain Yang when they follow or enforce proper procedures and rules, and Gain Chi when they are respectful of others and polite. The Book also grants them a +1 Success Level if they break a rule.</aside>
					<aside class="t13ne-scene"><strong>Scene: </strong> The Book Plot indicates a Plot that is reworking some other work. It may be a retelling of an older story, or a retelling of history, re-imagined in a new place or time. The new Plot will always have differences to the original (often completely inverting the Conflict or Final Resolution), but is fundamentally the same thing. Book Plots often rely heavily on Revelations and Characterizations in their Conflict, and have no hard limits on Extras for this reason.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-scene">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-scene-type">
						<strong>Scene: </strong> Gain
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">Gains give something to one of the Characters to help move the Story on. Sometimes this might be payment for a job they did, or it might be a discovery that will directly connect to the Story. This may be information. It is normal to play a separate card to define the actual Gain that is made.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-conflict-embodiment">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-conflict-embodiment">
						<strong>Conflict Embodiment: </strong> External
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">One side of the Conflict (usually the Pressed side) is embodied within the Characters (sometimes an NPC), the Dominant side is embodied as something external to all the Characters. Embodiments might include Tones, Locations, Descendants, Quests, Ordeals/Tests and suitable Obstacles.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>

			<span class="t13ne-facet-embodiment">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-facet-embodiment">
						<strong>Facet Embodiment: </strong> Ordeal/Test
					</summary>
					<section class="t13ne-description">The Conflict Embodies one Facet as an Ordeal (or Test). Simple and effective, but can be obvious and boring if used too often. A good embodiment during a Warp Fray, not very good across a whole Story.</section>
				</details>
			</span>
			 <div class="t13ne-archetypes"><details><summary><strong>Archetype Character: </strong>Teacher</summary> <details class="t13ne-Persona"><summary><strong>Persona: </strong><span class="t13ne-output">Mentor</span>
      </summary>
      <details class="t13ne-motivation"><summary><strong>Motivation: </strong></summary><span class="t13ne-description">The Mentor is motivated by sharing and teaching others their knowledge. They often care deeply about their student's well-being, and may feel very responsible for them.</span></details>
       <details class="t13ne-avoid"><summary><strong>Avoid: </strong></summary><span class="t13ne-description">The Mentor will always try to avoid losing their student's respect. A great mentor will never scold the student, instead turning any mistake they may make into a learning moment.</span></details>
       <details class="t13ne-gain-chi"><summary><strong>Gain Chi: </strong></summary><span class="t13ne-description"><span>Gain Chi when you educate, give advice, help, instruction, or Lecture another.</span><p>Ordeal gain Chi when you make a Lecture attack with or prepare a ♦. </p></span></details></details>   <details class="t13ne-core"><summary><strong>Core: </strong><span class="t13ne-output">Philosopher</span></summary><span class="t13ne-description">Additional I-Ching (can be changing or unchanging). The Player may choose any Hexagram that their Facets might produce (choosing Yin or Yang for Boon 13 Facets), or the Referee may assign one or two Hexagrams or allow both Hexagrams to be randomly rolled.</span></details><details class="t13ne-hitch"><summary><strong>Hitch: </strong><span class="t13ne-output">Impoverished</span></summary><span class="t13ne-description">Poor, disenfranchised or missing something in some way</span></details><p class="t13ne-description">Teachers are those who Teach. They may be religious people or school teachers, or they can even be Professors (although not Archetype Professors). Teachers usually have at least a Talent in what they Teach, but not always, their true Power lies in Teaching things they don't even know themselves.</p></details></div>
			 <span class="t13ne-hook">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-hook">
						<strong>Hook: </strong> Tone, Theme or Thesis
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">The Conflict hooks the Characters by a powerful scene that sets the Tone for the Story, or specifies the Theme or Tone of the COnflict with an immediate Revelation.</aside>
					<details class="t13ne-hook-aspect">
						<summary><strong>Hook Aspect: </strong>Geometry</summary>
						<section class="t13ne-description">The Hook targets the Character's Geometry. For example: In order to Hook a Circle Geometry simply offer to fulfil an ambition.</section>
					</details>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-obstacle-fancy"><details class="t13ne-obstacle"><summary><strong>Obstacle:</strong>Specialist Knowledge</summary><p class="t13ne-description">The way ahead isn't blocked exactly, but getting past requires some specialist knowledge, maybe you need to know what that sign says, or how to turn off the electricity... Knowledge is usually a specific required Proficiency.</p></details></span>
			<span class="t13ne-gain">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-gain">
						<strong>Gain </strong> 
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">Knowledge (Lore or Learning Descendant of Skill or Talent Level)</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-fray">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-fray">
						<strong>Fray: </strong> Research
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">Usually a Low Stakes Ordeal that involves a Character finding out some information, it may involve reading (webpages, books, articles, papers), visiting a Location (usually a Library, Archive, or a website), asking people questions, in interviews or by polling, or (more rarely) conducting experiments.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-snag">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-snag">
						<strong>Snag: </strong> Missing Page
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">The Book Snag is a missing page or piece of the puzzle. They may have done the research, or reached their goal, only to discover they don't have the final item they require. They may be able to find another copy, or someone who has read that page though, so all is not lost.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-sweeping">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-sweeping">
						<strong>Sweeping: </strong> RTFM
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">You find or are directed to a book, tome or manual that can give you a Gain of a Teaching in a Skill, etc. Example, after asking why a green light started flashing on his flight suit he is handed the Operators Manual by the Technician in the bay. They will still need to find time to read the manual...</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
		 <details class="t13ne-revelation-fancy"><summary><strong>Revelation</strong></summary><details class="t13ne-about"><summary><strong>About: </strong> Pressed </summary><p class="t13ne-description">The information is about the Pressed Conflict Embodiment, which is often a PC in a game, and is usually the antagonist in a Story.</p></details><details class="t13ne-info"><summary><strong>Info: </strong> Conclusions </summary><p class="t13ne-description">The available information points to a number of potential conclusions. Usually only one of them is true. You should prepare at least two different potential conclusions, and make the information point at both.</p></details><details class="t13ne-vector"><summary><strong>Vector: </strong> Minutia </summary><p class="t13ne-description">The information is encoded in the smallest details, a slipped word, or glossed over point, that 'Just one more thing...' question that busts everything open.</p></details><details class="t13ne-alternate"><summary><strong>Alternate: </strong> Chi </summary><p class="t13ne-description">The information gives some idea of the Chi cost (or Purchase Price), stored Chi (Wealth and Experience), or methods of Gaining Chi (ususally the Persona) of the Embodiment.</p></details><details class="t13ne-detail"><summary><strong>Detail: </strong> Linguistic </summary><p class="t13ne-description">A detail about the language (and culture) of a people or area. Such as the Western Orcs having no word for "<em>Mercy</em>", but Southern Orcs have the words "<em>Lenience</em>" and "<em>Ruth</em>" which mean the same thing.</p></details></details>
		 <details class="t13ne-ordeal-fancy"><summary><strong>Ordeal</strong></summary><p class="t13ne-ordeal-type"><strong>Ordeal-Type </strong> — Solitary Ordeal</p><p class="t13ne-stages"><strong>Stages </strong> — 1</p><p class="t13ne-ordeal-spread"><strong>Ordeal-Spread </strong> — Railroad</p><p class="t13ne-stakes"><strong>Stakes </strong> — High</p><p class="t13ne-test"><strong>Test </strong> — Fixing (Craft)</p><p class="t13ne-obstacles"><strong>Obstacles </strong> — Usually none</p><p class="t13ne-suggested-action"><strong>Suggested-Action </strong> — Jog</p><details class="t13ne-stage"><summary><strong>Stage: </strong> Small rise </summary><p class="t13ne-description">A short, but slight rise, a slope, step or few steps.</p></details><details class="t13ne-motional"><summary><strong>Motional: </strong> Ground Riding Dash </summary><p class="t13ne-description">Working alone, but riding a beast or a vehicle, quickly. Try to get as far as possible.</p></details><details class="t13ne-fight"><summary><strong>Fight: </strong> Assault </summary><p class="t13ne-description">Entrenched or armoured opponents that are greater in number, but lower in quality (start with Extras) or have lesser equipment than the attacking force.</p></details></details>
		 </details>
			</div>
				<div class="t13ne-card-lea"><details class="t13ne-lea"><summary><strong>Lea</strong></summary><p class="t13ne-colour"><strong>Path Colour —</strong> Brown/Blue Omnivore</p><p class="t13ne-bulmas"><strong>Allowed Bulmäs —</strong> Broccen, Vermis, Labyrinthine, All-Walkers</p><p class="t13ne-animal-category"><strong>Animal Category —</strong> Mesofauna</p><p class="t13ne-animal-example"><strong>Example Animal —</strong> Greenfly/Midge / Mosquito</p><p class="t13ne-notes"><strong>Notes —</strong> Tiny Arthropods, often larva forms of larger arthropods</p></details></div></div><div class="t13ne-cardwrap-jsvg" id="cardrewritebox4" data-mode="jsvg" data-wyrd="1" data-yarn="1"><img src="https://www.cjmoseley.dev.cc/wp-content/plugins/t13ne/images/cards-svg/5D.svg" class="t13ne-svgcard"><div class="t13ne-svgpips"><div class="t13ne-textpips">5<!-- write suit--><span class="t13ne-symbol"><span class="t13ne-redcard">♦</span></span><details class="t13ne-suit"><summary></summary><p class="t13ne-name"><strong>Suit: </strong>Diamonds <span class="t13ne-redcard">♦</span></p><p><strong>Ordeal Trump: </strong><span class="t13ne-trump">Preparation</span></p><p class="t13ne-desc"><strong>Description: </strong>Diamonds represent the finer things of life, they are bright and sparkling. In the Tarot, Diamonds are replaced with coins, which represent  not just Wealth, but elemental Earth as well.</p><p class="t13ne-tarot"><strong>Tarot Meaning: </strong>Travel, Money, Business, Contracts, Deals, Voyages.</p><p class="t13ne-otext"><strong>Ordeal Text: </strong>Ordeal Types: Travel (e.g. Chase, Race) / Creativity (e.g. Smithing, Painting). Diamonds act as Trump for the following Action Types: Art, Building, Charming, Crafting, Driving, Emoting, Forgiving, Keeping Your Cool, Listening, Meditation, Motion, Prayer, Preparation, Research, Sprinting and Working in general. Any type of action can be prepared with a Diamond.</p><p class="t13ne-parry"><strong>Parry: </strong><strong>Seize the initative:</strong> The cards of the original attack (less the matched card) are immediately added to the Defender’s Ordeal Pool. They may then play as many cards as their Descendant/Annex normally plays including the Parry card (1-4) usually into one of their Style Reserve, and then immediately discard any cards over Pool Limit.</p><p class="t13ne-soak"><strong>Soak: </strong><strong>Ready:</strong> The matched Attack card is immediately Prepared by the Defender (as they wish). The Soak card is discarded.</p></details></div></div><div class="t13ne-svg-facet"><div>Quiet</div></div><div class="t13ne-wound"><div><strong class="t13ne-woundname">Flesh</strong><details><summary></summary><strong>Flesh</strong><p class="t13ne-base-pips">Base Pips to summon: 5</p><p class="t13ne-min-pips">Minimum Pips Required: 5</p><p class="t13ne-desc">The Target is wounded and must stop and aknowledge that fact.</p><details class="t13ne-roll"><summary>Rules</summary>Lose one <strong>die</strong> from each pool on the next roll you make (e.g.3d6 + 2d10+5 becomes 2d6 + 1d10+5) then Discard</details><details class="t13ne-ordeal"><summary>Ordeal Rules</summary>Discard an Ordeal card from the next Full-Action or aggravate another Wound 1 Level and then Discard. </details><details class="t13ne-psych"><summary>Psych Wounds</summary><details class="t13ne-negative"><summary>Negative Psych</summary><p class="t13ne-normal"><strong>Normal</strong>Cannot approach source of negative emotion until Stabilized / Discarded</p><p class="t13ne-extreme"><strong>Extreme</strong>Paralysed (All Proficiency Dice are lost) until Stabilised / Discarded.</p></details><details class="t13ne-positive"><summary>Positive Psych</summary><p class="t13ne-normal"><strong>Normal</strong>Unwilling to leave source of positive emotion until discarded</p><p class="t13ne-extreme"><strong>Extreme</strong>Stimulated/Excited. Discard immediately, along with all GRT and RT Chips and act immediately (still with the dice or card penalties).</p></details></details></details></div></div><div class="t13ne-tarot-svg"><details><summary class="t13ne-wyrdtarot"><strong> Wyrd Tarot: </strong> Feint</summary><p class="t13ne-normal"><strong> Normal: </strong>Automatically apply 1d6 Distract Wounds to Target</p><p class="t13ne-crossed"><strong> Crossed: </strong>Gain a hint from the Ref</p></details></div><div class="t13ne-yarn-fancy">
			<details><summary><strong>Yarn</strong> <br>The Forest</summary>
			<span class="t13ne-description">Far from the City, beyond the farms lie the deep, dark woods, the ancient forests of the world. This is the card of quiet woodlands and quiet yearnings, the Forests are places of plenty, but they are also places of darkness. Animals that know no fear of man haunt these places. It is a card of loneliness, abandonment, and very real needs — such as comfort, warmth and security.</span>
			<span class="t13ne-significator">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-significator"><strong>Significator</strong></summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-plot"><strong>Plot: </strong> The Forest Plot is about making the Hooked Characters learn their place in the greater world. Often they learn their place is not where they thought it was (and it certainly isn't the Forest itself). In the deep, dark forest all Stakes are Higher than you might expect.</aside>
					<aside class="t13ne-char"><strong>Character: </strong> The Forest Significator indicates a Character who is uncomfortable in normal society, they Gain Yin when they are silent or alone. Gain Yang when they walk away, leave the path, or sniff the air. They Gain Chi when they enjoy the elements directly upon their body, wind in their hair, sun on their face. They also gain a +1 Success Level when in the shadows of trees.</aside>
					<aside class="t13ne-scene"><strong>Scene: </strong> The Forest Plot is about making the Hooked Characters learn their place in the greater world. Often they learn their place is not where they thought it was (and it certainly isn't the Forest itself). In the deep, dark forest all Stakes are Higher than you might expect.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-scene">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-scene-type">
						<strong>Scene: </strong> Revelation
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">Revelations reveal something about the Conflict or Plot to the Characters. Typically a Revelation tells the Characters something about the Villain, or the Prize they are after. Revelations usually consist of 2-5 cards that define: What the information is about, what the actual info is, the vector the Revelation is revealed by, and per haps some Alternative Information that might be revealed (in case you can't use the Info), and a detail that is involved somehow.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-conflict-embodiment">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-conflict-embodiment">
						<strong>Conflict Embodiment: </strong> External
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">One side of the Conflict (usually the Pressed side) is embodied within the Characters (sometimes an NPC), the Dominant side is embodied as something external to all the Characters. Embodiments might include Tones, Locations, Descendants, Quests, Ordeals/Tests and suitable Obstacles.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>

			<span class="t13ne-facet-embodiment">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-facet-embodiment">
						<strong>Facet Embodiment: </strong> Ordeal/Test
					</summary>
					<section class="t13ne-description">The Conflict Embodies one Facet as an Ordeal (or Test). Simple and effective, but can be obvious and boring if used too often. A good embodiment during a Warp Fray, not very good across a whole Story.</section>
				</details>
			</span>
			 <div class="t13ne-archetypes"><details><summary><strong>Archetype Character: </strong>Woodsman</summary> <details class="t13ne-Persona"><summary><strong>Persona: </strong><span class="t13ne-output">Hermit</span>
      </summary>
      <details class="t13ne-motivation"><summary><strong>Motivation: </strong></summary><span class="t13ne-description">The Hermit is motivated by either a sense of spiritual purity, or an asocial nature. They do not feel they gain anything by conversation, and may believe they lose something when they interact with others.</span></details>
       <details class="t13ne-avoid"><summary><strong>Avoid: </strong></summary><span class="t13ne-description">The Hermit will avoid crowds and conversations where possible, some will even takes vows of silence or holy orders in order to separate themselves from society.</span></details>
       <details class="t13ne-gain-chi"><summary><strong>Gain Chi: </strong></summary><span class="t13ne-description"><span>Gain Chi when you act silently, selfishly or alone. </span><p>Ordeal gain Chi when you draw a ♦ </p></span></details></details>   <details class="t13ne-core"><summary><strong>Core: </strong><span class="t13ne-output">Consumer</span></summary><span class="t13ne-description">Gain Chi when you consume something (food, cigarette, pills, drugs, vapes, money, patches, credits, alcohol, syringes, human hearts, bullets, souls, etc -- pick one).</span></details><details class="t13ne-hitch"><summary><strong>Hitch: </strong><span class="t13ne-output">Fatigued</span></summary><span class="t13ne-description">This hitch covers everything from laziness to sickness, some people and things just seem tired or useless most of the time.</span></details><p class="t13ne-description">The Woodsman is a forest worker or hunter, commonly found with an axe, a bow, or in modern times a chainsaw, and perhaps a shotgun or hunters rifle. The Woodsman usually has a family to support and is eager to return to them with food or having earned money working in the woods. They always have wilderness survival skills and often a high Craft.</p></details></div>
			 <span class="t13ne-hook">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-hook">
						<strong>Hook: </strong> Witch's Cottage
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">Traditionally the Witch's Cottage is a run down shack hidden in the woods. It is thought that no one lives there any more, at least until the first child went missing. The Conflict begins with a missing child, or a strange ruin found of the beaten path.</aside>
					<details class="t13ne-hook-aspect">
						<summary><strong>Hook Aspect: </strong>Geometry</summary>
						<section class="t13ne-description">The Hook targets the Character's Geometry. For example: In order to Hook a Circle Geometry simply offer to fulfil an ambition.</section>
					</details>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-obstacle-fancy"><details class="t13ne-obstacle"><summary><strong>Obstacle:</strong>Plants</summary><p class="t13ne-description">The way is blocked, by plants, such as roses, poison ivy, thorns, hawthorn hedges, stinging nettles, holly bushes, or some other "unpleasant" plant.</p></details></span>
			<span class="t13ne-gain">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-gain">
						<strong>Gain </strong> 
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">Serenity, the Character finds a small Location where they can relax and meditate. This Location can help them to rest and recover more quickly from stress and negative effects.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-fray">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-fray">
						<strong>Fray: </strong> Forced March
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">Usually a Medium Stakes Solitary Motional Ordeal, like hurrying to catch the bus, racing to the airport, getting the shopping done before the dinner party, or getting an army through the Forest before nightfall. A Forced March Ordeal usually involves an Obstacle or two.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-snag">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-snag">
						<strong>Snag: </strong> Dangerous Shadows
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">It is dark in the forest and difficult to see what is going on. Dangers lurk in the Shadows as well, from thorns and brambles that catch and scratch to the Big Bad Wolf. Even lighting fires for light will quickly increase the danger as a forest fire blazes around you. The Snag can either add additional Obstacles, darkness, dangerous conditions and/or enemies, to the Fray, and raise the Stakes; or it creates a second short Higher Stakes Ordeal immediately after it.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-sweeping">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-sweeping">
						<strong>Sweeping: </strong> The Enchanted Forest
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">The Enchanted Forest is a place of transformation. The Character that enters the Enchanted Forest has no pressing need to act, they may eat, drink, heal, rest and relax. Magic lies in the Enchanted Forest and the Character may return having had a Revelation, or having made a Gain, as well as having healed.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
		 <details class="t13ne-revelation-fancy"><summary><strong>Revelation</strong></summary><details class="t13ne-about"><summary><strong>About: </strong> Pressed </summary><p class="t13ne-description">The information is about the Pressed Conflict Embodiment, which is often a PC in a game, and is usually the antagonist in a Story.</p></details><details class="t13ne-info"><summary><strong>Info: </strong> Which? </summary><p class="t13ne-description">Information that reveals which Conclusion or option is correct. Like which way the next piece of the puzzle is, which wire should be cut, or which of your many enemies is actually on your trail.</p></details><details class="t13ne-vector"><summary><strong>Vector: </strong> Data </summary><p class="t13ne-description">Data reveals the truth. It might be a computer video file, or a numerical record of some sort, but the Data is important.</p></details><details class="t13ne-alternate"><summary><strong>Alternate: </strong> Core </summary><p class="t13ne-description">The information conveys core information, either about the Character's Personality Annex Core, or about the work, toil, labour or sacrifices that are key to the events to come.</p></details><details class="t13ne-detail"><summary><strong>Detail: </strong> Ecology </summary><p class="t13ne-description">Some Ecological detail about an area or people is shared, such as the Forest Elves bred the first horses, but since the animals have been declared Taboo, only to ridden by the priesthood or nobility, and none remain in their Forest.</p></details></details>
		 <details class="t13ne-ordeal-fancy"><summary><strong>Ordeal</strong></summary><p class="t13ne-ordeal-type"><strong>Ordeal-Type </strong> — Solitary Ordeal</p><p class="t13ne-stages"><strong>Stages </strong> — 2</p><p class="t13ne-ordeal-spread"><strong>Ordeal-Spread </strong> — Railroad</p><p class="t13ne-stakes"><strong>Stakes </strong> — Low</p><p class="t13ne-test"><strong>Test </strong> — Knowledge (Orthodox)</p><p class="t13ne-obstacles"><strong>Obstacles </strong> — 1 Obstacle in 1 Court Stage</p><p class="t13ne-suggested-action"><strong>Suggested-Action </strong> — Sprint</p><details class="t13ne-stage"><summary><strong>Stage: </strong> Medium Straight </summary><p class="t13ne-description">A medium length, straight path, street or road. Complex preparations or research.
