Working normally.</p></details><details class="t13ne-motional"><summary><strong>Motional: </strong> Water Vehicle Dash </summary><p class="t13ne-description">Pass through as many watery Ordeal stages as possible in your boat, before the time (Rounds or Phases) limit expires.</p></details><details class="t13ne-fight"><summary><strong>Fight: </strong> Minor Guardian </summary><p class="t13ne-description">A single opponent, at least as powerful as a PC (Extras match Highest Annex Boon), that has some interesting abilities (I-Ching, Core) and Annexes, or powerful Descendants.</p></details></details>
		 </details>
			</div>
				<div class="t13ne-card-lea"><details class="t13ne-lea"><summary><strong>Lea</strong></summary><p class="t13ne-colour"><strong>Path Colour —</strong> Brown/Blue Omnivore</p><p class="t13ne-bulmas"><strong>Allowed Bulmäs —</strong> Broccen, Vermis, Labyrinthine, All-Walkers</p><p class="t13ne-animal-category"><strong>Animal Category —</strong> Macrofauna</p><p class="t13ne-animal-example"><strong>Example Animal —</strong> Earthworm / Beetles / Ants / Minnows / Wasp</p><p class="t13ne-notes"><strong>Notes —</strong> Small (often burrowing) creatures. Smallest vertebrates.</p></details></div></div><div class="t13ne-cardwrap-jsvg" id="cardrewritebox5" data-mode="jsvg" data-wyrd="1" data-yarn="1"><img src="https://www.cjmoseley.dev.cc/wp-content/plugins/t13ne/images/cards-svg/6D.svg" class="t13ne-svgcard"><div class="t13ne-svgpips"><div class="t13ne-textpips">6<!-- write suit--><span class="t13ne-symbol"><span class="t13ne-redcard">♦</span></span><details class="t13ne-suit"><summary></summary><p class="t13ne-name"><strong>Suit: </strong>Diamonds <span class="t13ne-redcard">♦</span></p><p><strong>Ordeal Trump: </strong><span class="t13ne-trump">Preparation</span></p><p class="t13ne-desc"><strong>Description: </strong>Diamonds represent the finer things of life, they are bright and sparkling. In the Tarot, Diamonds are replaced with coins, which represent  not just Wealth, but elemental Earth as well.</p><p class="t13ne-tarot"><strong>Tarot Meaning: </strong>Travel, Money, Business, Contracts, Deals, Voyages.</p><p class="t13ne-otext"><strong>Ordeal Text: </strong>Ordeal Types: Travel (e.g. Chase, Race) / Creativity (e.g. Smithing, Painting). Diamonds act as Trump for the following Action Types: Art, Building, Charming, Crafting, Driving, Emoting, Forgiving, Keeping Your Cool, Listening, Meditation, Motion, Prayer, Preparation, Research, Sprinting and Working in general. Any type of action can be prepared with a Diamond.</p><p class="t13ne-parry"><strong>Parry: </strong><strong>Seize the initative:</strong> The cards of the original attack (less the matched card) are immediately added to the Defender’s Ordeal Pool. They may then play as many cards as their Descendant/Annex normally plays including the Parry card (1-4) usually into one of their Style Reserve, and then immediately discard any cards over Pool Limit.</p><p class="t13ne-soak"><strong>Soak: </strong><strong>Ready:</strong> The matched Attack card is immediately Prepared by the Defender (as they wish). The Soak card is discarded.</p></details></div></div><div class="t13ne-svg-facet"><div>Virtue</div></div><div class="t13ne-wound"><div><strong class="t13ne-woundname">Flesh</strong><details><summary></summary><strong>Flesh</strong><p class="t13ne-base-pips">Base Pips to summon: 5</p><p class="t13ne-min-pips">Minimum Pips Required: 5</p><p class="t13ne-desc">The Target is wounded and must stop and aknowledge that fact.</p><details class="t13ne-roll"><summary>Rules</summary>Lose one <strong>die</strong> from each pool on the next roll you make (e.g.3d6 + 2d10+5 becomes 2d6 + 1d10+5) then Discard</details><details class="t13ne-ordeal"><summary>Ordeal Rules</summary>Discard an Ordeal card from the next Full-Action or aggravate another Wound 1 Level and then Discard. </details><details class="t13ne-psych"><summary>Psych Wounds</summary><details class="t13ne-negative"><summary>Negative Psych</summary><p class="t13ne-normal"><strong>Normal</strong>Cannot approach source of negative emotion until Stabilized / Discarded</p><p class="t13ne-extreme"><strong>Extreme</strong>Paralysed (All Proficiency Dice are lost) until Stabilised / Discarded.</p></details><details class="t13ne-positive"><summary>Positive Psych</summary><p class="t13ne-normal"><strong>Normal</strong>Unwilling to leave source of positive emotion until discarded</p><p class="t13ne-extreme"><strong>Extreme</strong>Stimulated/Excited. Discard immediately, along with all GRT and RT Chips and act immediately (still with the dice or card penalties).</p></details></details></details></div></div><div class="t13ne-tarot-svg"><details><summary class="t13ne-wyrdtarot"><strong> Wyrd Tarot: </strong> Retcon</summary><p class="t13ne-normal"><strong> Normal: </strong>Player may describe a Scene from Target's past.</p><p class="t13ne-crossed"><strong> Crossed: </strong>Target describes a Scene from their past. Allows purchase of Proficiencies, Descendants, etc.</p></details></div><div class="t13ne-yarn-fancy">
			<details><summary><strong>Yarn</strong> <br>The Sun</summary>
			<span class="t13ne-description">The Card of light, warmth and hope. But one should also be aware that light can blind, warmth can burn and hope can be false. While everything about this card is direct and to the point, just as the sun appears to move straight across the sky, this is not the actual truth and things appear to change, as we see them in different lights.</span>
			<span class="t13ne-significator">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-significator"><strong>Significator</strong></summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-plot"><strong>Plot: </strong> The Sun Plot is about Hope. It offers hope to the hopeless, a new hope to the downtrodden, and reversals of fortunes. Of course, the truth of the the Sun Plot is that most of these hopes will never amount to anything. The poor will in general remain poor (probably even end up a little poorer).</aside>
					<aside class="t13ne-char"><strong>Character: </strong> The Sun Significator indicates a Character who Gains Yin when they are warm and pleasant, Gains Yang when they are blunt or harsh. They Gain Chi when they give people hope or inspire them. They also gain +1 Success Level when they are stood in sunshine.</aside>
					<aside class="t13ne-scene"><strong>Scene: </strong> The Sun Plot is about Hope. It offers hope to the hopeless, a new hope to the downtrodden, and reversals of fortunes. Of course, the truth of the the Sun Plot is that most of these hopes will never amount to anything. The poor will in general remain poor (probably even end up a little poorer).</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-scene">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-scene-type">
						<strong>Scene: </strong> Warp
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">Warps work the Conflict. They are broken into 3 parts (and require another 2 cards): <ul><li>The Ends (What the Players want, usually described as Quests ). Does not require a Yarn Card.</li><li>The Fray (what keeps the Characters from their Ends, how do they work through that Quest?). The Fray requires an extra Yarn Card.</li><li>The Snag (The complication that renders the actions of the Characters winning through the Fray moot somehow). The Snag also requires another Yarn Card.</li></ul>During Warps things tend to get worse for the Pressed side of the Conflict.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-conflict-embodiment">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-conflict-embodiment">
						<strong>Conflict Embodiment: </strong> External
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">One side of the Conflict (usually the Pressed side) is embodied within the Characters (sometimes an NPC), the Dominant side is embodied as something external to all the Characters. Embodiments might include Tones, Locations, Descendants, Quests, Ordeals/Tests and suitable Obstacles.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>

			<span class="t13ne-facet-embodiment">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-facet-embodiment">
						<strong>Facet Embodiment: </strong> Obstacle
					</summary>
					<section class="t13ne-description">The Conflict Embodies one Facet as an Obstacle that the player must overcome. The Obstacle should match Suit with the Facet (Obstacles are defined by Cards normally). Obstacle embodiments can be the most subtle, you need not reuse the same Obstacle over and over, and they allow for Red Herring Facets from the same Suit to misdirect easily.</section>
				</details>
			</span>
			 <div class="t13ne-archetypes"><details><summary><strong>Archetype Character: </strong>Nerd</summary> <details class="t13ne-Persona"><summary><strong>Persona: </strong><span class="t13ne-output">Fanatic</span>
      </summary>
      <details class="t13ne-motivation"><summary><strong>Motivation: </strong></summary><span class="t13ne-description">The Fanatic is motivated by an extreme and often uncritical enthusiasm for a cause, religion, hobby, job or form of entertainment. They may be a fan of a particular person (often an author, artist or performer), a character, a genre of music or fiction, a particular manufacturer's products or a class of Descendant. Some Fanatics are devoted to a specific cause, such as a particular religion, or car manufacturer, where as others consider themselves more generally fanatical, like Theologians or Petrol-Heads.</span></details>
       <details class="t13ne-avoid"><summary><strong>Avoid: </strong></summary><span class="t13ne-description">Most Fanatics will avoid and dismiss any and all criticism of their enthusiasm. If someone disagrees with their beliefs, the Fanatic will delight in proving them wrong, and making them see the errors of their ways.</span></details>
       <details class="t13ne-gain-chi"><summary><strong>Gain Chi: </strong></summary><span class="t13ne-description"><span>Gain Chi when you watch, engage in, or talk about what you are enthusiastic about.</span><p>Ordeal gain Chi when you play a ♦ as an action rather than attack or preparation.</p></span></details></details>   <details class="t13ne-core"><summary><strong>Core: </strong><span class="t13ne-output">Consumer</span></summary><span class="t13ne-description">Gain Chi when you consume something (food, cigarette, pills, drugs, vapes, money, patches, credits, alcohol, syringes, human hearts, bullets, souls, etc -- pick one).</span></details><details class="t13ne-hitch"><summary><strong>Hitch: </strong><span class="t13ne-output">Fatigued</span></summary><span class="t13ne-description">This hitch covers everything from laziness to sickness, some people and things just seem tired or useless most of the time.</span></details><p class="t13ne-description">The Nerd has some subject, genre, or technology that obsesses them. There's nothing they love more than to talk about or consume their "books / shows". </p></details></div>
			 <span class="t13ne-hook">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-hook">
						<strong>Hook: </strong> Dawn Chorus
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">The story hooks the character with a morning messenger (or other media such as the morning newspaper or update) bearing important news. It may be news that a friend is ill, or that the enemy is closing in, or that you have won a lottery. Regardless, this begins the Conflict with a Gain or Revelation.</aside>
					<details class="t13ne-hook-aspect">
						<summary><strong>Hook Aspect: </strong>Geometry</summary>
						<section class="t13ne-description">The Hook targets the Character's Geometry. For example: In order to Hook a Circle Geometry simply offer to fulfil an ambition.</section>
					</details>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-obstacle-fancy"><details class="t13ne-obstacle"><summary><strong>Obstacle:</strong>Bright</summary><p class="t13ne-description">The way is blocked, by bright lights that could ruin night and low-light vision. </p></details></span>
			<span class="t13ne-gain">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-gain">
						<strong>Gain </strong> 
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">Gold and Gems (Yarn Gain)</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-fray">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-fray">
						<strong>Fray: </strong> Burning Sun
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">A High Stakes Solitary Motional Ordeal, where you must survive a dangerous situation, travel and often accomplish other tasks along the way, it might be as simple as making it back to the oasis after your camel was bitten by a snake, or it could be dashing through rush hour traffic to deliver pizzas when a shoot-out takes place. There are usually Obstacles involved and occasionally Traps.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-snag">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-snag">
						<strong>Snag: </strong> Changing Light
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">Although the Characters may have achieved whatever was required, it is no longer enough, or they misunderstood what they were looking at. Changes to orders, Rule changes, Late entries, Disqualifications, Reinforcements, or even moving the goalposts on the Pressed side can all be caused by Changing Light. Often the changing light is the fault of the Dominant Embodiment, other times it just happens. The Snag may act as a Revelation, but what <em>exactly</em> was revealed by the Changing Light?</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-sweeping">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-sweeping">
						<strong>Sweeping: </strong> Basking
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">The Pressed side experiences the warmth and glory of the Sun, and gets to bask in it. The Basking Character usually makes a Gain as they are rewarded, and appreciated for what happened in the Fray.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
		 <details class="t13ne-revelation-fancy"><summary><strong>Revelation</strong></summary><details class="t13ne-about"><summary><strong>About: </strong> Pressed </summary><p class="t13ne-description">The information is about the Pressed Conflict Embodiment, which is often a PC in a game, and is usually the antagonist in a Story.</p></details><details class="t13ne-info"><summary><strong>Info: </strong> By What Means? </summary><p class="t13ne-description">Information about how far someone may go to achieve their Ends. Will they kill, or die for it? Or will they not even get out of bed in the morning?</p></details><details class="t13ne-vector"><summary><strong>Vector: </strong> Records </summary><p class="t13ne-description">This could be an audio recording, minutes of a meeting, a written transcript, or some jotted shorthand notes, but it reveals something important, often historical.</p></details><details class="t13ne-alternate"><summary><strong>Alternate: </strong> Success </summary><p class="t13ne-description">The information is about the Successes or accomplishments of a Character, Pact, or Descendant.</p></details><details class="t13ne-detail"><summary><strong>Detail: </strong> Fine </summary><p class="t13ne-description">Fine details include minutia about the Character etc. these might include one of the ways they gain Yin / Yang, or which brand of Laser gun they prefer.</p></details></details>
		 <details class="t13ne-ordeal-fancy"><summary><strong>Ordeal</strong></summary><p class="t13ne-ordeal-type"><strong>Ordeal-Type </strong> — Solitary Ordeal</p><p class="t13ne-stages"><strong>Stages </strong> — 2</p><p class="t13ne-ordeal-spread"><strong>Ordeal-Spread </strong> — Forking</p><p class="t13ne-stakes"><strong>Stakes </strong> — Medium</p><p class="t13ne-test"><strong>Test </strong> — Research (Orthodox)</p><p class="t13ne-obstacles"><strong>Obstacles </strong> — 1 Obstacle in 1 Court Stage</p><p class="t13ne-suggested-action"><strong>Suggested-Action </strong> — Research</p><details class="t13ne-stage"><summary><strong>Stage: </strong> Curve </summary><p class="t13ne-description">A medium length, curved path, street or road, a slightly odd situation or place.</p></details><details class="t13ne-motional"><summary><strong>Motional: </strong> Aerial Riding Dash </summary><p class="t13ne-description">Reach and complete the goal Stage as quickly as possible, usually riding a jet-bike, eagle or something similar.</p></details><details class="t13ne-fight"><summary><strong>Fight: </strong> Major Guardian </summary><p class="t13ne-description">A group of PC level opponents, or a single Monster with at least one Monster Facet and a Boon equal to the Value equal to at least three quarters of the total Value of all PCs Personality Annexes <!-- t13ne-shortcode "%7B%22type%22%3A%22facetsuitaspect%22%2C%22suit%22%3A%221%22%2C%22aspect%22%3A%22Monster%22%7D"// --></p><div class="t13ne-tablewrap"><table class="t13ne-table Monster"><thead><tr><th>&nbsp;&nbsp;Facet&nbsp;&nbsp;</th><th>&nbsp;&nbsp;Monster&nbsp;&nbsp;</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td>Awe</td><td><strong>Terror</strong> —The Monster is a Fear or Anxiety embodied. They are quite diverse in form, but are always able to cause Psychology Test attacks such as Fear, Terror, or Horror to any that see them act. They may add Awe Draw cards to their Monster Pool whenever they cause negative emotions in another Character. They automatically play Monster Pool Reduced Cards as a Psychology Test or Psyche Attack on any intimidation or physical attack, but must add at least one Card from their Monster Pool to any Psyche Attack. Examples of this Monster include Ghosts, some serial killers and some Preternatural Cosmic Horrors.</td></tr><tr><td>Craft</td><td><strong>Maker</strong> —The Monster is actually a creator of other Monsters. The spawn are often less powerful than the creator, but in some cases are unique and more powerful. The Creator may not be directly encountered until late in the Story. The Creator can draw Craft Draw cards and add them to its Monster Pool each time it creates or summons an Extra or Descendant. At least 1 card must be used from the Monster Pool each Summoning or Creation to help pay the Summoning costs (or as an Obstacle or NPC as Yarn-Telling). All currently summoned Extras and Descendants receive a bonus to their Annex Score equal to the Creator's Monster Pool size.</td></tr><tr><td>Orthodox</td><td><strong>Tyrant</strong> —The Orthodox Monster is a tyrant, often a part of the system (in that it acts as a method of control over a population). A Tyrant monster may have troops that protect it, or work for it, or it may simply be so personally powerful that it can achieve its own goals. Tyrants may add Orthodox Draw to their Monster Pool every time they are Cruel or give orders. Tyrants can draw and Play current Monster Pool Size Reduced cards on the actions of their subordinates and underlings. However, the Tyrant can play their Monster Pool on their own actions, and must play at least one card from their Monster Pool if they directly act towards their goals.</td></tr><tr><td>Quiet</td><td><strong>Hush</strong> —The Quiet Monster is exactly that, almost (or entirely) silent. It is the creeping death of a hidden illness, or the cat-footed predator striking without warning that vanishes without trace. Hush Monsters get to begin with Quiet Score cards in their Monster Pool. Hushes may add +1 for each card in their Monster Pool when try to they act silently. When a Hush creature makes a noise (often a scream or howl), it must play at least Quiet Draw cards from the Monster Pool upon the action. Creatures like giant owls, snakes or cats, that stalk and hunt silently are good examples, as is a really creepy, floating mime. Many ghosts are also silent, until they turn and scream creating a Duck attack.</td></tr><tr><td>Virtue</td><td><strong>Friend</strong> —The Monster is a ‘Friendly’ monster, that will almost certainly be misunderstood and attacked by some. They may add Virtue Draw cards to their Monster Pool by being kind to another, or when they are attacked. A friendly Monster must protect their friends from harm, and must spend their Monster Pool to defend them. Friendly Monsters can add Virtue Score to any positive action when playing a card from their Monster Pool. Examples might include a cynical, but big-hearted ogre; a being created from stitched together corpses; a dark-elf ranger with a unicorn necklace; or perhaps even an Electric Mouse.</td></tr><tr><td>Zeal</td><td><strong>Radical</strong> —The Monster is an extremist, usually religious or political in nature. When acting according to their nature, or following their belief system, they may draw Zeal Draw Ordeal cards into their Monster Pool, they may Play cards from their Monster Pool to directly increase a Wound as they cause it, or directly reduce a Wound they receive (Pip for Pip). However, if they are forced to act outside of their nature, they are restricted by an automatic GRT equal to their Zeal Draw. The monster could be an evil priest, a terrorist, a devil, a djinn, or perhaps even the Angel of Death.</td></tr></tbody><tfoot><tr><td>&nbsp;&nbsp;Facet&nbsp;&nbsp;</td><td>&nbsp;&nbsp;Monster&nbsp;&nbsp;</td></tr></tfoot></table></div><p></p></details></details>
		 </details>
			</div>
				<div class="t13ne-card-lea"><details class="t13ne-lea"><summary><strong>Lea</strong></summary><p class="t13ne-colour"><strong>Path Colour —</strong> Brown/Blue Omnivore</p><p class="t13ne-bulmas"><strong>Allowed Bulmäs —</strong> Broccen, Vermis, Labyrinthine, All-Walkers</p><p class="t13ne-animal-category"><strong>Animal Category —</strong> Megafauna – Tiny</p><p class="t13ne-animal-example"><strong>Example Animal —</strong> Mice / Hedgehog / Squirrel / Small Fish and most Birds including Pigeons</p><p class="t13ne-notes"><strong>Notes —</strong> Animals smaller than 40 Kilograms</p></details></div></div><div class="t13ne-cardwrap-jsvg" id="cardrewritebox6" data-mode="jsvg" data-wyrd="1" data-yarn="1"><img src="https://www.cjmoseley.dev.cc/wp-content/plugins/t13ne/images/cards-svg/7D.svg" class="t13ne-svgcard"><div class="t13ne-svgpips"><div class="t13ne-textpips">7<!-- write suit--><span class="t13ne-symbol"><span class="t13ne-redcard">♦</span></span><details class="t13ne-suit"><summary></summary><p class="t13ne-name"><strong>Suit: </strong>Diamonds <span class="t13ne-redcard">♦</span></p><p><strong>Ordeal Trump: </strong><span class="t13ne-trump">Preparation</span></p><p class="t13ne-desc"><strong>Description: </strong>Diamonds represent the finer things of life, they are bright and sparkling. In the Tarot, Diamonds are replaced with coins, which represent  not just Wealth, but elemental Earth as well.</p><p class="t13ne-tarot"><strong>Tarot Meaning: </strong>Travel, Money, Business, Contracts, Deals, Voyages.</p><p class="t13ne-otext"><strong>Ordeal Text: </strong>Ordeal Types: Travel (e.g. Chase, Race) / Creativity (e.g. Smithing, Painting). Diamonds act as Trump for the following Action Types: Art, Building, Charming, Crafting, Driving, Emoting, Forgiving, Keeping Your Cool, Listening, Meditation, Motion, Prayer, Preparation, Research, Sprinting and Working in general. Any type of action can be prepared with a Diamond.</p><p class="t13ne-parry"><strong>Parry: </strong><strong>Seize the initative:</strong> The cards of the original attack (less the matched card) are immediately added to the Defender’s Ordeal Pool. They may then play as many cards as their Descendant/Annex normally plays including the Parry card (1-4) usually into one of their Style Reserve, and then immediately discard any cards over Pool Limit.</p><p class="t13ne-soak"><strong>Soak: </strong><strong>Ready:</strong> The matched Attack card is immediately Prepared by the Defender (as they wish). The Soak card is discarded.</p></details></div></div><div class="t13ne-svg-facet"><div>Zeal</div></div><div class="t13ne-wound"><div><strong class="t13ne-woundname">Flesh</strong><details><summary></summary><strong>Flesh</strong><p class="t13ne-base-pips">Base Pips to summon: 5</p><p class="t13ne-min-pips">Minimum Pips Required: 5</p><p class="t13ne-desc">The Target is wounded and must stop and aknowledge that fact.</p><details class="t13ne-roll"><summary>Rules</summary>Lose one <strong>die</strong> from each pool on the next roll you make (e.g.3d6 + 2d10+5 becomes 2d6 + 1d10+5) then Discard</details><details class="t13ne-ordeal"><summary>Ordeal Rules</summary>Discard an Ordeal card from the next Full-Action or aggravate another Wound 1 Level and then Discard. </details><details class="t13ne-psych"><summary>Psych Wounds</summary><details class="t13ne-negative"><summary>Negative Psych</summary><p class="t13ne-normal"><strong>Normal</strong>Cannot approach source of negative emotion until Stabilized / Discarded</p><p class="t13ne-extreme"><strong>Extreme</strong>Paralysed (All Proficiency Dice are lost) until Stabilised / Discarded.</p></details><details class="t13ne-positive"><summary>Positive Psych</summary><p class="t13ne-normal"><strong>Normal</strong>Unwilling to leave source of positive emotion until discarded</p><p class="t13ne-extreme"><strong>Extreme</strong>Stimulated/Excited. Discard immediately, along with all GRT and RT Chips and act immediately (still with the dice or card penalties).</p></details></details></details></div></div><div class="t13ne-tarot-svg"><details><summary class="t13ne-wyrdtarot"><strong> Wyrd Tarot: </strong> Drop Prop</summary><p class="t13ne-normal"><strong> Normal: </strong>Target drops a random Prop (Physical Descendant).</p><p class="t13ne-crossed"><strong> Crossed: </strong>Instantly Fix or replace a Prop (Physical Descendant).</p></details></div><div class="t13ne-yarn-fancy">
			<details><summary><strong>Yarn</strong> <br>The Drive</summary>
			<span class="t13ne-description">The card of vehicles, engines and powerful motivations. The Drive is about speed, motion and pressing forwards, regardless of dangers or costs. Sometimes these motivations to move are internal to a Character, and they may be driven by Faith or Doubts, other times they are external, being driven on by some force beyond their control.</span>
			<span class="t13ne-significator">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-significator"><strong>Significator</strong></summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-plot"><strong>Plot: </strong> The Drive Plot is usually a journey or odyssey. Sometimes the Vehicle is important, becoming a Character (often a Rival), other times it is barely mentioned. The Journey though is always more important than the destination.</aside>
					<aside class="t13ne-char"><strong>Character: </strong> The Drive Significator indicates a Character who loves to drive (or pilot). They Gain Yin when they talk about vehicles, motors, engines, riding animals. Gain Yang when they look after, or maintain a vehicle or riding animal. Gain Chi when they drive or ride a vehicle or riding animal. They also gain a +1 Success Level when they are driving a vehicle or riding a riding animal.</aside>
					<aside class="t13ne-scene"><strong>Scene: </strong> The Drive Plot is usually a journey or odyssey. Sometimes the Vehicle is important, becoming a Character (often a Rival), other times it is barely mentioned. The Journey though is always more important than the destination.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-scene">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-scene-type">
						<strong>Scene: </strong> Weft
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">Wefts work the Conflict through 3 Parts: <ul><li>First by the Characters Recoiling from the Snag (They emotionally respond to the previous event) which does not require a Yarn card</li><li>Secondly Sweeping (where the Characters recover from what happened) which does require another Yarn Card</li><li>Finally, Picking (Where the Characters will have a choice how the Story will proceed) which does not require Yarn Cards, but can be represented by more cards during card play.</li></ul>During Wefts the Pressed side makes a little headway, or gets a little breather.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-conflict-embodiment">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-conflict-embodiment">
						<strong>Conflict Embodiment: </strong> External
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">One side of the Conflict (usually the Pressed side) is embodied within the Characters (sometimes an NPC), the Dominant side is embodied as something external to all the Characters. Embodiments might include Tones, Locations, Descendants, Quests, Ordeals/Tests and suitable Obstacles.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>

			<span class="t13ne-facet-embodiment">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-facet-embodiment">
						<strong>Facet Embodiment: </strong> Descendant
					</summary>
					<section class="t13ne-description">The Conflict Embodies one Facet as a Descendant Incarna. In the case of a Yonder Conflict this may result in a Location anyway. Conflicts embodied with Descendants can be very subtle. Commonly used by Creation Plots.</section>
				</details>
			</span>
			 <div class="t13ne-archetypes"><details><summary><strong>Archetype Character: </strong>Jockey</summary> <details class="t13ne-Persona"><summary><strong>Persona: </strong><span class="t13ne-output">Fanatic</span>
      </summary>
      <details class="t13ne-motivation"><summary><strong>Motivation: </strong></summary><span class="t13ne-description">The Fanatic is motivated by an extreme and often uncritical enthusiasm for a cause, religion, hobby, job or form of entertainment. They may be a fan of a particular person (often an author, artist or performer), a character, a genre of music or fiction, a particular manufacturer's products or a class of Descendant. Some Fanatics are devoted to a specific cause, such as a particular religion, or car manufacturer, where as others consider themselves more generally fanatical, like Theologians or Petrol-Heads.</span></details>
       <details class="t13ne-avoid"><summary><strong>Avoid: </strong></summary><span class="t13ne-description">Most Fanatics will avoid and dismiss any and all criticism of their enthusiasm. If someone disagrees with their beliefs, the Fanatic will delight in proving them wrong, and making them see the errors of their ways.</span></details>
       <details class="t13ne-gain-chi"><summary><strong>Gain Chi: </strong></summary><span class="t13ne-description"><span>Gain Chi when you watch, engage in, or talk about what you are enthusiastic about.</span><p>Ordeal gain Chi when you play a ♦ as an action rather than attack or preparation.</p></span></details></details>   <details class="t13ne-core"><summary><strong>Core: </strong><span class="t13ne-output">Philosopher</span></summary><span class="t13ne-description">Additional I-Ching (can be changing or unchanging). The Player may choose any Hexagram that their Facets might produce (choosing Yin or Yang for Boon 13 Facets), or the Referee may assign one or two Hexagrams or allow both Hexagrams to be randomly rolled.</span></details><details class="t13ne-hitch"><summary><strong>Hitch: </strong><span class="t13ne-output">Apathy</span></summary><span class="t13ne-description">The modern disease, apathy can simply be not wanting to do something, it can be a symptom of a serious problem like depression, or just be a projection on those who are unable to do, by those who can.</span></details><p class="t13ne-description">The Jockey is a professional vehicle or riding animal fan. They love the things they drive and ride, spending hours cleaning, grooming, tweaking, and modifying their rides. They drive (or ride) the vehicles (or animals) they love for a living, whether trucks, jet-fighters or thoroughbred horses.</p></details></div>
			 <span class="t13ne-hook">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-hook">
						<strong>Hook: </strong> Action
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">The Conflict opens with Action, usually a Motional Ordeal like a Car Chase, or Race.</aside>
					<details class="t13ne-hook-aspect">
						<summary><strong>Hook Aspect: </strong>Geometry</summary>
						<section class="t13ne-description">The Hook targets the Character's Geometry. For example: In order to Hook a Circle Geometry simply offer to fulfil an ambition.</section>
					</details>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-obstacle-fancy"><details class="t13ne-obstacle"><summary><strong>Obstacle:</strong>Moving Traffic</summary><p class="t13ne-description">The way is blocked, by fast paced, flowing traffic.</p></details></span>
			<span class="t13ne-gain">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-gain">
						<strong>Gain </strong> 
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">A Vehicle (A Vehicle Descendant) whether it is a run-down pick-up truck, a luxury yacht, or a Void-runner Space-liner.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-fray">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-fray">
						<strong>Fray: </strong> Furiously Fast
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">An Opposed Motional Ordeal, usually of High or Extreme Stakes. This is usually a car chase or race (although it may be a Flee! situation). There are ample opportunities for Stunts and Obstacles during this Ordeal and all drivers are expected to complete them.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-snag">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-snag">
						<strong>Snag: </strong> Collision
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">There is a traffic accident, or crash. Maybe someone runs into the PCs or they barrel into someone else. Either way Draw 5 Ordeal Cards and apply one each to each Driver, their Vehicles, and one for any bystanders/passengers (that usually Splashes). These Wounds may be soaked by safety systems and the Characters armour/Incarna as appropriate. If no vehicles are involved then you may Draw 3 and play one card on each 'Driver' who in this case is just running.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-sweeping">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-sweeping">
						<strong>Sweeping: </strong> Get a Ride
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">When a Character needs to travel a long way, they can get a ride, and arrive fresh and rested in a new location. Getting a Ride will sometimes result in a Gain or Revelation, but is mostly about travel and rest.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
		 <details class="t13ne-revelation-fancy"><summary><strong>Revelation</strong></summary><details class="t13ne-about"><summary><strong>About: </strong> Pressed </summary><p class="t13ne-description">The information is about the Pressed Conflict Embodiment, which is often a PC in a game, and is usually the antagonist in a Story.</p></details><details class="t13ne-info"><summary><strong>Info: </strong> With What? </summary><p class="t13ne-description">Information about a Descendant, that may help or be hindering.</p></details><details class="t13ne-vector"><summary><strong>Vector: </strong> Book </summary><p class="t13ne-description">The information is found in a book. It could be a diary, accounting ledgers, or perhaps a history, or encyclopaedia. It could even be encoded (or echoed) in a work of fiction.</p></details><details class="t13ne-alternate"><summary><strong>Alternate: </strong> Financial </summary><p class="t13ne-description">The information is about Finances, Bank accounts, takings, and money. It could simply be a measure of wealth, or perhaps access to another's account details.</p></details><details class="t13ne-detail"><summary><strong>Detail: </strong> Common Knowledge </summary><p class="t13ne-description">The detail is a piece of common knowledge that should be known to everyone in the world. Careful with the Narration that you give this Revelation, it can easily become "As you know, Bob" exposition, instead consider a child singing a nursery rhyme about it, or a local curse that includes the information, "He's gone like King Samand's marbles".</p></details></details>
		 <details class="t13ne-ordeal-fancy"><summary><strong>Ordeal</strong></summary><p class="t13ne-ordeal-type"><strong>Ordeal-Type </strong> — Solitary Ordeal</p><p class="t13ne-stages"><strong>Stages </strong> — 2</p><p class="t13ne-ordeal-spread"><strong>Ordeal-Spread </strong> — Pyramid</p><p class="t13ne-stakes"><strong>Stakes </strong> — High</p><p class="t13ne-test"><strong>Test </strong> — Meditation (Quiet)</p><p class="t13ne-obstacles"><strong>Obstacles </strong> — 1 Obstacle in 1 Court Stage</p><p class="t13ne-suggested-action"><strong>Suggested-Action </strong> — Wait</p><details class="t13ne-stage"><summary><strong>Stage: </strong> Descent </summary><p class="t13ne-description">A sloping route, or stairs down. A low spot or day.</p></details><details class="t13ne-motional"><summary><strong>Motional: </strong> Water Riding Dash </summary><p class="t13ne-description">Act quickly, and get through the watery Stages to the goal as fast as you, on your surfboard / giant-seahorse can.</p></details><details class="t13ne-fight"><summary><strong>Fight: </strong> Greater Guardian </summary><p class="t13ne-description">A single Monster as powerful as the whole Party, or a Group with each member at least as powerful as the most powerful PC.<!-- t13ne-shortcode "%7B%22type%22%3A%22facetsuitaspect%22%2C%22suit%22%3A%221%22%2C%22aspect%22%3A%22Monster%22%7D"// --></p><div class="t13ne-tablewrap"><table class="t13ne-table Monster"><thead><tr><th>&nbsp;&nbsp;Facet&nbsp;&nbsp;</th><th>&nbsp;&nbsp;Monster&nbsp;&nbsp;</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td>Awe</td><td><strong>Terror</strong> —The Monster is a Fear or Anxiety embodied. They are quite diverse in form, but are always able to cause Psychology Test attacks such as Fear, Terror, or Horror to any that see them act. They may add Awe Draw cards to their Monster Pool whenever they cause negative emotions in another Character. They automatically play Monster Pool Reduced Cards as a Psychology Test or Psyche Attack on any intimidation or physical attack, but must add at least one Card from their Monster Pool to any Psyche Attack. Examples of this Monster include Ghosts, some serial killers and some Preternatural Cosmic Horrors.</td></tr><tr><td>Craft</td><td><strong>Maker</strong> —The Monster is actually a creator of other Monsters. The spawn are often less powerful than the creator, but in some cases are unique and more powerful. The Creator may not be directly encountered until late in the Story. The Creator can draw Craft Draw cards and add them to its Monster Pool each time it creates or summons an Extra or Descendant. At least 1 card must be used from the Monster Pool each Summoning or Creation to help pay the Summoning costs (or as an Obstacle or NPC as Yarn-Telling). All currently summoned Extras and Descendants receive a bonus to their Annex Score equal to the Creator's Monster Pool size.</td></tr><tr><td>Orthodox</td><td><strong>Tyrant</strong> —The Orthodox Monster is a tyrant, often a part of the system (in that it acts as a method of control over a population). A Tyrant monster may have troops that protect it, or work for it, or it may simply be so personally powerful that it can achieve its own goals. Tyrants may add Orthodox Draw to their Monster Pool every time they are Cruel or give orders. Tyrants can draw and Play current Monster Pool Size Reduced cards on the actions of their subordinates and underlings. However, the Tyrant can play their Monster Pool on their own actions, and must play at least one card from their Monster Pool if they directly act towards their goals.</td></tr><tr><td>Quiet</td><td><strong>Hush</strong> —The Quiet Monster is exactly that, almost (or entirely) silent. It is the creeping death of a hidden illness, or the cat-footed predator striking without warning that vanishes without trace. Hush Monsters get to begin with Quiet Score cards in their Monster Pool. Hushes may add +1 for each card in their Monster Pool when try to they act silently. When a Hush creature makes a noise (often a scream or howl), it must play at least Quiet Draw cards from the Monster Pool upon the action. Creatures like giant owls, snakes or cats, that stalk and hunt silently are good examples, as is a really creepy, floating mime. Many ghosts are also silent, until they turn and scream creating a Duck attack.</td></tr><tr><td>Virtue</td><td><strong>Friend</strong> —The Monster is a ‘Friendly’ monster, that will almost certainly be misunderstood and attacked by some. They may add Virtue Draw cards to their Monster Pool by being kind to another, or when they are attacked. A friendly Monster must protect their friends from harm, and must spend their Monster Pool to defend them. Friendly Monsters can add Virtue Score to any positive action when playing a card from their Monster Pool. Examples might include a cynical, but big-hearted ogre; a being created from stitched together corpses; a dark-elf ranger with a unicorn necklace; or perhaps even an Electric Mouse.</td></tr><tr><td>Zeal</td><td><strong>Radical</strong> —The Monster is an extremist, usually religious or political in nature. When acting according to their nature, or following their belief system, they may draw Zeal Draw Ordeal cards into their Monster Pool, they may Play cards from their Monster Pool to directly increase a Wound as they cause it, or directly reduce a Wound they receive (Pip for Pip). However, if they are forced to act outside of their nature, they are restricted by an automatic GRT equal to their Zeal Draw. The monster could be an evil priest, a terrorist, a devil, a djinn, or perhaps even the Angel of Death.</td></tr></tbody><tfoot><tr><td>&nbsp;&nbsp;Facet&nbsp;&nbsp;</td><td>&nbsp;&nbsp;Monster&nbsp;&nbsp;</td></tr></tfoot></table></div><p></p></details></details>
		 </details>
			</div>
				<div class="t13ne-card-lea"><details class="t13ne-lea"><summary><strong>Lea</strong></summary><p class="t13ne-colour"><strong>Path Colour —</strong> Brown/Blue Omnivore</p><p class="t13ne-bulmas"><strong>Allowed Bulmäs —</strong> Broccen, Vermis, Labyrinthine, All-Walkers</p><p class="t13ne-animal-category"><strong>Animal Category —</strong> Megafauna – Small</p><p class="t13ne-animal-example"><strong>Example Animal —</strong> Rat / Chicken / Crow / Piranha / Small Turtles</p><p class="t13ne-notes"><strong>Notes —</strong> These paths often migrate long distances between two small territories.</p></details></div></div><div class="t13ne-cardwrap-jsvg" id="cardrewritebox7" data-mode="jsvg" data-wyrd="1" data-yarn="1"><img src="https://www.cjmoseley.dev.cc/wp-content/plugins/t13ne/images/cards-svg/8D.svg" class="t13ne-svgcard"><div class="t13ne-svgpips"><div class="t13ne-textpips">8<!-- write suit--><span class="t13ne-symbol"><span class="t13ne-redcard">♦</span></span><details class="t13ne-suit"><summary></summary><p class="t13ne-name"><strong>Suit: </strong>Diamonds <span class="t13ne-redcard">♦</span></p><p><strong>Ordeal Trump: </strong><span class="t13ne-trump">Preparation</span></p><p class="t13ne-desc"><strong>Description: </strong>Diamonds represent the finer things of life, they are bright and sparkling. In the Tarot, Diamonds are replaced with coins, which represent  not just Wealth, but elemental Earth as well.</p><p class="t13ne-tarot"><strong>Tarot Meaning: </strong>Travel, Money, Business, Contracts, Deals, Voyages.</p><p class="t13ne-otext"><strong>Ordeal Text: </strong>Ordeal Types: Travel (e.g. Chase, Race) / Creativity (e.g. Smithing, Painting). Diamonds act as Trump for the following Action Types: Art, Building, Charming, Crafting, Driving, Emoting, Forgiving, Keeping Your Cool, Listening, Meditation, Motion, Prayer, Preparation, Research, Sprinting and Working in general. Any type of action can be prepared with a Diamond.</p><p class="t13ne-parry"><strong>Parry: </strong><strong>Seize the initative:</strong> The cards of the original attack (less the matched card) are immediately added to the Defender’s Ordeal Pool. They may then play as many cards as their Descendant/Annex normally plays including the Parry card (1-4) usually into one of their Style Reserve, and then immediately discard any cards over Pool Limit.</p><p class="t13ne-soak"><strong>Soak: </strong><strong>Ready:</strong> The matched Attack card is immediately Prepared by the Defender (as they wish). The Soak card is discarded.</p></details></div></div><div class="t13ne-svg-facet"><div>Zeal</div></div><div class="t13ne-wound"><div><strong class="t13ne-woundname">Maim</strong><details><summary></summary><strong>Maim</strong><p class="t13ne-base-pips">Base Pips to summon: 8</p><p class="t13ne-min-pips">Minimum Pips Required: 8</p><p class="t13ne-desc">The Target is injured enough for it to affect their abilities.</p><details class="t13ne-roll"><summary>Rules</summary>Lose 1 Proficiency Slot from affected Annex (including Personality Proficiencies) and recalculate. If all Proficiencies are Maimed, the Annex is Crippled.</details><details class="t13ne-ordeal"><summary>Ordeal Rules</summary>Maimed Annexes lose Proficiency Slots too, but may lose access to Nimbeds affected or take a -1 Card Draw Penalty until Healed / Recalculated. If all Proficiencies are Maimed, the Annex is Crippled.</details><details class="t13ne-psych"><summary>Psych Wounds</summary><details class="t13ne-negative"><summary>Negative Psych</summary><p class="t13ne-normal"><strong>Normal</strong>Proficiency lost from Personality Profs. Must try to flee source of negative emotion until Stabilised or Discarded.</p><p class="t13ne-extreme"><strong>Extreme</strong>Proficiency lost from Personality Profs. All Proficiency Dice are reduced one dice size (1d6 becomes 1d4) until Stabilised.</p></details><details class="t13ne-positive"><summary>Positive Psych</summary><p class="t13ne-normal"><strong>Normal</strong>Gain 1 Proficiency (permanently) and must approach / follow source of positive emotion until Stabilised.</p><p class="t13ne-extreme"><strong>Extreme</strong>Gain 2 Proficiencies (permanently), plus must drop to their knees (or simlar) to express the emotion until Stabilised.</p></details></details></details></div></div><div class="t13ne-tarot-svg"><details><summary class="t13ne-wyrdtarot"><strong> Wyrd Tarot: </strong> Lose Chi</summary><p class="t13ne-normal"><strong> Normal: </strong>Target loses 8 Chi</p><p class="t13ne-crossed"><strong> Crossed: </strong>Target pulls and discards another card and gains Chi equal to the Pips</p></details></div><div class="t13ne-yarn-fancy">
			<details><summary><strong>Yarn</strong> <br>The Cultist</summary>
			<span class="t13ne-description">The Cultist is the card of the dark sides of belief and belonging. Everyone holds at least one belief that another would find dark, extremist, or just plain weird. Sometimes it is a religious belief, sometimes its just your weird head-cannon to tie the classic series to the reboot directly. This is the card that shows us those parts of a character. It asks questions of them, such as: What do they really believe? Do they identify more with a Villain or Hero? What is their inner darkness? How far will they go? What lines will they never cross? and exactly how bad was that last instalment/season?</span>
			<span class="t13ne-significator">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-significator"><strong>Significator</strong></summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-plot"><strong>Plot: </strong> The Cultist Plot is about conspiracies and Characters who are not what they first seem. There may be a secret cult, or a hidden group that is manipulating the situation. The Cultist Plot will often rely on occult details or supernatural effects in some genres, in others it may be a conspiracy of silence that is to blame.</aside>
					<aside class="t13ne-char"><strong>Character: </strong> The Cultist Significator indicates a Character who has odd, often dark, beliefs. They Gain Yin when they keep secrets, or make indirect references to their beliefs. Gain Yang when they lie about their actions, or reveal some aspect of their belief (swear by a weird god or saint). They Gain Chi when they practice their religion, or belief alone, or share it with others. They gain a +1 Success Level to all actions to do with the beliefs and lore of their Cult.</aside>
					<aside class="t13ne-scene"><strong>Scene: </strong> The Cultist Plot is about conspiracies and Characters who are not what they first seem. There may be a secret cult, or a hidden group that is manipulating the situation. The Cultist Plot will often rely on occult details or supernatural effects in some genres, in others it may be a conspiracy of silence that is to blame.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-scene">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-scene-type">
						<strong>Scene: </strong> Warp
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">Warps work the Conflict. They are broken into 3 parts (and require another 2 cards): <ul><li>The Ends (What the Players want, usually described as Quests ). Does not require a Yarn Card.</li><li>The Fray (what keeps the Characters from their Ends, how do they work through that Quest?). The Fray requires an extra Yarn Card.</li><li>The Snag (The complication that renders the actions of the Characters winning through the Fray moot somehow). The Snag also requires another Yarn Card.</li></ul>During Warps things tend to get worse for the Pressed side of the Conflict.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-conflict-embodiment">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-conflict-embodiment">
						<strong>Conflict Embodiment: </strong> External
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">One side of the Conflict (usually the Pressed side) is embodied within the Characters (sometimes an NPC), the Dominant side is embodied as something external to all the Characters. Embodiments might include Tones, Locations, Descendants, Quests, Ordeals/Tests and suitable Obstacles.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>

			<span class="t13ne-facet-embodiment">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-facet-embodiment">
						<strong>Facet Embodiment: </strong> Location
					</summary>
					<section class="t13ne-description">The Conflict Embodies one Facet as a Location. This can be a very subtle embodiment, while still allowing the Conflict to progress. There can be a danger of overusing the same Location as the Embodiment is such a satisfying one. But any Location where the action returns again and again is probably an Embodiment of something from one of the higher Conflicts, anyway.</section>
				</details>
			</span>
			 <div class="t13ne-archetypes"><details><summary><strong>Archetype Character: </strong>Fan</summary> <details class="t13ne-Persona"><summary><strong>Persona: </strong><span class="t13ne-output">Fanatic</span>
      </summary>
      <details class="t13ne-motivation"><summary><strong>Motivation: </strong></summary><span class="t13ne-description">The Fanatic is motivated by an extreme and often uncritical enthusiasm for a cause, religion, hobby, job or form of entertainment. They may be a fan of a particular person (often an author, artist or performer), a character, a genre of music or fiction, a particular manufacturer's products or a class of Descendant. Some Fanatics are devoted to a specific cause, such as a particular religion, or car manufacturer, where as others consider themselves more generally fanatical, like Theologians or Petrol-Heads.</span></details>
       <details class="t13ne-avoid"><summary><strong>Avoid: </strong></summary><span class="t13ne-description">Most Fanatics will avoid and dismiss any and all criticism of their enthusiasm. If someone disagrees with their beliefs, the Fanatic will delight in proving them wrong, and making them see the errors of their ways.</span></details>
       <details class="t13ne-gain-chi"><summary><strong>Gain Chi: </strong></summary><span class="t13ne-description"><span>Gain Chi when you watch, engage in, or talk about what you are enthusiastic about.</span><p>Ordeal gain Chi when you play a ♦ as an action rather than attack or preparation.</p></span></details></details>   <details class="t13ne-core"><summary><strong>Core: </strong><span class="t13ne-output">Consumer</span></summary><span class="t13ne-description">Gain Chi when you consume something (food, cigarette, pills, drugs, vapes, money, patches, credits, alcohol, syringes, human hearts, bullets, souls, etc -- pick one).</span></details><details class="t13ne-hitch"><summary><strong>Hitch: </strong><span class="t13ne-output">Fatigued</span></summary><span class="t13ne-description">This hitch covers everything from laziness to sickness, some people and things just seem tired or useless most of the time.</span></details><p class="t13ne-description">The Fan has some subject, genre, or technology that obsesses them. There's nothing they love more than to talk about or consume their "books / shows". Sometimes divided into cultists, fanboys, and fangirls, these people are naturally drawn to groups of similar minded people.</p></details></div>
			 <span class="t13ne-hook">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-hook">
						<strong>Hook: </strong> Craze
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">The Conflict hooks Characters with a new craze, fad or fashion that is related to the Conflict. Kids and young adults are often the early adopters of the new craze, but the fad may be more cultish. in extreme examples the Dominant Embodiment will have this fad cult based around themselves.</aside>
					<details class="t13ne-hook-aspect">
						<summary><strong>Hook Aspect: </strong>Geometry</summary>
						<section class="t13ne-description">The Hook targets the Character's Geometry. For example: In order to Hook a Circle Geometry simply offer to fulfil an ambition.</section>
					</details>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-obstacle-fancy"><details class="t13ne-obstacle"><summary><strong>Obstacle:</strong>Pedestrians</summary><p class="t13ne-description">The way is blocked, by a crowd of pedestrians, they may be troops, passers-by or pedestrians, depending on the Stakes.</p></details></span>
			<span class="t13ne-gain">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-gain">
						<strong>Gain </strong> 
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">Fan Club (a group of Chorus Allies who are "fans" of the Character</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-fray">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-fray">
						<strong>Fray: </strong> Persuasion
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">The Fray consists of trying to persuade someone (a group of potential allies, a mentor or guardian, an Opposed Embodiment, or any Rival) to your point of view. Persuasion is either a Sub-Ordeal conversation, a Zeal vs Personality Test, or a Low / Soul Stakes Ordeal, depending how the Yarn-Teller wants to run it. Of course matters may be complicated if you are trying to convince a braying mob of your innocence, and the ultimate goal of the Cultist is always to try and persuade another to join the same cult.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-snag">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-snag">
						<strong>Snag: </strong> Kidnapped
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">One of the Embodiment's allies, friends or family, goes missing and needs saving. Usually they have been kidnapped by Mooks(Vex/Chorus/Cast Extras) working for the Opposed Embodiment.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-sweeping">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-sweeping">
						<strong>Sweeping: </strong> Worshipped
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">The Pressed Embodiment Gains a following, usually represented as a Group Descendant, the following is normally Chorus and Cast Extras.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
		 <details class="t13ne-revelation-fancy"><summary><strong>Revelation</strong></summary><details class="t13ne-about"><summary><strong>About: </strong> Pressed </summary><p class="t13ne-description">The information is about the Pressed Conflict Embodiment, which is often a PC in a game, and is usually the antagonist in a Story.</p></details><details class="t13ne-info"><summary><strong>Info: </strong> What? </summary><p class="t13ne-description">What is a broad question, what is it? What does he want? What are their Goals / Plans / Ends?</p></details><details class="t13ne-vector"><summary><strong>Vector: </strong> Magazine </summary><p class="t13ne-description">The Information is found on a printed pamphlet, in a newspaper, or in a magazine. Ot just occasionally is hidden in the ammunition for a gun.</p></details><details class="t13ne-alternate"><summary><strong>Alternate: </strong> A Descendant </summary><p class="t13ne-description">The information is specifically about a Descendant, often a Prop, it may be a "<em>McGuffin</em>", or "<em>Plot Token</em>".</p></details><details class="t13ne-detail"><summary><strong>Detail: </strong> Fundamental </summary><p class="t13ne-description">Fundamental Details are the big pieces that we often need to make sense of a situation. They might include that two families are at war, or that two members of those families are in love, or that a culture has no magic, only technology. Fundamental Details are usually key plot points.</p></details></details>
		 <details class="t13ne-ordeal-fancy"><summary><strong>Ordeal</strong></summary><p class="t13ne-ordeal-type"><strong>Ordeal-Type </strong> — Solitary Ordeal</p><p class="t13ne-stages"><strong>Stages </strong> — 3</p><p class="t13ne-ordeal-spread"><strong>Ordeal-Spread </strong> — Railroad</p><p class="t13ne-stakes"><strong>Stakes </strong> — Low</p><p class="t13ne-test"><strong>Test </strong> — Listening (Quiet)</p><p class="t13ne-obstacles"><strong>Obstacles </strong> — 1 non-Court Stage Obstacle + Obstacles in all Court Stages</p><p class="t13ne-suggested-action"><strong>Suggested-Action </strong> — Prepare</p><details class="t13ne-stage"><summary><strong>Stage: </strong> Uphill Slog </summary><p class="t13ne-description">A long, straight, but steeply climbing way, or long hours of work, preparation or research.</p></details><details class="t13ne-motional"><summary><strong>Motional: </strong> Ground Riding Dash </summary><p class="t13ne-description">Whatever you are doing on that bike, or horse, you have to do it quickly.</p></details><details class="t13ne-fight"><summary><strong>Fight: </strong> Gang Battle </summary><p class="t13ne-description">Lesser opponents (Chorus Extras) that outnumber the PCs by at least 3 to 1.</p></details></details>
		 </details>
			</div>
				<div class="t13ne-card-lea"><details class="t13ne-lea"><summary><strong>Lea</strong></summary><p class="t13ne-colour"><strong>Path Colour —</strong> Brown/Blue Omnivore</p><p class="t13ne-bulmas"><strong>Allowed Bulmäs —</strong> Broccen, Vermis, Labyrinthine, All-Walkers</p><p class="t13ne-animal-category"><strong>Animal Category —</strong> Megafauna – Medium</p><p class="t13ne-animal-example"><strong>Example Animal —</strong> Badger (Brwc) / Raccoon / Small Primates/ Geese / Catfish / Sea Turtles</p><p class="t13ne-notes"><strong>Notes —</strong> The Badger (or Brwc) is where the Broccen get their name. These Animals tend to keep territories, although they may have a nomadic stage.</p></details></div></div><div class="t13ne-cardwrap-jsvg" id="cardrewritebox8" data-mode="jsvg" data-wyrd="1" data-yarn="1"><img src="https://www.cjmoseley.dev.cc/wp-content/plugins/t13ne/images/cards-svg/9D.svg" class="t13ne-svgcard"><div class="t13ne-svgpips"><div class="t13ne-textpips">9<!-- write suit--><span class="t13ne-symbol"><span class="t13ne-redcard">♦</span></span><details class="t13ne-suit"><summary></summary><p class="t13ne-name"><strong>Suit: </strong>Diamonds <span class="t13ne-redcard">♦</span></p><p><strong>Ordeal Trump: </strong><span class="t13ne-trump">Preparation</span></p><p class="t13ne-desc"><strong>Description: </strong>Diamonds represent the finer things of life, they are bright and sparkling. In the Tarot, Diamonds are replaced with coins, which represent  not just Wealth, but elemental Earth as well.</p><p class="t13ne-tarot"><strong>Tarot Meaning: </strong>Travel, Money, Business, Contracts, Deals, Voyages.</p><p class="t13ne-otext"><strong>Ordeal Text: </strong>Ordeal Types: Travel (e.g. Chase, Race) / Creativity (e.g. Smithing, Painting). Diamonds act as Trump for the following Action Types: Art, Building, Charming, Crafting, Driving, Emoting, Forgiving, Keeping Your Cool, Listening, Meditation, Motion, Prayer, Preparation, Research, Sprinting and Working in general. Any type of action can be prepared with a Diamond.</p><p class="t13ne-parry"><strong>Parry: </strong><strong>Seize the initative:</strong> The cards of the original attack (less the matched card) are immediately added to the Defender’s Ordeal Pool. They may then play as many cards as their Descendant/Annex normally plays including the Parry card (1-4) usually into one of their Style Reserve, and then immediately discard any cards over Pool Limit.</p><p class="t13ne-soak"><strong>Soak: </strong><strong>Ready:</strong> The matched Attack card is immediately Prepared by the Defender (as they wish). The Soak card is discarded.</p></details></div></div><div class="t13ne-svg-facet"><div>Virtue</div></div><div class="t13ne-wound"><div><strong class="t13ne-woundname">Maim</strong><details><summary></summary><strong>Maim</strong><p class="t13ne-base-pips">Base Pips to summon: 8</p><p class="t13ne-min-pips">Minimum Pips Required: 8</p><p class="t13ne-desc">The Target is injured enough for it to affect their abilities.</p><details class="t13ne-roll"><summary>Rules</summary>Lose 1 Proficiency Slot from affected Annex (including Personality Proficiencies) and recalculate. If all Proficiencies are Maimed, the Annex is Crippled.</details><details class="t13ne-ordeal"><summary>Ordeal Rules</summary>Maimed Annexes lose Proficiency Slots too, but may lose access to Nimbeds affected or take a -1 Card Draw Penalty until Healed / Recalculated. If all Proficiencies are Maimed, the Annex is Crippled.</details><details class="t13ne-psych"><summary>Psych Wounds</summary><details class="t13ne-negative"><summary>Negative Psych</summary><p class="t13ne-normal"><strong>Normal</strong>Proficiency lost from Personality Profs. Must try to flee source of negative emotion until Stabilised or Discarded.</p><p class="t13ne-extreme"><strong>Extreme</strong>Proficiency lost from Personality Profs. All Proficiency Dice are reduced one dice size (1d6 becomes 1d4) until Stabilised.</p></details><details class="t13ne-positive"><summary>Positive Psych</summary><p class="t13ne-normal"><strong>Normal</strong>Gain 1 Proficiency (permanently) and must approach / follow source of positive emotion until Stabilised.</p><p class="t13ne-extreme"><strong>Extreme</strong>Gain 2 Proficiencies (permanently), plus must drop to their knees (or simlar) to express the emotion until Stabilised.</p></details></details></details></div></div><div class="t13ne-tarot-svg"><details><summary class="t13ne-wyrdtarot"><strong> Wyrd Tarot: </strong> Ambush</summary><p class="t13ne-normal"><strong> Normal: </strong>The Target is Intercepted or Ambushed (usually by the Player).</p><p class="t13ne-crossed"><strong> Crossed: </strong>Target may leave the Location or Stage (without passing Diff). Cut to destination (where applicable).</p></details></div><div class="t13ne-yarn-fancy">
			<details><summary><strong>Yarn</strong> <br>The Priest</summary>
			<span class="t13ne-description">The Priest is the card of the mortal and divine interacting. This can be literally a Priest (or other religious figure), or it can be some interaction that seems charged by divinity, ethics or morality, one of those times when the universe seems to be teaching us a lesson. These lessons are usually some allegory for an aspect of a religion, or a universal deity of some sort, but can be humanistic lessons such as "Don't be a Dick.". These Lessons often involve loss, anguish and difficult ethical and moral choices — one could easily interpret the card as the death of a loved one, for example.</span>
			<span class="t13ne-significator">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-significator"><strong>Significator</strong></summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-plot"><strong>Plot: </strong> The Priest Plot is a Plot where the sacred and profane are both important. The worlds of mortal and divine mix and interfere. Often the Plot will centre around a hallowed character (such as a Priest, Monk, Nun, Priestess, etc).</aside>
					<aside class="t13ne-char"><strong>Character: </strong> The Priest Significator indicates a Character who has a strong, personal connection to the divine. They gain Yin by prayer or meditation, gain Yang by sharing stories or parables from their religion, and drawing comparisons between their current situation and those from their religious texts. They Gain Chi when they perform religious rituals or demonstrate divine or spiritual powers or prowess. They also gain +1 Success Level to do with all aspects of their religion or spirituality</aside>
					<aside class="t13ne-scene"><strong>Scene: </strong> The Priest Plot is a Plot where the sacred and profane are both important. The worlds of mortal and divine mix and interfere. Often the Plot will centre around a hallowed character (such as a Priest, Monk, Nun, Priestess, etc).</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-scene">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-scene-type">
						<strong>Scene: </strong> Weft
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">Wefts work the Conflict through 3 Parts: <ul><li>First by the Characters Recoiling from the Snag (They emotionally respond to the previous event) which does not require a Yarn card</li><li>Secondly Sweeping (where the Characters recover from what happened) which does require another Yarn Card</li><li>Finally, Picking (Where the Characters will have a choice how the Story will proceed) which does not require Yarn Cards, but can be represented by more cards during card play.</li></ul>During Wefts the Pressed side makes a little headway, or gets a little breather.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-conflict-embodiment">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-conflict-embodiment">
						<strong>Conflict Embodiment: </strong> External
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">One side of the Conflict (usually the Pressed side) is embodied within the Characters (sometimes an NPC), the Dominant side is embodied as something external to all the Characters. Embodiments might include Tones, Locations, Descendants, Quests, Ordeals/Tests and suitable Obstacles.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>

			<span class="t13ne-facet-embodiment">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-facet-embodiment">
						<strong>Facet Embodiment: </strong> Pact-Descendant
					</summary>
					<section class="t13ne-description">The Conflict Embodies one Facet as a Pact Descendant, or group of Characters, such as the Party or a Guild. This Embodiment is the most obvious to use when you are starting out. The Players become one side of the Conflict fighting the other side. It is also great for modelling any number of political and social situations.</section>
				</details>
			</span>
			 <div class="t13ne-archetypes"><details><summary><strong>Archetype Character: </strong>Martyr</summary> <details class="t13ne-Persona"><summary><strong>Persona: </strong><span class="t13ne-output">Fanatic</span>
      </summary>
      <details class="t13ne-motivation"><summary><strong>Motivation: </strong></summary><span class="t13ne-description">The Fanatic is motivated by an extreme and often uncritical enthusiasm for a cause, religion, hobby, job or form of entertainment. They may be a fan of a particular person (often an author, artist or performer), a character, a genre of music or fiction, a particular manufacturer's products or a class of Descendant. Some Fanatics are devoted to a specific cause, such as a particular religion, or car manufacturer, where as others consider themselves more generally fanatical, like Theologians or Petrol-Heads.</span></details>
       <details class="t13ne-avoid"><summary><strong>Avoid: </strong></summary><span class="t13ne-description">Most Fanatics will avoid and dismiss any and all criticism of their enthusiasm. If someone disagrees with their beliefs, the Fanatic will delight in proving them wrong, and making them see the errors of their ways.</span></details>
       <details class="t13ne-gain-chi"><summary><strong>Gain Chi: </strong></summary><span class="t13ne-description"><span>Gain Chi when you watch, engage in, or talk about what you are enthusiastic about.</span><p>Ordeal gain Chi when you play a ♦ as an action rather than attack or preparation.</p></span></details></details>   <details class="t13ne-core"><summary><strong>Core: </strong><span class="t13ne-output">Barber</span></summary><span class="t13ne-description">The character can move Wounds from one Character or Descendant to another with a cost equal to the Pips in Chi. This cost can be lowered by also moving a Wound the other way.</span></details><details class="t13ne-hitch"><summary><strong>Hitch: </strong><span class="t13ne-output">Madness</span></summary><span class="t13ne-description">Mental disabilities, injuries and illnesses, or just plain barking.</span></details><p class="t13ne-description">A Martyr is a fanatic dedicated to their cause who is willing to die (or at least get badly injured) for their beliefs.</p></details></div>
			 <span class="t13ne-hook">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-hook">
						<strong>Hook: </strong> A Prayer
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">The Hook of this Story is nothing more than a cry for help, an overheard prayer, confession or scream calls the PC to action. To Hook some Characters it is necessary to allow the Prayer to intrigue, or tempt the Character into action rather than directly ask them for help.</aside>
					<details class="t13ne-hook-aspect">
						<summary><strong>Hook Aspect: </strong>Geometry</summary>
						<section class="t13ne-description">The Hook targets the Character's Geometry. For example: In order to Hook a Circle Geometry simply offer to fulfil an ambition.</section>
					</details>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-obstacle-fancy"><details class="t13ne-obstacle"><summary><strong>Obstacle:</strong>Moral Compass</summary><p class="t13ne-description">The Obstacle is an Ethical or Moral Dilemma, such as a good man having to do evil for the greater good.</p></details></span>
			<span class="t13ne-gain">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-gain">
						<strong>Gain </strong> 
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">Parish, the Parish is an area or territory that a priest is made responsible for. An actual Parish is a Real Estate or Province held by the Church and wardened by a Priest. (Gain Large Location)</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-fray">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-fray">
						<strong>Fray: </strong> Shepherd
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">A usually Medium-Soul Stakes Ordeal that involves guiding, teaching and protecting a "<em>flock</em>" of animals or people. This can be as simple as leading goats to pasture, sitting with a dying person, or as complex as herding cats, or calming and consoling parents after a sectarian attack on a primary school.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-snag">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-snag">
						<strong>Snag: </strong> Humanitarian Crisis
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">Often this Snag is about the aftermath of the Fray, but sometimes it is part of the Fray. It involves a threat to the well-being of entire communities. Plagues, famines, droughts and wars are common causes of a crisis, as are other natural disasters.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-sweeping">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-sweeping">
						<strong>Sweeping: </strong> Sacred Treasure
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">The Pressed Embodiment gains a Sacred Treasure of some sort. It should be noted that Sacredness is recognised differently by different people, for some sports or film memororabilia can be sacred, for others only divine artifacts are truly sacred.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
		 <details class="t13ne-revelation-fancy"><summary><strong>Revelation</strong></summary><details class="t13ne-about"><summary><strong>About: </strong> Pressed </summary><p class="t13ne-description">The information is about the Pressed Conflict Embodiment, which is often a PC in a game, and is usually the antagonist in a Story.</p></details><details class="t13ne-info"><summary><strong>Info: </strong> When? </summary><p class="t13ne-description">Information about an event that is happening, will happen, or already happened. When is sometimes even more important than where or what.</p></details><details class="t13ne-vector"><summary><strong>Vector: </strong> Broadcast </summary><p class="t13ne-description">The information is announced or pronounced by official sources. Announced by a town crier, or all over the local press and TV stations, the information is Broadcast somewhere the PCs can hear or see it.</p></details><details class="t13ne-alternate"><summary><strong>Alternate: </strong> History </summary><p class="t13ne-description">Everyone comes from somewhere, and the information tells us something about that. Specifically it tells us about family, economic, political or local history, where someone is from, and how they got where they are today, or some of it anyway.</p></details><details class="t13ne-detail"><summary><strong>Detail: </strong> Historical </summary><p class="t13ne-description">Historical details tell us about the past of a Location, Character or Descendant.</p></details></details>
		 <details class="t13ne-ordeal-fancy"><summary><strong>Ordeal</strong></summary><p class="t13ne-ordeal-type"><strong>Ordeal-Type </strong> — Solitary Ordeal</p><p class="t13ne-stages"><strong>Stages </strong> — 3</p><p class="t13ne-ordeal-spread"><strong>Ordeal-Spread </strong> — Forking</p><p class="t13ne-stakes"><strong>Stakes </strong> — Medium</p><p class="t13ne-test"><strong>Test </strong> — Conversion (Virtue)</p><p class="t13ne-obstacles"><strong>Obstacles </strong> — 2 non-Court Stage Obstacles + 2 Obstacles in all Court Stages</p><p class="t13ne-suggested-action"><strong>Suggested-Action </strong> — Construct</p><details class="t13ne-stage"><summary><strong>Stage: </strong> Long Straight </summary><p class="t13ne-description">A long, straight path, street, road, or boulevard. Working hard, or long hours of preparation or research.</p></details><details class="t13ne-motional"><summary><strong>Motional: </strong> Dangerous Riding Dash </summary><p class="t13ne-description">Get to the end of the Ordeal in as few Phases as possible. Across dangerous territory on a horse or bike that can help.</p></details><details class="t13ne-fight"><summary><strong>Fight: </strong> Rival Battle </summary><p class="t13ne-description">A fight between perfectly matched opponents, may use Rivals, Dark, Mirrors, Corrupted Clones, Animus etc. You can create Dark Parties for example or mix and match.</p></details></details>
		 </details>
			</div>
				<div class="t13ne-card-lea"><details class="t13ne-lea"><summary><strong>Lea</strong></summary><p class="t13ne-colour"><strong>Path Colour —</strong> Brown/Blue Omnivore</p><p class="t13ne-bulmas"><strong>Allowed Bulmäs —</strong> Broccen, Vermis, Labyrinthine, All-Walkers</p><p class="t13ne-animal-category"><strong>Animal Category —</strong> Megafauna – Large</p><p class="t13ne-animal-example"><strong>Example Animal —</strong>  Pig / Boar/ Bear / Cassowary / Large Primate</p><p class="t13ne-notes"><strong>Notes —</strong> These animals keep and patrol large territories.</p></details></div></div><div class="t13ne-cardwrap-jsvg" id="cardrewritebox9" data-mode="jsvg" data-wyrd="1" data-yarn="1"><img src="https://www.cjmoseley.dev.cc/wp-content/plugins/t13ne/images/cards-svg/10D.svg" class="t13ne-svgcard"><div class="t13ne-svgpips"><div class="t13ne-textpips">10<!-- write suit--><span class="t13ne-symbol"><span class="t13ne-redcard">♦</span></span><details class="t13ne-suit"><summary></summary><p class="t13ne-name"><strong>Suit: </strong>Diamonds <span class="t13ne-redcard">♦</span></p><p><strong>Ordeal Trump: </strong><span class="t13ne-trump">Preparation</span></p><p class="t13ne-desc"><strong>Description: </strong>Diamonds represent the finer things of life, they are bright and sparkling. In the Tarot, Diamonds are replaced with coins, which represent  not just Wealth, but elemental Earth as well.</p><p class="t13ne-tarot"><strong>Tarot Meaning: </strong>Travel, Money, Business, Contracts, Deals, Voyages.</p><p class="t13ne-otext"><strong>Ordeal Text: </strong>Ordeal Types: Travel (e.g. Chase, Race) / Creativity (e.g. Smithing, Painting). Diamonds act as Trump for the following Action Types: Art, Building, Charming, Crafting, Driving, Emoting, Forgiving, Keeping Your Cool, Listening, Meditation, Motion, Prayer, Preparation, Research, Sprinting and Working in general. Any type of action can be prepared with a Diamond.</p><p class="t13ne-parry"><strong>Parry: </strong><strong>Seize the initative:</strong> The cards of the original attack (less the matched card) are immediately added to the Defender’s Ordeal Pool. They may then play as many cards as their Descendant/Annex normally plays including the Parry card (1-4) usually into one of their Style Reserve, and then immediately discard any cards over Pool Limit.</p><p class="t13ne-soak"><strong>Soak: </strong><strong>Ready:</strong> The matched Attack card is immediately Prepared by the Defender (as they wish). The Soak card is discarded.</p></details></div></div><div class="t13ne-svg-facet"><div>Quiet</div></div><div class="t13ne-wound"><div><strong class="t13ne-woundname">Cripple</strong><details><summary></summary><strong>Cripple</strong><p class="t13ne-base-pips">Base Pips to summon: 12</p><p class="t13ne-min-pips">Minimum Pips Required: 10</p><p class="t13ne-desc">The Target is seriously wounded, losing access to whole abilities. This can even result in Coma.</p><details class="t13ne-roll"><summary>Rules</summary>Lose 1 Annex Slot (usually the largest one involved in the Roll) until Healed. Once healed Proficiencies may be repurchased for 1 Chi each. Then the Annex will regenerate at Phoenix / Incarna Score Boons per Day. It may be purchased back for the normal cost (Boon in Chi + 2/5/8/12) instantly (or altered and purchased back as a new Annex, if narratively appropriate).</details><details class="t13ne-ordeal"><summary>Ordeal Rules</summary>Crippled Annexes cannot be used as long as the Wound is in play. If applied to a Personality Annex (or a Descendant Master Annex) then the Alternate is unconscious (or the Descendant is lost or useless). They may not use that Alternate (or Descendant) until the Wound is healed (or found / fixed).</details><details class="t13ne-psych"><summary>Psych Wounds</summary><details class="t13ne-negative"><summary>Negative Psych</summary><p class="t13ne-normal"><strong>Normal</strong>Automatically affects the Personality Annex.
