No Proficiencies or Annexes are lost, but Character loses consciousness at sight of the source until healed.</p><p class="t13ne-extreme"><strong>Extreme</strong>Automatically affects the Personality Annex. Loses consciousness until Healed (not just when the source is present).</p></details><details class="t13ne-positive"><summary>Positive Psych</summary><p class="t13ne-normal"><strong>Normal</strong>Consciousness is interrupted for a few moments (1 Phase). May Gain a Skill Annex/Descendant or 3 Proficiencies.</p><p class="t13ne-extreme"><strong>Extreme</strong>Target loses consciousness (or at least responsiveness) until Stabilised. Gains a new Skill Annex/Descendant.</p></details></details></details></div></div><div class="t13ne-tarot-svg"><details><summary class="t13ne-wyrdtarot"><strong> Wyrd Tarot: </strong> Disaster</summary><p class="t13ne-normal"><strong> Normal: </strong>Target fails with a Disaster (Score of -1 and -4 Success Levels)/ -5 Cards.</p><p class="t13ne-crossed"><strong> Crossed: </strong>Automatic Success +4 Additional Success Levels/ Draw Maximum Roll +4 Cards</p></details></div><div class="t13ne-yarn-fancy">
			<details><summary><strong>Yarn</strong> <br>The Loner</summary>
			<span class="t13ne-description">The Loner is the card of someone who while they may appear to succeed in some aspects of life, they are often failing in others. They may be rich, but lonely, or have many friends, but no true love. They may not feel they lack anything, and are content, but still are pitied by others. Then again the loner may seek solitude, moving far from home (to a new location), or driving others away for their own safety, as they know that what they undertake is not safe for others to assist. It is a card of collateral damage, Pyrrhic victories and great costs, but also huge rewards, if potential dangers are limited by careful planning.</span>
			<span class="t13ne-significator">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-significator"><strong>Significator</strong></summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-plot"><strong>Plot: </strong> The Loner Plot is unique and unconnected to other Plots and events. It is a one off, freak Plot, that may centre around an unusual individual (less often a small group). The Character is usually socially awkward or even socio — or psychopathic in nature. Loner plots are light on Revelations, and almost always leave open questions behind them (such as what was that about? Who was that guy?).</aside>
					<aside class="t13ne-char"><strong>Character: </strong> The Loner Significator indicates a Character who does not really enjoy the company of others. Gain Yin when you are alone. Gain Yang when you ignore others, or try to drive others away. Gain Chi when you move away from others, or they move away from you. They also gain +1 Success Level when acting on their own, or without backup.</aside>
					<aside class="t13ne-scene"><strong>Scene: </strong> The Loner Plot is unique and unconnected to other Plots and events. It is a one off, freak Plot, that may centre around an unusual individual (less often a small group). The Character is usually socially awkward or even socio — or psychopathic in nature. Loner plots are light on Revelations, and almost always leave open questions behind them (such as what was that about? Who was that guy?).</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-scene">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-scene-type">
						<strong>Scene: </strong> Finale
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">The Finale is a final Ordeal (or Quest or Warp), a great battle, or Revelation. This is where the Conflict comes to a head. The Stakes will be higher (High or Extreme) and there will terrible consequences for failure.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-conflict-embodiment">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-conflict-embodiment">
						<strong>Conflict Embodiment: </strong> External
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">One side of the Conflict (usually the Pressed side) is embodied within the Characters (sometimes an NPC), the Dominant side is embodied as something external to all the Characters. Embodiments might include Tones, Locations, Descendants, Quests, Ordeals/Tests and suitable Obstacles.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>

			<span class="t13ne-facet-embodiment">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-facet-embodiment">
						<strong>Facet Embodiment: </strong> Location
					</summary>
					<section class="t13ne-description">The Conflict Embodies one Facet as a Location. This can be a very subtle embodiment, while still allowing the Conflict to progress. There can be a danger of overusing the same Location as the Embodiment is such a satisfying one. But any Location where the action returns again and again is probably an Embodiment of something from one of the higher Conflicts, anyway.</section>
				</details>
			</span>
			 <div class="t13ne-archetypes"><details><summary><strong>Archetype Character: </strong>Miser</summary> <details class="t13ne-Persona"><summary><strong>Persona: </strong><span class="t13ne-output">Hermit</span>
      </summary>
      <details class="t13ne-motivation"><summary><strong>Motivation: </strong></summary><span class="t13ne-description">The Hermit is motivated by either a sense of spiritual purity, or an asocial nature. They do not feel they gain anything by conversation, and may believe they lose something when they interact with others.</span></details>
       <details class="t13ne-avoid"><summary><strong>Avoid: </strong></summary><span class="t13ne-description">The Hermit will avoid crowds and conversations where possible, some will even takes vows of silence or holy orders in order to separate themselves from society.</span></details>
       <details class="t13ne-gain-chi"><summary><strong>Gain Chi: </strong></summary><span class="t13ne-description"><span>Gain Chi when you act silently, selfishly or alone. </span><p>Ordeal gain Chi when you draw a ♦ </p></span></details></details>   <details class="t13ne-core"><summary><strong>Core: </strong><span class="t13ne-output">Crafter</span></summary><span class="t13ne-description">Add Craft Score (or die) to all Skill Annex rolls and all ♦ Ordeal cards.</span></details><details class="t13ne-hitch"><summary><strong>Hitch: </strong><span class="t13ne-output">Confined</span></summary><span class="t13ne-description">When imprisoned, bound, held, confined, locked up, unable to cross running water, you can handle all that with a Key Hitch.</span></details><p class="t13ne-description">The Miser is a lonely figure that loves their money more than any person in their life. They are usually quite rich, but do not like spending their money (which is often why they have so much).</p></details></div>
			 <span class="t13ne-hook">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-hook">
						<strong>Hook: </strong> Help!
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">The Hook is a direct call to help from someone (usually at least a Cast Extra) known to the Character often an Ally or Friend. The person calling for help usually believes (typically with good reason) that only the Character will help.</aside>
					<details class="t13ne-hook-aspect">
						<summary><strong>Hook Aspect: </strong>Geometry</summary>
						<section class="t13ne-description">The Hook targets the Character's Geometry. For example: In order to Hook a Circle Geometry simply offer to fulfil an ambition.</section>
					</details>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-obstacle-fancy"><details class="t13ne-obstacle"><summary><strong>Obstacle:</strong>Lone Guardian</summary><p class="t13ne-description">This way is blocked, by a Solitary Guardian, perhaps a Samurai, or maybe a sewer dragon.</p></details></span>
			<span class="t13ne-gain">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-gain">
						<strong>Gain </strong> 
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">Riches (Talent Treasure Descendant)</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-fray">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-fray">
						<strong>Fray: </strong> Lone Wolf
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">A Complex Ordeal for a single Character (or small group), where as well as trying to get through enemy territory (and accomplish some goal beyond survival), the Character will also be fighting a spread out, but vastly numerically superior force. Individual enemies may be weaker or similar in power level to Lone Wolf Character. The fight may be limited to Medium Stakes in some cases (such as during training exercises, or in cultures where firearms and blades are unfashionable), but is usually High, or Extreme Stakes.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-snag">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-snag">
						<strong>Snag: </strong> Pyrrhic Victory
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">After the Fray, the Embodiment should realise the cost of the battle was too high. Maybe there are too many dead on their side, too many dead on the other — maybe seeing a Cast/Chorus Extra from an earlier scene, now lying dead. There is always collateral damage, so even if everyone is alive, their ancestral home is collapsing in flames behind them.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-sweeping">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-sweeping">
						<strong>Sweeping: </strong> Independence
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">The Pressed Embodiment achieves some amount of independence in comparison to before. This may mean they move out of a guardian's home (gain Location), get a new job (gain Wages Treasure Descendant), it may also represent the Resolution of a Dependence Hitch.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
		 <details class="t13ne-revelation-fancy"><summary><strong>Revelation</strong></summary><details class="t13ne-about"><summary><strong>About: </strong> Pressed </summary><p class="t13ne-description">The information is about the Pressed Conflict Embodiment, which is often a PC in a game, and is usually the antagonist in a Story.</p></details><details class="t13ne-info"><summary><strong>Info: </strong> Where? </summary><p class="t13ne-description">Information about a Location, and possibly why it is important to the Narrative. Is it the Location where the last prophet ascended? Or is it where the Peace Accord will be signed, or is it just where the bad guy lives?</p></details><details class="t13ne-vector"><summary><strong>Vector: </strong> Aura </summary><p class="t13ne-description">The information is conveyed spiritually to the observer, who may observe some clue in an aura. this might be an actual Spiritual Aura or some other visual, and perhaps olfactory clues.</p></details><details class="t13ne-alternate"><summary><strong>Alternate: </strong> Gains </summary><p class="t13ne-description">The information is about a Gain that a Character might make, or how to gain the Descendant or Location. You might need to know how to gain an Ally, or how to retrieve an artefact of ancient power.</p></details><details class="t13ne-detail"><summary><strong>Detail: </strong> Geographical </summary><p class="t13ne-description">Geographical details tell us where things happened, or what the Location looked like, or where something may be found. It can sometimes mean a detailed map, and other times, just that the area is a periodically flooding river valley.</p></details></details>
		 <details class="t13ne-ordeal-fancy"><summary><strong>Ordeal</strong></summary><p class="t13ne-ordeal-type"><strong>Ordeal-Type </strong> — Solitary Ordeal</p><p class="t13ne-stages"><strong>Stages </strong> — 3</p><p class="t13ne-ordeal-spread"><strong>Ordeal-Spread </strong> — Pyramid</p><p class="t13ne-stakes"><strong>Stakes </strong> — High</p><p class="t13ne-test"><strong>Test </strong> — Prayer (Virtue)</p><p class="t13ne-obstacles"><strong>Obstacles </strong> — 1 Obstacle in each Stage with 2 in all Court Stages.</p><p class="t13ne-suggested-action"><strong>Suggested-Action </strong> — Kick</p><details class="t13ne-stage"><summary><strong>Stage: </strong> Fields </summary><p class="t13ne-description">A very long, wide-open fields, spaces, straight paths, roads, or highways. Very long, hard, days of labour, research or travel.</p></details><details class="t13ne-motional"><summary><strong>Motional: </strong> Ground Foot Dash </summary><p class="t13ne-description">Get through the Stages before the time limit (limited in Rounds), on foot, or face serious consequences.</p></details><details class="t13ne-fight"><summary><strong>Fight: </strong> Pitched Battle </summary><p class="t13ne-description">Attacked by a Group of at least as powerful as the PCs, but greater in number (usually by one or two).</p></details></details>
		 </details>
			</div>
				<div class="t13ne-card-lea"><details class="t13ne-lea"><summary><strong>Lea</strong></summary><p class="t13ne-colour"><strong>Path Colour —</strong> Brown/Blue Omnivore</p><p class="t13ne-bulmas"><strong>Allowed Bulmäs —</strong> Broccen, Vermis, Labyrinthine, All-Walkers</p><p class="t13ne-animal-category"><strong>Animal Category —</strong> Megafauna – Largest</p><p class="t13ne-animal-example"><strong>Example Animal —</strong> Cave Bear / Dinosaurs / Mystery Animals</p><p class="t13ne-notes"><strong>Notes —</strong> These Paths are often lost, causing the Bulmäs that use them to appear cryptozoological.</p></details></div></div><div class="t13ne-cardwrap-jsvg" id="cardrewritebox10" data-mode="jsvg" data-wyrd="1" data-yarn="1"><img src="https://www.cjmoseley.dev.cc/wp-content/plugins/t13ne/images/cards-svg/JD.svg" class="t13ne-svgcard"><div class="t13ne-svgpips"><div class="t13ne-textpips">11<!-- write suit--><span class="t13ne-symbol"><span class="t13ne-redcard">♦</span></span><details class="t13ne-suit"><summary></summary><p class="t13ne-name"><strong>Suit: </strong>Diamonds <span class="t13ne-redcard">♦</span></p><p><strong>Ordeal Trump: </strong><span class="t13ne-trump">Preparation</span></p><p class="t13ne-desc"><strong>Description: </strong>Diamonds represent the finer things of life, they are bright and sparkling. In the Tarot, Diamonds are replaced with coins, which represent  not just Wealth, but elemental Earth as well.</p><p class="t13ne-tarot"><strong>Tarot Meaning: </strong>Travel, Money, Business, Contracts, Deals, Voyages.</p><p class="t13ne-otext"><strong>Ordeal Text: </strong>Ordeal Types: Travel (e.g. Chase, Race) / Creativity (e.g. Smithing, Painting). Diamonds act as Trump for the following Action Types: Art, Building, Charming, Crafting, Driving, Emoting, Forgiving, Keeping Your Cool, Listening, Meditation, Motion, Prayer, Preparation, Research, Sprinting and Working in general. Any type of action can be prepared with a Diamond.</p><p class="t13ne-parry"><strong>Parry: </strong><strong>Seize the initative:</strong> The cards of the original attack (less the matched card) are immediately added to the Defender’s Ordeal Pool. They may then play as many cards as their Descendant/Annex normally plays including the Parry card (1-4) usually into one of their Style Reserve, and then immediately discard any cards over Pool Limit.</p><p class="t13ne-soak"><strong>Soak: </strong><strong>Ready:</strong> The matched Attack card is immediately Prepared by the Defender (as they wish). The Soak card is discarded.</p></details></div></div><div class="t13ne-svg-facet"><div>Orthodox</div></div><div class="t13ne-wound"><div><strong class="t13ne-woundname">Cripple</strong><details><summary></summary><strong>Cripple</strong><p class="t13ne-base-pips">Base Pips to summon: 12</p><p class="t13ne-min-pips">Minimum Pips Required: 10</p><p class="t13ne-desc">The Target is seriously wounded, losing access to whole abilities. This can even result in Coma.</p><details class="t13ne-roll"><summary>Rules</summary>Lose 1 Annex Slot (usually the largest one involved in the Roll) until Healed. Once healed Proficiencies may be repurchased for 1 Chi each. Then the Annex will regenerate at Phoenix / Incarna Score Boons per Day. It may be purchased back for the normal cost (Boon in Chi + 2/5/8/12) instantly (or altered and purchased back as a new Annex, if narratively appropriate).</details><details class="t13ne-ordeal"><summary>Ordeal Rules</summary>Crippled Annexes cannot be used as long as the Wound is in play. If applied to a Personality Annex (or a Descendant Master Annex) then the Alternate is unconscious (or the Descendant is lost or useless). They may not use that Alternate (or Descendant) until the Wound is healed (or found / fixed).</details><details class="t13ne-psych"><summary>Psych Wounds</summary><details class="t13ne-negative"><summary>Negative Psych</summary><p class="t13ne-normal"><strong>Normal</strong>Automatically affects the Personality Annex. No Proficiencies or Annexes are lost, but Character loses consciousness at sight of the source until healed.</p><p class="t13ne-extreme"><strong>Extreme</strong>Automatically affects the Personality Annex. Loses consciousness until Healed (not just when the source is present).</p></details><details class="t13ne-positive"><summary>Positive Psych</summary><p class="t13ne-normal"><strong>Normal</strong>Consciousness is interrupted for a few moments (1 Phase). May Gain a Skill Annex/Descendant or 3 Proficiencies.</p><p class="t13ne-extreme"><strong>Extreme</strong>Target loses consciousness (or at least responsiveness) until Stabilised. Gains a new Skill Annex/Descendant.</p></details></details></details></div></div><div class="t13ne-tarot-svg"><details><summary class="t13ne-wyrdtarot"><strong> Wyrd Tarot: </strong> Hitch</summary><p class="t13ne-normal"><strong> Normal: </strong>Target Doubles Chi gained from activated Hitches while atop pile.</p><p class="t13ne-crossed"><strong> Crossed: </strong>Target ignores all Hitches while atop pile.</p></details></div><div class="t13ne-yarn-fancy">
			<details><summary><strong>Yarn</strong> <br>The Teacher</summary>
			<span class="t13ne-description">The Teacher card represents not just teachers, but schools in general, and the Teachings that the Teacher gives. The Teacher represents patience and courtesy, but is also a taskmaster, that demands you demonstrate what you have learnt.</span>
			<span class="t13ne-significator">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-significator"><strong>Significator</strong></summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-plot"><strong>Plot: </strong> The Teacher Plot seeks to educate and elucidate. It may be through the use of metaphor or allegory, more often it is a little more direct. These Plots are based around facts and truisms, often of a religious or moral nature, occasionally of a scientific or spiritual one. The Teacher Plot is also about testing if the student is ready, and rites of passage in general will feature strongly in these tales.</aside>
					<aside class="t13ne-char"><strong>Character: </strong> The Teacher Significator indicates a Character who is a teacher. They Gain Yin when they ask a question or confirm an answer. Gain Yang when they explain, or answer a question. They Gain Chi when they set tasks, or someone else asks a question. They also gain +1 Success Level on any action to do with what they teach.</aside>
					<aside class="t13ne-scene"><strong>Scene: </strong> The Teacher Plot seeks to educate and elucidate. It may be through the use of metaphor or allegory, more often it is a little more direct. These Plots are based around facts and truisms, often of a religious or moral nature, occasionally of a scientific or spiritual one. The Teacher Plot is also about testing if the student is ready, and rites of passage in general will feature strongly in these tales.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-scene">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-scene-type">
						<strong>Scene: </strong> Gain
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">Gains give something to one of the Characters to help move the Story on. Sometimes this might be payment for a job they did, or it might be a discovery that will directly connect to the Story. This may be information. It is normal to play a separate card to define the actual Gain that is made.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-conflict-embodiment">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-conflict-embodiment">
						<strong>Conflict Embodiment: </strong> External
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">One side of the Conflict (usually the Pressed side) is embodied within the Characters (sometimes an NPC), the Dominant side is embodied as something external to all the Characters. Embodiments might include Tones, Locations, Descendants, Quests, Ordeals/Tests and suitable Obstacles.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>

			<span class="t13ne-facet-embodiment">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-facet-embodiment">
						<strong>Facet Embodiment: </strong> Lore
					</summary>
					<section class="t13ne-description">The Conflict embodies as a special type of Descendant called a Lore. Lores are the rumours the Players hear about the enemy and themselves as the story goes on. Lores might grant extra Descendants (including Pacts and Locations) to any Character, as it is revealed that they are a powerful wizard, a Necromancer, with a skeleton horde, armed with magickal longsword, etc.</section>
				</details>
			</span>
			 <div class="t13ne-archetypes"><details><summary><strong>Archetype Character: </strong>Professor</summary> <details class="t13ne-Persona"><summary><strong>Persona: </strong><span class="t13ne-output">Mentor</span>
      </summary>
      <details class="t13ne-motivation"><summary><strong>Motivation: </strong></summary><span class="t13ne-description">The Mentor is motivated by sharing and teaching others their knowledge. They often care deeply about their student's well-being, and may feel very responsible for them.</span></details>
       <details class="t13ne-avoid"><summary><strong>Avoid: </strong></summary><span class="t13ne-description">The Mentor will always try to avoid losing their student's respect. A great mentor will never scold the student, instead turning any mistake they may make into a learning moment.</span></details>
       <details class="t13ne-gain-chi"><summary><strong>Gain Chi: </strong></summary><span class="t13ne-description"><span>Gain Chi when you educate, give advice, help, instruction, or Lecture another.</span><p>Ordeal gain Chi when you make a Lecture attack with or prepare a ♦. </p></span></details></details>   <details class="t13ne-core"><summary><strong>Core: </strong><span class="t13ne-output">Philosopher</span></summary><span class="t13ne-description">Additional I-Ching (can be changing or unchanging). The Player may choose any Hexagram that their Facets might produce (choosing Yin or Yang for Boon 13 Facets), or the Referee may assign one or two Hexagrams or allow both Hexagrams to be randomly rolled.</span></details><details class="t13ne-hitch"><summary><strong>Hitch: </strong><span class="t13ne-output">Stagnation</span></summary><span class="t13ne-description">Things get old, battered, run down, ill, corrupt and slow. the longer something is left the more stagnant it becomes. This Hitch is for all those old things.</span></details><p class="t13ne-description">The Professor is an educator, always teaching and explaining. Of course, they don't always agree with the latest theories, but anything thirty years old or more is usually accepted.</p></details></div>
			 <span class="t13ne-hook">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-hook">
						<strong>Hook: </strong> Exposition
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">The Story opens with exposition, this may be "incluing" where hints about the world are scattered about the text, or more normally with this card, direct narration that describes the setting. This is usually exposition done my a figure of authority, such as a teacher or politician, reciting the information for the PCs and audience.</aside>
					<details class="t13ne-hook-aspect">
						<summary><strong>Hook Aspect: </strong>Geometry</summary>
						<section class="t13ne-description">The Hook targets the Character's Geometry. For example: In order to Hook a Circle Geometry simply offer to fulfil an ambition.</section>
					</details>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-obstacle-fancy"><details class="t13ne-obstacle"><summary><strong>Obstacle:</strong>Lesson</summary><p class="t13ne-description">The way is blocked by a method that in order to pass it a lesson must first be demonstrated to have been learned. This is usually a simple lesson, like sometimes we have to fail to find a better way.</p></details></span>
			<span class="t13ne-gain">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-gain">
						<strong>Gain </strong> 
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">Mentor Ally, Learning Descendant, Annex Training or Proficiencies</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-fray">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-fray">
						<strong>Fray: </strong> Study
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">The Study Ordeal consists of studying, researching, experimentation, and perhaps learning. It might be a Teaching Moment in a classroom, a meticulously planned and executed heist, a science experiment or sitting the bar exam. It may even lead to a scientific discovery, or similar paradigm changing moment.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-snag">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-snag">
						<strong>Snag: </strong> Disruptive
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">The Embodiment's plans, Descendants, or allies are damaged, or interruptive by disruptive forces. Like the child that behaves inappropriately the moment the teacher's back is turned, there is a disruptive force present that complicates all of the PCs plans, not necessarily out of opposition, but as often boredom, or ignorance.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-sweeping">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-sweeping">
						<strong>Sweeping: </strong> Mentor Ally
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">The Pressed side Gains an Ally who will act as a Trainer, Coach, or Mentor. This leads to additional Trained and Learning Descendants</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
		 <details class="t13ne-revelation-fancy"><summary><strong>Revelation</strong></summary><details class="t13ne-about"><summary><strong>About: </strong> Pressed </summary><p class="t13ne-description">The information is about the Pressed Conflict Embodiment, which is often a PC in a game, and is usually the antagonist in a Story.</p></details><details class="t13ne-info"><summary><strong>Info: </strong> Who? </summary><p class="t13ne-description">Information about a Character, usually an Embodiment. It might be their name, or genealogy, perhaps their CV.</p></details><details class="t13ne-vector"><summary><strong>Vector: </strong> Faith </summary><p class="t13ne-description">The information may come from a holy book, or via a cleric, or may just be a matter of personal belief for someone who shares it. It might even be an apparent answer to a prayer.</p></details><details class="t13ne-alternate"><summary><strong>Alternate: </strong> Method </summary><p class="t13ne-description">The information is about a method, list, or step-by-step plan. Villains often have a Method that they are all too willing to share with their captives before they finish them off...</p></details><details class="t13ne-detail"><summary><strong>Detail: </strong> Positive </summary><p class="t13ne-description">A positive detail is something pleasant about an otherwise terrible Character (such as how patient they are, or how charming), Location (Castle Donotenta has the most Ay-Maze-ing tapestries), or Descendant (Well, it's called a Death Ray, but actually it started out as a medical scanner).</p></details></details>
		 <details class="t13ne-ordeal-fancy"><summary><strong>Ordeal</strong></summary><p class="t13ne-ordeal-type"><strong>Ordeal-Type </strong> — Solitary Ordeal</p><p class="t13ne-stages"><strong>Stages </strong> — 4</p><p class="t13ne-ordeal-spread"><strong>Ordeal-Spread </strong> — Forking</p><p class="t13ne-stakes"><strong>Stakes </strong> — Blended Low-Medium</p><p class="t13ne-test"><strong>Test </strong> — Faith (Zeal)</p><p class="t13ne-obstacles"><strong>Obstacles </strong> — 1 Obstacle in each Stage with 2 in all Court Stages</p><p class="t13ne-suggested-action"><strong>Suggested-Action </strong> — Jump</p><details class="t13ne-stage"><summary><strong>Stage: </strong> Jump Stunt </summary><p class="t13ne-description">Leap forwards, across, or skip ahead a Stage or Obstacle if successful.</p></details><details class="t13ne-motional"><summary><strong>Motional: </strong> Water Swimming Dash </summary><p class="t13ne-description">Get to through the water as fast as you can, skipping anything you can skip. Swim or wade as required.</p></details><details class="t13ne-fight"><summary><strong>Fight: </strong> Knave </summary><p class="t13ne-description">A single Monster (usually with at least Two ♦ Monster Facets), that is equivalent Boon Value to any 2 PCs Personality Annex Values added`.<!-- t13ne-shortcode "%7B%22type%22%3A%22facetsuitaspect%22%2C%22suit%22%3A%221%22%2C%22aspect%22%3A%22Monster%22%7D"// --></p><div class="t13ne-tablewrap"><table class="t13ne-table Monster"><thead><tr><th>&nbsp;&nbsp;Facet&nbsp;&nbsp;</th><th>&nbsp;&nbsp;Monster&nbsp;&nbsp;</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td>Awe</td><td><strong>Terror</strong> —The Monster is a Fear or Anxiety embodied. They are quite diverse in form, but are always able to cause Psychology Test attacks such as Fear, Terror, or Horror to any that see them act. They may add Awe Draw cards to their Monster Pool whenever they cause negative emotions in another Character. They automatically play Monster Pool Reduced Cards as a Psychology Test or Psyche Attack on any intimidation or physical attack, but must add at least one Card from their Monster Pool to any Psyche Attack. Examples of this Monster include Ghosts, some serial killers and some Preternatural Cosmic Horrors.</td></tr><tr><td>Craft</td><td><strong>Maker</strong> —The Monster is actually a creator of other Monsters. The spawn are often less powerful than the creator, but in some cases are unique and more powerful. The Creator may not be directly encountered until late in the Story. The Creator can draw Craft Draw cards and add them to its Monster Pool each time it creates or summons an Extra or Descendant. At least 1 card must be used from the Monster Pool each Summoning or Creation to help pay the Summoning costs (or as an Obstacle or NPC as Yarn-Telling). All currently summoned Extras and Descendants receive a bonus to their Annex Score equal to the Creator's Monster Pool size.</td></tr><tr><td>Orthodox</td><td><strong>Tyrant</strong> —The Orthodox Monster is a tyrant, often a part of the system (in that it acts as a method of control over a population). A Tyrant monster may have troops that protect it, or work for it, or it may simply be so personally powerful that it can achieve its own goals. Tyrants may add Orthodox Draw to their Monster Pool every time they are Cruel or give orders. Tyrants can draw and Play current Monster Pool Size Reduced cards on the actions of their subordinates and underlings. However, the Tyrant can play their Monster Pool on their own actions, and must play at least one card from their Monster Pool if they directly act towards their goals.</td></tr><tr><td>Quiet</td><td><strong>Hush</strong> —The Quiet Monster is exactly that, almost (or entirely) silent. It is the creeping death of a hidden illness, or the cat-footed predator striking without warning that vanishes without trace. Hush Monsters get to begin with Quiet Score cards in their Monster Pool. Hushes may add +1 for each card in their Monster Pool when try to they act silently. When a Hush creature makes a noise (often a scream or howl), it must play at least Quiet Draw cards from the Monster Pool upon the action. Creatures like giant owls, snakes or cats, that stalk and hunt silently are good examples, as is a really creepy, floating mime. Many ghosts are also silent, until they turn and scream creating a Duck attack.</td></tr><tr><td>Virtue</td><td><strong>Friend</strong> —The Monster is a ‘Friendly’ monster, that will almost certainly be misunderstood and attacked by some. They may add Virtue Draw cards to their Monster Pool by being kind to another, or when they are attacked. A friendly Monster must protect their friends from harm, and must spend their Monster Pool to defend them. Friendly Monsters can add Virtue Score to any positive action when playing a card from their Monster Pool. Examples might include a cynical, but big-hearted ogre; a being created from stitched together corpses; a dark-elf ranger with a unicorn necklace; or perhaps even an Electric Mouse.</td></tr><tr><td>Zeal</td><td><strong>Radical</strong> —The Monster is an extremist, usually religious or political in nature. When acting according to their nature, or following their belief system, they may draw Zeal Draw Ordeal cards into their Monster Pool, they may Play cards from their Monster Pool to directly increase a Wound as they cause it, or directly reduce a Wound they receive (Pip for Pip). However, if they are forced to act outside of their nature, they are restricted by an automatic GRT equal to their Zeal Draw. The monster could be an evil priest, a terrorist, a devil, a djinn, or perhaps even the Angel of Death.</td></tr></tbody><tfoot><tr><td>&nbsp;&nbsp;Facet&nbsp;&nbsp;</td><td>&nbsp;&nbsp;Monster&nbsp;&nbsp;</td></tr></tfoot></table></div><p></p></details></details>
		 </details>
			</div>
				<div class="t13ne-card-lea"><details class="t13ne-lea"><summary><strong>Lea</strong></summary><p class="t13ne-colour"><strong>Path Colour —</strong> Brown/Blue Omnivore</p><p class="t13ne-bulmas"><strong>Allowed Bulmäs —</strong> Broccen, Vermis, Labyrinthine, All-Walkers</p><p class="t13ne-animal-category"><strong>Animal Category —</strong> Human-paths/Broccen Territory</p><p class="t13ne-animal-example"><strong>Example Animal —</strong> Human / Groonians / Eelafin / Eusaivean / Mer-person / Bigfoot / Giant / Goblin, etc</p><p class="t13ne-notes"><strong>Notes —</strong> Broccen may use Jack’s to walk human paths (social and technological beings), or to dig new tunnels in the Lea, creating species to fill niches. Jacks may also be used to create Chimerical changes, blending parts of different animals together, each Jack adds another place the Bulmäs can play Lea cards.</p></details></div></div><div class="t13ne-cardwrap-jsvg" id="cardrewritebox11" data-mode="jsvg" data-wyrd="1" data-yarn="1"><img src="https://www.cjmoseley.dev.cc/wp-content/plugins/t13ne/images/cards-svg/QD.svg" class="t13ne-svgcard"><div class="t13ne-svgpips"><div class="t13ne-textpips">12<!-- write suit--><span class="t13ne-symbol"><span class="t13ne-redcard">♦</span></span><details class="t13ne-suit"><summary></summary><p class="t13ne-name"><strong>Suit: </strong>Diamonds <span class="t13ne-redcard">♦</span></p><p><strong>Ordeal Trump: </strong><span class="t13ne-trump">Preparation</span></p><p class="t13ne-desc"><strong>Description: </strong>Diamonds represent the finer things of life, they are bright and sparkling. In the Tarot, Diamonds are replaced with coins, which represent  not just Wealth, but elemental Earth as well.</p><p class="t13ne-tarot"><strong>Tarot Meaning: </strong>Travel, Money, Business, Contracts, Deals, Voyages.</p><p class="t13ne-otext"><strong>Ordeal Text: </strong>Ordeal Types: Travel (e.g. Chase, Race) / Creativity (e.g. Smithing, Painting). Diamonds act as Trump for the following Action Types: Art, Building, Charming, Crafting, Driving, Emoting, Forgiving, Keeping Your Cool, Listening, Meditation, Motion, Prayer, Preparation, Research, Sprinting and Working in general. Any type of action can be prepared with a Diamond.</p><p class="t13ne-parry"><strong>Parry: </strong><strong>Seize the initative:</strong> The cards of the original attack (less the matched card) are immediately added to the Defender’s Ordeal Pool. They may then play as many cards as their Descendant/Annex normally plays including the Parry card (1-4) usually into one of their Style Reserve, and then immediately discard any cards over Pool Limit.</p><p class="t13ne-soak"><strong>Soak: </strong><strong>Ready:</strong> The matched Attack card is immediately Prepared by the Defender (as they wish). The Soak card is discarded.</p></details></div></div><div class="t13ne-svg-facet"><div>Craft</div></div><div class="t13ne-wound"><div><strong class="t13ne-woundname">Mortal</strong><details><summary></summary><strong>Mortal</strong><p class="t13ne-base-pips">Base Pips to summon: 16</p><p class="t13ne-min-pips">Minimum Pips Required: 12</p><p class="t13ne-desc">The Target is now dying from the wound.</p><details class="t13ne-roll"><summary>Rules</summary>Create a "Dying / Broken / Burning" quirk Hitch that slowly grows +1 Hitch Boon per Miasma Boon as Chi Limit Duration) until healed. If no Hitch can be increased by the Mortal Wound then decrease an appropriate Facet (Hitch, Incarna, Persona or Core usually) by a Boon equal to the Wounded Character’s Miasma Score. I-Ching, Personality, Proficiency slots and Annexes affected should be recalculated. If the Hitch becomes a Flaw the Wound will automatically upgrade to a Carnage Wound.</details><details class="t13ne-ordeal"><summary>Ordeal Rules</summary>Treat as though the Personality Annex or Master Annex has been Crippled, until the end of the Round. Then add a new "Dying / Broken / Burning" quirk Hitch with a Boon of Miasma Score (to a Max of Half the Incarna Boon). Or increase a quirk Hitch by 1 Boon at the end of each Round.</details><details class="t13ne-psych"><summary>Psych Wounds</summary><details class="t13ne-negative"><summary>Negative Psych</summary><p class="t13ne-normal"><strong>Normal</strong>As Normal but does not create a "Dying" Hitch, but instead a Scars (Mental/Emotional) / Fear / Fatigued / Lament / Taboo / Offensive / Doubt,  etc Hitch.  Plus Consciousness is usually lost until Stabilised.</p><p class="t13ne-extreme"><strong>Extreme</strong>Creates any normal Hitch type. At Yarn-Teller's discretion Mortal Wounds can also alter the Persona, or Core Facet choice (Ref arbitrates). Plus Consciousness is usually lost until healed.</p></details><details class="t13ne-positive"><summary>Positive Psych</summary><p class="t13ne-normal"><strong>Normal</strong>As Normal but creates a Scars (Mental/Emotional) / Duty / Dependance / Paranoia / Devotion / Madness / Vague, etc quirk Hitch.  Plus Consciousness is usually lost until Stabilised. May Gain a new Talent Annex / Descendant or Resolve a quirk Hitch.</p><p class="t13ne-extreme"><strong>Extreme</strong>May create any Flaw Hitch. At Yarn-Teller's discretion Mortal Wounds can also alter the wounded character’s Persona, or Core Facet choices. Consciousness is usually lost until healed. Gains a new Talent Annex / Descendant or Resolves a quirk Handicap.</p></details></details></details></div></div><div class="t13ne-tarot-svg"><details><summary class="t13ne-wyrdtarot"><strong> Wyrd Tarot: </strong> Smith</summary><p class="t13ne-normal"><strong> Normal: </strong>+2 Success Levels to all actions to do with technology, creativity, craft, creation and fixing things while atop pile.</p><p class="t13ne-crossed"><strong> Crossed: </strong>-2 Success Levels to all actions to do with technology, creativity, craft, creation and fixing things while atop pile.</p></details></div><div class="t13ne-yarn-fancy">
			<details><summary><strong>Yarn</strong> <br>The Forge</summary>
			<span class="t13ne-description">The Forge is the card of the workshop, or factory. It is a magical place, a womb of creation, where earth, air, water and fire are combined to create new objects like swords. The Forge is a place of sweat and hard work, but offers great rewards for this work.</span>
			<span class="t13ne-significator">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-significator"><strong>Significator</strong></summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-plot"><strong>Plot: </strong> The Forge Plot is a construction or creation plot. It is normal for such plots to be solely about the creation of a new technology or prototype, although there are always politics, market forces and other factors that revolves around a creation plot like this.</aside>
					<aside class="t13ne-char"><strong>Character: </strong> The Forge Significator indicates a Character who is most likely a Smith or Artist. The card makes them even more so... They Gain Yin by gathering resources, ores, fuel, and materials. They gain Yang by planning, drawing, and designing, writing notes on papers, preparing resources, etc. They Gain Chi by creating Descendants (helping to pay off some of the costs of the Creation). They also gain a +1 Success Level when they are creating.</aside>
					<aside class="t13ne-scene"><strong>Scene: </strong> The Forge Plot is a construction or creation plot. It is normal for such plots to be solely about the creation of a new technology or prototype, although there are always politics, market forces and other factors that revolves around a creation plot like this.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-scene">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-scene-type">
						<strong>Scene: </strong> Revelation
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">Revelations reveal something about the Conflict or Plot to the Characters. Typically a Revelation tells the Characters something about the Villain, or the Prize they are after. Revelations usually consist of 2-5 cards that define: What the information is about, what the actual info is, the vector the Revelation is revealed by, and per haps some Alternative Information that might be revealed (in case you can't use the Info), and a detail that is involved somehow.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-conflict-embodiment">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-conflict-embodiment">
						<strong>Conflict Embodiment: </strong> External
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">One side of the Conflict (usually the Pressed side) is embodied within the Characters (sometimes an NPC), the Dominant side is embodied as something external to all the Characters. Embodiments might include Tones, Locations, Descendants, Quests, Ordeals/Tests and suitable Obstacles.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>

			<span class="t13ne-facet-embodiment">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-facet-embodiment">
						<strong>Facet Embodiment: </strong> Quest
					</summary>
					<section class="t13ne-description">The Conflict Embodies one Facet as a Quest that the Character must attempt. This can be great for creating Chapters of Epics and is an obvious way of deciding NPC Ends, but be wary of using Quests in a substitute for Character development. Great fun, but best used sparingly.</section>
				</details>
			</span>
			 <div class="t13ne-archetypes"><details><summary><strong>Archetype Character: </strong>Alchemist</summary> <details class="t13ne-Persona"><summary><strong>Persona: </strong><span class="t13ne-output">Smith</span>
      </summary>
      <details class="t13ne-motivation"><summary><strong>Motivation: </strong></summary><span class="t13ne-description">Smiths are driven to create things (usually Descendants, although not always). They may sell the Descendants for a profit, but aren't interested in that side of things as much as creating the best Descendants (or otherwise) possible, be they weapons, armour, buildings, spaceships, or cakes.</span></details>
       <details class="t13ne-avoid"><summary><strong>Avoid: </strong></summary><span class="t13ne-description">Smiths tend to avoid waste, any unused materials are recycled into a new project.</span></details>
       <details class="t13ne-gain-chi"><summary><strong>Gain Chi: </strong></summary><span class="t13ne-description"><span>Gain Chi when you spend (Craft Boon as Yarn Limit Duration) time with your creations, complete an Ordeal Stage in a Creation Ordeal, or simply create something.</span><p>Ordeal gain Chi when you use a Descendant you have created in Ordeal.</p></span></details></details>   <details class="t13ne-core"><summary><strong>Core: </strong><span class="t13ne-output">Prevailer</span></summary><span class="t13ne-description">Ignore a Hitch when triggered by paying Sway equal to the half the normal reward.</span></details><details class="t13ne-hitch"><summary><strong>Hitch: </strong><span class="t13ne-output">Madness</span></summary><span class="t13ne-description">Mental disabilities, injuries and illnesses, or just plain barking.</span></details><p class="t13ne-description">The Alchemist, also known as the Mad Scientist or Inventor likes to Craft new things, but they are not always as useful as the Alchemist thinks they will be.</p></details></div>
			 <span class="t13ne-hook">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-hook">
						<strong>Hook: </strong> Loss
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">The Hook for the Character is a loss of something that can be replaced. Perhaps they have broken their sword, or their base is burnt down. The Conflict may drive them to replace this loss, or it may be part of a Villain's plan.</aside>
					<details class="t13ne-hook-aspect">
						<summary><strong>Hook Aspect: </strong>Geometry</summary>
						<section class="t13ne-description">The Hook targets the Character's Geometry. For example: In order to Hook a Circle Geometry simply offer to fulfil an ambition.</section>
					</details>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-obstacle-fancy"><details class="t13ne-obstacle"><summary><strong>Obstacle:</strong>Heat</summary><p class="t13ne-description">The way is ahead is blocked by heat, there is probably smoke or flame too, or it may be scorching desert asphalt.</p></details></span>
			<span class="t13ne-gain">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-gain">
						<strong>Gain </strong> 
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">Temporal, Material and Economic Power (Power Descendant)</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-fray">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-fray">
						<strong>Fray: </strong> Creativity
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">The Ordeal is about the Character acquiring a Descendant. Maybe they go shopping and hunt around for a deal, but usually they make one themselves (or summon one magically, perhaps). The point is that they have to beat a Summoning / Creation difficulty somehow.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-snag">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-snag">
						<strong>Snag: </strong> Requirements
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">Either during the Fray or after it, the PCs will discover that they require something that they do not have. It might be an ingredient, or component, a specific person or piece of information. The point is, they cannot succeed without this thing.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-sweeping">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-sweeping">
						<strong>Sweeping: </strong> Upgrade
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">The PC improves or replaces a Descendant with an improved version, additional Nimbeds or Umbrals may be added to make the Descendant more powerful.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
		 <details class="t13ne-revelation-fancy"><summary><strong>Revelation</strong></summary><details class="t13ne-about"><summary><strong>About: </strong> Pressed </summary><p class="t13ne-description">The information is about the Pressed Conflict Embodiment, which is often a PC in a game, and is usually the antagonist in a Story.</p></details><details class="t13ne-info"><summary><strong>Info: </strong> Why? </summary><p class="t13ne-description">Information that answers a Why question, like why are they doing it, why is it that way, or why won't the enemy leave their fortress?</p></details><details class="t13ne-vector"><summary><strong>Vector: </strong> Voices </summary><p class="t13ne-description">It must be true, the voices in your head told you so. It might be intuition or something more — they just may have guessed the answer — or perhaps they just overheard it somewhere, but can't remember where.</p></details><details class="t13ne-alternate"><summary><strong>Alternate: </strong> True Name </summary><p class="t13ne-description">Knowing a True Name is very important for some people. In T13 it lets you know a Character's Geometry, but it also let's us understand and control Increated a little better, since their True Name is the only reason they exist.</p></details><details class="t13ne-detail"><summary><strong>Detail: </strong> Important </summary><p class="t13ne-description">An important detail is revealed. This varies from case to case, but might include knowledge that the device has to charge for an hour before turning it on, or an invading force is on its way, or that unless stopped, the angels will end the world on Thursday. You know, things that people might like to know.</p></details></details>
		 <details class="t13ne-ordeal-fancy"><summary><strong>Ordeal</strong></summary><p class="t13ne-ordeal-type"><strong>Ordeal-Type </strong> — Solitary Ordeal</p><p class="t13ne-stages"><strong>Stages </strong> — 4</p><p class="t13ne-ordeal-spread"><strong>Ordeal-Spread </strong> — Pyramid</p><p class="t13ne-stakes"><strong>Stakes </strong> — Blended Medium-High</p><p class="t13ne-test"><strong>Test </strong> — Travel (Zeal)</p><p class="t13ne-obstacles"><strong>Obstacles </strong> — 2 Obstacles in every Stage and 3 in all Court Stages</p><p class="t13ne-suggested-action"><strong>Suggested-Action </strong> — </p><details class="t13ne-stage"><summary><strong>Stage: </strong> Quick Stunt </summary><p class="t13ne-description">A game of chicken, remember the facts, listen, pray, or race / sprint / speed</p></details><details class="t13ne-motional"><summary><strong>Motional: </strong> Aerial Winged Dash </summary><p class="t13ne-description">Get through the Stages as fast as possible, if the Character has natural flight this is just a flight, otherwise they may swing from a rope / spider-web / lianna, or have to balance, climb and jump from roof-top to rooftop.</p></details><details class="t13ne-fight"><summary><strong>Fight: </strong> Femme Fatale </summary><p class="t13ne-description">A Femme (or Homme) Fatale is a seductive enemy, that will manipulate and single out weaker members, to eliminate them. Usually equivalent of PC in power, although often a Monster of some type.<!-- t13ne-shortcode "%7B%22type%22%3A%22facetsuitaspect%22%2C%22suit%22%3A%221%22%2C%22aspect%22%3A%22Monster%22%7D"// --></p><div class="t13ne-tablewrap"><table class="t13ne-table Monster"><thead><tr><th>&nbsp;&nbsp;Facet&nbsp;&nbsp;</th><th>&nbsp;&nbsp;Monster&nbsp;&nbsp;</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td>Awe</td><td><strong>Terror</strong> —The Monster is a Fear or Anxiety embodied. They are quite diverse in form, but are always able to cause Psychology Test attacks such as Fear, Terror, or Horror to any that see them act. They may add Awe Draw cards to their Monster Pool whenever they cause negative emotions in another Character. They automatically play Monster Pool Reduced Cards as a Psychology Test or Psyche Attack on any intimidation or physical attack, but must add at least one Card from their Monster Pool to any Psyche Attack. Examples of this Monster include Ghosts, some serial killers and some Preternatural Cosmic Horrors.</td></tr><tr><td>Craft</td><td><strong>Maker</strong> —The Monster is actually a creator of other Monsters. The spawn are often less powerful than the creator, but in some cases are unique and more powerful. The Creator may not be directly encountered until late in the Story. The Creator can draw Craft Draw cards and add them to its Monster Pool each time it creates or summons an Extra or Descendant. At least 1 card must be used from the Monster Pool each Summoning or Creation to help pay the Summoning costs (or as an Obstacle or NPC as Yarn-Telling). All currently summoned Extras and Descendants receive a bonus to their Annex Score equal to the Creator's Monster Pool size.</td></tr><tr><td>Orthodox</td><td><strong>Tyrant</strong> —The Orthodox Monster is a tyrant, often a part of the system (in that it acts as a method of control over a population). A Tyrant monster may have troops that protect it, or work for it, or it may simply be so personally powerful that it can achieve its own goals. Tyrants may add Orthodox Draw to their Monster Pool every time they are Cruel or give orders. Tyrants can draw and Play current Monster Pool Size Reduced cards on the actions of their subordinates and underlings. However, the Tyrant can play their Monster Pool on their own actions, and must play at least one card from their Monster Pool if they directly act towards their goals.</td></tr><tr><td>Quiet</td><td><strong>Hush</strong> —The Quiet Monster is exactly that, almost (or entirely) silent. It is the creeping death of a hidden illness, or the cat-footed predator striking without warning that vanishes without trace. Hush Monsters get to begin with Quiet Score cards in their Monster Pool. Hushes may add +1 for each card in their Monster Pool when try to they act silently. When a Hush creature makes a noise (often a scream or howl), it must play at least Quiet Draw cards from the Monster Pool upon the action. Creatures like giant owls, snakes or cats, that stalk and hunt silently are good examples, as is a really creepy, floating mime. Many ghosts are also silent, until they turn and scream creating a Duck attack.</td></tr><tr><td>Virtue</td><td><strong>Friend</strong> —The Monster is a ‘Friendly’ monster, that will almost certainly be misunderstood and attacked by some. They may add Virtue Draw cards to their Monster Pool by being kind to another, or when they are attacked. A friendly Monster must protect their friends from harm, and must spend their Monster Pool to defend them. Friendly Monsters can add Virtue Score to any positive action when playing a card from their Monster Pool. Examples might include a cynical, but big-hearted ogre; a being created from stitched together corpses; a dark-elf ranger with a unicorn necklace; or perhaps even an Electric Mouse.</td></tr><tr><td>Zeal</td><td><strong>Radical</strong> —The Monster is an extremist, usually religious or political in nature. When acting according to their nature, or following their belief system, they may draw Zeal Draw Ordeal cards into their Monster Pool, they may Play cards from their Monster Pool to directly increase a Wound as they cause it, or directly reduce a Wound they receive (Pip for Pip). However, if they are forced to act outside of their nature, they are restricted by an automatic GRT equal to their Zeal Draw. The monster could be an evil priest, a terrorist, a devil, a djinn, or perhaps even the Angel of Death.</td></tr></tbody><tfoot><tr><td>&nbsp;&nbsp;Facet&nbsp;&nbsp;</td><td>&nbsp;&nbsp;Monster&nbsp;&nbsp;</td></tr></tfoot></table></div><p></p></details></details>
		 </details>
			</div>
				<div class="t13ne-card-lea"><details class="t13ne-lea"><summary><strong>Lea</strong></summary><p class="t13ne-colour"><strong>Path Colour —</strong> Brown/Blue Omnivore</p><p class="t13ne-bulmas"><strong>Allowed Bulmäs —</strong> Broccen, Vermis, Labyrinthine, All-Walkers</p><p class="t13ne-animal-category"><strong>Animal Category —</strong> Black paths and Migratory</p><p class="t13ne-animal-example"><strong>Example Animal —</strong> Native Form — Dragon</p><p class="t13ne-notes"><strong>Notes —</strong> These paths open and close naturally through the Lea from time to time, they are easily walked by all Bulmäs, but Vermis and All-Walkers may walk them in Swarm or Dragon configurations. Queens may also be used to create Chimeras.</p></details></div></div><div class="t13ne-cardwrap-jsvg" id="cardrewritebox12" data-mode="jsvg" data-wyrd="1" data-yarn="1"><img src="https://www.cjmoseley.dev.cc/wp-content/plugins/t13ne/images/cards-svg/KD.svg" class="t13ne-svgcard"><div class="t13ne-svgpips"><div class="t13ne-textpips">13<!-- write suit--><span class="t13ne-symbol"><span class="t13ne-redcard">♦</span></span><details class="t13ne-suit"><summary></summary><p class="t13ne-name"><strong>Suit: </strong>Diamonds <span class="t13ne-redcard">♦</span></p><p><strong>Ordeal Trump: </strong><span class="t13ne-trump">Preparation</span></p><p class="t13ne-desc"><strong>Description: </strong>Diamonds represent the finer things of life, they are bright and sparkling. The Soak card is discarded.</p></details></div></div><div class="t13ne-svg-facet"><div>Awe</div></div><div class="t13ne-wound"><div><strong class="t13ne-woundname">Mortal</strong><details><summary></summary><strong>Mortal</strong><p class="t13ne-base-pips">Base Pips to summon: 16</p><p class="t13ne-min-pips">Minimum Pips Required: 12</p><p class="t13ne-desc">The Target is now dying from the wound.</p><details class="t13ne-roll"><summary>Rules</summary>Create a "Dying / Broken / Burning" quirk Hitch that slowly grows +1 Hitch Boon per Miasma Boon as Chi Limit Duration) until healed. If no Hitch can be increased by the Mortal Wound then decrease an appropriate Facet (Hitch, Incarna, Persona or Core usually) by a Boon equal to the Wounded Character’s Miasma Score. I-Ching, Personality, Proficiency slots and Annexes affected should be recalculated. If the Hitch becomes a Flaw the Wound will automatically upgrade to a Carnage Wound.</details><details class="t13ne-ordeal"><summary>Ordeal Rules</summary>Treat as though the Personality Annex or Master Annex has been Crippled, until the end of the Round. Then add a new "Dying / Broken / Burning" quirk Hitch with a Boon of Miasma Score (to a Max of Half the Incarna Boon). Or increase a quirk Hitch by 1 Boon at the end of each Round.</details><details class="t13ne-psych"><summary>Psych Wounds</summary><details class="t13ne-negative"><summary>Negative Psych</summary><p class="t13ne-normal"><strong>Normal</strong>As Normal but does not create a "Dying" Hitch, but instead a Scars (Mental/Emotional) / Fear / Fatigued / Lament / Taboo / Offensive / Doubt,  etc Hitch.
