Plus Consciousness is usually lost until healed.</p></details><details class="t13ne-positive"><summary>Positive Psych</summary><p class="t13ne-normal"><strong>Normal</strong>As Normal but creates a Scars (Mental/Emotional) / Duty / Dependance / Paranoia / Devotion / Madness / Vague, etc quirk Hitch.  Plus Consciousness is usually lost until Stabilised. May Gain a new Talent Annex / Descendant or Resolve a quirk Hitch.</p><p class="t13ne-extreme"><strong>Extreme</strong>May create any Flaw Hitch. At Yarn-Teller's discretion Mortal Wounds can also alter the wounded character’s Persona, or Core Facet choices. Consciousness is usually lost until healed. Gains a new Talent Annex / Descendant or Resolves a quirk Handicap.</p></details></details></details></div></div><div class="t13ne-tarot-svg"><details><summary class="t13ne-wyrdtarot"><strong> Wyrd Tarot: </strong> Escape</summary><p class="t13ne-normal"><strong> Normal: </strong>+2 Success Levels to all motion Actions while atop pile.</p><p class="t13ne-crossed"><strong> Crossed: </strong>-2 Success Levels to motion Actions while atop pile.</p></details></div><div class="t13ne-yarn-fancy">
			<details><summary><strong>Yarn</strong> <br>The Star</summary>
			<span class="t13ne-description">The Star represents the pinnacle of success, the height of fame. It is the card of glory, excesses and undreamt of achievements, but it is also the card of jealousy, betrayals and rock and roll suicides. The Star may burn bright, but ultimately the night is dark.</span>
			<span class="t13ne-significator">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-significator"><strong>Significator</strong></summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-plot"><strong>Plot: </strong> The Star Plot is mostly about darkness, it may be the darkness in people, or the darkness of space, the cave or night. Darkness is danger, which surrounds the Star, or the Characters in this Plot. The Plot will tempt all Hooked Characters with Glory, Success and Fame, making them important heroes or villains that will shine brightly in the dangerous darkness.</aside>
					<aside class="t13ne-char"><strong>Character: </strong> The Star Significator indicates a Character who believes themselves to a Star. They Gain Yin when they are praised or others do things for them. They Gain Yang when they perform or practise. They Gain Chi when they affect another with a Psyche Attack (positive or negative).</aside>
					<aside class="t13ne-scene"><strong>Scene: </strong> The Star Plot is mostly about darkness, it may be the darkness in people, or the darkness of space, the cave or night. Darkness is danger, which surrounds the Star, or the Characters in this Plot. The Plot will tempt all Hooked Characters with Glory, Success and Fame, making them important heroes or villains that will shine brightly in the dangerous darkness.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-scene">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-scene-type">
						<strong>Scene: </strong> Completion
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">How the Story Completes and Resolves. depending on how the Characters do the Plot may be destroyed, demoted, or promoted.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-conflict-embodiment">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-conflict-embodiment">
						<strong>Conflict Embodiment: </strong> External
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">One side of the Conflict (usually the Pressed side) is embodied within the Characters (sometimes an NPC), the Dominant side is embodied as something external to all the Characters. Embodiments might include Tones, Locations, Descendants, Quests, Ordeals/Tests and suitable Obstacles.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>

			<span class="t13ne-facet-embodiment">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-facet-embodiment">
						<strong>Facet Embodiment: </strong> Monster
					</summary>
					<section class="t13ne-description">The Conflict Embodies one Facet as a Character Monster Facet.This Monster might be a Dragon, an Orc Horde, or just a human Villain. Monsters can be great fun, but are rarely subtle. Where possible when using Monster embodiments, the Plot may want to keep the exact nature of the Monster a secret for as long as it can.</section>
				</details>
			</span>
			 <div class="t13ne-archetypes"><details><summary><strong>Archetype Character: </strong>Star</summary> <details class="t13ne-Persona"><summary><strong>Persona: </strong><span class="t13ne-output">Idol</span>
      </summary>
      <details class="t13ne-motivation"><summary><strong>Motivation: </strong></summary><span class="t13ne-description">Idols want to be respected, honoured, worshipped and adored for who they are, or what they do.</span></details>
       <details class="t13ne-avoid"><summary><strong>Avoid: </strong></summary><span class="t13ne-description">Idols don't like to be alone, or ignored.</span></details>
       <details class="t13ne-gain-chi"><summary><strong>Gain Chi: </strong></summary><span class="t13ne-description"><span>Gain Chi when you speak, or draw attention to yourself.</span><p>Ordeal gain Chi when you play a ♦ to attack a target.</p></span></details></details>   <details class="t13ne-core"><summary><strong>Core: </strong><span class="t13ne-output">Enhancer</span></summary><span class="t13ne-description">The Character may choose one Nimbed effect and add it to any Annex for an activation cost of that Facet's Score Sway (Draw Chi).</span></details><details class="t13ne-hitch"><summary><strong>Hitch: </strong><span class="t13ne-output">Exposed</span></summary><span class="t13ne-description">Perhaps they are obvious, too distinctive, smelly, or brightly coloured, maybe they are wanted by the police, or are just incredible famous. Anyway, they attract more attention than normal because of this Hitch.</span></details><p class="t13ne-description">The Star is not just famous, they are at the pinnacle of their profession. They may not be as talented as some others in their field, but are famous outside of it.</p></details></div>
			 <span class="t13ne-hook">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-hook">
						<strong>Hook: </strong> Danger
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">The Hook just consists of some enormous danger, it may be looming, it may be coming, or it is right here, right now. The Hook may include a Revelation and/or a High-Stakes Ordeal.</aside>
					<details class="t13ne-hook-aspect">
						<summary><strong>Hook Aspect: </strong>Geometry</summary>
						<section class="t13ne-description">The Hook targets the Character's Geometry. For example: In order to Hook a Circle Geometry simply offer to fulfil an ambition.</section>
					</details>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-obstacle-fancy"><details class="t13ne-obstacle"><summary><strong>Obstacle:</strong>Dark</summary><p class="t13ne-description">The way ahead is dark, and full of terrors.</p></details></span>
			<span class="t13ne-gain">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-gain">
						<strong>Gain </strong> 
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">Stardom brings great wealth and recognition. Mansions, cars, space-yachts, all are available, with only one or two Hitches (usually Exposed and Dependence Hitches).</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-fray">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-fray">
						<strong>Fray: </strong> Wish
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">The Fray consists of a Quest, usually to accomplish something personally important to the PC (usually set in "The Ends")</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-snag">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-snag">
						<strong>Snag: </strong> A New Star Rises
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">An new Facet Embodiment appears, which destabilises the situation against the PCs, often making them work with Rivals or even former enemies. This could be a separate Facet joining the Conflict (or popping in from another usually larger Plot) or just another, stronger Embodiment of the Dominant side of this Conflict, such as when Guard and a Gladiator who have clashed on the battlefield before, join forces to fight a Dragon that threatens both armies.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-sweeping">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-sweeping">
						<strong>Sweeping: </strong> Darkness
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">The PC makes a powerful gain, but of a dark and terrible nature, such as a Dark Matter Descendant, Dark Territory, Demonic Pact, or a Monster Ally.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
		 <details class="t13ne-revelation-fancy"><summary><strong>Revelation</strong></summary><details class="t13ne-about"><summary><strong>About: </strong> Pressed </summary><p class="t13ne-description">The information is about the Pressed Conflict Embodiment, which is often a PC in a game, and is usually the antagonist in a Story.</p></details><details class="t13ne-info"><summary><strong>Info: </strong> How? </summary><p class="t13ne-description">How exactly is what they plan to do, going to be done? You may get the full plan or just the important pieces. You might get How to stop it, but not what they do. It all depends on the situation at hand.</p></details><details class="t13ne-vector"><summary><strong>Vector: </strong> Conversation </summary><p class="t13ne-description">It may be an overheard conversation, a chatty taxi driver, or simply crop up as a topic during a morning exchange.</p></details><details class="t13ne-alternate"><summary><strong>Alternate: </strong> Construction </summary><p class="t13ne-description">How something was built, or is being built can be very important, just think about giant space stations and the construction plans for them...</p></details><details class="t13ne-detail"><summary><strong>Detail: </strong> Practical </summary><p class="t13ne-description">Practical details are details you can act on, who cares that the Arch-Mage's Tower is constructed in the Lorican Style, instead the practical details that it is in a swamp, and can only be approached by boat, are more practical details.</p></details></details>
		 <details class="t13ne-ordeal-fancy"><summary><strong>Ordeal</strong></summary><p class="t13ne-ordeal-type"><strong>Ordeal-Type </strong> — Solitary Ordeal</p><p class="t13ne-stages"><strong>Stages </strong> — 5</p><p class="t13ne-ordeal-spread"><strong>Ordeal-Spread </strong> — Map-Grid</p><p class="t13ne-stakes"><strong>Stakes </strong> — Blended Low-Medium-High</p><p class="t13ne-test"><strong>Test </strong> — Sneaking (Zeal-Quiet)</p><p class="t13ne-obstacles"><strong>Obstacles </strong> — 2 Obstacles in every Stage and 3 in Court Stages</p><p class="t13ne-suggested-action"><strong>Suggested-Action </strong> — Sneak</p><details class="t13ne-stage"><summary><strong>Stage: </strong> Crossing </summary><p class="t13ne-description">A Creative Dilemma, intersection or busy, dangerous crossing.</p></details><details class="t13ne-motional"><summary><strong>Motional: </strong> Dangerous Gauntlet Dash </summary><p class="t13ne-description">Get to the end of the Ordeal as quickly as possible in one piece, on foot, while opponents defend each dangerous Stage.</p></details><details class="t13ne-fight"><summary><strong>Fight: </strong> Lieutenant </summary><p class="t13ne-description">One of the Villain's Henchmen (equivalent to any 2 PCs) accompanied by a number of lesser opponents at least equal in number to the Party.</p></details></details>
		 </details>
			</div>
				<div class="t13ne-card-lea"><details class="t13ne-lea"><summary><strong>Lea</strong></summary><p class="t13ne-colour"><strong>Path Colour —</strong> Brown/Blue Omnivore</p><p class="t13ne-bulmas"><strong>Allowed Bulmäs —</strong> Broccen, Vermis, Labyrinthine, All-Walkers</p><p class="t13ne-animal-category"><strong>Animal Category —</strong> Labyrinth</p><p class="t13ne-animal-example"><strong>Example Animal —</strong> Labrys patterned Minotaur</p><p class="t13ne-notes"><strong>Notes —</strong> Kings represent paths in the Labyrinth, and Cæster in the centre as well. A normal Bulmäs that tries to walk the Labyrinth would be lost, and would then be recruited into the Labyrinth. The Labyrinth can connect any point in space and time to any other, allowing Bulmäs to consciously time-travel through the Lea. A King can allow a Bulmäs to create or reload a Chronolith or side-step depending on the power-level of the Bulmäs.</p></details></div></div><div class="t13ne-cardwrap-jsvg" id="cardrewritebox13" data-mode="jsvg" data-wyrd="1" data-yarn="1"><img src="https://www.cjmoseley.dev.cc/wp-content/plugins/t13ne/images/cards-svg/AH.svg" class="t13ne-svgcard"><div class="t13ne-svgpips"><div class="t13ne-textpips">14<!-- write suit--><span class="t13ne-symbol"><span class="t13ne-redcard">♥</span></span><details class="t13ne-suit"><summary></summary><p class="t13ne-name"><strong>Suit: </strong>Hearts <span class="t13ne-redcard">♥</span></p><p><strong>Ordeal Trump: </strong><span class="t13ne-trump">Healing</span></p><p class="t13ne-desc"><strong>Description: </strong>Hearts represent Love, Family and the comforts of home. In the Tarot the suit is called Cups, and represents the feminine and elemental Water.</p><p class="t13ne-tarot"><strong>Tarot Meaning: </strong>Love, Relationships, Affections, Family, Sympathy, Peace.</p><p class="t13ne-otext"><strong>Ordeal Text: </strong>Ordeal Types: Nurturing, Farming, Relationships, Medical. Hearts act as Trumps for the following Actions: Blending in, Cooking, Escaping, Healing, Hiding, Hunting, Insulting, Joke-telling, Lying, Mimicry, Riddling, Spinning a tale, Surgery, and Survivalism. Any type of Wound can be healed with a Heart.</p><p class="t13ne-parry"><strong>Parry: </strong><strong>Locked Respite</strong> All unmatched Cards from the original attack are Discarded by the Defender, however with each card they Discard they may either use the card to Heal/Stabilise one of their own wounds (or damage to the Parrying Descendant), or use the card to slow their with additional RT (Pips) or GRT (Pips/5).</p><p class="t13ne-soak"><strong>Soak: </strong><strong>Disengage:</strong> The Soak Card acts as a Heal card, and is discarded along with the Attack card. This immediately Heals all Wounds in play that have less Pips, including the remaining Wounds in the Attack, by one Wound Level (discards Distracts). </p></details></div></div><div class="t13ne-svg-facet"><div>Enigma, Heresy, Jeer, Liberty, Nature, Phoenix</div></div><div class="t13ne-wound"><div><strong class="t13ne-woundname">Carnage</strong><details><summary></summary><strong>Carnage</strong><p class="t13ne-base-pips">Base Pips to summon: 21</p><p class="t13ne-min-pips">Minimum Pips Required: 14</p><p class="t13ne-desc">The Target should be dead, all exploded or turned to stone, or something. Sometimes though, they keep on going... for a while. And just occasionally, they get better.</p><details class="t13ne-roll"><summary>Rules</summary>Create a "Dead/Destroyed" Flaw Hitch, or increase the Boon of any Flaw Hitch by 1 Boon per Miasma Boon treated as Yarn Limit Duration outside of Ordeals) until healed. May also alter the target’s Incarna Facet choice (turning to stone, etc). Alternatively Decrease Character Scale by 1 or remove a Character's access to the Wounded Alternate.</details><details class="t13ne-ordeal"><summary>Ordeal Rules</summary>Immediately Treat Personality / Master Descendant as Wounded. At the end of the Round either create a new "Dead / Destroyed" Flaw Hitch (Half the Incarna Boon + Miasma Score) or increase a Flaw Hitch by Miasma Score. Alternatively Decrease Scale by 1, or alter Incarna / Persona / Core choices. If the Hitch Boon rises above Incarna Boon the Wound increases to Carnage+</details><details class="t13ne-psych"><summary>Psych Wounds</summary><details class="t13ne-negative"><summary>Negative Psych</summary><p class="t13ne-normal"><strong>Normal</strong>Can create a Flaw Hitch of any Type, as the Ref decides. Consciousness is always lost until the wound is Stabilised. Note may remove a Character's access to the Wounded Alternate.</p><p class="t13ne-extreme"><strong>Extreme</strong>Creates Maximised Flaw Hitches of any type but favours... Fear, Madness, Vague (Amnesia), Paranoia, Loss of Sense (e.g. Hysterical Blindness) or Taboo Hitches. Consciousness is always lost until Healed and may remove a Character's access to the Wounded Alternate. Can create a new Split Alternate.</p></details><details class="t13ne-positive"><summary>Positive Psych</summary><p class="t13ne-normal"><strong>Normal</strong>May Gain a new Power Annex / Descendant. Can Create a Flaw Hitch of any type, Consciousness is always lost until Stabilised, and access to the Alternate may be lost.</p><p class="t13ne-extreme"><strong>Extreme</strong> May create or Resolve Flaw Hitches of any type but favours... Duty, Dependance, Madness, Vague (Amnesia), Paranoia, Loss of Sense (e.g. Hysterical Blindness) or Taboo Hitches. Consciousness is always lost until Healed. Either creates a new Alternate, Intergrates an Alternate one Step toward Coalesce, or Gain a new Power Annex / Descendant.</p></details></details></details></div></div><div class="t13ne-tarot-svg"><details><summary class="t13ne-wyrdtarot"><strong> Wyrd Tarot: </strong> Ignore Wounds</summary><p class="t13ne-normal"><strong> Normal: </strong>Target may ignore all Wound effects while atop their pile.</p><p class="t13ne-crossed"><strong> Crossed: </strong>All Target Wounds affect as though one level higher.</p></details></div><div class="t13ne-yarn-fancy">
			<details><summary><strong>Yarn</strong> <br>The Lovers</summary>
			<span class="t13ne-description">The Lovers card represents the forces of romance and love. As a force Love is usually seen as positive, building and holding together families even through the most trying times, but love can also be destructive. The torrid affairs of untamed hearts ruin families' lives and destroy homes everyday. These are powerful forces, Soul Stakes that play with people' hearts. They are not for the faint of heart, but then what romantic is truly a coward?</span>
			<span class="t13ne-significator">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-significator"><strong>Significator</strong></summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-plot"><strong>Plot: </strong> The Lovers Plot is a Romance. These come in a number of types but usually involve a hero or heroine, at least one Love Interest, and a number of Love Rivals. Stories may be complicated by multiple Love Interests, or both sides having Love Rivals, or by additional Rivalries beyond love. Romances generally end with marriage or death, occasionally both.</aside>
					<aside class="t13ne-char"><strong>Character: </strong> The Lovers Significator indicates a Character who is a Lothario or temptress. They Gain Yin by being touched by another Character. They Gain Yang by flirting, or seducing another Character. They Gain Chi when they are intimate with others. They gain +1 Success Levels with all attempts at flirting, charm, kissing or similar socio-sexual acts.</aside>
					<aside class="t13ne-scene"><strong>Scene: </strong> The Lovers Plot is a Romance. These come in a number of types but usually involve a hero or heroine, at least one Love Interest, and a number of Love Rivals. Stories may be complicated by multiple Love Interests, or both sides having Love Rivals, or by additional Rivalries beyond love. Romances generally end with marriage or death, occasionally both.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-scene">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-scene-type">
						<strong>Scene: </strong> Hook
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">Hooks hook a character into a Conflict. A Hook offers a Character (or Group) a reason to get involved in the Story. This lets the Plot use the Conflict Facet Boon/Value of the Character to help build their Conflict Annex.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-conflict-embodiment">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-conflict-embodiment">
						<strong>Conflict Embodiment: </strong> Internal
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">The Conflict embodies both sides within one Character. The Conflict will play out within them, between two aspects of the Character such as Incarna, Personality, Core, Hitches, Annexes, Proficiencies, or an internal Quest.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>

			<span class="t13ne-facet-embodiment">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-facet-embodiment">
						<strong>Facet Embodiment: </strong> Incarna
					</summary>
					<section class="t13ne-description">The Conflict Embodies one Facet as a Character or Descendant Incarna. Often the easiest Embodiment to create Fantastical Characters, but not often suitable to all Genres and Conflicts. Also this tends to to make for a very obvious Conflict.</section>
				</details>
			</span>
			 <div class="t13ne-archetypes"><details><summary><strong>Archetype Character: </strong>Lover</summary> <details class="t13ne-Persona"><summary><strong>Persona: </strong><span class="t13ne-output">Rake</span>
      </summary>
      <details class="t13ne-motivation"><summary><strong>Motivation: </strong></summary><span class="t13ne-description">A Rake is motivated by hedonism, if it feels good, the rake will want to do it. Sometimes Rakes can develop sophisticated tastes, and they may prefer variety to volume, but not always. To the Rake all of life is a game, and if you aren't having fun, then you aren't doing it right.</span></details>
       <details class="t13ne-avoid"><summary><strong>Avoid: </strong></summary><span class="t13ne-description">Rakes will do everything they can to avoid confronting their problems, but can be remarkably insightful and helpful with other people's, especially if it will get them whatever they currently crave, or stops them asking the Rake if they are okay / <em>really</em> happy, etc.</span></details>
       <details class="t13ne-gain-chi"><summary><strong>Gain Chi: </strong></summary><span class="t13ne-description"><span>Gain Chi when you try to have whatever fun, or interesting experience, that you can, be it a drink, a meal, a seduction, a game, a drug, whatever.</span><p>Ordeal gain Chi when you prepare a ♥</p></span></details></details>   <details class="t13ne-core"><summary><strong>Core: </strong><span class="t13ne-output">Doctor</span></summary><span class="t13ne-description">The character may choose one Incarna, when using or trying to affect that Incarna add one level of success.</span></details><details class="t13ne-hitch"><summary><strong>Hitch: </strong><span class="t13ne-output">Exposed</span></summary><span class="t13ne-description">Perhaps they are obvious, too distinctive, smelly, or brightly coloured, maybe they are wanted by the police, or are just incredible famous. Anyway, they attract more attention than normal because of this Hitch.</span></details><p class="t13ne-description">The Lover, is a hedonist, who is a master of making themselves and others feel good. Lovers are usually beautiful, or at least distinctive looking (or raunchily dressed).</p></details></div>
			 <span class="t13ne-hook">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-hook">
						<strong>Hook: </strong> Who is That?
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">The Story opens with a new Character appearing who is of interest to the PC (usually as a friend or potential new love interest).</aside>
					<details class="t13ne-hook-aspect">
						<summary><strong>Hook Aspect: </strong>Persona</summary>
						<section class="t13ne-description">The Hook targets the Character's Persona. For example: In order to Hook a Sleuth, create a mystery for them to solve.</section>
					</details>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-obstacle-fancy"><details class="t13ne-obstacle"><summary><strong>Obstacle:</strong>Love Interest</summary><p class="t13ne-description">The Obstacle is an attractive individual who catches at least one Character's eye. Positive Emotional affects may cause love at first sight, and the Stakes may raise to Soul, then again they may just be an temptation easily ignored.</p></details></span>
			<span class="t13ne-gain">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-gain">
						<strong>Gain </strong> 
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">Monster Lover - A Powerful Ally that even has some Monster Facets.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-fray">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-fray">
						<strong>Fray: </strong> Test of Love
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">The Fray is a Test, Ordeal or Quest, usually set by the PC's Romantic Interest as a Test of their Love. Such tests can include humiliating yourself, surviving battle, or going against your normal nature, to prove that your have grown as an individual. Tests of Love can also include Quests where the Quest giver is a friend or member of the family.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-snag">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-snag">
						<strong>Snag: </strong> Love Triangle/Jealousy
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">The Embodiment's love interest (who may be the Opposed Embodiment / a Rival / another PC, etc) and the Embodiment's relationship is complicated by a third-party. This may be a Love Rival of the Embodiment, who will try to win the love interest's heart, or it may be an alternative love interest, or any variation (the adulterous husband/wife). This Snag can act as a Hook for a Story in its own right to explore the Tension inherent in the Love Triangle. Or perhaps The Character's Rival or the Opposition Gains a Romantic Interest. Ideally this should be someone the Character is already interested in, but will otherwise just be someone wonderful. If there is no Romantic sub-plot currently in play, then the Opposition makes an important alliance / treaty that will cause the PCs to become jealous.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-sweeping">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-sweeping">
						<strong>Sweeping: </strong> Love
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">The Pressed Embodiment finds love, or a reasonable facsimile there of. This might involve a Gain of an Ally, Romantic Interest, or even a pet. Or it could just be a de-stressing evening in a night club that turned into breakfast.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
		 <details class="t13ne-revelation-fancy"><summary><strong>Revelation</strong></summary><details class="t13ne-about"><summary><strong>About: </strong> Background </summary><p class="t13ne-description">The information is about the background, or setting, not the current plot or situation. It might include pertinent details for the current plot, but is usually just about expositional context in this case. The Player of the card should specify how the detail pertains to the current situation.</p></details><details class="t13ne-info"><summary><strong>Info: </strong> Insight </summary><p class="t13ne-description">An Insight is information that is valuable and useful, somehow. It may be a realisation that the plan won't work, or that the Villain is allergic to Garlic and not actually a Vampire.</p></details><details class="t13ne-vector"><summary><strong>Vector: </strong> Riddle </summary><p class="t13ne-description">The information is a secret is hidden in rhymes and puzzlingly ambiguous statements. Perhaps its an ancient prophecy or a half-completed message left by a dying man.</p></details><details class="t13ne-alternate"><summary><strong>Alternate: </strong> Happiness </summary><p class="t13ne-description">Information about the happiness, or not, of the Embodiment(s) or ppopulation at large. Perhaps there is a holiday approaching...</p></details><details class="t13ne-detail"><summary><strong>Detail: </strong> Production </summary><p class="t13ne-description">A production detail might tell someone how something was built, or trained, or a guess how many the enemy have available.</p></details></details>
		 <details class="t13ne-ordeal-fancy"><summary><strong>Ordeal</strong></summary><p class="t13ne-ordeal-type"><strong>Ordeal-Type </strong> — Opposed Ordeal</p><p class="t13ne-stages"><strong>Stages </strong> — 7</p><p class="t13ne-ordeal-spread"><strong>Ordeal-Spread </strong> — Map-Geography</p><p class="t13ne-stakes"><strong>Stakes </strong> — Blended Low-Medium-High-Extreme</p><p class="t13ne-test"><strong>Test </strong> — Escaping (Liberty)</p><p class="t13ne-obstacles"><strong>Obstacles </strong> — 1 per Stage and 3 per Court Stage</p><p class="t13ne-suggested-action"><strong>Suggested-Action </strong> — Magic</p><details class="t13ne-stage"><summary><strong>Stage: </strong> Fire Stunt </summary><p class="t13ne-description">A dangerous Stunt with flames and explosions, or a medical emergency.</p></details><details class="t13ne-motional"><summary><strong>Motional: </strong> Dangerous Vehicular Race </summary><p class="t13ne-description">This Opposed Ordeal involves trying to get through the Stages of dangerous terrain in a vehicle, before the Opposition.</p></details><details class="t13ne-fight"><summary><strong>Fight: </strong> Bout </summary><p class="t13ne-description">The fight is a training session, or bout, this is a Low Stakes Fight, intended to teach Proficiencies or similar.</p></details></details>
		 </details>
			</div>
				<div class="t13ne-card-lea"><details class="t13ne-lea"><summary><strong>Lea</strong></summary><p class="t13ne-colour"><strong>Path Colour —</strong> Blue Aquatic or Black Migratory</p><p class="t13ne-bulmas"><strong>Allowed Bulmäs —</strong> All</p><p class="t13ne-animal-category"><strong>Animal Category —</strong> All-path</p><p class="t13ne-animal-example"><strong>Example Animal —</strong> All-Walkers</p><p class="t13ne-notes"><strong>Notes —</strong> The Paths of the All-Walkers can allow Bulmäs to do extraordinary things, like walking an individual’s life-path, so that all of their personal history is known to them. All-Walkers can assume even the forms and souls of other people by using these paths, without directly eating of the Soul. All-Walkers can even Doom Weave and play other cards from their hands as Wyrd Tarot cards, once they have played an Ace on themselves.</p></details></div></div><div class="t13ne-cardwrap-jsvg" id="cardrewritebox14" data-mode="jsvg" data-wyrd="1" data-yarn="1"><img src="https://www.cjmoseley.dev.cc/wp-content/plugins/t13ne/images/cards-svg/2H.svg" class="t13ne-svgcard"><div class="t13ne-svgpips"><div class="t13ne-textpips">2<!-- write suit--><span class="t13ne-symbol"><span class="t13ne-redcard">♥</span></span><details class="t13ne-suit"><summary></summary><p class="t13ne-name"><strong>Suit: </strong>Hearts <span class="t13ne-redcard">♥</span></p><p><strong>Ordeal Trump: </strong><span class="t13ne-trump">Healing</span></p><p class="t13ne-desc"><strong>Description: </strong>Hearts represent Love, Family and the comforts of home. In the Tarot the suit is called Cups, and represents the feminine and elemental Water.</p><p class="t13ne-tarot"><strong>Tarot Meaning: </strong>Love, Relationships, Affections, Family, Sympathy, Peace.</p><p class="t13ne-otext"><strong>Ordeal Text: </strong>Ordeal Types: Nurturing, Farming, Relationships, Medical. Hearts act as Trumps for the following Actions: Blending in, Cooking, Escaping, Healing, Hiding, Hunting, Insulting, Joke-telling, Lying, Mimicry, Riddling, Spinning a tale, Surgery, and Survivalism. Any type of Wound can be healed with a Heart.</p><p class="t13ne-parry"><strong>Parry: </strong><strong>Locked Respite</strong> All unmatched Cards from the original attack are Discarded by the Defender, however with each card they Discard they may either use the card to Heal/Stabilise one of their own wounds (or damage to the Parrying Descendant), or use the card to slow their with additional RT (Pips) or GRT (Pips/5).</p><p class="t13ne-soak"><strong>Soak: </strong><strong>Disengage:</strong> The Soak Card acts as a Heal card, and is discarded along with the Attack card. This immediately Heals all Wounds in play that have less Pips, including the remaining Wounds in the Attack, by one Wound Level (discards Distracts). </p></details></div></div><div class="t13ne-svg-facet"><div>Phoenix</div></div><div class="t13ne-wound"><div><strong class="t13ne-woundname">Distract</strong><details><summary></summary><strong>Distract</strong><p class="t13ne-base-pips">Base Pips to summon: 2</p><p class="t13ne-min-pips">Minimum Pips Required: 2</p><p class="t13ne-desc">The Target is distracted by the attack.</p><details class="t13ne-roll"><summary>Rules</summary><strong>-6</strong> Score on next Roll the Character makes and then Discard.</details><details class="t13ne-ordeal"><summary>Ordeal Rules</summary>Lose a Success Level <ul><li>Draw -1 Card on next Draw and then Discard.</li><li> Or <strong>-3</strong> Pips to the next Card played and Discard.</li><li>Or <strong>+3</strong> Pips to the next Attack on the Character, then Discard.</li></ul></details><details class="t13ne-psych"><summary>Psych Wounds</summary><details class="t13ne-negative"><summary>Negative Psych</summary><p class="t13ne-normal"><strong>Normal</strong>Mild Emotional influence (tears in eyes, heightened heart rate, etc) until Stabilized / Discarded.</p><p class="t13ne-extreme"><strong>Extreme</strong>Must scream, swear, or otherwise express the emotion until Stabilized or Discarded.</p></details><details class="t13ne-positive"><summary>Positive Psych</summary><p class="t13ne-normal"><strong>Normal</strong>Mild Emotional Influence (Smile, Pupil Dilation, Eyebrow twitches, etc) until Stabilised / Discarded.</p><p class="t13ne-extreme"><strong>Extreme</strong>Must speak, laugh, gasp, moan, tick, or otherwise express the emotion until Stabilised or Discarded.</p></details></details></details></div></div><div class="t13ne-tarot-svg"><details><summary class="t13ne-wyrdtarot"><strong> Wyrd Tarot: </strong> Friend</summary><p class="t13ne-normal"><strong> Normal: </strong>Player and Target are "friendly".</p><p class="t13ne-crossed"><strong> Crossed: </strong>Player and Target are "foes"</p></details></div><div class="t13ne-yarn-fancy">
			<details><summary><strong>Yarn</strong> <br>The Burn-Out</summary>
			<span class="t13ne-description">The Burn-Out is the card that represents the lingering embers of the once great. Where once a mighty, powerful fire roared, now there is just smoke and warm ashes. It can represent collapse after a great effort, or a futility in beginning.</span>
			<span class="t13ne-significator">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-significator"><strong>Significator</strong></summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-plot"><strong>Plot: </strong> The Burn-Out Plot is about things from the past, that were once great, but have now fallen apart. It may be a plot about an ancient ruin, or an old civilization, something about this place or people has become relevant again. Perhaps a village is found in the Italian Alps where Latin is still spoken, or aliens appear over Stonehenge.</aside>
					<aside class="t13ne-char"><strong>Character: </strong> The Burn-Out Significator indicates a Character who was great, powerful or a genius, but is now less. They Gain Yin when they mope, don't bother getting properly dressed or prepared, or ignore a question. They Gain Yang when they volunteer information or solutions, help others, or offer food. They Gain Chi when they freak out about something (usually a Triggered Hitch). They also gain a +1 Success Level to any activity that can be done while wearing slippers and either eating cereal or watching video.</aside>
					<aside class="t13ne-scene"><strong>Scene: </strong> The Burn-Out Plot is about things from the past, that were once great, but have now fallen apart. It may be a plot about an ancient ruin, or an old civilization, something about this place or people has become relevant again. Perhaps a village is found in the Italian Alps where Latin is still spoken, or aliens appear over Stonehenge.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-scene">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-scene-type">
						<strong>Scene: </strong> Test
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">Some Scenes are nothing but a single Test, often with a lot of discussion around them. Tests are usually a single dice roll, like a Facet Test, and usually a Test of one of the Conflict Facets. A Ref should have some idea what the Difficulty is, what will occur on a pass or a fail, and what a Stalemate would look like. Occasionally Tests may be a single roll against an Opponent (such as the Villain). You can find example Tests in the Ordeal details for a card.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-conflict-embodiment">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-conflict-embodiment">
						<strong>Conflict Embodiment: </strong> Internal
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">The Conflict embodies both sides within one Character. The Conflict will play out within them, between two aspects of the Character such as Incarna, Personality, Core, Hitches, Annexes, Proficiencies, or an internal Quest.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>

			<span class="t13ne-facet-embodiment">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-facet-embodiment">
						<strong>Facet Embodiment: </strong> Proficiency
					</summary>
					<section class="t13ne-description">The Conflict Embodies one Facet as a single Proficiency from that Facet. Characters or Descendants that have the Proficiency may be Hooked to that side. This is a very subtle Embodiment when handled gently.</section>
				</details>
			</span>
			 <div class="t13ne-archetypes"><details><summary><strong>Archetype Character: </strong>Burn-out</summary> <details class="t13ne-Persona"><summary><strong>Persona: </strong><span class="t13ne-output">Mocker</span>
      </summary>
      <details class="t13ne-motivation"><summary><strong>Motivation: </strong></summary><span class="t13ne-description">Mockers like to mock things, they may do this by mimicry of the thing they mock, or by derision and jokes at the expense of those they mock. Mockers are sometimes known as Mimics, as they can be perfect mimics of others behaviours and even looks. Usually though the mocking is intended to be humorous, although it is often not without scorn and derision for those mocked.</span></details>
       <details class="t13ne-avoid"><summary><strong>Avoid: </strong></summary><span class="t13ne-description">Mockers tend to avoid being responsible. Some Mockers avoid originality, preferring to copy others, but some Mockers are original, but prefer not to be mocked themselves.</span></details>
       <details class="t13ne-gain-chi"><summary><strong>Gain Chi: </strong></summary><span class="t13ne-description"><span>Gain Chi when they mock (make jokes about, deride, or scorn another) or mimic (copy another's action), or create a mockery or copy of something.</span><p>Ordeal gain Chi when you play a card the same suit or Face Value (Pips) as the last card played.</p></span></details></details>   <details class="t13ne-core"><summary><strong>Core: </strong><span class="t13ne-output">Pretender</span></summary><span class="t13ne-description">Character may choose a second Persona and gain Chi from it also. Characters may change this second Persona for Heresy Boon points of Yang (Heresy Score Chi).</span></details><details class="t13ne-hitch"><summary><strong>Hitch: </strong><span class="t13ne-output">Doubt</span></summary><span class="t13ne-description">Doubt is the opposite of belief and yet walks hand in hand with it. Mistrust and Suspicion are related, and these Hitches affect a lot of people. Think about whether you trust your politicians, police or your friends.</span></details><p class="t13ne-description">Burn-Outs are sarcastic and usually pretend to be Sloths, rather than Heroes say. They would much rather not get involved , if they can help it.</p></details></div>
			 <span class="t13ne-hook">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-hook">
						<strong>Hook: </strong> Smoke Signals
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">There's no smoke without fire, people say. So when this Conflict starts rumours and gossip, leaving smoking guns and other clues that point to something being up, you can be sure the PCs will jump to all the wrong conclusions, or if they don't someone else will interpret the same signals as the PCs guilt.</aside>
					<details class="t13ne-hook-aspect">
						<summary><strong>Hook Aspect: </strong>Persona</summary>
						<section class="t13ne-description">The Hook targets the Character's Persona. For example: In order to Hook a Gladiator create a competition.</section>
					</details>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-obstacle-fancy"><details class="t13ne-obstacle"><summary><strong>Obstacle:</strong>Burn-Out</summary><p class="t13ne-description">The Obstacle is an area that has been weakened and damaged (usually by a fire), you will have to tread carefully to not bring something down.</p></details></span>
			<span class="t13ne-gain">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-gain">
						<strong>Gain </strong> 
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">Someone's (potential Rival, Ally or Opposed Embodiment) contact details</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-fray">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-fray">
						<strong>Fray: </strong> Burn-out
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">This Fray is an intense Ordeal designed to exhaust the PC to the point of collapse. This may involve staying active for far too long (working night and day for a week), or expending all of your fuel or ammo during the Ordeal. Exactly how the Fray exhausts the Character' resources will depend on the Character and Situation. Generally speaking the Stakes of the Ordeal are usually Medium or High. It is usual during a Burn-Out for the Plot to use Carnage Wounds (and Carnage+) as defence cards, not attack. Ideally the Ordeal will end with a Fatigue Hitch being triggered.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-snag">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-snag">
						<strong>Snag: </strong> Futile Pursuit
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">Regardless of the Fray result, the Snag makes clear that the Fray was a wasted effort. Even though you succeeded, it is a useless success, perhaps you plan the perfect Heist only to discover they emptied the Vault hours before, or you fight your way to the Vampire's castle, only to watch it vanish as you reach the walls.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-sweeping">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-sweeping">
						<strong>Sweeping: </strong> Ashes
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">The Pressed Side has an Test or minor Ordeal that they must deal with. Such as clearing the ash from the Forge after making a sword.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
		 <details class="t13ne-revelation-fancy"><summary><strong>Revelation</strong></summary><details class="t13ne-about"><summary><strong>About: </strong> Setting / Background </summary><p class="t13ne-description">The information is about the background, or setting, not the current plot or situation. It might include pertinent details for the current plot, but is usually just about expositional context in this case. The Player of the card should specify how the detail pertains to the current situation.</p></details><details class="t13ne-info"><summary><strong>Info: </strong> Hint </summary><p class="t13ne-description">Not necessarily information, but a pointer in the right direction. There is a lot of crossover with the Clue Wyrd Tarot Card here.</p></details><details class="t13ne-vector"><summary><strong>Vector: </strong> Puzzle </summary><p class="t13ne-description">It might be a coincidental clue in a crossword puzzle (or a crossword code), or a question asked by a Sphinx, but there is some puzzle that can reveal the information.</p></details><details class="t13ne-alternate"><summary><strong>Alternate: </strong> Friends </summary><p class="t13ne-description">Knowledge of a Character's friends and allies can tell you a lot about a person.</p></details><details class="t13ne-detail"><summary><strong>Detail: </strong> Subtle </summary><p class="t13ne-description">Subtle details can tell us minutia such as the presence of a particular Umbral or Nimbed, or what I-Ching a Character has.</p></details></details>
		 <details class="t13ne-ordeal-fancy"><summary><strong>Ordeal</strong></summary><p class="t13ne-ordeal-type"><strong>Ordeal-Type </strong> — Opposed Ordeal</p><p class="t13ne-stages"><strong>Stages </strong> — 1</p><p class="t13ne-ordeal-spread"><strong>Ordeal-Spread </strong> — Railroad</p><p class="t13ne-stakes"><strong>Stakes </strong> — Low</p><p class="t13ne-test"><strong>Test </strong> — Hiding (Enigma)</p><p class="t13ne-obstacles"><strong>Obstacles </strong> — None</p><p class="t13ne-suggested-action"><strong>Suggested-Action </strong> — Hide</p><details class="t13ne-stage"><summary><strong>Stage: </strong> Bustling </summary><p class="t13ne-description">A busy road, or path, a social occasion or situation.</p></details><details class="t13ne-motional"><summary><strong>Motional: </strong> Ground Vehicle Race </summary><p class="t13ne-description">Get you and your car through the Stages of the Ordeal faster than your Opponents.</p></details><details class="t13ne-fight"><summary><strong>Fight: </strong> Competition </summary><p class="t13ne-description">The Fight is a competitive bout of a contact sport, such as a boxing match, or fencing. Such Fights are usually Medium or Low Stakes against at least Cast Extras.</p></details></details>
		 </details>
			</div>
				<div class="t13ne-card-lea"><details class="t13ne-lea"><summary><strong>Lea</strong></summary><p class="t13ne-colour"><strong>Path Colour —</strong> Blue Aquatic or Black Migratory</p><p class="t13ne-bulmas"><strong>Allowed Bulmäs —</strong> All</p><p class="t13ne-animal-category"><strong>Animal Category —</strong> Microfauna</p><p class="t13ne-animal-example"><strong>Example Animal —</strong> Plankton / Zooplankton / Fungal spores / Bacteria</p><p class="t13ne-notes"><strong>Notes —</strong> Microscopic animal and bacterial paths, often overlooked.</p></details></div></div><div class="t13ne-cardwrap-jsvg" id="cardrewritebox15" data-mode="jsvg" data-wyrd="1" data-yarn="1"><img src="https://www.cjmoseley.dev.cc/wp-content/plugins/t13ne/images/cards-svg/3H.svg" class="t13ne-svgcard"><div class="t13ne-svgpips"><div class="t13ne-textpips">3<!-- write suit--><span class="t13ne-symbol"><span class="t13ne-redcard">♥</span></span><details class="t13ne-suit"><summary></summary><p class="t13ne-name"><strong>Suit: </strong>Hearts <span class="t13ne-redcard">♥</span></p><p><strong>Ordeal Trump: </strong><span class="t13ne-trump">Healing</span></p><p class="t13ne-desc"><strong>Description: </strong>Hearts represent Love, Family and the comforts of home. Any type of Wound can be healed with a Heart.</p><p class="t13ne-parry"><strong>Parry: </strong><strong>Locked Respite</strong> All unmatched Cards from the original attack are Discarded by the Defender, however with each card they Discard they may either use the card to Heal/Stabilise one of their own wounds (or damage to the Parrying Descendant), or use the card to slow their with additional RT (Pips) or GRT (Pips/5).</p><p class="t13ne-soak"><strong>Soak: </strong><strong>Disengage:</strong> The Soak Card acts as a Heal card, and is discarded along with the Attack card. This immediately Heals all Wounds in play that have less Pips, including the remaining Wounds in the Attack, by one Wound Level (discards Distracts). </p></details></div></div><div class="t13ne-svg-facet"><div>Enigma</div></div><div class="t13ne-wound"><div><strong class="t13ne-woundname">Distract</strong><details><summary></summary><strong>Distract</strong><p class="t13ne-base-pips">Base Pips to summon: 2</p><p class="t13ne-min-pips">Minimum Pips Required: 2</p><p class="t13ne-desc">The Target is distracted by the attack.</p><details Success Level <ul><li>Draw -1 Card on next Draw and then Discard.</li><li> Or <strong>-3</strong> Pips to the next Card played and Discard.</li><li>Or <strong>+3</strong> Pips to the next Attack on the Character, then Discard.</li></ul></details><details class="t13ne-psych"><summary>Psych Wounds</summary><details class="t13ne-negative"><summary>Negative Psych</summary><p class="t13ne-normal"><strong>Normal</strong>Mild Emotional influence (tears in eyes, heightened heart rate, etc) until Stabilized / Discarded.</p><p class="t13ne-extreme"><strong>Extreme</strong>Must scream, swear, or otherwise express the emotion until Stabilized or Discarded.</p></details><details class="t13ne-positive"><summary>Positive Psych</summary><p class="t13ne-tarot-svg"><details><summary class="t13ne-wyrdtarot"><strong> Wyrd Tarot: </strong> Inept</summary><p class="t13ne-normal"><strong> Normal: </strong>-1 Prof Die on Target's next roll.</p><p class="t13ne-crossed"><strong> Crossed: </strong>Player gains an additional, applicable Proficiency (May fill a slot).</p></details></div><div class="t13ne-yarn-fancy">
			<details><summary><strong>Yarn</strong> <br>The Question</summary>
			<span class="t13ne-description">The Question card stands for nothing more, or less, than a question. It could be an unspoken question, or a question that is asked over and over again. Sometimes the question will be the same, other times it will be unique. Sometimes the question has an answer, other times it is rhetorical, and just occasionally the answer itself is the question.</span>
			<span class="t13ne-significator">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-significator"><strong>Significator</strong></summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-plot"><strong>Plot: </strong> The Question Plot is at its simplest a Mystery Story. Usually the Question is "Whodunnit?", but there are hidden questions of how and why that may have equally satisfying answers. Any Mystery will fit within the Question.</aside>
					<aside class="t13ne-char"><strong>Character: </strong> The Question Significator indicates a Character that asks questions. Gain Yin when they listen to others speak. Gain Yang when they elicit information from someone without asking a direct question. They Gain Chi when they ask a question. They gain +1 Success Levels whenever they are interrogating or questioning someone.</aside>
					<aside class="t13ne-scene"><strong>Scene: </strong> The Question Plot is at its simplest a Mystery Story. Usually the Question is "Whodunnit?", but there are hidden questions of how and why that may have equally satisfying answers. Any Mystery will fit within the Question.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-scene">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-scene-type">
						<strong>Scene: </strong> Ordeal
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">Ordeals test Characters in a complex way (more than a single dice roll or a Facet Test). Ordeals can range from Low Stakes Ordeals (like doing the Crossword), to High Stakes Ordeals, such as solving a crossword puzzle, while exchanging gunfire with enemies, during a car chase. Action generally, and Fights in particular, involve an Ordeal. Ordeals are usually created by playing a number of cards to define everything about the Ordeal (from the Type, Stakes, Number of Obstacles you should draw and add, to a suggested action that could be taken, or a description of a Stage).</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-conflict-embodiment">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-conflict-embodiment">
						<strong>Conflict Embodiment: </strong> Internal
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">The Conflict embodies both sides within one Character. The Conflict will play out within them, between two aspects of the Character such as Incarna, Personality, Core, Hitches, Annexes, Proficiencies, or an internal Quest.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>

			<span class="t13ne-facet-embodiment">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-facet-embodiment">
						<strong>Facet Embodiment: </strong> Proficiency
					</summary>
					<section class="t13ne-description">The Conflict Embodies one Facet as a single Proficiency from that Facet. Characters or Descendants that have the Proficiency may be Hooked to that side. This is a very subtle Embodiment when handled gently.</section>
				</details>
			</span>
			 <div class="t13ne-archetypes"><details><summary><strong>Archetype Character: </strong>Inquisitor</summary> <details class="t13ne-Persona"><summary><strong>Persona: </strong><span class="t13ne-output">Sphinx</span>
      </summary>
      <details class="t13ne-motivation"><summary><strong>Motivation: </strong></summary><span class="t13ne-description">Sphinxes live for a puzzled expression on another's face. They like nothing more than a good question, or riddle that they can ask. Sphinxes may also enjoy offering cryptic advice and tips.</span></details>
       <details class="t13ne-avoid"><summary><strong>Avoid: </strong></summary><span class="t13ne-description">Sphinxes hate giving straight answers to questions, they'll even rather answer a different question to the one asked, if they can get away with it.</span></details>
       <details class="t13ne-gain-chi"><summary><strong>Gain Chi: </strong></summary><span class="t13ne-description"><span>Gain Chi when you confuse another Character or ask (a question / a riddle).</span><p>Ordeal gain Chi when you play any Distract card.</p></span></details></details>   <details class="t13ne-core"><summary><strong>Core: </strong><span class="t13ne-output">Master</span></summary><span class="t13ne-description">The character has one chosen environment in which they automatically gain one level of success.</span></details><details class="t13ne-hitch"><summary><strong>Hitch: </strong><span class="t13ne-output">Taboo</span></summary><span class="t13ne-description">Whether religious, superstitious or just dietary, Taboos are things your character just doesn't want to do</span></details><p class="t13ne-description">The Inquisitor asks questions, and rely on methods of persuasion (such as how their interrogation room / torture chamber is set up), to pressure people into answering.</p></details></div>
			 <span class="t13ne-hook">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-hook">
						<strong>Hook: </strong> Question
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">The Conflict Hooks the Character a question. This Question may be one they ask themselves, "What was that noise?", "Who is at the door?", or it might be a question that they overhear, "How many men have you got for the job?". The Hook may even be a mystery in its own right.</aside>
					<details class="t13ne-hook-aspect">
						<summary><strong>Hook Aspect: </strong>Persona</summary>
						<section class="t13ne-description">The Hook targets the Character's Persona. For example: In order to Hook a Mentor create a worthy pupil.</section>
					</details>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-obstacle-fancy"><details class="t13ne-obstacle"><summary><strong>Obstacle:</strong>Question</summary><p class="t13ne-description">The way ahead is blocked by a question, this may be a riddle, or conundrum, that halts finding the next Stage (common to mysteries, who is the killer?), or it may be a guardian that demands an answer.</p></details></span>
			<span class="t13ne-gain">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-gain">
						<strong>Gain </strong> 
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">An truthful, honest answer (sometimes the best Gain of all).</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-fray">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-fray">
						<strong>Fray: </strong> Questioning
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">Questioning can be a terrible Fray. Inquisitions, interrogations, inquiries, examinations. Questioning can be a Low to Soul Stakes Ordeal, or even a simple Facet Test. Usually the PCs are the one's questioned, as the Challenge is normally greater that way.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-snag">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-snag">
						<strong>Snag: </strong> Puzzle
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">The Snag is a Puzzle, some additional complication. Maybe the Fray is set in a Maze, or having completed the Fray the PCs are now faced with a riddle that must be solved to continue. Perhaps Clues must be deciphered, or the PCs have to unlock a combination lock to open a door, or maybe the door itself is hidden, and the Puzzle is a secret room.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-sweeping">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-sweeping">
						<strong>Sweeping: </strong> Deeper Questions
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">The PCs make a Revelation, that reveals more complications and deeper questions for the future.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
		 <details class="t13ne-revelation-fancy"><summary><strong>Revelation</strong></summary><details class="t13ne-about"><summary><strong>About: </strong> Setting / Background </summary><p class="t13ne-description">The information is about the background, or setting, not the current plot or situation. It might include pertinent details for the current plot, but is usually just about expositional context in this case. The Player of the card should specify how the detail pertains to the current situation.</p></details><details class="t13ne-info"><summary><strong>Info: </strong> Facets </summary><p class="t13ne-description">Information about the Facets of a Character or Plot, what are they good at, what are they bad at. The information can be relative to the Character themselves (she is much Stronger in attack than defence), or to another (he is much better-looking than you), or be absolute (the standard unit can lift 3 tonnes, but only moves at a slow walking pace).</p></details><details class="t13ne-vector"><summary><strong>Vector: </strong> Red Herring </summary><p class="t13ne-description">False information can be as useful as true information, sometimes. Maybe not this time.</p></details><details class="t13ne-alternate"><summary><strong>Alternate: </strong> Injuries </summary><p class="t13ne-description">The information is about injuries, perhaps damage that something has taken in the past, or what <em>really</em> happened to the test-pilot...</p></details><details class="t13ne-detail"><summary><strong>Detail: </strong> Problems </summary><p class="t13ne-description">Everyone has problems, these details are problems that a project, or person may have. Perhaps a vehicle has a blind spot, the river in a Location floods regularly, or perhaps Bad Bradley is just the product of his terrible relationship with his abusive mother.</p></details></details>
		 <details class="t13ne-ordeal-fancy"><summary><strong>Ordeal</strong></summary><p class="t13ne-ordeal-type"><strong>Ordeal-Type </strong> — Opposed Ordeal</p><p class="t13ne-stages"><strong>Stages </strong> — 2</p><p class="t13ne-ordeal-spread"><strong>Ordeal-Spread </strong> — Railroad</p><p class="t13ne-stakes"><strong>Stakes </strong> — Medium</p><p class="t13ne-test"><strong>Test </strong> — Tale-telling (Heresy)</p><p class="t13ne-obstacles"><strong>Obstacles </strong> — None</p><p class="t13ne-suggested-action"><strong>Suggested-Action </strong> — Lie</p><details class="t13ne-stage"><summary><strong>Stage: </strong> Hubbub </summary><p class="t13ne-description">A busy market, square or park, a social or professional engagement.</p></details><details class="t13ne-motional"><summary><strong>Motional: </strong> Flying Machine Race </summary><p class="t13ne-description">Those magnificent men are trying to get through the Stages faster than you.</p></details><details class="t13ne-fight"><summary><strong>Fight: </strong> Scrap </summary><p class="t13ne-description">The Scrap is a usually Medium Stakes punch-up or street fight, between more than two people (at least Chorus Extras), who each are fighting individually in a chaotic, last-man-standing way. The Scrap can shift to High Stakes if someone pulls a weapon, or starts stamping on necks.</p></details></details>
		 </details>
			</div>
				<div class="t13ne-card-lea"><details class="t13ne-lea"><summary><strong>Lea</strong></summary><p class="t13ne-colour"><strong>Path Colour —</strong> Blue Aquatic or Black Migratory</p><p class="t13ne-bulmas"><strong>Allowed Bulmäs —</strong> All</p><p class="t13ne-animal-category"><strong>Animal Category —</strong> Meiofauna</p><p class="t13ne-animal-example"><strong>Example Animal —</strong> Small Nematode worms / shrimps</p><p class="t13ne-notes"><strong>Notes —</strong> A lot of parasite paths at this level</p></details></div></div><div class="t13ne-cardwrap-jsvg" id="cardrewritebox16" data-mode="jsvg" data-wyrd="1" data-yarn="1"><img src="https://www.cjmoseley.dev.cc/wp-content/plugins/t13ne/images/cards-svg/4H.svg" class="t13ne-svgcard"><div class="t13ne-svgpips"><div class="t13ne-textpips">4<!-- write suit--><span class="t13ne-symbol"><span class="t13ne-redcard">♥</span></span><details class="t13ne-suit"><summary></summary><p class="t13ne-name"><strong>Suit: </strong>Hearts <span class="t13ne-redcard">♥</span></p><p><strong>Ordeal Trump: </strong><span class="t13ne-trump">Healing</span></p><p class="t13ne-desc"><strong>Description: </strong>Hearts represent Love, Family and the comforts of home. In the Tarot the suit is called Cups, and represents the feminine and elemental Water.</p><p class="t13ne-tarot"><strong>Tarot Meaning: </strong>Love, Relationships, Affections, Family, Sympathy, Peace.</p><p class="t13ne-otext"><strong>Ordeal Text: </strong>Ordeal Types: Nurturing, Farming, Relationships, Medical. Hearts act as Trumps for the following Actions: Blending in, Cooking, Escaping, Healing, Hiding, Hunting, Insulting, Joke-telling, Lying, Mimicry, Riddling, Spinning a tale, Surgery, and Survivalism. Any type of Wound can be healed with a Heart.</p><p class="t13ne-parry"><strong>Parry: </strong><strong>Locked Respite</strong> All unmatched Cards from the original attack are Discarded by the Defender, however with each card they Discard they may either use the card to Heal/Stabilise one of their own wounds (or damage to the Parrying Descendant), or use the card to slow their with additional RT (Pips) or GRT (Pips/5).</p><p class="t13ne-soak"><strong>Soak: </strong><strong>Disengage:</strong> The Soak Card acts as a Heal card, and is discarded along with the Attack card. This immediately Heals all Wounds in play that have less Pips, including the remaining Wounds in the Attack, by one Wound Level (discards Distracts). </p></details></div></div><div class="t13ne-svg-facet"><div>Heresy</div></div><div class="t13ne-wound"><div><strong class="t13ne-woundname">Distract</strong><details><summary></summary><strong>Distract</strong><p class="t13ne-base-pips">Base Pips to summon: 2</p><p class="t13ne-min-pips">Minimum Pips Required: 2</p><p class="t13ne-desc">The Target is distracted by the attack.</p><details class="t13ne-roll"><summary>Rules</summary><strong>-6</strong> Score on next Roll the Character makes and then Discard.</details><details class="t13ne-ordeal"><summary>Ordeal Rules</summary>Lose a Success Level <ul><li>Draw -1 Card on next Draw and then Discard.</li><li> Or <strong>-3</strong> Pips to the next Card played and Discard.</li><li>Or <strong>+3</strong> Pips to the next Attack on the Character, then Discard.</li></ul></details><details class="t13ne-psych"><summary>Psych Wounds</summary><details class="t13ne-negative"><summary>Negative Psych</summary><p class="t13ne-normal"><strong>Normal</strong>Mild Emotional influence (tears in eyes, heightened heart rate, etc) until Stabilized / Discarded.</p><p class="t13ne-extreme"><strong>Extreme</strong>Must scream, swear, or otherwise express the emotion until Stabilized or Discarded.</p></details><details class="t13ne-positive"><summary>Positive Psych</summary><p class="t13ne-normal"><strong>Normal</strong>Mild Emotional Influence (Smile, Pupil Dilation, Eyebrow twitches, etc) until Stabilised / Discarded.</p><p class="t13ne-extreme"><strong>Extreme</strong>Must speak, laugh, gasp, moan, tick, or otherwise express the emotion until Stabilised or Discarded.</p></details></details></details></div></div><div class="t13ne-tarot-svg"><details><summary class="t13ne-wyrdtarot"><strong> Wyrd Tarot: </strong> Rally</summary><p class="t13ne-normal"><strong> Normal: </strong>+1 Success Level on any Psychology Test</p><p class="t13ne-crossed"><strong> Crossed: </strong>-1 Success Level on Any Psychology Test</p></details></div><div class="t13ne-yarn-fancy">
			<details><summary><strong>Yarn</strong> <br>The Myth</summary>
			<span class="t13ne-description">The Myth represents those untruths that can gather a truth of their own. It maybe a simple lie that was believed, a complex misunderstanding, or story told as an allegory or warning, such as the stories of Ginny Greenteeth teach children to avoid water, and the myth of the the Big Bad Wolf teach them to avoid the dark woods.</span>
			<span class="t13ne-significator">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-significator"><strong>Significator</strong></summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-plot"><strong>Plot: </strong> The Myth Plot is a plot that concerns enormous lies or ancient legends. The Plot usually utilises ancient stories and myths, but recast within the terms of the current Conflict. This might create a modern version of Arthurian Legend, or a cyberspace version of an ancient Jewish Demon. There is always some aspect of the update that makes a lie of the original Myth, while retaining some Truth from it.</aside>
					<aside class="t13ne-char"><strong>Character: </strong> The Myth Significator indicates a Character who lies easily and fluently. They Gain Yin when they bend the truth, or present it in a way that makes it unbelievable. They Gain Yang when they lie, or "embellish" a story. They Gain Chi when their (mostly) true stories are disbelieved, or their lies and embellishments are repeated as fact. They also gain +1 Success Levels to all actions to do with belief, lies and myths.</aside>
					<aside class="t13ne-scene"><strong>Scene: </strong> The Myth Plot is a plot that concerns enormous lies or ancient legends. The Plot usually utilises ancient stories and myths, but recast within the terms of the current Conflict. This might create a modern version of Arthurian Legend, or a cyberspace version of an ancient Jewish Demon. There is always some aspect of the update that makes a lie of the original Myth, while retaining some Truth from it.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-scene">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-scene-type">
						<strong>Scene: </strong> Gain
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">Gains give something to one of the Characters to help move the Story on. Sometimes this might be payment for a job they did, or it might be a discovery that will directly connect to the Story. This may be information. It is normal to play a separate card to define the actual Gain that is made.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-conflict-embodiment">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-conflict-embodiment">
						<strong>Conflict Embodiment: </strong> Internal
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">The Conflict embodies both sides within one Character. The Conflict will play out within them, between two aspects of the Character such as Incarna, Personality, Core, Hitches, Annexes, Proficiencies, or an internal Quest.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>

			<span class="t13ne-facet-embodiment">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-facet-embodiment">
						<strong>Facet Embodiment: </strong> Annex
					</summary>
					<section class="t13ne-description">The Conflict Embodies one Facet within an Annex, this could be a Subtle Nimbed or Umbral, or be as obvious as being Root, Channel and at least one Umbral. The Annex may be within Descendants, or Characters.</section>
				</details>
			</span>
			 <div class="t13ne-archetypes"><details><summary><strong>Archetype Character: </strong>Fabulist</summary> <details class="t13ne-Persona"><summary><strong>Persona: </strong><span class="t13ne-output">Deviant</span>
      </summary>
      <details class="t13ne-motivation"><summary><strong>Motivation: </strong></summary><span class="t13ne-description">Deviants are motivated by being different from others. They like to see themselves as a lone wolf, or a trendsetter, never as a follower or leader. Deviants may have unique beliefs, or a code of practice that puts them at odds with the larger society around them, as is the case with many Cults or secret societies. Deviants may have goals, that they pursue by deception and manipulation, but are as often aimlessly reacting to what happens around them.</span></details>
       <details class="t13ne-avoid"><summary><strong>Avoid: </strong></summary><span class="t13ne-description">Deviants hate acting like everyone else, and dislike being predictable.</span></details>
       <details class="t13ne-gain-chi"><summary><strong>Gain Chi: </strong></summary><span class="t13ne-description"><span>Gain Chi when you act / react differently to everyone else, or when you deceive or surprise another.</span><p>Ordeal gain Chi when you play a ♥ to attack.</p></span></details></details>   <details class="t13ne-core"><summary><strong>Core: </strong><span class="t13ne-output">Pretender</span></summary><span class="t13ne-description">Character may choose a second Persona and gain Chi from it also. Characters may change this second Persona for Heresy Boon points of Yang (Heresy Score Chi).</span></details><details class="t13ne-hitch"><summary><strong>Hitch: </strong><span class="t13ne-output">Persecuted</span></summary><span class="t13ne-description">Some people are abused or controlled by bullies, or society, others are persecuted by the gods themselves. Either way the Persecuted Hitch is how you model this.</span></details><p class="t13ne-description">Fabulists like making things up, sometimes they don't even know that they are imagining these things, they might just be hallucinations that bear a passing resemblance to reality. Then again some people are pathological liars, they just can't help adjusting the narrative of their life.</p></details></div>
			 <span class="t13ne-hook">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-hook">
						<strong>Hook: </strong> Mistake
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">The story hooks the character with an apparent mistake. A misdelivered parcel, or message, or some other confusion. Often opens a comedy.</aside>
					<details class="t13ne-hook-aspect">
						<summary><strong>Hook Aspect: </strong>Persona</summary>
						<section class="t13ne-description">The Hook targets the Character's Persona. For example: In order to Hook a Guard you must threaten someone.</section>
					</details>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-obstacle-fancy"><details class="t13ne-obstacle"><summary><strong>Obstacle:</strong>Myth</summary><p class="t13ne-description">When a Myth blocks the way, you are either talking about some Superstition that must be overcome, some commonly believed factoid that must be discarded, or Guardians that are actually Mythic (Dragons, Knight of Round Table, etc)</p></details></span>
			<span class="t13ne-gain">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-gain">
						<strong>Gain </strong> 
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">A Lore Descendant (as part of a Revelation about themselves)</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-fray">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-fray">
						<strong>Fray: </strong> Legend
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">The Fray concerns a Legend. Maybe the PCs need to solve an ancient mystery to find a legendary treasure, defeat a legendary Monster, find a legendary place, or otherwise achieve the apparently impossible.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-snag">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-snag">
						<strong>Snag: </strong> Fable
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">Either the Fray involves the PCs having to lie for all they're worth, or they make a false Revelation. The False Revelation may retcon the last Revelation (making everything learned false) or it can make the next Revelation false, as the Yarn-Teller prefers.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-sweeping">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-sweeping">
						<strong>Sweeping: </strong> Myth Revealed
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">A Lie from earlier is revealed (such as information from an earlier Revelation, or some assumption the PCs made).</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
		 <details class="t13ne-revelation-fancy"><summary><strong>Revelation</strong></summary><details class="t13ne-about"><summary><strong>About: </strong> Setting / Background </summary><p class="t13ne-description">The information is about the background, or setting, not the current plot or situation. It might include pertinent details for the current plot, but is usually just about expositional context in this case. The Player of the card should specify how the detail pertains to the current situation.</p></details><details class="t13ne-info"><summary><strong>Info: </strong> Decisions </summary><p class="t13ne-description">The Information is about Decisions that have been made, or will have to made. Perhaps the king decided to go to war for glory, and is now raising taxes to finance his crusade or invasion. Or maybe there is a vote looming in parliament.</p></details><details class="t13ne-vector"><summary><strong>Vector: </strong> A Lie </summary><p class="t13ne-description">A Lie reveals the information somehow. Perhaps the falsehood protects someone else, or the lie may be part of an alibi that cannot be true, or maybe the lie is an extrapolation of an actual truth that they didn't realise they were revealing, and do they know they have been caught out?</p></details><details class="t13ne-alternate"><summary><strong>Alternate: </strong> Pursuits </summary><p class="t13ne-description">Pursuits are things that we do to spend the time we have. Does the bad guy work tirelessly on learning new magic, or do they train daily with live weapons? Is the national Hobby flower-arranging or mounted-archery? Important things to know when you are invading.</p></details><details class="t13ne-detail"><summary><strong>Detail: </strong> Cultural </summary><p class="t13ne-description">Cultural details tell us about the culture of a Character, or the people that constructed, or live around a Descendant. You might want to reveal they are a warrior people, how is this expressed? Does every adult carry a sword, a shield, or a gun at all times? Do they wear armour as part of their uniform, or is it something more subtle, like chess being the national game, or ammo pockets and bandoleers are really in fashion right now?</p></details></details>
		 <details class="t13ne-ordeal-fancy"><summary><strong>Ordeal</strong></summary><p class="t13ne-ordeal-type"><strong>Ordeal-Type </strong> — Opposed Ordeal</p><p class="t13ne-stages"><strong>Stages </strong> — 2</p><p class="t13ne-ordeal-spread"><strong>Ordeal-Spread </strong> — Forking</p><p class="t13ne-stakes"><strong>Stakes </strong> — High</p><p class="t13ne-test"><strong>Test </strong> — Blasphemy (Heresy)</p><p class="t13ne-obstacles"><strong>Obstacles </strong> — None</p><p class="t13ne-suggested-action"><strong>Suggested-Action </strong> — Blame</p><details class="t13ne-stage"><summary><strong>Stage: </strong> Crowds </summary><p class="t13ne-description">A large crowd of people, an audience, a stadium or theatre. A convention or conference.</p></details><details class="t13ne-motional"><summary><strong>Motional: </strong> Road Race </summary><p class="t13ne-description">First to finish wins. Sometimes they race for pink slips.</p></details><details class="t13ne-fight"><summary><strong>Fight: </strong> Hold-fast </summary><p class="t13ne-description">The fight takes place in a single, usually, defensive location. The location is assaulted by a stream, or waves, of lesser or equal (Vex - Cast)opponents. Generally, the defending side is waiting for back-up, or relief to arrive.</p></details></details>
		 </details>
			</div>
				<div class="t13ne-card-lea"><details class="t13ne-lea"><summary><strong>Lea</strong></summary><p class="t13ne-colour"><strong>Path Colour —</strong> Blue Aquatic or Black Migratory</p><p class="t13ne-bulmas"><strong>Allowed Bulmäs —</strong> All</p><p class="t13ne-animal-category"><strong>Animal Category —</strong> Mesofauna</p><p class="t13ne-animal-example"><strong>Example Animal —</strong> Greenfly / Midge / Maggots / Woodlice</p><p class="t13ne-notes"><strong>Notes —</strong> Tiny Arthropods</p></details></div></div><div class="t13ne-cardwrap-jsvg" id="cardrewritebox17" data-mode="jsvg" data-wyrd="1" data-yarn="1"><img src="https://www.cjmoseley.dev.cc/wp-content/plugins/t13ne/images/cards-svg/5H.svg" class="t13ne-svgcard"><div class="t13ne-svgpips"><div class="t13ne-textpips">5<!-- write suit--><span class="t13ne-symbol"><span class="t13ne-redcard">♥</span></span><details class="t13ne-suit"><summary></summary><p class="t13ne-name"><strong>Suit: </strong>Hearts <span class="t13ne-redcard">♥</span></p><p><strong>Ordeal Trump: </strong><span class="t13ne-trump">Healing</span></p><p class="t13ne-desc"><strong>Description: </strong>Hearts represent Love, Family and the comforts of home. In the Tarot the suit is called Cups, and represents the feminine and elemental Water.</p><p class="t13ne-tarot"><strong>Tarot Meaning: </strong>Love, Relationships, Affections, Family, Sympathy, Peace.</p><p class="t13ne-otext"><strong>Ordeal Text: </strong>Ordeal Types: Nurturing, Farming, Relationships, Medical. Hearts act as Trumps for the following Actions: Blending in, Cooking, Escaping, Healing, Hiding, Hunting, Insulting, Joke-telling, Lying, Mimicry, Riddling, Spinning a tale, Surgery, and Survivalism. Any type of Wound can be healed with a Heart.</p><p class="t13ne-parry"><strong>Parry: </strong><strong>Locked Respite</strong> All unmatched Cards from the original attack are Discarded by the Defender, however with each card they Discard they may either use the card to Heal/Stabilise one of their own wounds (or damage to the Parrying Descendant), or use the card to slow their with additional RT (Pips) or GRT (Pips/5).</p><p class="t13ne-soak"><strong>Soak: </strong><strong>Disengage:</strong> The Soak Card acts as a Heal card, and is discarded along with the Attack card.
