This immediately Heals all Wounds in play that have less Pips, including the remaining Wounds in the Attack, by one Wound Level (discards Distracts). </p></details></div></div><div class="t13ne-svg-facet"><div>Jeer</div></div><div class="t13ne-wound"><div><strong class="t13ne-woundname">Flesh</strong><details><summary></summary><strong>Flesh</strong><p class="t13ne-base-pips">Base Pips to summon: 5</p><p class="t13ne-min-pips">Minimum Pips Required: 5</p><p class="t13ne-desc">The Target is wounded and must stop and aknowledge that fact.</p><details class="t13ne-roll"><summary>Rules</summary>Lose one <strong>die</strong> from each pool on the next roll you make (e.g.3d6 + 2d10+5 becomes 2d6 + 1d10+5) then Discard</details><details class="t13ne-ordeal"><summary>Ordeal Rules</summary>Discard an Ordeal card from the next Full-Action or aggravate another Wound 1 Level and then Discard. </details><details class="t13ne-psych"><summary>Psych Wounds</summary><details class="t13ne-negative"><summary>Negative Psych</summary><p class="t13ne-normal"><strong>Normal</strong>Cannot approach source of negative emotion until Stabilized / Discarded</p><p class="t13ne-extreme"><strong>Extreme</strong>Paralysed (All Proficiency Dice are lost) until Stabilised / Discarded.</p></details><details class="t13ne-positive"><summary>Positive Psych</summary><p class="t13ne-normal"><strong>Normal</strong>Unwilling to leave source of positive emotion until discarded</p><p class="t13ne-extreme"><strong>Extreme</strong>Stimulated/Excited. Discard immediately, along with all GRT and RT Chips and act immediately (still with the dice or card penalties).</p></details></details></details></div></div><div class="t13ne-tarot-svg"><details><summary class="t13ne-wyrdtarot"><strong> Wyrd Tarot: </strong> Clue</summary><p class="t13ne-normal"><strong> Normal: </strong>Gain a hint from the Ref</p><p class="t13ne-crossed"><strong> Crossed: </strong>Automatically apply 1d6 Distract Wounds to Target</p></details></div><div class="t13ne-yarn-fancy">
			<details><summary><strong>Yarn</strong> <br>The Joke</summary>
			<span class="t13ne-description">The Joke card represents a funny situation. It may be as simple as a pun, or as complicated as a wildly complex sequence of rational, normal seeming events that leads to an ultimately absurd situation.</span>
			<span class="t13ne-significator">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-significator"><strong>Significator</strong></summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-plot"><strong>Plot: </strong> The Joke Plot is traditionally known as a Comedy. Comedies are usually built around a central misunderstanding, or confusion that creates the comedic situation. Classically these are usually cases of mistaken identity, suspected infidelity, misunderstandings, gossip and rumour. In more modern comedies these components may be present along with satire, parody, witty banter, comedic insults, social awkwardness, and slapstick to create funny, farcical, or just cringe-worthy situations.</aside>
					<aside class="t13ne-char"><strong>Character: </strong> The Joke Significator indicates a Character who is a comedian. They Gain Yin when they act stupid or uncomprehending. They Gain Yang when they tell jokes, or attempt humour. They Gain Chi when anyone laughs. They also gain +1 Success Levels on any Positive Psyche Attacks that they may attempt.</aside>
					<aside class="t13ne-scene"><strong>Scene: </strong> The Joke Plot is traditionally known as a Comedy. Comedies are usually built around a central misunderstanding, or confusion that creates the comedic situation. Classically these are usually cases of mistaken identity, suspected infidelity, misunderstandings, gossip and rumour. In more modern comedies these components may be present along with satire, parody, witty banter, comedic insults, social awkwardness, and slapstick to create funny, farcical, or just cringe-worthy situations.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-scene">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-scene-type">
						<strong>Scene: </strong> Revelation
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">Revelations reveal something about the Conflict or Plot to the Characters. Typically a Revelation tells the Characters something about the Villain, or the Prize they are after. Revelations usually consist of 2-5 cards that define: What the information is about, what the actual info is, the vector the Revelation is revealed by, and per haps some Alternative Information that might be revealed (in case you can't use the Info), and a detail that is involved somehow.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-conflict-embodiment">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-conflict-embodiment">
						<strong>Conflict Embodiment: </strong> Internal
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">The Conflict embodies both sides within one Character. The Conflict will play out within them, between two aspects of the Character such as Incarna, Personality, Core, Hitches, Annexes, Proficiencies, or an internal Quest.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>

			<span class="t13ne-facet-embodiment">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-facet-embodiment">
						<strong>Facet Embodiment: </strong> Hitch
					</summary>
					<section class="t13ne-description">The Conflict Embodies one Facet as a Character Hitch. This can create quite secretive and subtle embodiments that can be very difficult to spot. Other times Hitch Embodiments can be very obvious, and in the case of Woes, glaring.</section>
				</details>
			</span>
			 <div class="t13ne-archetypes"><details><summary><strong>Archetype Character: </strong>Clown</summary> <details class="t13ne-Persona"><summary><strong>Persona: </strong><span class="t13ne-output">Deviant</span>
      </summary>
      <details class="t13ne-motivation"><summary><strong>Motivation: </strong></summary><span class="t13ne-description">Deviants are motivated by being different from others. They like to see themselves as a lone wolf, or a trendsetter, never as a follower or leader. Deviants may have unique beliefs, or a code of practice that puts them at odds with the larger society around them, as is the case with many Cults or secret societies. Deviants may have goals, that they pursue by deception and manipulation, but are as often aimlessly reacting to what happens around them.</span></details>
       <details class="t13ne-avoid"><summary><strong>Avoid: </strong></summary><span class="t13ne-description">Deviants hate acting like everyone else, and dislike being predictable.</span></details>
       <details class="t13ne-gain-chi"><summary><strong>Gain Chi: </strong></summary><span class="t13ne-description"><span>Gain Chi when you act / react differently to everyone else, or when you deceive or surprise another.</span><p>Ordeal gain Chi when you play a ♥ to attack.</p></span></details></details>   <details class="t13ne-core"><summary><strong>Core: </strong><span class="t13ne-output">Joker</span></summary><span class="t13ne-description">Gain Chi and +1 success level when another nearby laughs.</span></details><details class="t13ne-hitch"><summary><strong>Hitch: </strong><span class="t13ne-output">Exposed</span></summary><span class="t13ne-description">Perhaps they are obvious, too distinctive, smelly, or brightly coloured, maybe they are wanted by the police, or are just incredible famous. Anyway, they attract more attention than normal because of this Hitch.</span></details><p class="t13ne-description">Clowns are a type of entertainer that act as though they are separate to society and its norms. The Clown's performance while comedic is based on the idea that the absurd is normal for the Character, whereas the normal is extraordinary. Not to be confused with the Fool.</p></details></div>
			 <span class="t13ne-hook">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-hook">
						<strong>Hook: </strong> Set up
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">The set up is the beginning of the joke, in a comedy this is usually a misunderstanding, in a shorter joke this is a question, somewhere in between it is a sentence or two that creates a narrative framework that misleads the audience. At its very simplest it is a cliche, such as a bunch of adventurers walk into a bar. Occasionally, it is a physical set up to a prank or stunt that will be important later. And every now and then it is a literal set up, with a situation created to put the PCs in the Narrative.</aside>
					<details class="t13ne-hook-aspect">
						<summary><strong>Hook Aspect: </strong>Persona</summary>
						<section class="t13ne-description">The Hook targets the Character's Persona. For example: In order to Hook a Mocker they will need someone to mock or copy.</section>
					</details>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-obstacle-fancy"><details class="t13ne-obstacle"><summary><strong>Obstacle:</strong>Joke</summary><p class="t13ne-description">Sometimes the way is blocked by some prank or facade, pranks are a type of psych snare, that can provide both positive and negative emotional effects, usually negative to the target but with positive effects on an audience. A bucket of water over a door, or a lubed banana-skin under a rug are examples. You may draw again and create a humorous version of that Obstacle.</p></details></span>
			<span class="t13ne-gain">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-gain">
						<strong>Gain </strong> 
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">Friends (a Group of 2-12 Vexes, or 2-6 Chorus Extras)</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-fray">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-fray">
						<strong>Fray: </strong> Caper
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">Capers are usually Low to Medium Stakes (although Higher Stakes Capers are possible) where multiple deceptions, confusions and complex physical activities occur that are all connected to trying to achieve some goal, often a heist or con-game. Humour is usually part of a Caper, such as swapping priceless jewels for plastic kids jewellery, or million-dollar bonds for monopoly money. In a real criminal enterprise such frivolities would create additional leads for the police, during a Caper though they will just confound, bemuse and amuse.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-snag">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-snag">
						<strong>Snag: </strong> Shenanigans
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">Someone, or occasionally something, is out to cause mischief. The Fray may be complicated by additional pranks, deceptions, antics and high-jinx. Then again, perhaps following the Fray there is some additional escapades of tom-foolery that the PCs must suffer through. This Snag can often be a sign that a capricious foe or Rival is involved in the Plot, such as those who carry Faery-blood.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-sweeping">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-sweeping">
						<strong>Sweeping: </strong> Comic Relief
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">Comedy is an easy way to release Tension. A joke during a Tense moment can ruin a Thriller though, so use carefully. Comedy Relief can help Characters come to terms with recent events though. Often a Character may be comic relief, a Comic relief Character receives </aside>
				</details>
			</span>
		 <details class="t13ne-revelation-fancy"><summary><strong>Revelation</strong></summary><details class="t13ne-about"><summary><strong>About: </strong> Setting / Background </summary><p class="t13ne-description">The information is about the background, or setting, not the current plot or situation. It might include pertinent details for the current plot, but is usually just about expositional context in this case. The Player of the card should specify how the detail pertains to the current situation.</p></details><details class="t13ne-info"><summary><strong>Info: </strong> Form </summary><p class="t13ne-description">What form might an embodiment take, or what shape is the country. Form can follow function, or it may hinder it. This can, of course, include information about the </p></details><details class="t13ne-vector"><summary><strong>Vector: </strong> Joke </summary><p class="t13ne-description">The information is disguised within a joke that someone tells. It might even be the punchline, but is more likely to be just a part of the joke.</p></details><details class="t13ne-alternate"><summary><strong>Alternate: </strong> Treachery </summary><p class="t13ne-description">Information about a traitor in your midst is always useful, if you can act on it. Information about a traitor in the enemy camp can be even more useful.</p></details><details class="t13ne-detail"><summary><strong>Detail: </strong> Biological </summary><p class="t13ne-description">Biological details tell us about the biology of a Character or people. They may include genetic markers, or just knowledge about their species or bloodline. The Carracam for example, clone themselves on death, birthing at least 3 larval children that eat their way out of the corpse.</p></details></details>
		 <details class="t13ne-ordeal-fancy"><summary><strong>Ordeal</strong></summary><p class="t13ne-ordeal-type"><strong>Ordeal-Type </strong> — Opposed Ordeal</p><p class="t13ne-stages"><strong>Stages </strong> — 3</p><p class="t13ne-ordeal-spread"><strong>Ordeal-Spread </strong> — Forking</p><p class="t13ne-stakes"><strong>Stakes </strong> — Medium</p><p class="t13ne-test"><strong>Test </strong> — Joke-telling (Jeer)</p><p class="t13ne-obstacles"><strong>Obstacles </strong> — None</p><p class="t13ne-suggested-action"><strong>Suggested-Action </strong> — Quip</p><details class="t13ne-stage"><summary><strong>Stage: </strong> Bridge </summary><p class="t13ne-description">A small gathering of influential, or professional people, or a route over something.</p></details><details class="t13ne-motional"><summary><strong>Motional: </strong> Boat Race </summary><p class="t13ne-description">First to get their boat through the final Stage, wins.</p></details><details class="t13ne-fight"><summary><strong>Fight: </strong> Minor Monster </summary><p class="t13ne-description">A solitary Monster, at least comparable to a PC, that has at least two ♥ Monster Facets.<!-- t13ne-shortcode "%7B%22type%22%3A%22facetsuitaspect%22%2C%22suit%22%3A%222%22%2C%22aspect%22%3A%22Monster%22%7D"// --></p><div class="t13ne-tablewrap"><table class="t13ne-table Monster"><thead><tr><th>&nbsp;&nbsp;Facet&nbsp;&nbsp;</th><th>&nbsp;&nbsp;Monster&nbsp;&nbsp;</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td>Enigma</td><td><strong>Skulker</strong> —The Monster is a shadowy figure pulling strings behind the scenes. This may just be an ordinary villain, but their ability to pass unnoticed should be bordering upon the unnatural. Perhaps they use psychic powers or magic to cloud the minds of witnesses, perhaps they are a hacking whiz, able to bounce phone calls through international relays with some multinational or another picking up the tab without realizing, perhaps they are one of the Huldra, the hidden people - who may be the Fæ or maybe they are actually the imaginary friend of one of the characters now made real(ish) by an alien or a Dæmon. Skulkers start with a Monster Pool of Enigma Score. They may add Monster Pool Size Reduced Success Levels to any stealthy or hidden actions or preparations. Skulkers may play Monster Pool cards as Preparation for anothers actions. E.g. The Hacker can prepare a Hack attack, that is performed by a Church Website in another town. A mind-bending villain could prepare a Psyche Defence in the mind of his puppet.</td></tr><tr><td>Heresy</td><td><strong>Masker</strong> —The Monster is looks entirely like something else. Perhaps they are a Demon in the form of an innocent child, or maybe they are a shape-shifter or face-dancer, able to change their appearance as they like, then again perhaps they're just the old Janitor in a monster costume. The Masker may create a Heresy Score sized Monster Pool whenever they hide completely for Heresy Boon as Chi Extend Duration, or are believed in their lies. The Monster Pool adds its Size Reduced to any Score to do with hiding, maintaining the mask, or lying. The Masker must play at least one card from their Monster Pool any time they wish to change their Mask or appearance.</td></tr><tr><td>Jeer</td><td><strong>Mockery</strong> —The monster is a mockery — It might be illusionary, or Undead (usually a corpse animated by a low-level Increated), but it may be a ghost, a machine or even an animal that mocks something else. The Mockery is created with a Monster Pool of Jeer Score cards, that must be played to maintain the Mockery (when disbelieved, attacked, etc). While the Monster Pool exists the Monster can pull and play Monster Pool Size Reduced cards (initially Jeer Draw)for any action the Mockery form takes that it has no suitable Annexes for. E.g. A Skeleton Mockery might have a "Mortality Reminder" Annex, but is given a Monster Pool of 5, so it can initially pull and play 2 cards for its mocking actions (like moving around and clawing the living). However, once attacked (or disbelieved) the Skeleton must spend at least one card from the Mockery Pool, thereafter it can only draw and Play 1 card for any Action that doesn't involve reminding someone of their own Mortality.</td></tr><tr><td>Liberty</td><td><strong>Nightmare</strong> —The Monster is nightmarish, or dreamlike. Such Monsters either exist in a purely psychological realm, appearing as dreams and hallucinations, that cannot really be fought and don’t physically exist, or they are extremely dangerous and powerful Monsters that really exist and may be magical or supernatural in power. Nightmares may add Liberty Draw cards to their Monster Pool if they cross a threshold, liminal space or are dreamed about. While they have cards in their Monster Pool they may add Liberty Boon Dice to everything they do. The Nightmare must spend at least one card from their Monster Pool when they do something impossible. They might use it to fly through the air, instantly teleport, let bullets pass through themselves, or appear to be a real thing and not a dream. Flower-faced Humanoids and Canines from a nightmare realm, handsome soap-sellers with an odd idea for gentlemen’s clubs, facially-burned, gardening-gloved janitors turned serial killers, six foot tall invisible rabbits, night hags, shadow beings seen when falling asleep, and any character in a dream, are all good examples of the Liberty Monster.</td></tr><tr><td>Nature</td><td><strong>Elemental</strong> —The Monster is an elemental force of nature, perhaps a literal elemental or more likely metaphorical. The Elemental will add Nature Draw cards to its Monster Pool when it encounters a natural source of its element, or when it causes collateral damage to a new location. Elementals gain +1 Score per Monster Pool card to any action to do with their natural element. The Elemental must spend cards from its Monster Pool on Physical, Energy or Magical attacks based on its Element. Salamanders, Sylphs, Gnomes, and Undines, Giants (of most types), Storms as well as Tsunamis, landslides, pyroclastic flows and even blizzards (most of which might be Giants anyway).</td></tr><tr><td>Phoenix</td><td><strong>Paragon</strong> —The Monster is the embodiment of a positive force or emotion. Such Monsters are often a counter point, or are opposed to, another Monster. Phoenix Monsters are usually capable of causing positive (and sometimes negative) emotions via a Psychology Test / Psyche Attack in those that see them, and while they may be positive in nature, they are not necessarily good, or altruistic in their actions. The Paragon may have a Monster Pool of cards equal to their Phoenix Score and add cards from the Pool to any action they wish. The Paragon can discard half of their Monster Pool and multiply their Score by 1+ their Phoenix Dice. The Paragon's Monster Pool is replenished in full if the Paragon can find an external example of their chosen emotion. The Phoenix itself is the embodiment of Resurrection, Hope and Faith, but is quite capable of burning destruction. The Golden Goose is another example, the embodiment of charity, but it evokes profound avarice in those who would own it. The unicorn is a symbol of purity, but is dangerous in the extreme to those it considers impure. Depictions of Angels are also perfect examples of positive embodiments.</td></tr></tbody><tfoot><tr><td>&nbsp;&nbsp;Facet&nbsp;&nbsp;</td><td>&nbsp;&nbsp;Monster&nbsp;&nbsp;</td></tr></tfoot></table></div><p></p></details></details>
		 </details>
			</div>
				<div class="t13ne-card-lea"><details class="t13ne-lea"><summary><strong>Lea</strong></summary><p class="t13ne-colour"><strong>Path Colour —</strong> Blue Aquatic or Black Migratory</p><p class="t13ne-bulmas"><strong>Allowed Bulmäs —</strong> All</p><p class="t13ne-animal-category"><strong>Animal Category —</strong> Macrofauna</p><p class="t13ne-animal-example"><strong>Example Animal —</strong> Fly / Earthworm / Mole / Moth / Wasp /</p><p class="t13ne-notes"><strong>Notes —</strong> Small (often burrowing) creatures</p></details></div></div><div class="t13ne-cardwrap-jsvg" id="cardrewritebox18" data-mode="jsvg" data-wyrd="1" data-yarn="1"><img src="https://www.cjmoseley.dev.cc/wp-content/plugins/t13ne/images/cards-svg/6H.svg" class="t13ne-svgcard"><div class="t13ne-svgpips"><div class="t13ne-textpips">6<!-- write suit--><span class="t13ne-symbol"><span class="t13ne-redcard">♥</span></span><details class="t13ne-suit"><summary></summary><p class="t13ne-name"><strong>Suit: </strong>Hearts <span class="t13ne-redcard">♥</span></p><p><strong>Ordeal Trump: </strong><span class="t13ne-trump">Healing</span></p><p class="t13ne-desc"><strong>Description: </strong>Hearts represent Love, Family and the comforts of home. In the Tarot the suit is called Cups, and represents the feminine and elemental Water.</p><p class="t13ne-tarot"><strong>Tarot Meaning: </strong>Love, Relationships, Affections, Family, Sympathy, Peace.</p><p class="t13ne-otext"><strong>Ordeal Text: </strong>Ordeal Types: Nurturing, Farming, Relationships, Medical. Hearts act as Trumps for the following Actions: Blending in, Cooking, Escaping, Healing, Hiding, Hunting, Insulting, Joke-telling, Lying, Mimicry, Riddling, Spinning a tale, Surgery, and Survivalism. Any type of Wound can be healed with a Heart.</p><p class="t13ne-parry"><strong>Parry: </strong><strong>Locked Respite</strong> All unmatched Cards from the original attack are Discarded by the Defender, however with each card they Discard they may either use the card to Heal/Stabilise one of their own wounds (or damage to the Parrying Descendant), or use the card to slow their with additional RT (Pips) or GRT (Pips/5).</p><p class="t13ne-soak"><strong>Soak: </strong><strong>Disengage:</strong> The Soak Card acts as a Heal card, and is discarded along with the Attack card. This immediately Heals all Wounds in play that have less Pips, including the remaining Wounds in the Attack, by one Wound Level (discards Distracts). </p></details></div></div><div class="t13ne-svg-facet"><div>Liberty</div></div><div class="t13ne-wound"><div><strong class="t13ne-woundname">Flesh</strong><details><summary></summary><strong>Flesh</strong><p class="t13ne-base-pips">Base Pips to summon: 5</p><p class="t13ne-min-pips">Minimum Pips Required: 5</p><p class="t13ne-desc">The Target is wounded and must stop and aknowledge that fact.</p><details class="t13ne-roll"><summary>Rules</summary>Lose one <strong>die</strong> from each pool on the next roll you make (e.g.3d6 + 2d10+5 becomes 2d6 + 1d10+5) then Discard</details><details class="t13ne-ordeal"><summary>Ordeal Rules</summary>Discard an Ordeal card from the next Full-Action or aggravate another Wound 1 Level and then Discard. </details><details class="t13ne-psych"><summary>Psych Wounds</summary><details class="t13ne-negative"><summary>Negative Psych</summary><p class="t13ne-normal"><strong>Normal</strong>Cannot approach source of negative emotion until Stabilized / Discarded</p><p class="t13ne-extreme"><strong>Extreme</strong>Paralysed (All Proficiency Dice are lost) until Stabilised / Discarded.</p></details><details class="t13ne-positive"><summary>Positive Psych</summary><p class="t13ne-normal"><strong>Normal</strong>Unwilling to leave source of positive emotion until discarded</p><p class="t13ne-extreme"><strong>Extreme</strong>Stimulated/Excited. Discard immediately, along with all GRT and RT Chips and act immediately (still with the dice or card penalties).</p></details></details></details></div></div><div class="t13ne-tarot-svg"><details><summary class="t13ne-wyrdtarot"><strong> Wyrd Tarot: </strong> History</summary><p class="t13ne-normal"><strong> Normal: </strong>Target describes a Scene from their past. Allows purchase of Proficiencies, Descendants, etc.</p><p class="t13ne-crossed"><strong> Crossed: </strong>Player may describe a Scene from Target's past.</p></details></div><div class="t13ne-yarn-fancy">
			<details><summary><strong>Yarn</strong> <br>The Unicorn</summary>
			<span class="t13ne-description">The Unicorn card represents either a powerful, magical engine of transformation and freedom, that is difficult to find, obtain or master, or it is something entirely imaginary. It is a dream that is chased, sometimes the dream is achieved, often it is not, but may have led you to a better or worse place than you started. Occasionally the Unicorn is nothing but a Nightmare.</span>
			<span class="t13ne-significator">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-significator"><strong>Significator</strong></summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-plot"><strong>Plot: </strong> The Unicorn Plot is usually described as a "Snark hunt", which is a hunt for something that may not exist (Unicorn and Boojum Hunt are also used). This might be a wild goose chase, or an investigation into a conspiracy that does not exist. Occasionally a Unicorn plot will actually involve a powerful magical creature (although rarely in a fantasy context) or artefact. Unicorn Plots often involve a Monster, although this is usually a human Monster who Hunts the Unicorn.</aside>
					<aside class="t13ne-char"><strong>Character: </strong> The Unicorn Significator indicates a Character given to flights of fantasy (who may also be a powerful supernatural creature in their own right). They Gain Yin when they daydream, talk to themselves, or pretend to be things they do not appear to be. They Gain Yang when they are speak with authority or demonstrate knowledge or wisdom. They Gain Chi when they use magic, or misdirection and deception to get what they want.</aside>
					<aside class="t13ne-scene"><strong>Scene: </strong> The Unicorn Plot is usually described as a "Snark hunt", which is a hunt for something that may not exist (Unicorn and Boojum Hunt are also used). This might be a wild goose chase, or an investigation into a conspiracy that does not exist. Occasionally a Unicorn plot will actually involve a powerful magical creature (although rarely in a fantasy context) or artefact. Unicorn Plots often involve a Monster, although this is usually a human Monster who Hunts the Unicorn.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-scene">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-scene-type">
						<strong>Scene: </strong> Warp
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">Warps work the Conflict. They are broken into 3 parts (and require another 2 cards): <ul><li>The Ends (What the Players want, usually described as Quests ). Does not require a Yarn Card.</li><li>The Fray (what keeps the Characters from their Ends, how do they work through that Quest?). The Fray requires an extra Yarn Card.</li><li>The Snag (The complication that renders the actions of the Characters winning through the Fray moot somehow). The Snag also requires another Yarn Card.</li></ul>During Warps things tend to get worse for the Pressed side of the Conflict.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-conflict-embodiment">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-conflict-embodiment">
						<strong>Conflict Embodiment: </strong> Internal
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">The Conflict embodies both sides within one Character. The Conflict will play out within them, between two aspects of the Character such as Incarna, Personality, Core, Hitches, Annexes, Proficiencies, or an internal Quest.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>

			<span class="t13ne-facet-embodiment">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-facet-embodiment">
						<strong>Facet Embodiment: </strong> Resolved Hitch
					</summary>
					<section class="t13ne-description">The Conflict Embodies one Facet as a Character's Resolved Hitch. As with all Character Embodiments, these can be obvious under the right circumstances.</section>
				</details>
			</span>
			 <div class="t13ne-archetypes"><details><summary><strong>Archetype Character: </strong>Artiste</summary> <details class="t13ne-Persona"><summary><strong>Persona: </strong><span class="t13ne-output">Rake</span>
      </summary>
      <details class="t13ne-motivation"><summary><strong>Motivation: </strong></summary><span class="t13ne-description">A Rake is motivated by hedonism, if it feels good, the rake will want to do it. Sometimes Rakes can develop sophisticated tastes, and they may prefer variety to volume, but not always. To the Rake all of life is a game, and if you aren't having fun, then you aren't doing it right.</span></details>
       <details class="t13ne-avoid"><summary><strong>Avoid: </strong></summary><span class="t13ne-description">Rakes will do everything they can to avoid confronting their problems, but can be remarkably insightful and helpful with other people's, especially if it will get them whatever they currently crave, or stops them asking the Rake if they are okay / <em>really</em> happy, etc.</span></details>
       <details class="t13ne-gain-chi"><summary><strong>Gain Chi: </strong></summary><span class="t13ne-description"><span>Gain Chi when you try to have whatever fun, or interesting experience, that you can, be it a drink, a meal, a seduction, a game, a drug, whatever.</span><p>Ordeal gain Chi when you prepare a ♥</p></span></details></details>   <details class="t13ne-core"><summary><strong>Core: </strong><span class="t13ne-output">Crafter</span></summary><span class="t13ne-description">Add Craft Score (or die) to all Skill Annex rolls and all ♦ Ordeal cards.</span></details><details class="t13ne-hitch"><summary><strong>Hitch: </strong><span class="t13ne-output">Impoverished</span></summary><span class="t13ne-description">Poor, disenfranchised or missing something in some way</span></details><p class="t13ne-description">The starving artist is an ancient archetype, this isn't <strong><em>strictly</em></strong> that archetype. These may be starving, and they may even create art, but they get much more pleasure from the trappings of the art world than actually doing anything as uncouth as selling art. For them to be known as an artist is enough.</p></details></div>
			 <span class="t13ne-hook">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-hook">
						<strong>Hook: </strong> Unicorn's Promise
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">The Unicorn's Promise is a complex Hook, that can express in many ways. It might be a dream revelation, or a situation that seems dire until someone suggests what sounds like a simple solution, but is actually a more difficult, or dangerous situation than the original, it can be a situation that seems impossible, but that there is some magical solution that is perhaps achievable.</aside>
					<details class="t13ne-hook-aspect">
						<summary><strong>Hook Aspect: </strong>Persona</summary>
						<section class="t13ne-description">The Hook targets the Character's Persona. For example: In order to Hook an Idol create something they can Star in, or an audience to adore them.</section>
					</details>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-obstacle-fancy"><details class="t13ne-obstacle"><summary><strong>Obstacle:</strong>Unicorn</summary><p class="t13ne-description">The way is blocked either by a magical illusions, figment of the imagination, an aspect of a dream/nightmare, or a powerful faery-creature or minor Increated.</p></details></span>
			<span class="t13ne-gain">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-gain">
						<strong>Gain </strong> 
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">Dream (sometimes a new dream or goal is the best Gain, other times getting what you dreamed of is better).</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-fray">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-fray">
						<strong>Fray: </strong> Tame the Untameable
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">This is a Fray that seems impossible. In fact the impossible task is possible, but you have to look at the problem in a whole new way. Just as the fierce Unicorn is entirely untameable, and a vicious killer, and yet if a virgin sits nearby and waits, the Unicorn will approach her, and fall asleep on her lap, allowing itself to be tamed.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-snag">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-snag">
						<strong>Snag: </strong> Played
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">The Fray, whatever it was, was just a distraction while the Opposed Embodiment acted to achieve their Ends. Or the PCs discover that they have been misled and have worked towards the Opposed Embodiment's goal, which makes the Fray a ploy used by the Opposed Embodiment. It is usual at this point for the Opposed Embodiment to reveal that they were actually the feeble old man all along, or whoever.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-sweeping">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-sweeping">
						<strong>Sweeping: </strong> Dream / Nightmare
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">The PCs manage to sleep, but have strong powerful dreams. </aside>
				</details>
			</span>
		 <details class="t13ne-revelation-fancy"><summary><strong>Revelation</strong></summary><details class="t13ne-about"><summary><strong>About: </strong> Setting / Background </summary><p class="t13ne-description">The information is about the background, or setting, not the current plot or situation. It might include pertinent details for the current plot, but is usually just about expositional context in this case. The Player of the card should specify how the detail pertains to the current situation.</p></details><details class="t13ne-info"><summary><strong>Info: </strong> To What Degree? </summary><p class="t13ne-description">How bad will or can it get? Is everyone a traitor or just a select few? How many will die of the plague? How many swords march against you?</p></details><details class="t13ne-vector"><summary><strong>Vector: </strong> Vision </summary><p class="t13ne-description">We pick up a lot of information by looking around. Pictures after all are worth a thousand words. Then again, if you want the Vision can be a hallucination or mirage, that somehow still conveys plausible truth, perhaps the Visions have been proven right before...</p></details><details class="t13ne-alternate"><summary><strong>Alternate: </strong> News </summary><p class="t13ne-description">Information about what is happening right now. The latest intelligence from the front, or just a local news update can pass a lot of information, some of which may even be fact checked.</p></details><details class="t13ne-detail"><summary><strong>Detail: </strong> Up to date </summary><p class="t13ne-description">Up to date details are the very latest facts, theories and rumours about the Embodiment or Story. It won't all be correct, in fact up to 60% will be completely wrong, and at least 90% will be factually inaccurate to some degree.</p></details></details>
		 <details class="t13ne-ordeal-fancy"><summary><strong>Ordeal</strong></summary><p class="t13ne-ordeal-type"><strong>Ordeal-Type </strong> — Opposed Ordeal</p><p class="t13ne-stages"><strong>Stages </strong> — 3</p><p class="t13ne-ordeal-spread"><strong>Ordeal-Spread </strong> — Pyramid</p><p class="t13ne-stakes"><strong>Stakes </strong> — Low</p><p class="t13ne-test"><strong>Test </strong> — Mimicry (Jeer)</p><p class="t13ne-obstacles"><strong>Obstacles </strong> — None</p><p class="t13ne-suggested-action"><strong>Suggested-Action </strong> — Perform</p><details class="t13ne-stage"><summary><strong>Stage: </strong> Tunnel </summary><p class="t13ne-description">A straight, often narrow route, under something, or extremely hard work.</p></details><details class="t13ne-motional"><summary><strong>Motional: </strong> Aerial Riding Race </summary><p class="t13ne-description">Whichever dragon and rider crosses the line first, wins.</p></details><details class="t13ne-fight"><summary><strong>Fight: </strong> Monsters </summary><p class="t13ne-description">A Group of Monsters (usually the same type) comparable to a PC that has at least 2 ♥ Monster Facets.<!-- t13ne-shortcode "%7B%22type%22%3A%22facetsuitaspect%22%2C%22suit%22%3A%222%22%2C%22aspect%22%3A%22Monster%22%7D"// --></p><div class="t13ne-tablewrap"><table class="t13ne-table Monster"><thead><tr><th>&nbsp;&nbsp;Facet&nbsp;&nbsp;</th><th>&nbsp;&nbsp;Monster&nbsp;&nbsp;</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td>Enigma</td><td><strong>Skulker</strong> —The Monster is a shadowy figure pulling strings behind the scenes. This may just be an ordinary villain, but their ability to pass unnoticed should be bordering upon the unnatural. Perhaps they use psychic powers or magic to cloud the minds of witnesses, perhaps they are a hacking whiz, able to bounce phone calls through international relays with some multinational or another picking up the tab without realizing, perhaps they are one of the Huldra, the hidden people - who may be the Fæ or maybe they are actually the imaginary friend of one of the characters now made real(ish) by an alien or a Dæmon. Skulkers start with a Monster Pool of Enigma Score. They may add Monster Pool Size Reduced Success Levels to any stealthy or hidden actions or preparations. Skulkers may play Monster Pool cards as Preparation for anothers actions. E.g. The Hacker can prepare a Hack attack, that is performed by a Church Website in another town. A mind-bending villain could prepare a Psyche Defence in the mind of his puppet.</td></tr><tr><td>Heresy</td><td><strong>Masker</strong> —The Monster is looks entirely like something else. Perhaps they are a Demon in the form of an innocent child, or maybe they are a shape-shifter or face-dancer, able to change their appearance as they like, then again perhaps they're just the old Janitor in a monster costume. The Masker may create a Heresy Score sized Monster Pool whenever they hide completely for Heresy Boon as Chi Extend Duration, or are believed in their lies. The Monster Pool adds its Size Reduced to any Score to do with hiding, maintaining the mask, or lying. The Masker must play at least one card from their Monster Pool any time they wish to change their Mask or appearance.</td></tr><tr><td>Jeer</td><td><strong>Mockery</strong> —The monster is a mockery — It might be illusionary, or Undead (usually a corpse animated by a low-level Increated), but it may be a ghost, a machine or even an animal that mocks something else. The Mockery is created with a Monster Pool of Jeer Score cards, that must be played to maintain the Mockery (when disbelieved, attacked, etc). While the Monster Pool exists the Monster can pull and play Monster Pool Size Reduced cards (initially Jeer Draw)for any action the Mockery form takes that it has no suitable Annexes for. E.g. A Skeleton Mockery might have a "Mortality Reminder" Annex, but is given a Monster Pool of 5, so it can initially pull and play 2 cards for its mocking actions (like moving around and clawing the living). However, once attacked (or disbelieved) the Skeleton must spend at least one card from the Mockery Pool, thereafter it can only draw and Play 1 card for any Action that doesn't involve reminding someone of their own Mortality.</td></tr><tr><td>Liberty</td><td><strong>Nightmare</strong> —The Monster is nightmarish, or dreamlike. Such Monsters either exist in a purely psychological realm, appearing as dreams and hallucinations, that cannot really be fought and don’t physically exist, or they are extremely dangerous and powerful Monsters that really exist and may be magical or supernatural in power. Nightmares may add Liberty Draw cards to their Monster Pool if they cross a threshold, liminal space or are dreamed about. While they have cards in their Monster Pool they may add Liberty Boon Dice to everything they do. The Nightmare must spend at least one card from their Monster Pool when they do something impossible. They might use it to fly through the air, instantly teleport, let bullets pass through themselves, or appear to be a real thing and not a dream. Flower-faced Humanoids and Canines from a nightmare realm, handsome soap-sellers with an odd idea for gentlemen’s clubs, facially-burned, gardening-gloved janitors turned serial killers, six foot tall invisible rabbits, night hags, shadow beings seen when falling asleep, and any character in a dream, are all good examples of the Liberty Monster.</td></tr><tr><td>Nature</td><td><strong>Elemental</strong> —The Monster is an elemental force of nature, perhaps a literal elemental or more likely metaphorical. The Elemental will add Nature Draw cards to its Monster Pool when it encounters a natural source of its element, or when it causes collateral damage to a new location. Elementals gain +1 Score per Monster Pool card to any action to do with their natural element. The Elemental must spend cards from its Monster Pool on Physical, Energy or Magical attacks based on its Element. Salamanders, Sylphs, Gnomes, and Undines, Giants (of most types), Storms as well as Tsunamis, landslides, pyroclastic flows and even blizzards (most of which might be Giants anyway).</td></tr><tr><td>Phoenix</td><td><strong>Paragon</strong> —The Monster is the embodiment of a positive force or emotion. Such Monsters are often a counter point, or are opposed to, another Monster. Phoenix Monsters are usually capable of causing positive (and sometimes negative) emotions via a Psychology Test / Psyche Attack in those that see them, and while they may be positive in nature, they are not necessarily good, or altruistic in their actions. The Paragon may have a Monster Pool of cards equal to their Phoenix Score and add cards from the Pool to any action they wish. The Paragon can discard half of their Monster Pool and multiply their Score by 1+ their Phoenix Dice. The Paragon's Monster Pool is replenished in full if the Paragon can find an external example of their chosen emotion. The Phoenix itself is the embodiment of Resurrection, Hope and Faith, but is quite capable of burning destruction. The Golden Goose is another example, the embodiment of charity, but it evokes profound avarice in those who would own it. The unicorn is a symbol of purity, but is dangerous in the extreme to those it considers impure. Depictions of Angels are also perfect examples of positive embodiments.</td></tr></tbody><tfoot><tr><td>&nbsp;&nbsp;Facet&nbsp;&nbsp;</td><td>&nbsp;&nbsp;Monster&nbsp;&nbsp;</td></tr></tfoot></table></div><p></p></details></details>
		 </details>
			</div>
				<div class="t13ne-card-lea"><details class="t13ne-lea"><summary><strong>Lea</strong></summary><p class="t13ne-colour"><strong>Path Colour —</strong> Blue Aquatic or Black Migratory</p><p class="t13ne-bulmas"><strong>Allowed Bulmäs —</strong> All</p><p class="t13ne-animal-category"><strong>Animal Category —</strong> Megafauna – Tiny</p><p class="t13ne-animal-example"><strong>Example Animal —</strong> Rat / Starfish / Corvids / Migratory birds</p><p class="t13ne-notes"><strong>Notes —</strong> Animals smaller than 40 Kilograms</p></details></div></div><div class="t13ne-cardwrap-jsvg" id="cardrewritebox19" data-mode="jsvg" data-wyrd="1" data-yarn="1"><img src="https://www.cjmoseley.dev.cc/wp-content/plugins/t13ne/images/cards-svg/7H.svg" class="t13ne-svgcard"><div class="t13ne-svgpips"><div class="t13ne-textpips">7<!-- write suit--><span class="t13ne-symbol"><span class="t13ne-redcard">♥</span></span><details class="t13ne-suit"><summary></summary><p class="t13ne-name"><strong>Suit: </strong>Hearts <span class="t13ne-redcard">♥</span></p><p><strong>Ordeal Trump: </strong><span class="t13ne-trump">Healing</span></p><p class="t13ne-desc"><strong>Description: </strong>Hearts represent Love, Family and the comforts of home. In the Tarot the suit is called Cups, and represents the feminine and elemental Water.</p><p class="t13ne-tarot"><strong>Tarot Meaning: </strong>Love, Relationships, Affections, Family, Sympathy, Peace.</p><p class="t13ne-otext"><strong>Ordeal Text: </strong>Ordeal Types: Nurturing, Farming, Relationships, Medical. Hearts act as Trumps for the following Actions: Blending in, Cooking, Escaping, Healing, Hiding, Hunting, Insulting, Joke-telling, Lying, Mimicry, Riddling, Spinning a tale, Surgery, and Survivalism. Any type of Wound can be healed with a Heart.</p><p class="t13ne-parry"><strong>Parry: </strong><strong>Locked Respite</strong> All unmatched Cards from the original attack are Discarded by the Defender, however with each card they Discard they may either use the card to Heal/Stabilise one of their own wounds (or damage to the Parrying Descendant), or use the card to slow their with additional RT (Pips) or GRT (Pips/5).</p><p class="t13ne-soak"><strong>Soak: </strong><strong>Disengage:</strong> The Soak Card acts as a Heal card, and is discarded along with the Attack card. This immediately Heals all Wounds in play that have less Pips, including the remaining Wounds in the Attack, by one Wound Level (discards Distracts). </p></details></div></div><div class="t13ne-svg-facet"><div>Nature</div></div><div class="t13ne-wound"><div><strong class="t13ne-woundname">Flesh</strong><details><summary></summary><strong>Flesh</strong><p class="t13ne-base-pips">Base Pips to summon: 5</p><p class="t13ne-min-pips">Minimum Pips Required: 5</p><p class="t13ne-desc">The Target is wounded and must stop and aknowledge that fact.</p><details class="t13ne-roll"><summary>Rules</summary>Lose one <strong>die</strong> from each pool on the next roll you make (e.g.3d6 + 2d10+5 becomes 2d6 + 1d10+5) then Discard</details><details class="t13ne-ordeal"><summary>Ordeal Rules</summary>Discard an Ordeal card from the next Full-Action or aggravate another Wound 1 Level and then Discard. </details><details class="t13ne-psych"><summary>Psych Wounds</summary><details class="t13ne-negative"><summary>Negative Psych</summary><p class="t13ne-normal"><strong>Normal</strong>Cannot approach source of negative emotion until Stabilized / Discarded</p><p class="t13ne-extreme"><strong>Extreme</strong>Paralysed (All Proficiency Dice are lost) until Stabilised / Discarded.</p></details><details class="t13ne-positive"><summary>Positive Psych</summary><p class="t13ne-normal"><strong>Normal</strong>Unwilling to leave source of positive emotion until discarded</p><p class="t13ne-extreme"><strong>Extreme</strong>Stimulated/Excited. Discard immediately, along with all GRT and RT Chips and act immediately (still with the dice or card penalties).</p></details></details></details></div></div><div class="t13ne-tarot-svg"><details><summary class="t13ne-wyrdtarot"><strong> Wyrd Tarot: </strong> Prop</summary><p class="t13ne-normal"><strong> Normal: </strong>Instantly Fix or replace a Prop (Physical Descendant).</p><p class="t13ne-crossed"><strong> Crossed: </strong>Target drops a random Prop (Physical Descendant).</p></details></div><div class="t13ne-yarn-fancy">
			<details><summary><strong>Yarn</strong> <br>The Stag</summary>
			<span class="t13ne-description">The Stag card represents nature in the form of animal life. Animals are driven by base desires, the simplest principles, and so the card shows us this, indicating that the events are concerned with an individual's strength and survival, and perhaps more importantly the long term survival of the species. The Stag is usually timid and will avoid threats, but may be quick to fight during the rutting season, and vicious with those antlers. It is this rutting behaviour that sets the Stag apart.</span>
			<span class="t13ne-significator">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-significator"><strong>Significator</strong></summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-plot"><strong>Plot: </strong> The Stag Plot is a natural story, concerned with the normal conflicts of nature, the difficulties of survival, hunting, mating and child-rearing. These can be stories of nature, or children raised by wolves, bears and lions, or they can be coming of age tales, that mark important and natural events in any life.</aside>
					<aside class="t13ne-char"><strong>Character: </strong> The Stag Significator indicates a Character who is physical, athletic and sexual. They Gain Yin when they practice, train, or exercise. They Gain Yang when they compete with another, or show off. They Gain Chi when their accomplishments are recognised. They gain +1 Success Level on any purely physically athletic action (such as rutting, running, jumping, swimming, punching, kicking, head-butting).</aside>
					<aside class="t13ne-scene"><strong>Scene: </strong> The Stag Plot is a natural story, concerned with the normal conflicts of nature, the difficulties of survival, hunting, mating and child-rearing. These can be stories of nature, or children raised by wolves, bears and lions, or they can be coming of age tales, that mark important and natural events in any life.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-scene">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-scene-type">
						<strong>Scene: </strong> Weft
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">Wefts work the Conflict through 3 Parts: <ul><li>First by the Characters Recoiling from the Snag (They emotionally respond to the previous event) which does not require a Yarn card</li><li>Secondly Sweeping (where the Characters recover from what happened) which does require another Yarn Card</li><li>Finally, Picking (Where the Characters will have a choice how the Story will proceed) which does not require Yarn Cards, but can be represented by more cards during card play.</li></ul>During Wefts the Pressed side makes a little headway, or gets a little breather.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-conflict-embodiment">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-conflict-embodiment">
						<strong>Conflict Embodiment: </strong> Internal
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">The Conflict embodies both sides within one Character. The Conflict will play out within them, between two aspects of the Character such as Incarna, Personality, Core, Hitches, Annexes, Proficiencies, or an internal Quest.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>

			<span class="t13ne-facet-embodiment">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-facet-embodiment">
						<strong>Facet Embodiment: </strong> Personality
					</summary>
					<section class="t13ne-description">The Conflict Embodies one Facet as a Character Personality. Often the most satisfying embodiment, but can lead to easy identification, and unsubtle Plots.</section>
				</details>
			</span>
			 <div class="t13ne-archetypes"><details><summary><strong>Archetype Character: </strong>Flirt</summary> <details class="t13ne-Persona"><summary><strong>Persona: </strong><span class="t13ne-output">Rake</span>
      </summary>
      <details class="t13ne-motivation"><summary><strong>Motivation: </strong></summary><span class="t13ne-description">A Rake is motivated by hedonism, if it feels good, the rake will want to do it. Sometimes Rakes can develop sophisticated tastes, and they may prefer variety to volume, but not always. To the Rake all of life is a game, and if you aren't having fun, then you aren't doing it right.</span></details>
       <details class="t13ne-avoid"><summary><strong>Avoid: </strong></summary><span class="t13ne-description">Rakes will do everything they can to avoid confronting their problems, but can be remarkably insightful and helpful with other people's, especially if it will get them whatever they currently crave, or stops them asking the Rake if they are okay / <em>really</em> happy, etc.</span></details>
       <details class="t13ne-gain-chi"><summary><strong>Gain Chi: </strong></summary><span class="t13ne-description"><span>Gain Chi when you try to have whatever fun, or interesting experience, that you can, be it a drink, a meal, a seduction, a game, a drug, whatever.</span><p>Ordeal gain Chi when you prepare a ♥</p></span></details></details>   <details class="t13ne-core"><summary><strong>Core: </strong><span class="t13ne-output">Doctor</span></summary><span class="t13ne-description">The character may choose one Incarna, when using or trying to affect that Incarna add one level of success.</span></details><details class="t13ne-hitch"><summary><strong>Hitch: </strong><span class="t13ne-output">Exposed</span></summary><span class="t13ne-description">Perhaps they are obvious, too distinctive, smelly, or brightly coloured, maybe they are wanted by the police, or are just incredible famous. Anyway, they attract more attention than normal because of this Hitch.</span></details><p class="t13ne-description">Flirts, are incredibly common liberated societies, they may be common in more conservative times and places, but must do their best to keep secret their nature. Which is hard, as being flirty exposes them quite well (they wouldn't want a persecuted Hitch too!).</p></details></div>
			 <span class="t13ne-hook">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-hook">
						<strong>Hook: </strong> Taste of Desire
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">The Story hooks the character by offering what they currently desire (although not perhaps what they really need). This temptation usually includes a taste of what is to come, a kiss from a potential lover, a hit of their addiction to sooth the tremors, a morsel of food for the starving man, but it is not enough to satiate them, yet.</aside>
					<details class="t13ne-hook-aspect">
						<summary><strong>Hook Aspect: </strong>Persona</summary>
						<section class="t13ne-description">The Hook targets the Character's Persona. For example: In order to Hook a Reaper you need to offer them something to collect or gain.</section>
					</details>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-obstacle-fancy"><details class="t13ne-obstacle"><summary><strong>Obstacle:</strong>Animals</summary><p class="t13ne-description">The way is blocked by a large group of animals (or drunken adults) who may pass naturally but may be aggressive if threatened.</p></details></span>
			<span class="t13ne-gain">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-gain">
						<strong>Gain </strong> 
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">Herd (3-60) Cast Extra Allies</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-fray">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-fray">
						<strong>Fray: </strong> Hunt
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">A Hunt is a High Stakes Complex Motional Chase Ordeal. The PCs may be trying to find food, or they may be the hunted, trying to escape, then again not all hunts are for food, the search for a mate or a place to live can also be hunts. Hunts can involve Snares, Traps and Obstacles, as well as deadly predators and dangerous prey animals.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-snag">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-snag">
						<strong>Snag: </strong> Seductions
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">After the Fray the PCs or their allies are offered temptations. These may be tempting offers to change sides, or actual seductions, by a love interest (or a love interests Rivals) in some cases. When a PC is seduced it is usual that the some thing reveals later that they have slept with the enemy, and may be betrayed </aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-sweeping">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-sweeping">
						<strong>Sweeping: </strong> Stag Challenge
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">The PCs get to rest, meanwhile their opponents the Dominant Embodiments are challenged by one of their Rivals.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
		 <details class="t13ne-revelation-fancy"><summary><strong>Revelation</strong></summary><details class="t13ne-about"><summary><strong>About: </strong> Setting / Background </summary><p class="t13ne-description">The information is about the background, or setting, not the current plot or situation. It might include pertinent details for the current plot, but is usually just about expositional context in this case. The Player of the card should specify how the detail pertains to the current situation.</p></details><details class="t13ne-info"><summary><strong>Info: </strong> What for? </summary><p class="t13ne-description">The information is about what something is for. not necessarily what it is or what is the expected result.</p></details><details class="t13ne-vector"><summary><strong>Vector: </strong> Dream </summary><p class="t13ne-description">There is a long history of dreams being an important part of a discovery, they can reveal information that it can be difficult to obtain in other ways. Information presented in dreams is usually symbolic in some way, rather than easily understood.</p></details><details class="t13ne-alternate"><summary><strong>Alternate: </strong> Fantasy </summary><p class="t13ne-description">The lies about something and the fantastic claims made about them, can lead you to finding the kernels of truth behind the exaggerations.</p></details><details class="t13ne-detail"><summary><strong>Detail: </strong> Nuance </summary><p class="t13ne-description">A nuance detail can shine a light on some previously overlooked element of an Embodiment. If a vicious warlord spared half a village after he saw their shrine, does that mean he was from that village? Or that he believes in the same Saint? If later he spares another town, bypassing it completely because it is named for the same Saint, then you can decide.</p></details></details>
		 <details class="t13ne-ordeal-fancy"><summary><strong>Ordeal</strong></summary><p class="t13ne-ordeal-type"><strong>Ordeal-Type </strong> — Opposed Ordeal</p><p class="t13ne-stages"><strong>Stages </strong> — 3</p><p class="t13ne-ordeal-spread"><strong>Ordeal-Spread </strong> — Block</p><p class="t13ne-stakes"><strong>Stakes </strong> — Medium</p><p class="t13ne-test"><strong>Test </strong> — Riddle (Enigma)</p><p class="t13ne-obstacles"><strong>Obstacles </strong> — None</p><p class="t13ne-suggested-action"><strong>Suggested-Action </strong> — Speak</p><details class="t13ne-stage"><summary><strong>Stage: </strong> Question </summary><p class="t13ne-description">Either you need to formulate a question or an answer, usually while lots of other people ask theirs. This might be the first stage of the Scientific method, or you may be at a press conference. Otherwise you are looking at a cul-de-sac style turn, that might have a way out, if you are willing to think outside the box (or at least off the road).</p></details><details class="t13ne-motional"><summary><strong>Motional: </strong> Aquatic Riding Race </summary><p class="t13ne-description">Get to the end of the Ordeal, on your jet-ski, battle-ray, or riding-shark  through all the Checkpoints, before everyone else.</p></details><details class="t13ne-fight"><summary><strong>Fight: </strong> Monstrous Horde </summary><p class="t13ne-description">A Group of Monsters (Cast Extra), with at least 1 ♥ Monster Facet and that outnumber the party at least 3 to 1.<!-- t13ne-shortcode "%7B%22type%22%3A%22facetsuitaspect%22%2C%22suit%22%3A%222%22%2C%22aspect%22%3A%22Monster%22%7D"// --></p><div class="t13ne-tablewrap"><table class="t13ne-table Monster"><thead><tr><th>&nbsp;&nbsp;Facet&nbsp;&nbsp;</th><th>&nbsp;&nbsp;Monster&nbsp;&nbsp;</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td>Enigma</td><td><strong>Skulker</strong> —The Monster is a shadowy figure pulling strings behind the scenes. This may just be an ordinary villain, but their ability to pass unnoticed should be bordering upon the unnatural. Perhaps they use psychic powers or magic to cloud the minds of witnesses, perhaps they are a hacking whiz, able to bounce phone calls through international relays with some multinational or another picking up the tab without realizing, perhaps they are one of the Huldra, the hidden people - who may be the Fæ or maybe they are actually the imaginary friend of one of the characters now made real(ish) by an alien or a Dæmon. Skulkers start with a Monster Pool of Enigma Score. They may add Monster Pool Size Reduced Success Levels to any stealthy or hidden actions or preparations. Skulkers may play Monster Pool cards as Preparation for anothers actions. E.g. The Hacker can prepare a Hack attack, that is performed by a Church Website in another town. A mind-bending villain could prepare a Psyche Defence in the mind of his puppet.</td></tr><tr><td>Heresy</td><td><strong>Masker</strong> —The Monster is looks entirely like something else. Perhaps they are a Demon in the form of an innocent child, or maybe they are a shape-shifter or face-dancer, able to change their appearance as they like, then again perhaps they're just the old Janitor in a monster costume. The Masker may create a Heresy Score sized Monster Pool whenever they hide completely for Heresy Boon as Chi Extend Duration, or are believed in their lies. The Monster Pool adds its Size Reduced to any Score to do with hiding, maintaining the mask, or lying. The Masker must play at least one card from their Monster Pool any time they wish to change their Mask or appearance.</td></tr><tr><td>Jeer</td><td><strong>Mockery</strong> —The monster is a mockery — It might be illusionary, or Undead (usually a corpse animated by a low-level Increated), but it may be a ghost, a machine or even an animal that mocks something else. The Mockery is created with a Monster Pool of Jeer Score cards, that must be played to maintain the Mockery (when disbelieved, attacked, etc). While the Monster Pool exists the Monster can pull and play Monster Pool Size Reduced cards (initially Jeer Draw)for any action the Mockery form takes that it has no suitable Annexes for. E.g. A Skeleton Mockery might have a "Mortality Reminder" Annex, but is given a Monster Pool of 5, so it can initially pull and play 2 cards for its mocking actions (like moving around and clawing the living). However, once attacked (or disbelieved) the Skeleton must spend at least one card from the Mockery Pool, thereafter it can only draw and Play 1 card for any Action that doesn't involve reminding someone of their own Mortality.</td></tr><tr><td>Liberty</td><td><strong>Nightmare</strong> —The Monster is nightmarish, or dreamlike. Such Monsters either exist in a purely psychological realm, appearing as dreams and hallucinations, that cannot really be fought and don’t physically exist, or they are extremely dangerous and powerful Monsters that really exist and may be magical or supernatural in power. Nightmares may add Liberty Draw cards to their Monster Pool if they cross a threshold, liminal space or are dreamed about. While they have cards in their Monster Pool they may add Liberty Boon Dice to everything they do. The Nightmare must spend at least one card from their Monster Pool when they do something impossible. They might use it to fly through the air, instantly teleport, let bullets pass through themselves, or appear to be a real thing and not a dream. Flower-faced Humanoids and Canines from a nightmare realm, handsome soap-sellers with an odd idea for gentlemen’s clubs, facially-burned, gardening-gloved janitors turned serial killers, six foot tall invisible rabbits, night hags, shadow beings seen when falling asleep, and any character in a dream, are all good examples of the Liberty Monster.</td></tr><tr><td>Nature</td><td><strong>Elemental</strong> —The Monster is an elemental force of nature, perhaps a literal elemental or more likely metaphorical. The Elemental will add Nature Draw cards to its Monster Pool when it encounters a natural source of its element, or when it causes collateral damage to a new location. Elementals gain +1 Score per Monster Pool card to any action to do with their natural element. The Elemental must spend cards from its Monster Pool on Physical, Energy or Magical attacks based on its Element. Salamanders, Sylphs, Gnomes, and Undines, Giants (of most types), Storms as well as Tsunamis, landslides, pyroclastic flows and even blizzards (most of which might be Giants anyway).</td></tr><tr><td>Phoenix</td><td><strong>Paragon</strong> —The Monster is the embodiment of a positive force or emotion. Such Monsters are often a counter point, or are opposed to, another Monster. Phoenix Monsters are usually capable of causing positive (and sometimes negative) emotions via a Psychology Test / Psyche Attack in those that see them, and while they may be positive in nature, they are not necessarily good, or altruistic in their actions. The Paragon may have a Monster Pool of cards equal to their Phoenix Score and add cards from the Pool to any action they wish. The Paragon can discard half of their Monster Pool and multiply their Score by 1+ their Phoenix Dice. The Paragon's Monster Pool is replenished in full if the Paragon can find an external example of their chosen emotion. The Phoenix itself is the embodiment of Resurrection, Hope and Faith, but is quite capable of burning destruction. The Golden Goose is another example, the embodiment of charity, but it evokes profound avarice in those who would own it. The unicorn is a symbol of purity, but is dangerous in the extreme to those it considers impure. Depictions of Angels are also perfect examples of positive embodiments.</td></tr></tbody><tfoot><tr><td>&nbsp;&nbsp;Facet&nbsp;&nbsp;</td><td>&nbsp;&nbsp;Monster&nbsp;&nbsp;</td></tr></tfoot></table></div><p></p></details></details>
		 </details>
			</div>
				<div class="t13ne-card-lea"><details class="t13ne-lea"><summary><strong>Lea</strong></summary><p class="t13ne-colour"><strong>Path Colour —</strong> Blue Aquatic or Black Migratory</p><p class="t13ne-bulmas"><strong>Allowed Bulmäs —</strong> All</p><p class="t13ne-animal-category"><strong>Animal Category —</strong> Megafauna – Small</p><p class="t13ne-animal-example"><strong>Example Animal —</strong> Rays / Skates / Vulture</p><p class="t13ne-notes"><strong>Notes —</strong> Larger migratory birds</p></details></div></div><div class="t13ne-cardwrap-jsvg" id="cardrewritebox20" data-mode="jsvg" data-wyrd="1" data-yarn="1"><img src="https://www.cjmoseley.dev.cc/wp-content/plugins/t13ne/images/cards-svg/8H.svg" class="t13ne-svgcard"><div class="t13ne-svgpips"><div class="t13ne-textpips">8<!-- write suit--><span class="t13ne-symbol"><span class="t13ne-redcard">♥</span></span><details class="t13ne-suit"><summary></summary><p class="t13ne-name"><strong>Suit: </strong>Hearts <span class="t13ne-redcard">♥</span></p><p><strong>Ordeal Trump: </strong><span class="t13ne-trump">Healing</span></p><p class="t13ne-desc"><strong>Description: </strong>Hearts represent Love, Family and the comforts of home. In the Tarot the suit is called Cups, and represents the feminine and elemental Water.</p><p class="t13ne-tarot"><strong>Tarot Meaning: </strong>Love, Relationships, Affections, Family, Sympathy, Peace.</p><p class="t13ne-otext"><strong>Ordeal Text: </strong>Ordeal Types: Nurturing, Farming, Relationships, Medical. Hearts act as Trumps for the following Actions: Blending in, Cooking, Escaping, Healing, Hiding, Hunting, Insulting, Joke-telling, Lying, Mimicry, Riddling, Spinning a tale, Surgery, and Survivalism. Any type of Wound can be healed with a Heart.</p><p class="t13ne-parry"><strong>Parry: </strong><strong>Locked Respite</strong> All unmatched Cards from the original attack are Discarded by the Defender, however with each card they Discard they may either use the card to Heal/Stabilise one of their own wounds (or damage to the Parrying Descendant), or use the card to slow their with additional RT (Pips) or GRT (Pips/5).</p><p class="t13ne-soak"><strong>Soak: </strong><strong>Disengage:</strong> The Soak Card acts as a Heal card, and is discarded along with the Attack card. This immediately Heals all Wounds in play that have less Pips, including the remaining Wounds in the Attack, by one Wound Level (discards Distracts). </p></details></div></div><div class="t13ne-svg-facet"><div>Nature</div></div><div class="t13ne-wound"><div><strong class="t13ne-woundname">Maim</strong><details><summary></summary><strong>Maim</strong><p class="t13ne-base-pips">Base Pips to summon: 8</p><p class="t13ne-min-pips">Minimum Pips Required: 8</p><p class="t13ne-desc">The Target is injured enough for it to affect their abilities.</p><details class="t13ne-roll"><summary>Rules</summary>Lose 1 Proficiency Slot from affected Annex (including Personality Proficiencies) and recalculate. If all Proficiencies are Maimed, the Annex is Crippled.</details><details class="t13ne-ordeal"><summary>Ordeal Rules</summary>Maimed Annexes lose Proficiency Slots too, but may lose access to Nimbeds affected or take a -1 Card Draw Penalty until Healed / Recalculated. If all Proficiencies are Maimed, the Annex is Crippled.</details><details class="t13ne-psych"><summary>Psych Wounds</summary><details class="t13ne-negative"><summary>Negative Psych</summary><p class="t13ne-normal"><strong>Normal</strong>Proficiency lost from Personality Profs. Must try to flee source of negative emotion until Stabilised or Discarded.</p><p class="t13ne-extreme"><strong>Extreme</strong>Proficiency lost from Personality Profs. All Proficiency Dice are reduced one dice size (1d6 becomes 1d4) until Stabilised.</p></details><details class="t13ne-positive"><summary>Positive Psych</summary><p class="t13ne-normal"><strong>Normal</strong>Gain 1 Proficiency (permanently) and must approach / follow source of positive emotion until Stabilised.</p><p class="t13ne-extreme"><strong>Extreme</strong>Gain 2 Proficiencies (permanently), plus must drop to their knees (or simlar) to express the emotion until Stabilised.</p></details></details></details></div></div><div class="t13ne-tarot-svg"><details><summary class="t13ne-wyrdtarot"><strong> Wyrd Tarot: </strong> Chi</summary><p class="t13ne-normal"><strong> Normal: </strong>Target pulls another card and gains Chi equal to 7 + the Pips.</p><p class="t13ne-crossed"><strong> Crossed: </strong>Target loses 15 Chi.</p></details></div><div class="t13ne-yarn-fancy">
			<details><summary><strong>Yarn</strong> <br>The Wizard</summary>
			<span class="t13ne-description">The Wizard card represents a person with complete mastery of their surroundings and in complete control of themselves. A talented, skilful, and powerful person who has the ability to create and achieve almost any goal they set themselves.</span>
			<span class="t13ne-significator">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-significator"><strong>Significator</strong></summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-plot"><strong>Plot: </strong> The Wizard plot is a world changing plot. Nothing that follows a Wizard Plot will ever be the same again. Huge events that change the nature of reality, or the fabric of society are the order of the day.</aside>
					<aside class="t13ne-char"><strong>Character: </strong> The Wizard Significator indicates a Character who is an ace in their field. They are a wizard at what they do. They Gain Yin when they share wisdom, or tips with another. They gain from learning as well as teaching tips. They Gain Yang when they work at what they are good at (research, or practical). They Gain Chi when they don't do exactly what they are asked for, complicate matters, or achieve something unique or important. They also gain a +1 Success Level on any Test, Ordeal or Action that they are a "Wizard" at.</aside>
					<aside class="t13ne-scene"><strong>Scene: </strong> The Wizard plot is a world changing plot. Nothing that follows a Wizard Plot will ever be the same again. Huge events that change the nature of reality, or the fabric of society are the order of the day.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-scene">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-scene-type">
						<strong>Scene: </strong> Warp
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">Warps work the Conflict. They are broken into 3 parts (and require another 2 cards): <ul><li>The Ends (What the Players want, usually described as Quests ). Does not require a Yarn Card.</li><li>The Fray (what keeps the Characters from their Ends, how do they work through that Quest?). The Fray requires an extra Yarn Card.</li><li>The Snag (The complication that renders the actions of the Characters winning through the Fray moot somehow). The Snag also requires another Yarn Card.</li></ul>During Warps things tend to get worse for the Pressed side of the Conflict.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-conflict-embodiment">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-conflict-embodiment">
						<strong>Conflict Embodiment: </strong> Internal
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">The Conflict embodies both sides within one Character. The Conflict will play out within them, between two aspects of the Character such as Incarna, Personality, Core, Hitches, Annexes, Proficiencies, or an internal Quest.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>

			<span class="t13ne-facet-embodiment">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-facet-embodiment">
						<strong>Facet Embodiment: </strong> Core
					</summary>
					<section class="t13ne-description">The Conflict Embodies one Facet as a Character Core. This can create very satisfying embodiments that can be suitable for most Genres.</section>
				</details>
			</span>
			 <div class="t13ne-archetypes"><details><summary><strong>Archetype Character: </strong>Expert</summary> <details class="t13ne-Persona"><summary><strong>Persona: </strong><span class="t13ne-output">Rake</span>
      </summary>
      <details class="t13ne-motivation"><summary><strong>Motivation: </strong></summary><span class="t13ne-description">A Rake is motivated by hedonism, if it feels good, the rake will want to do it. Sometimes Rakes can develop sophisticated tastes, and they may prefer variety to volume, but not always. To the Rake all of life is a game, and if you aren't having fun, then you aren't doing it right.</span></details>
       <details class="t13ne-avoid"><summary><strong>Avoid: </strong></summary><span class="t13ne-description">Rakes will do everything they can to avoid confronting their problems, but can be remarkably insightful and helpful with other people's, especially if it will get them whatever they currently crave, or stops them asking the Rake if they are okay / <em>really</em> happy, etc.</span></details>
       <details class="t13ne-gain-chi"><summary><strong>Gain Chi: </strong></summary><span class="t13ne-description"><span>Gain Chi when you try to have whatever fun, or interesting experience, that you can, be it a drink, a meal, a seduction, a game, a drug, whatever.</span><p>Ordeal gain Chi when you prepare a ♥</p></span></details></details>   <details class="t13ne-core"><summary><strong>Core: </strong><span class="t13ne-output">Prevailer</span></summary><span class="t13ne-description">Ignore a Hitch when triggered by paying Sway equal to the half the normal reward.</span></details><details class="t13ne-hitch"><summary><strong>Hitch: </strong><span class="t13ne-output">Fatigued</span></summary><span class="t13ne-description">This hitch covers everything from laziness to sickness, some people and things just seem tired or useless most of the time.</span></details><p class="t13ne-description">The Expert is a master of their subject environment, and as such stand with one foot in the normal world and another in their field. They can be Witch-Doctors literally carrying profound messages between the spirit world and the physical world, or they can be IT specialists, interfacing electronic fora and digital FAQs to the recurring malware vector in marketing via direct meat-space social linguistics.</p></details></div>
			 <span class="t13ne-hook">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-hook">
						<strong>Hook: </strong> Summoning
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">The story opens a summoning, this may mean a message, an invitation, police at the door, or magical circles across dimensions. Sometimes the PCs are the summoners, but usually they are the Summoned. Someone powerful wants to see them, or has something to give them, or take from them.</aside>
					<details class="t13ne-hook-aspect">
						<summary><strong>Hook Aspect: </strong>Persona</summary>
						<section class="t13ne-description">The Hook targets the Character's Persona. For example: In order to Hook a Shaman you need to threaten one of their worlds, often from the other.</section>
					</details>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-obstacle-fancy"><details class="t13ne-obstacle"><summary><strong>Obstacle:</strong>Ward</summary><p class="t13ne-description">The Obstacle is a magical ward, or curse, that either magically or psychologically blocks the way. Magical Wards can be very specific what they block and what they allow through, which can allow Yarn-Tellers to split parties and teach lessons.</p></details></span>
			<span class="t13ne-gain">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-gain">
						<strong>Gain </strong> 
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">Magic Spell / Tool / Treasure (Skill Descendant / Talent / Power Descendant)</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-fray">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-fray">
						<strong>Fray: </strong> Wizard Duel
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">A Wizard Duel should be a unique experience. Every Wizard is a master of themselves and their environment, so when two Duel it will reflect their individualities blended together. The Duel will be an appropriate Stake for both Wizards, from Low to Extreme, Soul, or Paradoxical. Unlike most Ordeals during Proficiencies each Wizard must agree the Proficiencies of the other. During this "negotiation" the rules will usually be stated, with a later intent to bend or break them for an advantage.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-snag">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-snag">
						<strong>Snag: </strong> Strange Ways
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">Wizards naturally complicate everything. They over think situations and plans, or use incredible obscure or clever ideas. They build secret ways, like escape routes, or magically create extra ways to complicate things. The Snag complicates the Fray in unique, complex ways, at the very least adding secret passages for people to vanish into or arrive out of.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-sweeping">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-sweeping">
						<strong>Sweeping: </strong> Magical Gain
					</summary>
