					<aside class="t13ne-description">This can be an actual Gain, such as a magickal spell, tomb or sword, or more likely it is a magically gained Revelation, such as a portent or vision.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
		 <details class="t13ne-revelation-fancy"><summary><strong>Revelation</strong></summary><details class="t13ne-about"><summary><strong>About: </strong> Setting / Background </summary><p class="t13ne-description">The information is about the background, or setting, not the current plot or situation. It might include pertinent details for the current plot, but is usually just about expositional context in this case. The Player of the card should specify how the detail pertains to the current situation.</p></details><details class="t13ne-info"><summary><strong>Info: </strong> How do you feel? </summary><p class="t13ne-description">Information about emotions can be useful and subtle. It can also be useless and overplayed. This shouldn't be a blithe assessment, but something deeply important to the character or people involved. The Villain isn't angry at the village for the fete, they are angry because it was this time of year when their true love died.</p></details><details class="t13ne-vector"><summary><strong>Vector: </strong> Nightmare </summary><p class="t13ne-description">Nightmares are dreams that have gone bad. They are filled with darkness, anxiety and fear, and somewhere in the horrors are clues to the information revealed.</p></details><details class="t13ne-alternate"><summary><strong>Alternate: </strong> Forsaken </summary><p class="t13ne-description">Information about who or what someone has forsaken, or who they have been abandoned by, can reveal a lot about a Character.</p></details><details class="t13ne-detail"><summary><strong>Detail: </strong> Famous </summary><p class="t13ne-description">Some details are famous. These details are known to everyone, or at least most people. Those who know the story, delight in telling those that have never heard.</p></details></details>
		 <details class="t13ne-ordeal-fancy"><summary><strong>Ordeal</strong></summary><p class="t13ne-ordeal-type"><strong>Ordeal-Type </strong> — Opposed Ordeal</p><p class="t13ne-stages"><strong>Stages </strong> — 4</p><p class="t13ne-ordeal-spread"><strong>Ordeal-Spread </strong> — Forking</p><p class="t13ne-stakes"><strong>Stakes </strong> — High</p><p class="t13ne-test"><strong>Test </strong> — Party (Liberty)</p><p class="t13ne-obstacles"><strong>Obstacles </strong> — 1 per Court Stage</p><p class="t13ne-suggested-action"><strong>Suggested-Action </strong> — Dance</p><details class="t13ne-stage"><summary><strong>Stage: </strong> Harmony </summary><p class="t13ne-description">Music fills the space, perhaps there is Muzak playing in the mall, or an outdoor concert in the park, maybe its just the music blaring from some kids loud car stereo. There might be crowds of people, maybe dancing. Or this could just be about everyone moving along the same way.</p></details><details class="t13ne-motional"><summary><strong>Motional: </strong> Horse Race </summary><p class="t13ne-description">Everyone else should be behind you, or unhorsed, when you reach that last Stage.</p></details><details class="t13ne-fight"><summary><strong>Fight: </strong> Skirmish </summary><p class="t13ne-description">A Skirmish is an unplanned encounter between two enemy forces. Skirmishes are often Medium or High Stakes (even during Warfare), and will become a fighting retreat after the first Round.</p></details></details>
		 </details>
			</div>
				<div class="t13ne-card-lea"><details class="t13ne-lea"><summary><strong>Lea</strong></summary><p class="t13ne-colour"><strong>Path Colour —</strong> Blue Aquatic or Black Migratory</p><p class="t13ne-bulmas"><strong>Allowed Bulmäs —</strong> All</p><p class="t13ne-animal-category"><strong>Animal Category —</strong> Megafauna – Medium</p><p class="t13ne-animal-example"><strong>Example Animal —</strong> Hyena / Condor / Albatross</p><p class="t13ne-notes"><strong>Notes —</strong> The largest of the living Black Paths.</p></details></div></div><div class="t13ne-cardwrap-jsvg" id="cardrewritebox21" data-mode="jsvg" data-wyrd="1" data-yarn="1"><img src="https://www.cjmoseley.dev.cc/wp-content/plugins/t13ne/images/cards-svg/9H.svg" class="t13ne-svgcard"><div class="t13ne-svgpips"><div class="t13ne-textpips">9<!-- write suit--><span class="t13ne-symbol"><span class="t13ne-redcard">♥</span></span><details class="t13ne-suit"><summary></summary><p class="t13ne-name"><strong>Suit: </strong>Hearts <span class="t13ne-redcard">♥</span></p><p><strong>Ordeal Trump: </strong><span class="t13ne-trump">Healing</span></p><p class="t13ne-desc"><strong>Description: </strong>Hearts represent Love, Family and the comforts of home. In the Tarot the suit is called Cups, and represents the feminine and elemental Water.</p><p class="t13ne-tarot"><strong>Tarot Meaning: </strong>Love, Relationships, Affections, Family, Sympathy, Peace.</p><p class="t13ne-otext"><strong>Ordeal Text: </strong>Ordeal Types: Nurturing, Farming, Relationships, Medical. Hearts act as Trumps for the following Actions: Blending in, Cooking, Escaping, Healing, Hiding, Hunting, Insulting, Joke-telling, Lying, Mimicry, Riddling, Spinning a tale, Surgery, and Survivalism. Any type of Wound can be healed with a Heart.</p><p class="t13ne-parry"><strong>Parry: </strong><strong>Locked Respite</strong> All unmatched Cards from the original attack are Discarded by the Defender, however with each card they Discard they may either use the card to Heal/Stabilise one of their own wounds (or damage to the Parrying Descendant), or use the card to slow their with additional RT (Pips) or GRT (Pips/5).</p><p class="t13ne-soak"><strong>Soak: </strong><strong>Disengage:</strong> The Soak Card acts as a Heal card, and is discarded along with the Attack card. This immediately Heals all Wounds in play that have less Pips, including the remaining Wounds in the Attack, by one Wound Level (discards Distracts). </p></details></div></div><div class="t13ne-svg-facet"><div>Liberty</div></div><div class="t13ne-wound"><div><strong class="t13ne-woundname">Maim</strong><details><summary></summary><strong>Maim</strong><p class="t13ne-base-pips">Base Pips to summon: 8</p><p class="t13ne-min-pips">Minimum Pips Required: 8</p><p class="t13ne-desc">The Target is injured enough for it to affect their abilities.</p><details class="t13ne-roll"><summary>Rules</summary>Lose 1 Proficiency Slot from affected Annex (including Personality Proficiencies) and recalculate. If all Proficiencies are Maimed, the Annex is Crippled.</details><details class="t13ne-ordeal"><summary>Ordeal Rules</summary>Maimed Annexes lose Proficiency Slots too, but may lose access to Nimbeds affected or take a -1 Card Draw Penalty until Healed / Recalculated. If all Proficiencies are Maimed, the Annex is Crippled.</details><details class="t13ne-psych"><summary>Psych Wounds</summary><details class="t13ne-negative"><summary>Negative Psych</summary><p class="t13ne-normal"><strong>Normal</strong>Proficiency lost from Personality Profs. Must try to flee source of negative emotion until Stabilised or Discarded.</p><p class="t13ne-extreme"><strong>Extreme</strong>Proficiency lost from Personality Profs. All Proficiency Dice are reduced one dice size (1d6 becomes 1d4) until Stabilised.</p></details><details class="t13ne-positive"><summary>Positive Psych</summary><p class="t13ne-normal"><strong>Normal</strong>Gain 1 Proficiency (permanently) and must approach / follow source of positive emotion until Stabilised.</p><p class="t13ne-extreme"><strong>Extreme</strong>Gain 2 Proficiencies (permanently), plus must drop to their knees (or simlar) to express the emotion until Stabilised.</p></details></details></details></div></div><div class="t13ne-tarot-svg"><details><summary class="t13ne-wyrdtarot"><strong> Wyrd Tarot: </strong> Safe Passage</summary><p class="t13ne-normal"><strong> Normal: </strong>Target may leave the Location or Stage (without passing Diff). Cut to destination (where applicable).</p><p class="t13ne-crossed"><strong> Crossed: </strong>The Target is Intercepted or Ambushed (usually by the Player).</p></details></div><div class="t13ne-yarn-fancy">
			<details><summary><strong>Yarn</strong> <br>The Lady</summary>
			<span class="t13ne-description">The Lady card represents Luck, freedom and liberty. Symbolic of gambling and games of chance, the lady can seem carefree and easy, but she can turn on you in an instant, especially if you disrespect her. The Lady is the card of random factors, bizarre coincidences, and insightful synchronicities, never dismiss her as dumb luck.</span>
			<span class="t13ne-significator">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-significator"><strong>Significator</strong></summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-plot"><strong>Plot: </strong> The Lady Plot is one of luck. Good luck, bad luck, coincidence and random events. These plots tend to favour certain types of Characters, naive fools, beginners, risk-takers, gamblers and their ilk. What is true though is the Lady often loses interest in her Hooked Characters, and being fickle may decide to start all over again.</aside>
					<aside class="t13ne-char"><strong>Character: </strong> The Lady Significator indicates a Character who is a risk-taker, a gambler or gamer. They Gain Yin when they play a game, or keep a score. Gain Yang when they make, or take, a bet. Gain Chi when they win a bet. They also gain a +1 Success Level on any game of Skill or Chance.</aside>
					<aside class="t13ne-scene"><strong>Scene: </strong> The Lady Plot is one of luck. Good luck, bad luck, coincidence and random events. These plots tend to favour certain types of Characters, naive fools, beginners, risk-takers, gamblers and their ilk. What is true though is the Lady often loses interest in her Hooked Characters, and being fickle may decide to start all over again.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-scene">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-scene-type">
						<strong>Scene: </strong> Weft
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">Wefts work the Conflict through 3 Parts: <ul><li>First by the Characters Recoiling from the Snag (They emotionally respond to the previous event) which does not require a Yarn card</li><li>Secondly Sweeping (where the Characters recover from what happened) which does require another Yarn Card</li><li>Finally, Picking (Where the Characters will have a choice how the Story will proceed) which does not require Yarn Cards, but can be represented by more cards during card play.</li></ul>During Wefts the Pressed side makes a little headway, or gets a little breather.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-conflict-embodiment">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-conflict-embodiment">
						<strong>Conflict Embodiment: </strong> Internal
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">The Conflict embodies both sides within one Character. The Conflict will play out within them, between two aspects of the Character such as Incarna, Personality, Core, Hitches, Annexes, Proficiencies, or an internal Quest.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>

			<span class="t13ne-facet-embodiment">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-facet-embodiment">
						<strong>Facet Embodiment: </strong> Hitch
					</summary>
					<section class="t13ne-description">The Conflict Embodies one Facet as a Character Hitch. This can create quite secretive and subtle embodiments that can be very difficult to spot. Other times Hitch Embodiments can be very obvious, and in the case of Woes, glaring.</section>
				</details>
			</span>
			 <div class="t13ne-archetypes"><details><summary><strong>Archetype Character: </strong>Lady</summary> <details class="t13ne-Persona"><summary><strong>Persona: </strong><span class="t13ne-output">Rake</span>
      </summary>
      <details class="t13ne-motivation"><summary><strong>Motivation: </strong></summary><span class="t13ne-description">A Rake is motivated by hedonism, if it feels good, the rake will want to do it. Sometimes Rakes can develop sophisticated tastes, and they may prefer variety to volume, but not always. To the Rake all of life is a game, and if you aren't having fun, then you aren't doing it right.</span></details>
       <details class="t13ne-avoid"><summary><strong>Avoid: </strong></summary><span class="t13ne-description">Rakes will do everything they can to avoid confronting their problems, but can be remarkably insightful and helpful with other people's, especially if it will get them whatever they currently crave, or stops them asking the Rake if they are okay / <em>really</em> happy, etc.</span></details>
       <details class="t13ne-gain-chi"><summary><strong>Gain Chi: </strong></summary><span class="t13ne-description"><span>Gain Chi when you try to have whatever fun, or interesting experience, that you can, be it a drink, a meal, a seduction, a game, a drug, whatever.</span><p>Ordeal gain Chi when you prepare a ♥</p></span></details></details>   <details class="t13ne-core"><summary><strong>Core: </strong><span class="t13ne-output">Pretender</span></summary><span class="t13ne-description">Character may choose a second Persona and gain Chi from it also. Characters may change this second Persona for Heresy Boon points of Yang (Heresy Score Chi).</span></details><details class="t13ne-hitch"><summary><strong>Hitch: </strong><span class="t13ne-output">Fatigued</span></summary><span class="t13ne-description">This hitch covers everything from laziness to sickness, some people and things just seem tired or useless most of the time.</span></details><p class="t13ne-description">A Lady is a deceptive manipulating Rake who relies as much on pretending to be something they are not as being a Rake. They often Pretend to be Leaders, Healers or Fanatics.</p></details></div>
			 <span class="t13ne-hook">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-hook">
						<strong>Hook: </strong> Coincidences
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">The Hook of this Plot is about lucky breaks, coincidental leads, chance encounters or random factors aligning. The PCs are usually involved in some other business (like a high Stakes Motional Ordeal) when suddenly they run into the next Bad Guy's minions or more coincidentally the return of an old one.</aside>
					<details class="t13ne-hook-aspect">
						<summary><strong>Hook Aspect: </strong>Core</summary>
						<section class="t13ne-description">The Hook targets the Character's Core. For example: In order to Hook an Enhancer Core, create a situation that only someone with access to that Nimbed could solve.</section>
					</details>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-obstacle-fancy"><details class="t13ne-obstacle"><summary><strong>Obstacle:</strong>Wheel of Fortune</summary><p class="t13ne-description">The way is blocked by some random effect or portal, passing through the Obstacle will result in a random effect. Yarn-Tellers should prepare their own table for each one, similar to this "example". e.g. </p><div class="t13ne-tablewrap"><table class="t13ne-table"><thead><tr><td>Dice (d6)</td><td>Suggested Results</td><td>Secondary Effect</td></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td>1</td><td>Wounded</td><td>Add 1d6 to Pips/Boon</td></tr><tr><td>2</td><td>Changed Appearance (hair colour, style, skin tone, eye colour)</td><td>Subtract 1d6 from Pips/Boon</td></tr><tr><td>3</td><td>Changed Body (gender, species, Incarna, Hitch)</td><td>Swap for another Facet/Proficiency</td></tr><tr><td>4</td><td>Changed Character (Annex, Persona, Core, I-Ching, Facets, Alternates)</td><td>Alter name / Hitch name / Annex Title / Descendant Name</td></tr><tr><td>5</td><td>Changed Time-line (past, present, future, side-step, retcon) </td><td>Swap Umbrals for Nimbeds and Vice Versa</td></tr><tr><td>6</td><td>Prize (Chi Gain or Skill Annex Descendant)</td><td>Roll Twice apply both</td></tr></tbody></table></div><p></p></details></span>
			<span class="t13ne-gain">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-gain">
						<strong>Gain </strong> 
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">Cash, Car, Companionship (Gain Chi, A Descendant and at least a Cast Extra Ally)</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-fray">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-fray">
						<strong>Fray: </strong> Games
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">The Fray is a game, it could be a game of chance, skill or athleticism. Games are obviously usually Low Stakes, but higher Stakes games exist. Playing Chess with Death would count as Soul Stakes (at least), a game of Russian Roulette is Extreme Stakes, and so on. Games can be teaching moments, with Mentors teaching tactics in Chess or ethics, morals or any amount of knowledge being passed via the game. Games can be Ordeals or Tests, or can be played out for real if desired.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-snag">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-snag">
						<strong>Snag: </strong> Accident
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">A freak chain of events, or lack of attention, causes a Character to hurt themselves. Maybe they pull a muscle training, drop a knife on their foot, or step on an upturned nail. Draw 3 cards and apply the most appropriate Card or Trick as an attack on the Hooked Character/Party.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-sweeping">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-sweeping">
						<strong>Sweeping: </strong> Lucky Break
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">Lucky Breaks can be big Gains, Revelations or just a rest depending on how badly Pressed the Characters are. Lucky Breaks can grant all three, but there will be some chance or random factor (often a Liberty or Key Test) to make Gains or Revelations</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
		 <details class="t13ne-revelation-fancy"><summary><strong>Revelation</strong></summary><details class="t13ne-about"><summary><strong>About: </strong> Setting / Background </summary><p class="t13ne-description">The information is about the background, or setting, not the current plot or situation. It might include pertinent details for the current plot, but is usually just about expositional context in this case. The Player of the card should specify how the detail pertains to the current situation.</p></details><details class="t13ne-info"><summary><strong>Info: </strong> Appointments </summary><p class="t13ne-description">Appointments can be information about an event, or time, or it can be about who is getting a job, or position.</p></details><details class="t13ne-vector"><summary><strong>Vector: </strong> Blood </summary><p class="t13ne-description">Blood is usually a good indicator something bad has happened, but at times it can convey a lot more information to either a mage, a cleric, detective, or a forensic biologist.</p></details><details class="t13ne-alternate"><summary><strong>Alternate: </strong> Claims </summary><p class="t13ne-description">Claims are things that people believe the Embodiment has achieved in the past. Often these claims were made by the Embodiment themselves (or their Creator), and may or may not be true.</p></details><details class="t13ne-detail"><summary><strong>Detail: </strong> Cold Fact </summary><p class="t13ne-description">Cold Fact details are simple facts (that are usually also true, but not always). There is no embellishment or opinion associated with the information, just the bare minimum facts.</p></details></details>
		 <details class="t13ne-ordeal-fancy"><summary><strong>Ordeal</strong></summary><p class="t13ne-ordeal-type"><strong>Ordeal-Type </strong> — Opposed Ordeal</p><p class="t13ne-stages"><strong>Stages </strong> — 4</p><p class="t13ne-ordeal-spread"><strong>Ordeal-Spread </strong> — Pyramid</p><p class="t13ne-stakes"><strong>Stakes </strong> — High</p><p class="t13ne-test"><strong>Test </strong> — Survival (Nature)</p><p class="t13ne-obstacles"><strong>Obstacles </strong> — 1 per Court Stage</p><p class="t13ne-suggested-action"><strong>Suggested-Action </strong> — Crawl</p><details class="t13ne-stage"><summary><strong>Stage: </strong> Dust </summary><p class="t13ne-description">The Stage is dusty and windswept, there are few features to break up the monotony, perhaps footprints in the dust. The dust could be lying on the ground, a lack of cleaning, or it might hang in the air, a result of bad air filters, or a light, fine sandy desert.</p></details><details class="t13ne-motional"><summary><strong>Motional: </strong> Dangerous Steeplechase </summary><p class="t13ne-description">Survive all the way to the end of the slog through dangerous territory, and even try to make it there first, if you can keep the horse alive.</p></details><details class="t13ne-fight"><summary><strong>Fight: </strong> Encounter </summary><p class="t13ne-description">An Encounter with a not necessarily hostile force, often a Group of (Chorus Extra) Monsters.<!-- t13ne-shortcode "%7B%22type%22%3A%22facetsuitaspect%22%2C%22suit%22%3A%222%22%2C%22aspect%22%3A%22Monster%22%7D"// --></p><div class="t13ne-tablewrap"><table class="t13ne-table Monster"><thead><tr><th>&nbsp;&nbsp;Facet&nbsp;&nbsp;</th><th>&nbsp;&nbsp;Monster&nbsp;&nbsp;</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td>Enigma</td><td><strong>Skulker</strong> —The Monster is a shadowy figure pulling strings behind the scenes. This may just be an ordinary villain, but their ability to pass unnoticed should be bordering upon the unnatural. Perhaps they use psychic powers or magic to cloud the minds of witnesses, perhaps they are a hacking whiz, able to bounce phone calls through international relays with some multinational or another picking up the tab without realizing, perhaps they are one of the Huldra, the hidden people - who may be the Fæ or maybe they are actually the imaginary friend of one of the characters now made real(ish) by an alien or a Dæmon. Skulkers start with a Monster Pool of Enigma Score. They may add Monster Pool Size Reduced Success Levels to any stealthy or hidden actions or preparations. Skulkers may play Monster Pool cards as Preparation for anothers actions. E.g. The Hacker can prepare a Hack attack, that is performed by a Church Website in another town. A mind-bending villain could prepare a Psyche Defence in the mind of his puppet.</td></tr><tr><td>Heresy</td><td><strong>Masker</strong> —The Monster is looks entirely like something else. Perhaps they are a Demon in the form of an innocent child, or maybe they are a shape-shifter or face-dancer, able to change their appearance as they like, then again perhaps they're just the old Janitor in a monster costume. The Masker may create a Heresy Score sized Monster Pool whenever they hide completely for Heresy Boon as Chi Extend Duration, or are believed in their lies. The Monster Pool adds its Size Reduced to any Score to do with hiding, maintaining the mask, or lying. The Masker must play at least one card from their Monster Pool any time they wish to change their Mask or appearance.</td></tr><tr><td>Jeer</td><td><strong>Mockery</strong> —The monster is a mockery — It might be illusionary, or Undead (usually a corpse animated by a low-level Increated), but it may be a ghost, a machine or even an animal that mocks something else. The Mockery is created with a Monster Pool of Jeer Score cards, that must be played to maintain the Mockery (when disbelieved, attacked, etc). While the Monster Pool exists the Monster can pull and play Monster Pool Size Reduced cards (initially Jeer Draw)for any action the Mockery form takes that it has no suitable Annexes for. E.g. A Skeleton Mockery might have a "Mortality Reminder" Annex, but is given a Monster Pool of 5, so it can initially pull and play 2 cards for its mocking actions (like moving around and clawing the living). However, once attacked (or disbelieved) the Skeleton must spend at least one card from the Mockery Pool, thereafter it can only draw and Play 1 card for any Action that doesn't involve reminding someone of their own Mortality.</td></tr><tr><td>Liberty</td><td><strong>Nightmare</strong> —The Monster is nightmarish, or dreamlike. Such Monsters either exist in a purely psychological realm, appearing as dreams and hallucinations, that cannot really be fought and don’t physically exist, or they are extremely dangerous and powerful Monsters that really exist and may be magical or supernatural in power. Nightmares may add Liberty Draw cards to their Monster Pool if they cross a threshold, liminal space or are dreamed about. While they have cards in their Monster Pool they may add Liberty Boon Dice to everything they do. The Nightmare must spend at least one card from their Monster Pool when they do something impossible. They might use it to fly through the air, instantly teleport, let bullets pass through themselves, or appear to be a real thing and not a dream. Flower-faced Humanoids and Canines from a nightmare realm, handsome soap-sellers with an odd idea for gentlemen’s clubs, facially-burned, gardening-gloved janitors turned serial killers, six foot tall invisible rabbits, night hags, shadow beings seen when falling asleep, and any character in a dream, are all good examples of the Liberty Monster.</td></tr><tr><td>Nature</td><td><strong>Elemental</strong> —The Monster is an elemental force of nature, perhaps a literal elemental or more likely metaphorical. The Elemental will add Nature Draw cards to its Monster Pool when it encounters a natural source of its element, or when it causes collateral damage to a new location. Elementals gain +1 Score per Monster Pool card to any action to do with their natural element. The Elemental must spend cards from its Monster Pool on Physical, Energy or Magical attacks based on its Element. Salamanders, Sylphs, Gnomes, and Undines, Giants (of most types), Storms as well as Tsunamis, landslides, pyroclastic flows and even blizzards (most of which might be Giants anyway).</td></tr><tr><td>Phoenix</td><td><strong>Paragon</strong> —The Monster is the embodiment of a positive force or emotion. Such Monsters are often a counter point, or are opposed to, another Monster. Phoenix Monsters are usually capable of causing positive (and sometimes negative) emotions via a Psychology Test / Psyche Attack in those that see them, and while they may be positive in nature, they are not necessarily good, or altruistic in their actions. The Paragon may have a Monster Pool of cards equal to their Phoenix Score and add cards from the Pool to any action they wish. The Paragon can discard half of their Monster Pool and multiply their Score by 1+ their Phoenix Dice. The Paragon's Monster Pool is replenished in full if the Paragon can find an external example of their chosen emotion. The Phoenix itself is the embodiment of Resurrection, Hope and Faith, but is quite capable of burning destruction. The Golden Goose is another example, the embodiment of charity, but it evokes profound avarice in those who would own it. The unicorn is a symbol of purity, but is dangerous in the extreme to those it considers impure. Depictions of Angels are also perfect examples of positive embodiments.</td></tr></tbody><tfoot><tr><td>&nbsp;&nbsp;Facet&nbsp;&nbsp;</td><td>&nbsp;&nbsp;Monster&nbsp;&nbsp;</td></tr></tfoot></table></div><p></p></details></details>
		 </details>
			</div>
				<div class="t13ne-card-lea"><details class="t13ne-lea"><summary><strong>Lea</strong></summary><p class="t13ne-colour"><strong>Path Colour —</strong> Blue Aquatic or Black Migratory</p><p class="t13ne-bulmas"><strong>Allowed Bulmäs —</strong> All</p><p class="t13ne-animal-category"><strong>Animal Category —</strong> Megafauna – Large</p><p class="t13ne-animal-example"><strong>Example Animal —</strong> Horse / Pachycrocuta (Prehistoric Striped Hyena)</p><p class="t13ne-notes"><strong>Notes —</strong> The Black Horse Paths are actually ancient trade and invasion routes. They are called Warhorse paths by the Hunters, and they may end the path in a Centauroid form if appropriate.</p></details></div></div><div class="t13ne-cardwrap-jsvg" id="cardrewritebox22" data-mode="jsvg" data-wyrd="1" data-yarn="1"><img src="https://www.cjmoseley.dev.cc/wp-content/plugins/t13ne/images/cards-svg/10H.svg" class="t13ne-svgcard"><div class="t13ne-svgpips"><div class="t13ne-textpips">10<!-- write suit--><span class="t13ne-symbol"><span class="t13ne-redcard">♥</span></span><details class="t13ne-suit"><summary></summary><p class="t13ne-name"><strong>Suit: </strong>Hearts <span class="t13ne-redcard">♥</span></p><p><strong>Ordeal Trump: </strong><span class="t13ne-trump">Healing</span></p><p class="t13ne-desc"><strong>Description: </strong>Hearts represent Love, Family and the comforts of home. In the Tarot the suit is called Cups, and represents the feminine and elemental Water.</p><p class="t13ne-tarot"><strong>Tarot Meaning: </strong>Love, Relationships, Affections, Family, Sympathy, Peace.</p><p class="t13ne-otext"><strong>Ordeal Text: </strong>Ordeal Types: Nurturing, Farming, Relationships, Medical. Hearts act as Trumps for the following Actions: Blending in, Cooking, Escaping, Healing, Hiding, Hunting, Insulting, Joke-telling, Lying, Mimicry, Riddling, Spinning a tale, Surgery, and Survivalism. Any type of Wound can be healed with a Heart.</p><p class="t13ne-parry"><strong>Parry: </strong><strong>Locked Respite</strong> All unmatched Cards from the original attack are Discarded by the Defender, however with each card they Discard they may either use the card to Heal/Stabilise one of their own wounds (or damage to the Parrying Descendant), or use the card to slow their with additional RT (Pips) or GRT (Pips/5).</p><p class="t13ne-soak"><strong>Soak: </strong><strong>Disengage:</strong> The Soak Card acts as a Heal card, and is discarded along with the Attack card. This immediately Heals all Wounds in play that have less Pips, including the remaining Wounds in the Attack, by one Wound Level (discards Distracts). </p></details></div></div><div class="t13ne-svg-facet"><div>Jeer</div></div><div class="t13ne-wound"><div><strong class="t13ne-woundname">Cripple</strong><details><summary></summary><strong>Cripple</strong><p class="t13ne-base-pips">Base Pips to summon: 12</p><p class="t13ne-min-pips">Minimum Pips Required: 10</p><p class="t13ne-desc">The Target is seriously wounded, losing access to whole abilities. This can even result in Coma.</p><details class="t13ne-roll"><summary>Rules</summary>Lose 1 Annex Slot (usually the largest one involved in the Roll) until Healed. Once healed Proficiencies may be repurchased for 1 Chi each. Then the Annex will regenerate at Phoenix / Incarna Score Boons per Day. It may be purchased back for the normal cost (Boon in Chi + 2/5/8/12) instantly (or altered and purchased back as a new Annex, if narratively appropriate).</details><details class="t13ne-ordeal"><summary>Ordeal Rules</summary>Crippled Annexes cannot be used as long as the Wound is in play. If applied to a Personality Annex (or a Descendant Master Annex) then the Alternate is unconscious (or the Descendant is lost or useless). They may not use that Alternate (or Descendant) until the Wound is healed (or found / fixed).</details><details class="t13ne-psych"><summary>Psych Wounds</summary><details class="t13ne-negative"><summary>Negative Psych</summary><p class="t13ne-normal"><strong>Normal</strong>Automatically affects the Personality Annex. No Proficiencies or Annexes are lost, but Character loses consciousness at sight of the source until healed.</p><p class="t13ne-extreme"><strong>Extreme</strong>Automatically affects the Personality Annex. Loses consciousness until Healed (not just when the source is present).</p></details><details class="t13ne-positive"><summary>Positive Psych</summary><p class="t13ne-normal"><strong>Normal</strong>Consciousness is interrupted for a few moments (1 Phase). May Gain a Skill Annex/Descendant or 3 Proficiencies.</p><p class="t13ne-extreme"><strong>Extreme</strong>Target loses consciousness (or at least responsiveness) until Stabilised. Gains a new Skill Annex/Descendant.</p></details></details></details></div></div><div class="t13ne-tarot-svg"><details><summary class="t13ne-wyrdtarot"><strong> Wyrd Tarot: </strong> Miracle</summary><p class="t13ne-normal"><strong> Normal: </strong>Automatic Success +4 Additional Success Levels/ Draw Maximum Roll +4 Cards</p><p class="t13ne-crossed"><strong> Crossed: </strong>Target fails with a Disaster (Score of -1 and -4 Success Levels)/ -5 Cards.</p></details></div><div class="t13ne-yarn-fancy">
			<details><summary><strong>Yarn</strong> <br>The Jester</summary>
			<span class="t13ne-description">The Jester card represents satire, comedy, and the absurd. It might be a witty exchange of insults, a bit of rec-room banter, a carefully arranged practical joke, or simply a sense of disjunction in a world turned upside-down. There might be mimicry, disguises, acting, buffoonery, or the delicate terror of a vacant stare.</span>
			<span class="t13ne-significator">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-significator"><strong>Significator</strong></summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-plot"><strong>Plot: </strong> The Jester Plot is also a comedy, although unlike the Joke, the Jester is more parody and satire of something else than a comedy in its own right. A Jester has things to say, about politics, about society, about the audience, where as the Joke just wants to make you laugh the Jester wants to make you laugh and think. The Jester doesn't just make a Pun at random, the Pun will fully reflect the situation at hand.</aside>
					<aside class="t13ne-char"><strong>Character: </strong> The Jester Significator indicates a Character who pokes fun at their betters. They Gain Yin when they mock or make jokes about their superiors, leaders or friends. They Gain Yang when they defend someone, or distract an attacker with humour. They Gain Chi when they mock or poke fun at some one of higher status than themselves.</aside>
					<aside class="t13ne-scene"><strong>Scene: </strong> The Jester Plot is also a comedy, although unlike the Joke, the Jester is more parody and satire of something else than a comedy in its own right. A Jester has things to say, about politics, about society, about the audience, where as the Joke just wants to make you laugh the Jester wants to make you laugh and think. The Jester doesn't just make a Pun at random, the Pun will fully reflect the situation at hand.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-scene">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-scene-type">
						<strong>Scene: </strong> Finale
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">The Finale is a final Ordeal (or Quest or Warp), a great battle, or Revelation. This is where the Conflict comes to a head. The Stakes will be higher (High or Extreme) and there will terrible consequences for failure.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-conflict-embodiment">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-conflict-embodiment">
						<strong>Conflict Embodiment: </strong> Internal
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">The Conflict embodies both sides within one Character. The Conflict will play out within them, between two aspects of the Character such as Incarna, Personality, Core, Hitches, Annexes, Proficiencies, or an internal Quest.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>

			<span class="t13ne-facet-embodiment">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-facet-embodiment">
						<strong>Facet Embodiment: </strong> Proficiency
					</summary>
					<section class="t13ne-description">The Conflict Embodies one Facet as a single Proficiency from that Facet. Characters or Descendants that have the Proficiency may be Hooked to that side. This is a very subtle Embodiment when handled gently.</section>
				</details>
			</span>
			 <div class="t13ne-archetypes"><details><summary><strong>Archetype Character: </strong>Comedian</summary> <details class="t13ne-Persona"><summary><strong>Persona: </strong><span class="t13ne-output">Sphinx</span>
      </summary>
      <details class="t13ne-motivation"><summary><strong>Motivation: </strong></summary><span class="t13ne-description">Sphinxes live for a puzzled expression on another's face. They like nothing more than a good question, or riddle that they can ask. Sphinxes may also enjoy offering cryptic advice and tips.</span></details>
       <details class="t13ne-avoid"><summary><strong>Avoid: </strong></summary><span class="t13ne-description">Sphinxes hate giving straight answers to questions, they'll even rather answer a different question to the one asked, if they can get away with it.</span></details>
       <details class="t13ne-gain-chi"><summary><strong>Gain Chi: </strong></summary><span class="t13ne-description"><span>Gain Chi when you confuse another Character or ask (a question / a riddle).</span><p>Ordeal gain Chi when you play any Distract card.</p></span></details></details>   <details class="t13ne-core"><summary><strong>Core: </strong><span class="t13ne-output">Joker</span></summary><span class="t13ne-description">Gain Chi and +1 success level when another nearby laughs.</span></details><details class="t13ne-hitch"><summary><strong>Hitch: </strong><span class="t13ne-output">Taboo</span></summary><span class="t13ne-description">Whether religious, superstitious or just dietary, Taboos are things your character just doesn't want to do</span></details><p class="t13ne-description">The Comedian asks questions and expects only laughs as answers. They use humour to deflect and manipulate people, and can usually create powerful Psych attacks with a "Jokes" Power.</p></details></div>
			 <span class="t13ne-hook">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-hook">
						<strong>Hook: </strong> Entrance
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">The Hook is literally and entrance. This usually refers to the sudden, dramatic appearance of a new or returning Embodiment, but it can also be an opening, door or any threshold, or cliff-edge moment. In Old Comedies the players would sing as they paraded in, setting up the story with the first song, in old serials they would show how the Cliff-Hanger ending of the last Chapter was resolved (often with a retcon).</aside>
					<details class="t13ne-hook-aspect">
						<summary><strong>Hook Aspect: </strong>Core</summary>
						<section class="t13ne-description">The Hook targets the Character's Core. For example: In order to Hook a Joker some needs to start laughing at them.</section>
					</details>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-obstacle-fancy"><details class="t13ne-obstacle"><summary><strong>Obstacle:</strong>Ridicule</summary><p class="t13ne-description">The way is blocked unless the Character subjects themselves to some ridicule or mockery. This is usually a psych attack and may have a Character who performs the ridicule. Or you may draw again and create a humorous, but more dangerous (dark-humour) version of that Obstacle.</p></details></span>
			<span class="t13ne-gain">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-gain">
						<strong>Gain </strong> 
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">An unlikely, but powerful Ally (A Eusaivean with a Bulmäs best friend, a Knight with a Dragon steed, a cyborg with a pet Rust monster)</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-fray">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-fray">
						<strong>Fray: </strong> Insult Tennis
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">Insult tennis is a Low-Medium Stakes Opposed Ordeal, the PCs and their foes or Rivals exchange insults, and cutting remarks. In effect the Tennis is a battle or duel fought to the first blood (although in this case first emotional outburst) with Psych attacks.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-snag">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-snag">
						<strong>Snag: </strong> Upside-Down
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">Nothing makes sense, the good becomes bad, the powerful become weak, and the weak become powerful. Even though they may have won the Fray, everything is turned on its head. Perhaps it turns out that they were deceived all along, and were working for the bad guys, or maybe they never really grasped what was going on and picked the wrong side.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-sweeping">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-sweeping">
						<strong>Sweeping: </strong> Wandering Fool
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">The PC moves (usually via a Motional Ordeal) to a new Location. For the PCs this journey may be aimless wandering, but in fact it is a hunt for something they need. Often the Fool will encounter a lesson in the new location, or maybe just rest, heal up a bit, and maybe get a bite to eat or a little spending cash on the way.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
		 <details class="t13ne-revelation-fancy"><summary><strong>Revelation</strong></summary><details class="t13ne-about"><summary><strong>About: </strong> Setting / Background </summary><p class="t13ne-description">The information is about the background, or setting, not the current plot or situation. It might include pertinent details for the current plot, but is usually just about expositional context in this case. The Player of the card should specify how the detail pertains to the current situation.</p></details><details class="t13ne-info"><summary><strong>Info: </strong> Position </summary><p class="t13ne-description">Positions are important, knowing where you are or what is expected of you can be enough for some people. Knowing those things about someone can tell you a lot about them. Of course knowing the position of a castle can help plan an assault.</p></details><details class="t13ne-vector"><summary><strong>Vector: </strong> DNA </summary><p class="t13ne-description">We leave DNA everywhere we go in blood, skin cells, hairs. This might seem important to a scientist, but sympathetic magic and even a good hunting dog can also make use of this information source. Perhaps though the information itself is encoded genetically into a plant or animal.</p></details><details class="t13ne-alternate"><summary><strong>Alternate: </strong> Home </summary><p class="t13ne-description">Where someone lives currently can be important information about a person, especially if you are looking for them. Where they came from can also be useful.</p></details><details class="t13ne-detail"><summary><strong>Detail: </strong> Waste </summary><p class="t13ne-description">Waste details are information about the debris and detritus that surrounds the Embodiment. It might be as detail about their garbage collection schedule, or whether they recycle, or that after the supra-weapon was constructed there were pieces of incredibly strong crystal found on the floor of the workshop.</p></details></details>
		 <details class="t13ne-ordeal-fancy"><summary><strong>Ordeal</strong></summary><p class="t13ne-ordeal-type"><strong>Ordeal-Type </strong> — Opposed Ordeal</p><p class="t13ne-stages"><strong>Stages </strong> — 4</p><p class="t13ne-ordeal-spread"><strong>Ordeal-Spread </strong> — Block</p><p class="t13ne-stakes"><strong>Stakes </strong> — Extreme</p><p class="t13ne-test"><strong>Test </strong> — Hunting (Nature)</p><p class="t13ne-obstacles"><strong>Obstacles </strong> — 1 per Stage</p><p class="t13ne-suggested-action"><strong>Suggested-Action </strong> — Hunt</p><details class="t13ne-stage"><summary><strong>Stage: </strong> Countryside </summary><p class="t13ne-description">Rolling hills, grasslands, trees and countryside, or maybe just a really good park, or an incredible garden</p></details><details class="t13ne-motional"><summary><strong>Motional: </strong> Foot Race </summary><p class="t13ne-description">Reach the goal on foot, before anyone else to secure the victory.</p></details><details class="t13ne-fight"><summary><strong>Fight: </strong> Fire fight </summary><p class="t13ne-description">A battle against opponents with prepared defences and ranged (and / or heavy) weapons. The opponent should be at least equal in number and power to the party.</p></details></details>
		 </details>
			</div>
				<div class="t13ne-card-lea"><details class="t13ne-lea"><summary><strong>Lea</strong></summary><p class="t13ne-colour"><strong>Path Colour —</strong> Blue Aquatic or Black Migratory</p><p class="t13ne-bulmas"><strong>Allowed Bulmäs —</strong> All</p><p class="t13ne-animal-category"><strong>Animal Category —</strong> Megafauna – Largest</p><p class="t13ne-animal-example"><strong>Example Animal —</strong> Pseudo-Dragon</p><p class="t13ne-notes"><strong>Notes —</strong> The Pseudo-Dragons are large reptiles, monitor lizards and the like, usually ancient paths like these can become blended with later animal paths leading to complex monstrous chimeras. Such as the Wyvern, a Lizard / Pterosaur / Bat Hybrid, Thunderbird (a Pterosaur / Condor Hybrid), Wyrms ( Snake / Dinosaur / Eel Hybrids) or Lake Monster (a blend of Dinosaur / Sturgeon / Seal / Eel Hybrids).</p></details></div></div><div class="t13ne-cardwrap-jsvg" id="cardrewritebox23" data-mode="jsvg" data-wyrd="1" data-yarn="1"><img src="https://www.cjmoseley.dev.cc/wp-content/plugins/t13ne/images/cards-svg/JH.svg" class="t13ne-svgcard"><div class="t13ne-svgpips"><div class="t13ne-textpips">11<!-- write suit--><span class="t13ne-symbol"><span class="t13ne-redcard">♥</span></span><details class="t13ne-suit"><summary></summary><p class="t13ne-name"><strong>Suit: </strong>Hearts <span class="t13ne-redcard">♥</span></p><p><strong>Ordeal Trump: </strong><span class="t13ne-trump">Healing</span></p><p class="t13ne-desc"><strong>Description: </strong>Hearts represent Love, Family and the comforts of home. In the Tarot the suit is called Cups, and represents the feminine and elemental Water.</p><p class="t13ne-tarot"><strong>Tarot Meaning: </strong>Love, Relationships, Affections, Family, Sympathy, Peace.</p><p class="t13ne-otext"><strong>Ordeal Text: </strong>Ordeal Types: Nurturing, Farming, Relationships, Medical. Hearts act as Trumps for the following Actions: Blending in, Cooking, Escaping, Healing, Hiding, Hunting, Insulting, Joke-telling, Lying, Mimicry, Riddling, Spinning a tale, Surgery, and Survivalism. Any type of Wound can be healed with a Heart.</p><p class="t13ne-parry"><strong>Parry: </strong><strong>Locked Respite</strong> All unmatched Cards from the original attack are Discarded by the Defender, however with each card they Discard they may either use the card to Heal/Stabilise one of their own wounds (or damage to the Parrying Descendant), or use the card to slow their with additional RT (Pips) or GRT (Pips/5).</p><p class="t13ne-soak"><strong>Soak: </strong><strong>Disengage:</strong> The Soak Card acts as a Heal card, and is discarded along with the Attack card. This immediately Heals all Wounds in play that have less Pips, including the remaining Wounds in the Attack, by one Wound Level (discards Distracts). </p></details></div></div><div class="t13ne-svg-facet"><div>Heresy</div></div><div class="t13ne-wound"><div><strong class="t13ne-woundname">Cripple</strong><details><summary></summary><strong>Cripple</strong><p class="t13ne-base-pips">Base Pips to summon: 12</p><p class="t13ne-min-pips">Minimum Pips Required: 10</p><p class="t13ne-desc">The Target is seriously wounded, losing access to whole abilities. This can even result in Coma.</p><details class="t13ne-roll"><summary>Rules</summary>Lose 1 Annex Slot (usually the largest one involved in the Roll) until Healed. Once healed Proficiencies may be repurchased for 1 Chi each. Then the Annex will regenerate at Phoenix / Incarna Score Boons per Day. It may be purchased back for the normal cost (Boon in Chi + 2/5/8/12) instantly (or altered and purchased back as a new Annex, if narratively appropriate).</details><details class="t13ne-ordeal"><summary>Ordeal Rules</summary>Crippled Annexes cannot be used as long as the Wound is in play. If applied to a Personality Annex (or a Descendant Master Annex) then the Alternate is unconscious (or the Descendant is lost or useless). They may not use that Alternate (or Descendant) until the Wound is healed (or found / fixed).</details><details class="t13ne-psych"><summary>Psych Wounds</summary><details class="t13ne-negative"><summary>Negative Psych</summary><p class="t13ne-normal"><strong>Normal</strong>Automatically affects the Personality Annex. No Proficiencies or Annexes are lost, but Character loses consciousness at sight of the source until healed.</p><p class="t13ne-extreme"><strong>Extreme</strong>Automatically affects the Personality Annex. Loses consciousness until Healed (not just when the source is present).</p></details><details class="t13ne-positive"><summary>Positive Psych</summary><p class="t13ne-normal"><strong>Normal</strong>Consciousness is interrupted for a few moments (1 Phase). May Gain a Skill Annex/Descendant or 3 Proficiencies.</p><p class="t13ne-extreme"><strong>Extreme</strong>Target loses consciousness (or at least responsiveness) until Stabilised. Gains a new Skill Annex/Descendant.</p></details></details></details></div></div><div class="t13ne-tarot-svg"><details><summary class="t13ne-wyrdtarot"><strong> Wyrd Tarot: </strong> Double Bluff</summary><p class="t13ne-normal"><strong> Normal: </strong>Grants Target an additional pile where cards may be played upon them.</p><p class="t13ne-crossed"><strong> Crossed: </strong>Closes Pile. All cards beneath the Double Bluff are discarded,
 no card may be played on the Double Bluff until a Crisis removes it.</p></details></div><div class="t13ne-yarn-fancy">
			<details><summary><strong>Yarn</strong> <br>The Mask</summary>
			<span class="t13ne-description">The Mask card represents the false fronts that we play. There are few people who are completely open and honest with everyone in their life, most of us choose to hide aspects of ourselves from view and don a mask. For some, undercover agents and those running for their lives, the mask becomes their only existence.</span>
			<span class="t13ne-significator">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-significator"><strong>Significator</strong></summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-plot"><strong>Plot: </strong> The Mask Plot is a a plot about secret identities and double-lives. The Plot might be about Superheroes, dealing with a real life and a costumed identity, or an undercover cop in a crime syndicate, or a cyberninja infiltrating an opposed mega-zaibatsu. Mask Plots can become farcical unless there is real Tension that they will be discovered and that it will have profound consequences. It is sometimes a necessary part of the Mask plot to have the hero discover exactly how bad it can get, with loved ones threatened or killed by the Plot.</aside>
					<aside class="t13ne-char"><strong>Character: </strong> The Mask Significator indicates a Character who can pretend to be things they are not. They Gain Yin when they wear a mask, disguise or shape-shift. They Gain Yang when they deceive another or pretend to be something they are not. They Gain Chi when they are not recognised for who, or what, they are. They also gain +1 Success Level with all disguise, masks and make-up actions.</aside>
					<aside class="t13ne-scene"><strong>Scene: </strong> The Mask Plot is a a plot about secret identities and double-lives. The Plot might be about Superheroes, dealing with a real life and a costumed identity, or an undercover cop in a crime syndicate, or a cyberninja infiltrating an opposed mega-zaibatsu. Mask Plots can become farcical unless there is real Tension that they will be discovered and that it will have profound consequences. It is sometimes a necessary part of the Mask plot to have the hero discover exactly how bad it can get, with loved ones threatened or killed by the Plot.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-scene">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-scene-type">
						<strong>Scene: </strong> Gain
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">Gains give something to one of the Characters to help move the Story on. Sometimes this might be payment for a job they did, or it might be a discovery that will directly connect to the Story. This may be information. It is normal to play a separate card to define the actual Gain that is made.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-conflict-embodiment">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-conflict-embodiment">
						<strong>Conflict Embodiment: </strong> Internal
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">The Conflict embodies both sides within one Character. The Conflict will play out within them, between two aspects of the Character such as Incarna, Personality, Core, Hitches, Annexes, Proficiencies, or an internal Quest.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>

			<span class="t13ne-facet-embodiment">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-facet-embodiment">
						<strong>Facet Embodiment: </strong> Incarna
					</summary>
					<section class="t13ne-description">The Conflict Embodies one Facet as a Character or Descendant Incarna. Often the easiest Embodiment to create Fantastical Characters, but not often suitable to all Genres and Conflicts. Also this tends to to make for a very obvious Conflict.</section>
				</details>
			</span>
			 <div class="t13ne-archetypes"><details><summary><strong>Archetype Character: </strong>Spy</summary> <details class="t13ne-Persona"><summary><strong>Persona: </strong><span class="t13ne-output">Deviant</span>
      </summary>
      <details class="t13ne-motivation"><summary><strong>Motivation: </strong></summary><span class="t13ne-description">Deviants are motivated by being different from others. They like to see themselves as a lone wolf, or a trendsetter, never as a follower or leader. Deviants may have unique beliefs, or a code of practice that puts them at odds with the larger society around them, as is the case with many Cults or secret societies. Deviants may have goals, that they pursue by deception and manipulation, but are as often aimlessly reacting to what happens around them.</span></details>
       <details class="t13ne-avoid"><summary><strong>Avoid: </strong></summary><span class="t13ne-description">Deviants hate acting like everyone else, and dislike being predictable.</span></details>
       <details class="t13ne-gain-chi"><summary><strong>Gain Chi: </strong></summary><span class="t13ne-description"><span>Gain Chi when you act / react differently to everyone else, or when you deceive or surprise another.</span><p>Ordeal gain Chi when you play a ♥ to attack.</p></span></details></details>   <details class="t13ne-core"><summary><strong>Core: </strong><span class="t13ne-output">Pretender</span></summary><span class="t13ne-description">Character may choose a second Persona and gain Chi from it also. Characters may change this second Persona for Heresy Boon points of Yang (Heresy Score Chi).</span></details><details class="t13ne-hitch"><summary><strong>Hitch: </strong><span class="t13ne-output">Scarred</span></summary><span class="t13ne-description">Time heals all wounds it is said, but anyone who carries a prominent facial scar knows that while the pain may fade, the scars are always present. Scars don't have to be physical or visible to affect a character forever, they just need to worth a Hitch.</span></details><p class="t13ne-description">The Spy lives quietly, hiding amongst their enemies, they are a Pretender who will become whatever they need to be to avoid suspicion, while constantly working on their devious, deviant, plans. During active missions Spies will often become Leaders, or Scouts to maximise the gains they make.</p></details></div>
			 <span class="t13ne-hook">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-hook">
						<strong>Hook: </strong> False Face
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">The Story opens with the Plot presenting a false face, it may be a pretext for something else, or a pretence of some sort, but the plot does not represent itself truthfully. It may hide its Conflict Embodiments (perhaps making the Player Characters not the Pressed Characters), make false Revelations, or false promises in general.</aside>
					<details class="t13ne-hook-aspect">
						<summary><strong>Hook Aspect: </strong>Core</summary>
						<section class="t13ne-description">The Hook targets the Character's Core. For example: In order to Hook a Traveller, create a barrier that stops them from travelling.</section>
					</details>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-obstacle-fancy"><details class="t13ne-obstacle"><summary><strong>Obstacle:</strong>Lie</summary><p class="t13ne-description">The Obstacle requires you to Lie for all you're worth and pretend to be something you are not. It might be that the way ahead is only allowed for someone of a particular rank, gender, religion or race, and you have to bluff your way past Guardians.</p></details></span>
			<span class="t13ne-gain">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-gain">
						<strong>Gain </strong> 
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">Secret Identity (this can just be a disguise Descendant, or a whole lifestyle including Locations, or even a whole new Alternate)</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-fray">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-fray">
						<strong>Fray: </strong> Masquerade
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">Masquerades can be simple fancy dress parties, or they can be complex political or covert Ordeals full of under-the-table deals, or thinking bigger they can be their own Stories with sub-rosa themes, Quests to deceive, mislead and be underhanded with others, or even a Witness Protection Program or Deep Cover / Sleeper Agent situations that last decades.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-snag">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-snag">
						<strong>Snag: </strong> Red Herring
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">There is some false information around, it may have already have misled the PCs, but worse yet the next Revelation they make will be deliberate misinformation by the Yarn-Teller or Plot. Perhaps they will be told the truth in the end, or might even spot the lie eventually, but this isn't a simple lie, it will be close to the truth and they will find supporting evidence.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-sweeping">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-sweeping">
						<strong>Sweeping: </strong> Lost-in-the-crowd
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">No matter the Fray the PCs get to vanish in the wind after it. They change or adopt disguises, head to a safe-house, or vanish into the night. They can rest, recover, shop, create or train for the next Fray.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
		 <details class="t13ne-revelation-fancy"><summary><strong>Revelation</strong></summary><details class="t13ne-about"><summary><strong>About: </strong> Pressed </summary><p class="t13ne-description">The information is about the Pressed Conflict Embodiment, which is often a PC in a game, and is usually the antagonist in a Story.</p></details><details class="t13ne-info"><summary><strong>Info: </strong> With Whom? </summary><p class="t13ne-description">You can find out a lot about a person from the company they keep. And it always helps to know how many are in the gang you are hunting.</p></details><details class="t13ne-vector"><summary><strong>Vector: </strong> Blatant </summary><p class="t13ne-description">Sometimes there is an obvious clue, something so blatant that it cannot be ignored. Like a video of the killer in the act of killing. The problem with blatant revelations is that they are so blatant they seem too good to be true, and sometimes they are.</p></details><details class="t13ne-alternate"><summary><strong>Alternate: </strong> Proposal </summary><p class="t13ne-description">Proposals present information about alliances by marriage and future plans. A proposal might also be a variant of the current plot that was set aside for some reason. "The rumour is that he's allied with Garthun the Reaver, but Garthun died a week ago in Morrowfalls, the Reavers are all returning to the mountains, so he must have found another ally." he said and stared once again at the map, as though it would provide him answer.</p></details><details class="t13ne-detail"><summary><strong>Detail: </strong> Temporal </summary><p class="t13ne-description">Temporal details include things like when a thing was made, or how it old it is. It might be explicit or hinted at through records or pictures. "Why does the Count look exactly the same as this bloke in the portrait from 1647?"</p></details></details>
		 <details class="t13ne-ordeal-fancy"><summary><strong>Ordeal</strong></summary><p class="t13ne-ordeal-type"><strong>Ordeal-Type </strong> — Opposed Ordeal</p><p class="t13ne-stages"><strong>Stages </strong> — 5</p><p class="t13ne-ordeal-spread"><strong>Ordeal-Spread </strong> — Pyramid</p><p class="t13ne-stakes"><strong>Stakes </strong> — Blended Low-Medium</p><p class="t13ne-test"><strong>Test </strong> — Healing (Phoenix)</p><p class="t13ne-obstacles"><strong>Obstacles </strong> — 1 per Stage</p><p class="t13ne-suggested-action"><strong>Suggested-Action </strong> — Jump Down</p><details class="t13ne-stage"><summary><strong>Stage: </strong> Jump Stunt </summary><p class="t13ne-description">Jump down, Jump into shadows, a Joke, a Quantum Leap.</p></details><details class="t13ne-motional"><summary><strong>Motional: </strong> Swimming Race </summary><p class="t13ne-description">Swim for the goal, before anyone else, to secure the victory.</p></details><details class="t13ne-fight"><summary><strong>Fight: </strong> Knave </summary><p class="t13ne-description">A single Monster (usually with at least Two ♦ Monster Facets), that is at least equivalent to any 2 PCs.<!-- t13ne-shortcode "%7B%22type%22%3A%22facetsuitaspect%22%2C%22suit%22%3A%222%22%2C%22aspect%22%3A%22Monster%22%7D"// --></p><div class="t13ne-tablewrap"><table class="t13ne-table Monster"><thead><tr><th>&nbsp;&nbsp;Facet&nbsp;&nbsp;</th><th>&nbsp;&nbsp;Monster&nbsp;&nbsp;</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td>Enigma</td><td><strong>Skulker</strong> —The Monster is a shadowy figure pulling strings behind the scenes. This may just be an ordinary villain, but their ability to pass unnoticed should be bordering upon the unnatural. Perhaps they use psychic powers or magic to cloud the minds of witnesses, perhaps they are a hacking whiz, able to bounce phone calls through international relays with some multinational or another picking up the tab without realizing, perhaps they are one of the Huldra, the hidden people - who may be the Fæ or maybe they are actually the imaginary friend of one of the characters now made real(ish) by an alien or a Dæmon. Skulkers start with a Monster Pool of Enigma Score. They may add Monster Pool Size Reduced Success Levels to any stealthy or hidden actions or preparations. Skulkers may play Monster Pool cards as Preparation for anothers actions. E.g. The Hacker can prepare a Hack attack, that is performed by a Church Website in another town. A mind-bending villain could prepare a Psyche Defence in the mind of his puppet.</td></tr><tr><td>Heresy</td><td><strong>Masker</strong> —The Monster is looks entirely like something else. Perhaps they are a Demon in the form of an innocent child, or maybe they are a shape-shifter or face-dancer, able to change their appearance as they like, then again perhaps they're just the old Janitor in a monster costume. The Masker may create a Heresy Score sized Monster Pool whenever they hide completely for Heresy Boon as Chi Extend Duration, or are believed in their lies. The Monster Pool adds its Size Reduced to any Score to do with hiding, maintaining the mask, or lying. The Masker must play at least one card from their Monster Pool any time they wish to change their Mask or appearance.</td></tr><tr><td>Jeer</td><td><strong>Mockery</strong> —The monster is a mockery — It might be illusionary, or Undead (usually a corpse animated by a low-level Increated), but it may be a ghost, a machine or even an animal that mocks something else. The Mockery is created with a Monster Pool of Jeer Score cards, that must be played to maintain the Mockery (when disbelieved, attacked, etc). While the Monster Pool exists the Monster can pull and play Monster Pool Size Reduced cards (initially Jeer Draw)for any action the Mockery form takes that it has no suitable Annexes for. E.g. A Skeleton Mockery might have a "Mortality Reminder" Annex, but is given a Monster Pool of 5, so it can initially pull and play 2 cards for its mocking actions (like moving around and clawing the living). However, once attacked (or disbelieved) the Skeleton must spend at least one card from the Mockery Pool, thereafter it can only draw and Play 1 card for any Action that doesn't involve reminding someone of their own Mortality.</td></tr><tr><td>Liberty</td><td><strong>Nightmare</strong> —The Monster is nightmarish, or dreamlike. Such Monsters either exist in a purely psychological realm, appearing as dreams and hallucinations, that cannot really be fought and don’t physically exist, or they are extremely dangerous and powerful Monsters that really exist and may be magical or supernatural in power. Nightmares may add Liberty Draw cards to their Monster Pool if they cross a threshold, liminal space or are dreamed about. While they have cards in their Monster Pool they may add Liberty Boon Dice to everything they do. The Nightmare must spend at least one card from their Monster Pool when they do something impossible. They might use it to fly through the air, instantly teleport, let bullets pass through themselves, or appear to be a real thing and not a dream. Flower-faced Humanoids and Canines from a nightmare realm, handsome soap-sellers with an odd idea for gentlemen’s clubs, facially-burned, gardening-gloved janitors turned serial killers, six foot tall invisible rabbits, night hags, shadow beings seen when falling asleep, and any character in a dream, are all good examples of the Liberty Monster.</td></tr><tr><td>Nature</td><td><strong>Elemental</strong> —The Monster is an elemental force of nature, perhaps a literal elemental or more likely metaphorical. The Elemental will add Nature Draw cards to its Monster Pool when it encounters a natural source of its element, or when it causes collateral damage to a new location. Elementals gain +1 Score per Monster Pool card to any action to do with their natural element. The Elemental must spend cards from its Monster Pool on Physical, Energy or Magical attacks based on its Element. Salamanders, Sylphs, Gnomes, and Undines, Giants (of most types), Storms as well as Tsunamis, landslides, pyroclastic flows and even blizzards (most of which might be Giants anyway).</td></tr><tr><td>Phoenix</td><td><strong>Paragon</strong> —The Monster is the embodiment of a positive force or emotion. Such Monsters are often a counter point, or are opposed to, another Monster. Phoenix Monsters are usually capable of causing positive (and sometimes negative) emotions via a Psychology Test / Psyche Attack in those that see them, and while they may be positive in nature, they are not necessarily good, or altruistic in their actions. The Paragon may have a Monster Pool of cards equal to their Phoenix Score and add cards from the Pool to any action they wish. The Paragon can discard half of their Monster Pool and multiply their Score by 1+ their Phoenix Dice. The Paragon's Monster Pool is replenished in full if the Paragon can find an external example of their chosen emotion. The Phoenix itself is the embodiment of Resurrection, Hope and Faith, but is quite capable of burning destruction. The Golden Goose is another example, the embodiment of charity, but it evokes profound avarice in those who would own it. The unicorn is a symbol of purity, but is dangerous in the extreme to those it considers impure. Depictions of Angels are also perfect examples of positive embodiments.</td></tr></tbody><tfoot><tr><td>&nbsp;&nbsp;Facet&nbsp;&nbsp;</td><td>&nbsp;&nbsp;Monster&nbsp;&nbsp;</td></tr></tfoot></table></div><p></p></details></details>
		 </details>
			</div>
				<div class="t13ne-card-lea"><details class="t13ne-lea"><summary><strong>Lea</strong></summary><p class="t13ne-colour"><strong>Path Colour —</strong> Blue Aquatic or Black Migratory</p><p class="t13ne-bulmas"><strong>Allowed Bulmäs —</strong> All</p><p class="t13ne-animal-category"><strong>Animal Category —</strong> Man-paths/Broccen Territory</p><p class="t13ne-animal-example"><strong>Example Animal —</strong> Human / Groonians / Eelafin / Eusaivean / Mer-person / Bigfoot / Giant / Goblin, etc</p><p class="t13ne-notes"><strong>Notes —</strong> Broccen may use Jack’s to dig new tunnels in the Lea, creating species to fill niches. Jacks may also be used to create Chimerical changes, blending parts of different animals together, each Jack adds another place the Bulmäs can play Lea cards.</p></details></div></div><div class="t13ne-cardwrap-jsvg" id="cardrewritebox24" data-mode="jsvg" data-wyrd="1" data-yarn="1"><img src="https://www.cjmoseley.dev.cc/wp-content/plugins/t13ne/images/cards-svg/QH.svg" class="t13ne-svgcard"><div class="t13ne-svgpips"><div class="t13ne-textpips">12<!-- write suit--><span class="t13ne-symbol"><span class="t13ne-redcard">♥</span></span><details class="t13ne-suit"><summary></summary><p class="t13ne-name"><strong>Suit: </strong>Hearts <span class="t13ne-redcard">♥</span></p><p><strong>Ordeal Trump: </strong><span class="t13ne-trump">Healing</span></p><p class="t13ne-desc"><strong>Description: </strong>Hearts represent Love, Family and the comforts of home. In the Tarot the suit is called Cups, and represents the feminine and elemental Water.</p><p class="t13ne-tarot"><strong>Tarot Meaning: </strong>Love, Relationships, Affections, Family, Sympathy, Peace.</p><p class="t13ne-otext"><strong>Ordeal Text: </strong>Ordeal Types: Nurturing, Farming, Relationships, Medical. Hearts act as Trumps for the following Actions: Blending in, Cooking, Escaping, Healing, Hiding, Hunting, Insulting, Joke-telling, Lying, Mimicry, Riddling, Spinning a tale, Surgery, and Survivalism. Any type of Wound can be healed with a Heart.</p><p class="t13ne-parry"><strong>Parry: </strong><strong>Locked Respite</strong> All unmatched Cards from the original attack are Discarded by the Defender, however with each card they Discard they may either use the card to Heal/Stabilise one of their own wounds (or damage to the Parrying Descendant), or use the card to slow their with additional RT (Pips) or GRT (Pips/5).</p><p class="t13ne-soak"><strong>Soak: </strong><strong>Disengage:</strong> The Soak Card acts as a Heal card, and is discarded along with the Attack card. This immediately Heals all Wounds in play that have less Pips, including the remaining Wounds in the Attack, by one Wound Level (discards Distracts). </p></details></div></div><div class="t13ne-svg-facet"><div>Enigma</div></div><div class="t13ne-wound"><div><strong class="t13ne-woundname">Mortal</strong><details><summary></summary><strong>Mortal</strong><p class="t13ne-base-pips">Base Pips to summon: 16</p><p class="t13ne-min-pips">Minimum Pips Required: 12</p><p class="t13ne-desc">The Target is now dying from the wound.</p><details class="t13ne-roll"><summary>Rules</summary>Create a "Dying / Broken / Burning" quirk Hitch that slowly grows +1 Hitch Boon per Miasma Boon as Chi Limit Duration) until healed. If no Hitch can be increased by the Mortal Wound then decrease an appropriate Facet (Hitch, Incarna, Persona or Core usually) by a Boon equal to the Wounded Character’s Miasma Score. I-Ching, Personality, Proficiency slots and Annexes affected should be recalculated. If the Hitch becomes a Flaw the Wound will automatically upgrade to a Carnage Wound.</details><details class="t13ne-ordeal"><summary>Ordeal Rules</summary>Treat as though the Personality Annex or Master Annex has been Crippled, until the end of the Round. Then add a new "Dying / Broken / Burning" quirk Hitch with a Boon of Miasma Score (to a Max of Half the Incarna Boon). Or increase a quirk Hitch by 1 Boon at the end of each Round.</details><details class="t13ne-psych"><summary>Psych Wounds</summary><details class="t13ne-negative"><summary>Negative Psych</summary><p class="t13ne-normal"><strong>Normal</strong>As Normal but does not create a "Dying" Hitch, but instead a Scars (Mental/Emotional) / Fear / Fatigued / Lament / Taboo / Offensive / Doubt,  etc Hitch.  Plus Consciousness is usually lost until Stabilised.</p><p class="t13ne-extreme"><strong>Extreme</strong>Creates any normal Hitch type. At Yarn-Teller's discretion Mortal Wounds can also alter the Persona, or Core Facet choice (Ref arbitrates). Plus Consciousness is usually lost until healed.</p></details><details class="t13ne-positive"><summary>Positive Psych</summary><p class="t13ne-normal"><strong>Normal</strong>As Normal but creates a Scars (Mental/Emotional) / Duty / Dependance / Paranoia / Devotion / Madness / Vague, etc quirk Hitch.  Plus Consciousness is usually lost until Stabilised. May Gain a new Talent Annex / Descendant or Resolve a quirk Hitch.</p><p class="t13ne-extreme"><strong>Extreme</strong>May create any Flaw Hitch. At Yarn-Teller's discretion Mortal Wounds can also alter the wounded character’s Persona, or Core Facet choices. Consciousness is usually lost until healed. Gains a new Talent Annex / Descendant or Resolves a quirk Handicap.</p></details></details></details></div></div><div class="t13ne-tarot-svg"><details><summary class="t13ne-wyrdtarot"><strong> Wyrd Tarot: </strong> Double Score</summary><p class="t13ne-normal"><strong> Normal: </strong>Doubles all Scores (x2/x3) the Target generates (allowing more cards to be Drawn,
 etc) while atop the pile.</p><p class="t13ne-crossed"><strong> Crossed: </strong>Halves all Scores the Target generates whilst atop their pile.</p></details></div><div class="t13ne-yarn-fancy">
			<details><summary><strong>Yarn</strong> <br>The Cloak</summary>
			<span class="t13ne-description">The Cloak card is symbolic of the warmth and protection of a good coat, privileges of rank, and the hidden aspects of life. This can be those parts that are private, or actual secrets, perhaps even hidden passages, or secret tunnels. It is the card of secrets, thieves and cowards, as well as nobility, cunning, and superheroes.</span>
			<span class="t13ne-significator">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-significator"><strong>Significator</strong></summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-plot"><strong>Plot: </strong> The Cloak Plot is usually a Spy or Superhero Story like the Mask. Unlike the Mask The Cloak is about secrets, and privileges, not dual identities, so while the Characters' secret identities may be threatened, their real identity should not be directly involved.</aside>
					<aside class="t13ne-char"><strong>Character: </strong> The Cloak Significator indicates a Character who keeps their real self hidden. They Gain Yin when they hide or avoid conversation. They Gain Yang when they lie or tell a story. They Gain Chi when they ignore their Persona. They also gain +1 Success Level to any hide actions, or remaining hidden.</aside>
					<aside class="t13ne-scene"><strong>Scene: </strong> The Cloak Plot is usually a Spy or Superhero Story like the Mask. Unlike the Mask The Cloak is about secrets, and privileges, not dual identities, so while the Characters' secret identities may be threatened, their real identity should not be directly involved.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-scene">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-scene-type">
						<strong>Scene: </strong> Revelation
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">Revelations reveal something about the Conflict or Plot to the Characters. Typically a Revelation tells the Characters something about the Villain, or the Prize they are after. Revelations usually consist of 2-5 cards that define: What the information is about, what the actual info is, the vector the Revelation is revealed by, and per haps some Alternative Information that might be revealed (in case you can't use the Info), and a detail that is involved somehow.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-conflict-embodiment">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-conflict-embodiment">
						<strong>Conflict Embodiment: </strong> Internal
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">The Conflict embodies both sides within one Character. The Conflict will play out within them, between two aspects of the Character such as Incarna, Personality, Core, Hitches, Annexes, Proficiencies, or an internal Quest.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>

			<span class="t13ne-facet-embodiment">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-facet-embodiment">
						<strong>Facet Embodiment: </strong> Personality
					</summary>
					<section class="t13ne-description">The Conflict Embodies one Facet as a Character Personality. Often the most satisfying embodiment, but can lead to easy identification, and unsubtle Plots.</section>
				</details>
			</span>
			 <div class="t13ne-archetypes"><details><summary><strong>Archetype Character: </strong>Rogue</summary> <details class="t13ne-Persona"><summary><strong>Persona: </strong><span class="t13ne-output">Deviant</span>
      </summary>
      <details class="t13ne-motivation"><summary><strong>Motivation: </strong></summary><span class="t13ne-description">Deviants are motivated by being different from others. They like to see themselves as a lone wolf, or a trendsetter, never as a follower or leader. Deviants may have unique beliefs, or a code of practice that puts them at odds with the larger society around them, as is the case with many Cults or secret societies. Deviants may have goals, that they pursue by deception and manipulation, but are as often aimlessly reacting to what happens around them.</span></details>
       <details class="t13ne-avoid"><summary><strong>Avoid: </strong></summary><span class="t13ne-description">Deviants hate acting like everyone else, and dislike being predictable.</span></details>
       <details class="t13ne-gain-chi"><summary><strong>Gain Chi: </strong></summary><span class="t13ne-description"><span>Gain Chi when you act / react differently to everyone else, or when you deceive or surprise another.</span><p>Ordeal gain Chi when you play a ♥ to attack.</p></span></details></details>   <details class="t13ne-core"><summary><strong>Core: </strong><span class="t13ne-output">Master</span></summary><span class="t13ne-description">The character has one chosen environment in which they automatically gain one level of success.</span></details><details class="t13ne-hitch"><summary><strong>Hitch: </strong><span class="t13ne-output">Taboo</span></summary><span class="t13ne-description">Whether religious, superstitious or just dietary, Taboos are things your character just doesn't want to do</span></details><p class="t13ne-description">Rogues are scoundrels, who rarely attack in a fair fight. Ambushes and sniping tactics are more their Style, perhaps with a little smuggling, pickpocketing or burglary along the way.</p></details></div>
			 <span class="t13ne-hook">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-hook">
						<strong>Hook: </strong> Cloaked Threat
					</summary>
