					<aside class="t13ne-description">The Hook tells the Character directly of some looming, but secretive danger, rumours of a new mob boss, prototype fighter with incredible stats, or any mysterious stranger or player.</aside>
					<details class="t13ne-hook-aspect">
						<summary><strong>Hook Aspect: </strong>Hitch</summary>
						<section class="t13ne-description">The Hook targets one of the Character's Hitch. For example: In order to Hook Duty (Honour-bound) Hitch simply threaten the Character's honour.</section>
					</details>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-obstacle-fancy"><details class="t13ne-obstacle"><summary><strong>Obstacle:</strong>Hidden</summary><p class="t13ne-description">The way ahead is hidden or secret. Secret doors, hidden trapdoors, wall safes and the like, must be searched or spotted to find.</p></details></span>
			<span class="t13ne-gain">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-gain">
						<strong>Gain </strong> 
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">Secret Alliance (the creation of a secretive Pact Descendant)</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-fray">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-fray">
						<strong>Fray: </strong> Hide!
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">These Ordeals may involve hiding, or concealing something. The PCs may have to hide themselves from detection, or they may have to conceal something else, perhaps not letting the guards take the kid who just burst into the room and hid under the sofa. As such Hiding can be as much about bluffing as it is physical concealment, but is an Opposed Ordeal of almost any Stakes.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-snag">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-snag">
						<strong>Snag: </strong> No Witnesses
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">Whatever happened during the Fray, there is no one to bear witness to the result. This means the truth of what happened will be clouded by speculation and suspicions, mostly directed at the PCs. Alternatively, the Fray is complicated by the PCs having to insure they are not spotted, and that their involvement remains secret.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-sweeping">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-sweeping">
						<strong>Sweeping: </strong> Magic Cloak
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">The PCs may gain an actual cloak of invisibility, or more likely they gain a Revelation that they could not have got any other way, perhaps overhearing or intercepting a communication from the Opposed Embodiment.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
		 <details class="t13ne-revelation-fancy"><summary><strong>Revelation</strong></summary><details class="t13ne-about"><summary><strong>About: </strong> Pressed </summary><p class="t13ne-description">The information is about the Pressed Conflict Embodiment, which is often a PC in a game, and is usually the antagonist in a Story.</p></details><details class="t13ne-info"><summary><strong>Info: </strong> Cause </summary><p class="t13ne-description">Causes can be why people do things, or they can be the reasons certain things happen. It is always good to understand the reasons things are happening the way they are.</p></details><details class="t13ne-vector"><summary><strong>Vector: </strong> Obvious Lie </summary><p class="t13ne-description">Sometimes information includes something so preposterous that it is an obvious lie. Sometimes these are just that, other times not so much. Worse yet is when the obvious lie is actually concealing a subtle truth.</p></details><details class="t13ne-alternate"><summary><strong>Alternate: </strong> Imaginative </summary><p class="t13ne-description">There is actually very little information in this revelation, it is almost entirely a work of imagination. Of course, if its information about an Increated it may still be true.</p></details><details class="t13ne-detail"><summary><strong>Detail: </strong> Poetic </summary><p class="t13ne-description">A poetic detail is one that poets always include that might not be actually true, but has some spirit of the truth.</p></details></details>
		 <details class="t13ne-ordeal-fancy"><summary><strong>Ordeal</strong></summary><p class="t13ne-ordeal-type"><strong>Ordeal-Type </strong> — Opposed Ordeal</p><p class="t13ne-stages"><strong>Stages </strong> — 5</p><p class="t13ne-ordeal-spread"><strong>Ordeal-Spread </strong> — Block</p><p class="t13ne-stakes"><strong>Stakes </strong> — Blended Medium-High</p><p class="t13ne-test"><strong>Test </strong> — Regeneration (Phoenix)</p><p class="t13ne-obstacles"><strong>Obstacles </strong> — 1 per Stage and 2 per Court Stage</p><p class="t13ne-suggested-action"><strong>Suggested-Action </strong> — Dodge / Evade</p><details class="t13ne-stage"><summary><strong>Stage: </strong> Evade </summary><p class="t13ne-description">Evade or dodge something, come up with a story, a Blending in opportunity, or Medical emergency.</p></details><details class="t13ne-motional"><summary><strong>Motional: </strong> Winged Race </summary><p class="t13ne-description">If you can fly then you might stand a chance, if you aren't gifted with wings you are going to have to climb, swing, balance and jump.</p></details><details class="t13ne-fight"><summary><strong>Fight: </strong> Argument </summary><p class="t13ne-description">The fight is normally a Low or Soul Stakes argument between two Characters (at least Chorus Extra). The opponent may be a Monster of some sort.<!-- t13ne-shortcode "%7B%22type%22%3A%22facetsuitaspect%22%2C%22suit%22%3A%222%22%2C%22aspect%22%3A%22Monster%22%7D"// --></p><div class="t13ne-tablewrap"><table class="t13ne-table Monster"><thead><tr><th>&nbsp;&nbsp;Facet&nbsp;&nbsp;</th><th>&nbsp;&nbsp;Monster&nbsp;&nbsp;</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td>Enigma</td><td><strong>Skulker</strong> —The Monster is a shadowy figure pulling strings behind the scenes. This may just be an ordinary villain, but their ability to pass unnoticed should be bordering upon the unnatural. Perhaps they use psychic powers or magic to cloud the minds of witnesses, perhaps they are a hacking whiz, able to bounce phone calls through international relays with some multinational or another picking up the tab without realizing, perhaps they are one of the Huldra, the hidden people - who may be the Fæ or maybe they are actually the imaginary friend of one of the characters now made real(ish) by an alien or a Dæmon. Skulkers start with a Monster Pool of Enigma Score. They may add Monster Pool Size Reduced Success Levels to any stealthy or hidden actions or preparations. Skulkers may play Monster Pool cards as Preparation for anothers actions. E.g. The Hacker can prepare a Hack attack, that is performed by a Church Website in another town. A mind-bending villain could prepare a Psyche Defence in the mind of his puppet.</td></tr><tr><td>Heresy</td><td><strong>Masker</strong> —The Monster is looks entirely like something else. Perhaps they are a Demon in the form of an innocent child, or maybe they are a shape-shifter or face-dancer, able to change their appearance as they like, then again perhaps they're just the old Janitor in a monster costume. The Masker may create a Heresy Score sized Monster Pool whenever they hide completely for Heresy Boon as Chi Extend Duration, or are believed in their lies. The Monster Pool adds its Size Reduced to any Score to do with hiding, maintaining the mask, or lying. The Masker must play at least one card from their Monster Pool any time they wish to change their Mask or appearance.</td></tr><tr><td>Jeer</td><td><strong>Mockery</strong> —The monster is a mockery — It might be illusionary, or Undead (usually a corpse animated by a low-level Increated), but it may be a ghost, a machine or even an animal that mocks something else. The Mockery is created with a Monster Pool of Jeer Score cards, that must be played to maintain the Mockery (when disbelieved, attacked, etc). While the Monster Pool exists the Monster can pull and play Monster Pool Size Reduced cards (initially Jeer Draw)for any action the Mockery form takes that it has no suitable Annexes for. E.g. A Skeleton Mockery might have a "Mortality Reminder" Annex, but is given a Monster Pool of 5, so it can initially pull and play 2 cards for its mocking actions (like moving around and clawing the living). However, once attacked (or disbelieved) the Skeleton must spend at least one card from the Mockery Pool, thereafter it can only draw and Play 1 card for any Action that doesn't involve reminding someone of their own Mortality.</td></tr><tr><td>Liberty</td><td><strong>Nightmare</strong> —The Monster is nightmarish, or dreamlike. Such Monsters either exist in a purely psychological realm, appearing as dreams and hallucinations, that cannot really be fought and don’t physically exist, or they are extremely dangerous and powerful Monsters that really exist and may be magical or supernatural in power. Nightmares may add Liberty Draw cards to their Monster Pool if they cross a threshold, liminal space or are dreamed about. While they have cards in their Monster Pool they may add Liberty Boon Dice to everything they do. The Nightmare must spend at least one card from their Monster Pool when they do something impossible. They might use it to fly through the air, instantly teleport, let bullets pass through themselves, or appear to be a real thing and not a dream. Flower-faced Humanoids and Canines from a nightmare realm, handsome soap-sellers with an odd idea for gentlemen’s clubs, facially-burned, gardening-gloved janitors turned serial killers, six foot tall invisible rabbits, night hags, shadow beings seen when falling asleep, and any character in a dream, are all good examples of the Liberty Monster.</td></tr><tr><td>Nature</td><td><strong>Elemental</strong> —The Monster is an elemental force of nature, perhaps a literal elemental or more likely metaphorical. The Elemental will add Nature Draw cards to its Monster Pool when it encounters a natural source of its element, or when it causes collateral damage to a new location. Elementals gain +1 Score per Monster Pool card to any action to do with their natural element. The Elemental must spend cards from its Monster Pool on Physical, Energy or Magical attacks based on its Element. Salamanders, Sylphs, Gnomes, and Undines, Giants (of most types), Storms as well as Tsunamis, landslides, pyroclastic flows and even blizzards (most of which might be Giants anyway).</td></tr><tr><td>Phoenix</td><td><strong>Paragon</strong> —The Monster is the embodiment of a positive force or emotion. Such Monsters are often a counter point, or are opposed to, another Monster. Phoenix Monsters are usually capable of causing positive (and sometimes negative) emotions via a Psychology Test / Psyche Attack in those that see them, and while they may be positive in nature, they are not necessarily good, or altruistic in their actions. The Paragon may have a Monster Pool of cards equal to their Phoenix Score and add cards from the Pool to any action they wish. The Paragon can discard half of their Monster Pool and multiply their Score by 1+ their Phoenix Dice. The Paragon's Monster Pool is replenished in full if the Paragon can find an external example of their chosen emotion. The Phoenix itself is the embodiment of Resurrection, Hope and Faith, but is quite capable of burning destruction. The Golden Goose is another example, the embodiment of charity, but it evokes profound avarice in those who would own it. The unicorn is a symbol of purity, but is dangerous in the extreme to those it considers impure. Depictions of Angels are also perfect examples of positive embodiments.</td></tr></tbody><tfoot><tr><td>&nbsp;&nbsp;Facet&nbsp;&nbsp;</td><td>&nbsp;&nbsp;Monster&nbsp;&nbsp;</td></tr></tfoot></table></div><p></p></details></details>
		 </details>
			</div>
				<div class="t13ne-card-lea"><details class="t13ne-lea"><summary><strong>Lea</strong></summary><p class="t13ne-colour"><strong>Path Colour —</strong> Blue Aquatic or Black Migratory</p><p class="t13ne-bulmas"><strong>Allowed Bulmäs —</strong> All</p><p class="t13ne-animal-category"><strong>Animal Category —</strong> Black paths and Migratory</p><p class="t13ne-animal-example"><strong>Example Animal —</strong> Native Form / Dragon / Swarm</p><p class="t13ne-notes"><strong>Notes —</strong> These paths open and close naturally through the Lea from time to time, they are easily walked by all Bulmäs, but Vermis and All-Walkers may walk them in Swarm or True Dragon configurations.</p></details></div></div><div class="t13ne-cardwrap-jsvg" id="cardrewritebox25" data-mode="jsvg" data-wyrd="1" data-yarn="1"><img src="https://www.cjmoseley.dev.cc/wp-content/plugins/t13ne/images/cards-svg/KH.svg" class="t13ne-svgcard"><div class="t13ne-svgpips"><div class="t13ne-textpips">13<!-- write suit--><span class="t13ne-symbol"><span class="t13ne-redcard">♥</span></span><details class="t13ne-suit"><summary></summary><p class="t13ne-name"><strong>Suit: </strong>Hearts <span class="t13ne-redcard">♥</span></p><p><strong>Ordeal Trump: </strong><span class="t13ne-trump">Healing</span></p><p class="t13ne-desc"><strong>Description: </strong>Hearts represent Love, Family and the comforts of home. In the Tarot the suit is called Cups, and represents the feminine and elemental Water.</p><p class="t13ne-tarot"><strong>Tarot Meaning: </strong>Love, Relationships, Affections, Family, Sympathy, Peace.</p><p class="t13ne-otext"><strong>Ordeal Text: </strong>Ordeal Types: Nurturing, Farming, Relationships, Medical. Hearts act as Trumps for the following Actions: Blending in, Cooking, Escaping, Healing, Hiding, Hunting, Insulting, Joke-telling, Lying, Mimicry, Riddling, Spinning a tale, Surgery, and Survivalism. Any type of Wound can be healed with a Heart.</p><p class="t13ne-parry"><strong>Parry: </strong><strong>Locked Respite</strong> All unmatched Cards from the original attack are Discarded by the Defender, however with each card they Discard they may either use the card to Heal/Stabilise one of their own wounds (or damage to the Parrying Descendant), or use the card to slow their with additional RT (Pips) or GRT (Pips/5).</p><p class="t13ne-soak"><strong>Soak: </strong><strong>Disengage:</strong> The Soak Card acts as a Heal card, and is discarded along with the Attack card. This immediately Heals all Wounds in play that have less Pips, including the remaining Wounds in the Attack, by one Wound Level (discards Distracts). </p></details></div></div><div class="t13ne-svg-facet"><div>Phoenix</div></div><div class="t13ne-wound"><div><strong class="t13ne-woundname">Mortal</strong><details><summary></summary><strong>Mortal</strong><p class="t13ne-base-pips">Base Pips to summon: 16</p><p class="t13ne-min-pips">Minimum Pips Required: 12</p><p class="t13ne-desc">The Target is now dying from the wound.</p><details class="t13ne-roll"><summary>Rules</summary>Create a "Dying / Broken / Burning" quirk Hitch that slowly grows +1 Hitch Boon per Miasma Boon as Chi Limit Duration) until healed. If no Hitch can be increased by the Mortal Wound then decrease an appropriate Facet (Hitch, Incarna, Persona or Core usually) by a Boon equal to the Wounded Character’s Miasma Score. I-Ching, Personality, Proficiency slots and Annexes affected should be recalculated. If the Hitch becomes a Flaw the Wound will automatically upgrade to a Carnage Wound.</details><details class="t13ne-ordeal"><summary>Ordeal Rules</summary>Treat as though the Personality Annex or Master Annex has been Crippled, until the end of the Round. Then add a new "Dying / Broken / Burning" quirk Hitch with a Boon of Miasma Score (to a Max of Half the Incarna Boon). Or increase a quirk Hitch by 1 Boon at the end of each Round.</details><details class="t13ne-psych"><summary>Psych Wounds</summary><details class="t13ne-negative"><summary>Negative Psych</summary><p class="t13ne-normal"><strong>Normal</strong>As Normal but does not create a "Dying" Hitch, but instead a Scars (Mental/Emotional) / Fear / Fatigued / Lament / Taboo / Offensive / Doubt,  etc Hitch.  Plus Consciousness is usually lost until Stabilised.</p><p class="t13ne-extreme"><strong>Extreme</strong>Creates any normal Hitch type. At Yarn-Teller's discretion Mortal Wounds can also alter the Persona, or Core Facet choice (Ref arbitrates). Plus Consciousness is usually lost until healed.</p></details><details class="t13ne-positive"><summary>Positive Psych</summary><p class="t13ne-normal"><strong>Normal</strong>As Normal but creates a Scars (Mental/Emotional) / Duty / Dependance / Paranoia / Devotion / Madness / Vague, etc quirk Hitch.  Plus Consciousness is usually lost until Stabilised. May Gain a new Talent Annex / Descendant or Resolve a quirk Hitch.</p><p class="t13ne-extreme"><strong>Extreme</strong>May create any Flaw Hitch. At Yarn-Teller's discretion Mortal Wounds can also alter the wounded character’s Persona, or Core Facet choices. Consciousness is usually lost until healed. Gains a new Talent Annex / Descendant or Resolves a quirk Handicap.</p></details></details></details></div></div><div class="t13ne-tarot-svg"><details><summary class="t13ne-wyrdtarot"><strong> Wyrd Tarot: </strong> Heal Wounds</summary><p class="t13ne-normal"><strong> Normal: </strong>+2 Success Levels to all attempts to treat Wounds on or by the Target while atop the pile.</p><p class="t13ne-crossed"><strong> Crossed: </strong>-2 Success Levels to all attempts to treat Heal Wounds on or by the Target while atop the pile.</p></details></div><div class="t13ne-yarn-fancy">
			<details><summary><strong>Yarn</strong> <br>The Physician</summary>
			<span class="t13ne-description">The Physician card is representative of medicine, healing and bad news. If a character is injured, or sick they can seek relief and respite through the Physician. If they aren't then the bad news could be simply that the Apple-a-day isn't actually medical insurance, but more likely there is a new Hitch on some character's horizons.</span>
			<span class="t13ne-significator">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-significator"><strong>Significator</strong></summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-plot"><strong>Plot: </strong> The Physician Plot is all about a medical situation, common to all medical dramas. It may be that the Plot centres on a disease, or plague that must be contained, and treated. Or the Plot may be about a specific Physician, perhaps new to a practice, or who has decidedly unorthodox practices.</aside>
					<aside class="t13ne-char"><strong>Character: </strong> The Physician Significator indicates a Character who cares deeply about the well-being of others. They Gain Yin when they rest, read or examine something or someone. They Gain Yang when they ask questions, make observations, or write notes. The Gain Chi when they solve a problem, heal a Wound, or reduce a Hitch. They gain +1 Success Levels to all attempts to Heal Wounds or reduce Hitches.</aside>
					<aside class="t13ne-scene"><strong>Scene: </strong> The Physician Plot is all about a medical situation, common to all medical dramas. It may be that the Plot centres on a disease, or plague that must be contained, and treated. Or the Plot may be about a specific Physician, perhaps new to a practice, or who has decidedly unorthodox practices.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-scene">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-scene-type">
						<strong>Scene: </strong> Completion
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">How the Story Completes and Resolves. depending on how the Characters do the Plot may be destroyed, demoted, or promoted.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-conflict-embodiment">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-conflict-embodiment">
						<strong>Conflict Embodiment: </strong> Internal
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">The Conflict embodies both sides within one Character. The Conflict will play out within them, between two aspects of the Character such as Incarna, Personality, Core, Hitches, Annexes, Proficiencies, or an internal Quest.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>

			<span class="t13ne-facet-embodiment">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-facet-embodiment">
						<strong>Facet Embodiment: </strong> Monster
					</summary>
					<section class="t13ne-description">The Conflict Embodies one Facet as a Character Monster Facet.This Monster might be a Dragon, an Orc Horde, or just a human Villain. Monsters can be great fun, but are rarely subtle. Where possible when using Monster embodiments, the Plot may want to keep the exact nature of the Monster a secret for as long as it can.</section>
				</details>
			</span>
			 <div class="t13ne-archetypes"><details><summary><strong>Archetype Character: </strong>Medic</summary> <details class="t13ne-Persona"><summary><strong>Persona: </strong><span class="t13ne-output">Healer</span>
      </summary>
      <details class="t13ne-motivation"><summary><strong>Motivation: </strong></summary><span class="t13ne-description">The Healer is motivated to help those who are in pain. They will try to relieve pain, heal wounds, and treat injuries to the best of their ability and knowledge.</span></details>
       <details class="t13ne-avoid"><summary><strong>Avoid: </strong></summary><span class="t13ne-description">All Healers try to avoid harming their patients any more than is necessary to cure or treat the affliction, this especially extends to not breaking the patient's confidences. Their patient's secrets are the Healer's to keep.</span></details>
       <details class="t13ne-gain-chi"><summary><strong>Gain Chi: </strong></summary><span class="t13ne-description"><span>Gain Chi when you give solace, aid, comfort, healing, treatment, advice or medicine to someone in physical, metaphysical, or metaphorical pain. </span><p>Ordeal gain Chi when you play a ♥ to heal.</p></span></details></details>   <details class="t13ne-core"><summary><strong>Core: </strong><span class="t13ne-output">Barber</span></summary><span class="t13ne-description">The character can move Wounds from one Character or Descendant to another with a cost equal to the Pips in Chi. This cost can be lowered by also moving a Wound the other way.</span></details><details class="t13ne-hitch"><summary><strong>Hitch: </strong><span class="t13ne-output">Lament</span></summary><span class="t13ne-description">When something has been lost, or forgotten, or you never had what everyone else has. It is the Hitch of Orphans and Widows, of Lost Loves and Haunting Memories</span></details><p class="t13ne-description">The Medic is a healer, a surgeon, or a doctor. They seek to treat and heal injuries and disease wherever they find it.</p></details></div>
			 <span class="t13ne-hook">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-hook">
						<strong>Hook: </strong> Bad News
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">The Hook just consists of terrible bad news, often medical in nature, such as an incurable (or at least perilous) disease diagnosis.</aside>
					<details class="t13ne-hook-aspect">
						<summary><strong>Hook Aspect: </strong>Hitch</summary>
						<section class="t13ne-description">The Hook targets one of the Character's Hitches. For example: In order to Hook a Arachnophobe Character, one should simply have to threaten them with a spider, or better yet, make the bad guy into a spider and have word of them reach the PC.</section>
					</details>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-obstacle-fancy"><details class="t13ne-obstacle"><summary><strong>Obstacle:</strong>Medical</summary><p class="t13ne-description">The way is blocked by something medical, it may be a scan or fitness test, or a contagious disease, or anaesthetic gas may block the way.</p></details></span>
			<span class="t13ne-gain">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-gain">
						<strong>Gain </strong> 
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">Savant — Powerful Ally (Demon/Solo/Fae/Grand-Mercari)</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-fray">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-fray">
						<strong>Fray: </strong> Medical Situation
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">This is a medical situation, maybe the PCs need Surgery (or to conduct it), perhaps they need to deal with a large scale accident, disaster, or plague.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-snag">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-snag">
						<strong>Snag: </strong> Medical Ethics
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">The Fray is complicated by a question of medical ethics, there are four main forms, Autonomy (the right to refuse treatment), Beneficence (acting in the best interest of the patient), Non-maleficence (Do no harm), and Justice (insuring the most worthy get the necessary help). This may be as simple as rich client verses poor client or as complex as the cure being as bad as the disease.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-sweeping">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-sweeping">
						<strong>Sweeping: </strong> Medical Attention
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">The PCs get all the healing they can take, often plus some recovery time. This sort of intense medical attention can usually reduce, remove or resolve quirk and even Flaw Hitches, it might reduce a Woe, but can rarely remove it.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
		 <details class="t13ne-revelation-fancy"><summary><strong>Revelation</strong></summary><details class="t13ne-about"><summary><strong>About: </strong> Pressed </summary><p class="t13ne-description">The information is about the Pressed Conflict Embodiment, which is often a PC in a game, and is usually the antagonist in a Story.</p></details><details class="t13ne-info"><summary><strong>Info: </strong> How? </summary><p class="t13ne-description">How exactly is what they plan to do, going to be done? You may get the full plan or just the important pieces. You might get How to stop it, but not what they do. It all depends on the situation at hand.</p></details><details class="t13ne-vector"><summary><strong>Vector: </strong> Phantasm </summary><p class="t13ne-description">The information is conveyed by some supernatural means, often a ghost or spirit that appears and brings word of importance (often their own passing). If you are not comfortable with a ghost in your cyberpunk universe, you can always use a Cyberwraith, a digital soul, or transhuman. You could even use a holographic image, hallucination, or telepathy helmet.</p></details><details class="t13ne-alternate"><summary><strong>Alternate: </strong> Violence </summary><p class="t13ne-description">Acts of violence are something that people tend to discuss, whether it is the violence that they did, or that was done to them (often both). It is the reputation of a warlord, the CV of an assassin, and the dark secret of a cleric or medic.</p></details><details class="t13ne-detail"><summary><strong>Detail: </strong> Cunning </summary><p class="t13ne-description">Cunning details are the carefully hidden touches of a design, secrets, and of course the tales of actual cunning that people tell about a Character.</p></details></details>
		 <details class="t13ne-ordeal-fancy"><summary><strong>Ordeal</strong></summary><p class="t13ne-ordeal-type"><strong>Ordeal-Type </strong> — Opposed Ordeal</p><p class="t13ne-stages"><strong>Stages </strong> — 6</p><p class="t13ne-ordeal-spread"><strong>Ordeal-Spread </strong> — Map-Hex</p><p class="t13ne-stakes"><strong>Stakes </strong> — Blended Medium-High-Extreme</p><p class="t13ne-test"><strong>Test </strong> — Occult Lore (Enigma-Heresy)</p><p class="t13ne-obstacles"><strong>Obstacles </strong> — 1 per Stage and 2 per Court Stage</p><p class="t13ne-suggested-action"><strong>Suggested-Action </strong> — Play</p><details class="t13ne-stage"><summary><strong>Stage: </strong> Skip ahead </summary><p class="t13ne-description">A short-cut, chance to skip ahead, or Lucky Break</p></details><details class="t13ne-motional"><summary><strong>Motional: </strong> Dangerous Foot Race </summary><p class="t13ne-description">First survive the Stages, then worry about if anyone made it to the end before you.</p></details><details class="t13ne-fight"><summary><strong>Fight: </strong> Honour Duel </summary><p class="t13ne-description">The Fight is a Duel, usually between two unbalanced individuals (one will be of higher power level than the other). Rules are formal, and normally observed at least by the more powerful or higher ranked opponent. Opponent may be a Monster. <!-- t13ne-shortcode "%7B%22type%22%3A%22facetsuitaspect%22%2C%22suit%22%3A%222%22%2C%22aspect%22%3A%22Monster%22%7D"// --></p><div class="t13ne-tablewrap"><table class="t13ne-table Monster"><thead><tr><th>&nbsp;&nbsp;Facet&nbsp;&nbsp;</th><th>&nbsp;&nbsp;Monster&nbsp;&nbsp;</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td>Enigma</td><td><strong>Skulker</strong> —The Monster is a shadowy figure pulling strings behind the scenes. This may just be an ordinary villain, but their ability to pass unnoticed should be bordering upon the unnatural. Perhaps they use psychic powers or magic to cloud the minds of witnesses, perhaps they are a hacking whiz, able to bounce phone calls through international relays with some multinational or another picking up the tab without realizing, perhaps they are one of the Huldra, the hidden people - who may be the Fæ or maybe they are actually the imaginary friend of one of the characters now made real(ish) by an alien or a Dæmon. Skulkers start with a Monster Pool of Enigma Score. They may add Monster Pool Size Reduced Success Levels to any stealthy or hidden actions or preparations. Skulkers may play Monster Pool cards as Preparation for anothers actions. E.g. The Hacker can prepare a Hack attack, that is performed by a Church Website in another town. A mind-bending villain could prepare a Psyche Defence in the mind of his puppet.</td></tr><tr><td>Heresy</td><td><strong>Masker</strong> —The Monster is looks entirely like something else. Perhaps they are a Demon in the form of an innocent child, or maybe they are a shape-shifter or face-dancer, able to change their appearance as they like, then again perhaps they're just the old Janitor in a monster costume. The Masker may create a Heresy Score sized Monster Pool whenever they hide completely for Heresy Boon as Chi Extend Duration, or are believed in their lies. The Monster Pool adds its Size Reduced to any Score to do with hiding, maintaining the mask, or lying. The Masker must play at least one card from their Monster Pool any time they wish to change their Mask or appearance.</td></tr><tr><td>Jeer</td><td><strong>Mockery</strong> —The monster is a mockery — It might be illusionary, or Undead (usually a corpse animated by a low-level Increated), but it may be a ghost, a machine or even an animal that mocks something else. The Mockery is created with a Monster Pool of Jeer Score cards, that must be played to maintain the Mockery (when disbelieved, attacked, etc). While the Monster Pool exists the Monster can pull and play Monster Pool Size Reduced cards (initially Jeer Draw)for any action the Mockery form takes that it has no suitable Annexes for. E.g. A Skeleton Mockery might have a "Mortality Reminder" Annex, but is given a Monster Pool of 5, so it can initially pull and play 2 cards for its mocking actions (like moving around and clawing the living). However, once attacked (or disbelieved) the Skeleton must spend at least one card from the Mockery Pool, thereafter it can only draw and Play 1 card for any Action that doesn't involve reminding someone of their own Mortality.</td></tr><tr><td>Liberty</td><td><strong>Nightmare</strong> —The Monster is nightmarish, or dreamlike. Such Monsters either exist in a purely psychological realm, appearing as dreams and hallucinations, that cannot really be fought and don’t physically exist, or they are extremely dangerous and powerful Monsters that really exist and may be magical or supernatural in power. Nightmares may add Liberty Draw cards to their Monster Pool if they cross a threshold, liminal space or are dreamed about. While they have cards in their Monster Pool they may add Liberty Boon Dice to everything they do. The Nightmare must spend at least one card from their Monster Pool when they do something impossible. They might use it to fly through the air, instantly teleport, let bullets pass through themselves, or appear to be a real thing and not a dream. Flower-faced Humanoids and Canines from a nightmare realm, handsome soap-sellers with an odd idea for gentlemen’s clubs, facially-burned, gardening-gloved janitors turned serial killers, six foot tall invisible rabbits, night hags, shadow beings seen when falling asleep, and any character in a dream, are all good examples of the Liberty Monster.</td></tr><tr><td>Nature</td><td><strong>Elemental</strong> —The Monster is an elemental force of nature, perhaps a literal elemental or more likely metaphorical. The Elemental will add Nature Draw cards to its Monster Pool when it encounters a natural source of its element, or when it causes collateral damage to a new location. Elementals gain +1 Score per Monster Pool card to any action to do with their natural element. The Elemental must spend cards from its Monster Pool on Physical, Energy or Magical attacks based on its Element. Salamanders, Sylphs, Gnomes, and Undines, Giants (of most types), Storms as well as Tsunamis, landslides, pyroclastic flows and even blizzards (most of which might be Giants anyway).</td></tr><tr><td>Phoenix</td><td><strong>Paragon</strong> —The Monster is the embodiment of a positive force or emotion. Such Monsters are often a counter point, or are opposed to, another Monster. Phoenix Monsters are usually capable of causing positive (and sometimes negative) emotions via a Psychology Test / Psyche Attack in those that see them, and while they may be positive in nature, they are not necessarily good, or altruistic in their actions. The Paragon may have a Monster Pool of cards equal to their Phoenix Score and add cards from the Pool to any action they wish. The Paragon can discard half of their Monster Pool and multiply their Score by 1+ their Phoenix Dice. The Paragon's Monster Pool is replenished in full if the Paragon can find an external example of their chosen emotion. The Phoenix itself is the embodiment of Resurrection, Hope and Faith, but is quite capable of burning destruction. The Golden Goose is another example, the embodiment of charity, but it evokes profound avarice in those who would own it. The unicorn is a symbol of purity, but is dangerous in the extreme to those it considers impure. Depictions of Angels are also perfect examples of positive embodiments.</td></tr></tbody><tfoot><tr><td>&nbsp;&nbsp;Facet&nbsp;&nbsp;</td><td>&nbsp;&nbsp;Monster&nbsp;&nbsp;</td></tr></tfoot></table></div><p></p></details></details>
		 </details>
			</div>
				<div class="t13ne-card-lea"><details class="t13ne-lea"><summary><strong>Lea</strong></summary><p class="t13ne-colour"><strong>Path Colour —</strong> Blue Aquatic or Black Migratory</p><p class="t13ne-bulmas"><strong>Allowed Bulmäs —</strong> All</p><p class="t13ne-animal-category"><strong>Animal Category —</strong> Labyrinth</p><p class="t13ne-animal-example"><strong>Example Animal —</strong> Labrys patterned Minotaur</p><p class="t13ne-notes"><strong>Notes —</strong> Kings represent paths in the Labyrinth, and Cæster in the centre as well. A normal Bulmäs that tries to walk the Labyrinth would be lost, and would then be recruited into the Labyrinth. The Labyrinth can connect any point in space and time to any other, allowing Bulmäs to consciously time-travel through the Lea.  A King can allow a Bulmäs to create or reload a Chronolith or side-step depending on the power-level of the Bulmäs.</p></details></div></div><div class="t13ne-cardwrap-jsvg" id="cardrewritebox26" data-mode="jsvg" data-wyrd="1" data-yarn="1"><img src="https://www.cjmoseley.dev.cc/wp-content/plugins/t13ne/images/cards-svg/AC.svg" class="t13ne-svgcard"><div class="t13ne-svgpips"><div class="t13ne-textpips">14<!-- write suit--><span class="t13ne-symbol"><span class="t13ne-blackcard">♣</span></span><details class="t13ne-suit"><summary></summary><p class="t13ne-name"><strong>Suit: </strong>Clubs <span class="t13ne-blackcard">♣</span></p><p><strong>Ordeal Trump: </strong><span class="t13ne-trump">Defence</span></p><p class="t13ne-desc"><strong>Description: </strong>Clubs represent the power of politics and a stoic materialism. In the Tarot the Clubs are called Staves or Wands, in some traditions they represent Intellectual Air, but in most they are Fire.</p><p class="t13ne-tarot"><strong>Tarot Meaning: </strong>Power, Fame, Politics, Ability, Money.</p><p class="t13ne-otext"><strong>Ordeal Text: </strong>Ordeal Types: Wealth, Politics, Power. Clubs act as Trump for following Actions: Bartering, Controlling, Corrupting, Emotional-intensity, Guarding, Magic, Displaying might, Politics, Self-Defence, Socializing, and Trade Negotiations. Clubs can be used to defend any type of attack.</p><p class="t13ne-parry"><strong>Parry: </strong><strong>Disarm:</strong> All the cards of the attack are immediately discarded, along with the Parry Card. The remaining cards of the Defender's Parry can now each be used to cause the Attacker to drop a Descendant (starting with the attacking weapon usually), can also cut armour straps, belts etc, or steal hats. This basically adds RT equal to the card Pips to the Annex that was used to attack. Every 5 Pips also creates a point of GRT for the Character (representing how much time the Character must spend picking up, or readying the weapon again. Court cards additionally may damage the Descendant that was disarmed, as standard for the Stakes.</p><p class="t13ne-soak"><strong>Soak: </strong><strong>Ward:</strong> The matched Attack card, and one other randomly selected Attack card are discarded.</p></details></div></div><div class="t13ne-svg-facet"><div>Burden, Dominion, Fury, Rook, Sin, Wyrd</div></div><div class="t13ne-wound"><div><strong class="t13ne-woundname">Carnage</strong><details><summary></summary><strong>Carnage</strong><p class="t13ne-base-pips">Base Pips to summon: 21</p><p class="t13ne-min-pips">Minimum Pips Required: 14</p><p class="t13ne-desc">The Target should be dead, all exploded or turned to stone, or something. Sometimes though, they keep on going... for a while. And just occasionally, they get better.</p><details class="t13ne-roll"><summary>Rules</summary>Create a "Dead/Destroyed" Flaw Hitch, or increase the Boon of any Flaw Hitch by 1 Boon per Miasma Boon treated as Yarn Limit Duration outside of Ordeals) until healed. May also alter the target’s Incarna Facet choice (turning to stone, etc). Alternatively Decrease Character Scale by 1 or remove a Character's access to the Wounded Alternate.</details><details class="t13ne-ordeal"><summary>Ordeal Rules</summary>Immediately Treat Personality / Master Descendant as Wounded. At the end of the Round either create a new "Dead / Destroyed" Flaw Hitch (Half the Incarna Boon + Miasma Score) or increase a Flaw Hitch by Miasma Score. Alternatively Decrease Scale by 1, or alter Incarna / Persona / Core choices. If the Hitch Boon rises above Incarna Boon the Wound increases to Carnage+</details><details class="t13ne-psych"><summary>Psych Wounds</summary><details class="t13ne-negative"><summary>Negative Psych</summary><p class="t13ne-normal"><strong>Normal</strong>Can create a Flaw Hitch of any Type, as the Ref decides. Consciousness is always lost until the wound is Stabilised. Note may remove a Character's access to the Wounded Alternate.</p><p class="t13ne-extreme"><strong>Extreme</strong>Creates Maximised Flaw Hitches of any type but favours... Fear, Madness, Vague (Amnesia), Paranoia, Loss of Sense (e.g. Hysterical Blindness) or Taboo Hitches. Consciousness is always lost until Healed and may remove a Character's access to the Wounded Alternate. Can create a new Split Alternate.</p></details><details class="t13ne-positive"><summary>Positive Psych</summary><p class="t13ne-normal"><strong>Normal</strong>May Gain a new Power Annex / Descendant. Can Create a Flaw Hitch of any type, Consciousness is always lost until Stabilised, and access to the Alternate may be lost.</p><p class="t13ne-extreme"><strong>Extreme</strong> May create or Resolve Flaw Hitches of any type but favours... Duty, Dependance, Madness, Vague (Amnesia), Paranoia, Loss of Sense (e.g. Hysterical Blindness) or Taboo Hitches. Consciousness is always lost until Healed. Either creates a new Alternate, Intergrates an Alternate one Step toward Coalesce, or Gain a new Power Annex / Descendant.</p></details></details></details></div></div><div class="t13ne-tarot-svg"><details><summary class="t13ne-wyrdtarot"><strong> Wyrd Tarot: </strong> Trap</summary><p class="t13ne-normal"><strong> Normal: </strong>+2 Success Levels to all Actions to do with setting, designing and causing damage from traps (Snare Attacks).</p><p class="t13ne-crossed"><strong> Crossed: </strong>-2 success levels to all Actions to do with disarming, avoiding, and soaking damage from traps.</p></details></div><div class="t13ne-yarn-fancy">
			<details><summary><strong>Yarn</strong> <br>The Court</summary>
			<span class="t13ne-description">The Court card represents both the Courts of Law and the Legal System, and Royal Courts and Parliaments. It can represent legal and political situations, but also social judgements too.</span>
			<span class="t13ne-significator">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-significator"><strong>Significator</strong></summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-plot"><strong>Plot: </strong> The Court Plot is usually a Legal affair, often a Court case, although it can be political intrigues or social in nature as required. The Plot usually results in one Hooked Character accusing another of something (a crime, an affair, an insult). They must then struggle to either prove their innocence, defy the Court, or accept or change some aspect of themselves.</aside>
					<aside class="t13ne-char"><strong>Character: </strong> The Court Significator indicates a Character who has some authority. They Gain Yin when they work for the good of society. Gain Yang when they support someone else with authority. They Gain Chi when they direct others behaviour, either by speech, writing, or example. They gain +1 Success Level when at least three people acknowledge their authority.</aside>
					<aside class="t13ne-scene"><strong>Scene: </strong> The Court Plot is usually a Legal affair, often a Court case, although it can be political intrigues or social in nature as required. The Plot usually results in one Hooked Character accusing another of something (a crime, an affair, an insult). They must then struggle to either prove their innocence, defy the Court, or accept or change some aspect of themselves.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-scene">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-scene-type">
						<strong>Scene: </strong> Hook
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">Hooks hook a character into a Conflict. A Hook offers a Character (or Group) a reason to get involved in the Story. This lets the Plot use the Conflict Facet Boon/Value of the Character to help build their Conflict Annex.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-conflict-embodiment">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-conflict-embodiment">
						<strong>Conflict Embodiment: </strong> Tension
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">The Conflict embodies within two Characters, but they do not come into direct Conflict. They have some relationship, that Embodies the Conflict as arguments, discussions, jokes, etc.  common to Romance and Buddy movies.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>

			<span class="t13ne-facet-embodiment">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-facet-embodiment">
						<strong>Facet Embodiment: </strong> Incarna
					</summary>
					<section class="t13ne-description">The Conflict Embodies one Facet as a Character or Descendant Incarna. Often the easiest Embodiment to create Fantastical Characters, but not often suitable to all Genres and Conflicts. Also this tends to to make for a very obvious Conflict.</section>
				</details>
			</span>
			 <div class="t13ne-archetypes"><details><summary><strong>Archetype Character: </strong>Arbiter</summary> <details class="t13ne-Persona"><summary><strong>Persona: </strong><span class="t13ne-output">Judge</span>
      </summary>
      <details class="t13ne-motivation"><summary><strong>Motivation: </strong></summary><span class="t13ne-description">A Judge is driven by a strong sense of fairness and justice. A Judge may feel that some people should be rewarded, for their deeds, or less commonly a Judge may punish those who they feel deserve it. Judges may be legal professional, or more often is just a member of society who has strong opinions about how others should behave.</span></details>
       <details class="t13ne-avoid"><summary><strong>Avoid: </strong></summary><span class="t13ne-description">A Judge will always try to avoid being judged unfair. They aren't keen on being judged themselves, but have an almost unnatural need to be seen as fair, or at least consistent, in their dealings. Most Judges will also avoid hypocrisy, if they can, as they see it as an extension of unfairness.</span></details>
       <details class="t13ne-gain-chi"><summary><strong>Gain Chi: </strong></summary><span class="t13ne-description"><span>Gain Chi when you ask questions, pass judgement on someone, or something, or when you examine evidence.</span><p>Ordeal gain Chi when you set a Trap or Snare or Prepare a ♣</p></span></details></details>   <details class="t13ne-core"><summary><strong>Core: </strong><span class="t13ne-output">Dominator</span></summary><span class="t13ne-description">Gain Chi when you give orders, exert authority or power.</span></details><details class="t13ne-hitch"><summary><strong>Hitch: </strong><span class="t13ne-output">Vulnerable</span></summary><span class="t13ne-description">Perhaps they are flammable or easily startled, they might have an allergy, or similar weakness.</span></details><p class="t13ne-description">The Arbiter is a judge, referee, lawyer, magistrate or noble, they specialise in listening to arguments and making decisions based on what they've heard. Arbiters interpret emotions, intuitions, law, logic, science, precedent and rhetoric as required by the rules they arbitrate, to decide who wins an argument they sit in judgement of.</p></details></div>
			 <span class="t13ne-hook">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-hook">
						<strong>Hook: </strong> Arrested
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">The Story opens with the PCs captured, arrested or detained. It may be a case of mistaken identity, or they could be suspects, or maybe they did break a law or two on the way. This is perhaps justice weighing in on their past misdeeds. Sometimes there may be a bit of a Chase or Escape attempt before they are arrested, in fact a whole Epic can arise from being outlaws.</aside>
					<details class="t13ne-hook-aspect">
						<summary><strong>Hook Aspect: </strong>I-Ching</summary>
						<section class="t13ne-description">The Hook targets the Character's I-Ching. For example: If a Character has the Cauldron (50) I-Ching, then the Hook needs to have someone hungry turn up asking for help.</section>
					</details>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-obstacle-fancy"><details class="t13ne-obstacle"><summary><strong>Obstacle:</strong>Legal</summary><p class="t13ne-description">The way ahead is blocked by some legal obstacle (such as a one way street, crime scene tape, lack of warrant, etc). This must be "lawyered" and negotiated around, or there will be legal repercussions.</p></details></span>
			<span class="t13ne-gain">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-gain">
						<strong>Gain </strong> 
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">Accolade (A knighthood, title, medal or treasure, A Super-Skill Artefact Descendant/Province)</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-fray">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-fray">
						<strong>Fray: </strong> Court Appearance
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">The Fray is a Court Appearance, this may mean that the PCs will be the defendants in a Court Case, or that they will be judged socially when they appear in the "Royal Court" of the friends of a powerful person. Either way is an Opposed Ordeal (or Test) that has variable Stakes from Low to Paradoxical.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-snag">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-snag">
						<strong>Snag: </strong> Dispute
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">The Snag is an additional dispute either during the Fray or arising from the Fray. Often this Dispute results from the Fray as a form of Blame-Storming, as everyone discusses where they went wrong and whose fault it was.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-sweeping">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-sweeping">
						<strong>Sweeping: </strong> Sweet Justice
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">The PCs get some justice, assuming how they behaved this could mean they are awarded damages, accolades, destinies, cause for celebration, get the love interest, or perhaps just laments. This at least grants the PCs a rest, and usually a Gain.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
		 <details class="t13ne-revelation-fancy"><summary><strong>Revelation</strong></summary><details class="t13ne-about"><summary><strong>About: </strong> The Story </summary><p class="t13ne-description">The information is about the current situation and plot. It might include information about one side of the Conflict, or all. It is normal when providing information to only reveal information about a single Side of the Conflict, usually the Dominant or Above sides.</p></details><details class="t13ne-info"><summary><strong>Info: </strong> Costly </summary><p class="t13ne-description">Information that is valuable and useful, somehow always has a cost. The costs can vary from a simple money transaction to longer, more permanent costs.</p></details><details class="t13ne-vector"><summary><strong>Vector: </strong> Accounts </summary><p class="t13ne-description">Accounts can be tally marks on clay tablets, written records, or even several slightly contradictory witness statements. Regardless of how they tell us, they always have something to share, if we know how to interpret them correctly.</p></details><details class="t13ne-alternate"><summary><strong>Alternate: </strong> Defences </summary><p class="t13ne-description">The information is about the defences of the Embodiment. Do they always have an anti-stab vest, or security detail, is the location a veritable fortress, or does the computer have an AI Firewall?</p></details><details class="t13ne-detail"><summary><strong>Detail: </strong> Occupational </summary><p class="t13ne-description">An occupational detail is the sort of detail that only a member of a particular occupation would notice. Like a Heating Services engineer noticing that the factory across the street has far bigger, and more complicated Air Conditioners than it should have. Why would a button factory require Level 3 Biosecurity rated Air filtration system?</p></details></details>
		 <details class="t13ne-ordeal-fancy"><summary><strong>Ordeal</strong></summary><p class="t13ne-ordeal-type"><strong>Ordeal-Type </strong> — Opposed Ordeal</p><p class="t13ne-stages"><strong>Stages </strong> — 8</p><p class="t13ne-ordeal-spread"><strong>Ordeal-Spread </strong> — Map-Geography</p><p class="t13ne-stakes"><strong>Stakes </strong> — Blended Low-Medium-High</p><p class="t13ne-test"><strong>Test </strong> — Control (Dominion)</p><p class="t13ne-obstacles"><strong>Obstacles </strong> — 3 Obstacles per Stage</p><p class="t13ne-suggested-action"><strong>Suggested-Action </strong> — Finesse</p><details class="t13ne-stage"><summary><strong>Stage: </strong> Control Stunt </summary><p class="t13ne-description">Do something that requires immense amounts of control over your vehicle, or self-control. There is always at least one Obstacle in a Control Stunt Stage.</p></details><details class="t13ne-motional"><summary><strong>Motional: </strong> Dangerous Vehicular Chase </summary><p class="t13ne-description">Someone is trying to get away, across the lava fields / glacier / tidal bridge / mountain-goat road you have to chase them, in a Jeep, for some reason.</p></details><details class="t13ne-fight"><summary><strong>Fight: </strong> Prepared Ambush </summary><p class="t13ne-description">The Fight is an ambush by a well prepared opponent with defences and escape strategies in place. The opponents are usually weaker than the party, but have good defences and specialised weapons and tactics that give them at least one Preparation Phase, before combat is begun.</p></details></details>
		 </details>
			</div>
				<div class="t13ne-card-lea"><details class="t13ne-lea"><summary><strong>Lea</strong></summary><p class="t13ne-colour"><strong>Path Colour —</strong> Green/Turquoise Herbivore</p><p class="t13ne-bulmas"><strong>Allowed Bulmäs —</strong> Herd, Broccen, Vermis, Labyrinthine, All-Walkers</p><p class="t13ne-animal-category"><strong>Animal Category —</strong> All-path</p><p class="t13ne-animal-example"><strong>Example Animal —</strong> All-Walkers / Brahman</p><p class="t13ne-notes"><strong>Notes —</strong> The Paths of the All-Walkers and Brahman can allow Bulmäs to do extraordinary things, like walking an individual’s life-path, so that all of their personal history is known to them. All-Walkers can assume even the forms and souls of other people by using these paths, without directly eating of the Soul. All-Walkers can even Doom Weave and play other cards from their hands as Wyrd Tarot cards, once they have played an Ace on themselves.</p></details></div></div><div class="t13ne-cardwrap-jsvg" id="cardrewritebox27" data-mode="jsvg" data-wyrd="1" data-yarn="1"><img src="https://www.cjmoseley.dev.cc/wp-content/plugins/t13ne/images/cards-svg/2C.svg" class="t13ne-svgcard"><div class="t13ne-svgpips"><div class="t13ne-textpips">2<!-- write suit--><span class="t13ne-symbol"><span class="t13ne-blackcard">♣</span></span><details class="t13ne-suit"><summary></summary><p class="t13ne-name"><strong>Suit: </strong>Clubs <span class="t13ne-blackcard">♣</span></p><p><strong>Ordeal Trump: </strong><span class="t13ne-trump">Defence</span></p><p class="t13ne-desc"><strong>Description: </strong>Clubs represent the power of politics and a stoic materialism. In the Tarot the Clubs are called Staves or Wands, in some traditions they represent Intellectual Air, but in most they are Fire.</p><p class="t13ne-tarot"><strong>Tarot Meaning: </strong>Power, Fame, Politics, Ability, Money.</p><p class="t13ne-otext"><strong>Ordeal Text: </strong>Ordeal Types: Wealth, Politics, Power. Clubs act as Trump for following Actions: Bartering, Controlling, Corrupting, Emotional-intensity, Guarding, Magic, Displaying might, Politics, Self-Defence, Socializing, and Trade Negotiations. Clubs can be used to defend any type of attack.</p><p class="t13ne-parry"><strong>Parry: </strong><strong>Disarm:</strong> All the cards of the attack are immediately discarded, along with the Parry Card. The remaining cards of the Defender's Parry can now each be used to cause the Attacker to drop a Descendant (starting with the attacking weapon usually), can also cut armour straps, belts etc, or steal hats. This basically adds RT equal to the card Pips to the Annex that was used to attack. Every 5 Pips also creates a point of GRT for the Character (representing how much time the Character must spend picking up, or readying the weapon again. Court cards additionally may damage the Descendant that was disarmed, as standard for the Stakes.</p><p class="t13ne-soak"><strong>Soak: </strong><strong>Ward:</strong> The matched Attack card, and one other randomly selected Attack card are discarded.</p></details></div></div><div class="t13ne-svg-facet"><div>Wyrd</div></div><div class="t13ne-wound"><div><strong class="t13ne-woundname">Distract</strong><details><summary></summary><strong>Distract</strong><p class="t13ne-base-pips">Base Pips to summon: 2</p><p class="t13ne-min-pips">Minimum Pips Required: 2</p><p class="t13ne-desc">The Target is distracted by the attack.</p><details class="t13ne-roll"><summary>Rules</summary><strong>-6</strong> Score on next Roll the Character makes and then Discard.</details><details class="t13ne-ordeal"><summary>Ordeal Rules</summary>Lose a Success Level <ul><li>Draw -1 Card on next Draw and then Discard.</li><li> Or <strong>-3</strong> Pips to the next Card played and Discard.</li><li>Or <strong>+3</strong> Pips to the next Attack on the Character, then Discard.</li></ul></details><details class="t13ne-psych"><summary>Psych Wounds</summary><details class="t13ne-negative"><summary>Negative Psych</summary><p class="t13ne-normal"><strong>Normal</strong>Mild Emotional influence (tears in eyes, heightened heart rate, etc) until Stabilized / Discarded.</p><p class="t13ne-extreme"><strong>Extreme</strong>Must scream, swear, or otherwise express the emotion until Stabilized or Discarded.</p></details><details class="t13ne-positive"><summary>Positive Psych</summary><p class="t13ne-normal"><strong>Normal</strong>Mild Emotional Influence (Smile, Pupil Dilation, Eyebrow twitches, etc) until Stabilised / Discarded.</p><p class="t13ne-extreme"><strong>Extreme</strong>Must speak, laugh, gasp, moan, tick, or otherwise express the emotion until Stabilised or Discarded.</p></details></details></details></div></div><div class="t13ne-tarot-svg"><details><summary class="t13ne-wyrdtarot"><strong> Wyrd Tarot: </strong> Crisis</summary><p class="t13ne-normal"><strong> Normal: </strong>Automatic Proficiency Crisis. Add a Proficiency to an Annex (or merge two Proficiencies into a Skill).</p><p class="t13ne-crossed"><strong> Crossed: </strong>Reroll a Proficiency Crisis.</p></details></div><div class="t13ne-yarn-fancy">
			<details><summary><strong>Yarn</strong> <br>The Eelafin</summary>
			<span class="t13ne-description">The Eelafin card is representative of the hybrid-faeries, who are characterised as having strong, graceful bodies, with just a hint of madness or wanderlust. Normal humans fear the Eelafin Doom-Weaving, that they usually imagine as curses. The Fae consider themselves half-doom and half-dream, the Eelafin they usually call Changeling, and consider them part-doom, part-doomed, part-dream, part-dreamer, and fear their Liberty and envy their mortality.</span>
			<span class="t13ne-significator">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-significator"><strong>Significator</strong></summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-plot"><strong>Plot: </strong> The Eelafin Plot is a hybrid itself, combining other types of Plots together to create something unique. You can draw for the hybrids, or simply choose. You might make a Romance-Detective Plot, where the "Who-dunnit" is as important than the "Will-they-won't-they". Or maybe throw in a Masked Vigilante, who is also a serial killer...</aside>
					<aside class="t13ne-char"><strong>Character: </strong> The Eelafin Significator indicates a Character who seems at odds with themselves. They Gain Yin when they act fatalistic, pessimistic, or depressed. They Gain Yang when they act optimistic, positive, or excited. They Gain Chi when they day-dream, or deal with, or talk about, a situation as it really is. They gain +1 Success Level to the separate actions when they perform two or more at a time (shoot two or three targets, shoot while somersaulting, etc).</aside>
					<aside class="t13ne-scene"><strong>Scene: </strong> The Eelafin Plot is a hybrid itself, combining other types of Plots together to create something unique. You can draw for the hybrids, or simply choose. You might make a Romance-Detective Plot, where the "Who-dunnit" is as important than the "Will-they-won't-they". Or maybe throw in a Masked Vigilante, who is also a serial killer...</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-scene">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-scene-type">
						<strong>Scene: </strong> Test
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">Some Scenes are nothing but a single Test, often with a lot of discussion around them. Tests are usually a single dice roll, like a Facet Test, and usually a Test of one of the Conflict Facets. A Ref should have some idea what the Difficulty is, what will occur on a pass or a fail, and what a Stalemate would look like. Occasionally Tests may be a single roll against an Opponent (such as the Villain). You can find example Tests in the Ordeal details for a card.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-conflict-embodiment">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-conflict-embodiment">
						<strong>Conflict Embodiment: </strong> Tension
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">The Conflict embodies within two Characters, but they do not come into direct Conflict. They have some relationship, that Embodies the Conflict as arguments, discussions, jokes, etc.  common to Romance and Buddy movies.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>

			<span class="t13ne-facet-embodiment">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-facet-embodiment">
						<strong>Facet Embodiment: </strong> Personality
					</summary>
					<section class="t13ne-description">The Conflict Embodies one Facet as a Character Personality. Often the most satisfying embodiment, but can lead to easy identification, and unsubtle Plots.</section>
				</details>
			</span>
			 <div class="t13ne-archetypes"><details><summary><strong>Archetype Character: </strong>Fae-Touched</summary> <details class="t13ne-Persona"><summary><strong>Persona: </strong><span class="t13ne-output">Fiend</span>
      </summary>
      <details class="t13ne-motivation"><summary><strong>Motivation: </strong></summary><span class="t13ne-description">The Fiend is motivated by selfishness, hatred, corruption, and the temptations of amoral living. They are well represented amongst criminal elements, and often seem to delight in simply breaking rules and laws.</span></details>
       <details class="t13ne-avoid"><summary><strong>Avoid: </strong></summary><span class="t13ne-description">The Fiend sees no reason to follow the rules of the rest of society, and will often deliberately break moral codes simply because they are moral codes.</span></details>
       <details class="t13ne-gain-chi"><summary><strong>Gain Chi: </strong></summary><span class="t13ne-description"><span>Gain Chi when you break a rule or law (including etiquette and unwritten rules), act “evil”, rude, or self-important, or succumb to temptations. </span><p>Ordeal gain Chi when you discard a low card to keep a higher one.</p></span></details></details>   <details class="t13ne-core"><summary><strong>Core: </strong><span class="t13ne-output">Cheater</span></summary><span class="t13ne-description">Chi/Sway cost or difficulty for one chosen effect is halved. (E.g. Reload for 4 Chi, specific sidestep for half price, increase Scale for 1/4 Max Chi, etc.)</span></details><details class="t13ne-hitch"><summary><strong>Hitch: </strong><span class="t13ne-output">Madness</span></summary><span class="t13ne-description">Mental disabilities, injuries and illnesses, or just plain barking.</span></details><p class="t13ne-description">Those who spend too much time around the Fae become different, usually indifferent to mortal rules and morals, their incredible experiences often leave them mad, but gifted with some ability most mortals require years of dedicated practice to learn.</p></details></div>
			 <span class="t13ne-hook">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-hook">
						<strong>Hook: </strong> Sensed
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">The Eelafin are know not just for their Doom-sense, but for their heightened senses in general. Perhaps a cry for help is heard, or a crime is witnessed by the Character to Hook them.</aside>
					<details class="t13ne-hook-aspect">
						<summary><strong>Hook Aspect: </strong>I-Ching</summary>
						<section class="t13ne-description">The Hook targets the Character's I-Ching. For example: If a Character has the Cauldron (50) I-Ching, then the Hook needs to have someone hungry turn up asking for help.</section>
					</details>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-obstacle-fancy"><details class="t13ne-obstacle"><summary><strong>Obstacle:</strong>Unstable</summary><p class="t13ne-description">The ground ahead is unstable, it might be loose rocks, scree, rubble, or fallen logs, who knows. You'll have to be careful and have great balance, though. It looks like the kind of place that people say, "...and that's how I lost my leg" about.</p></details></span>
			<span class="t13ne-gain">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-gain">
						<strong>Gain </strong> 
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">Bloodline/Doom Descendant (some bloodlines and destinies grant extra Skills, Talents or even Powers, the Character discovers one of theirs)</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-fray">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-fray">
						<strong>Fray: </strong> Doom
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">A Doom is a profound, Chronolithed event, a predestination paradox, or an event that is part of history and should not be changed, they are always Paradoxical Stakes Ordeals. Dooms are sensed approaching by every Mercari, Eelafin, or Yarn-Teller present. While a Doom is influenced and affected by the cards played, it is only the Ordeal cards that define the final success of the Doom, be careful, you may find yourself on the wrong side of history.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-snag">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-snag">
						<strong>Snag: </strong> Hitches
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">The Fray is complicated by a situation that involved many of the PCs Hitches. If a Character has a Woe that hasn't been triggered recently, now is the time. If they have a a few quirks, find a scenario that triggers them all, sequentially or together. For fairness, every Character involved in the Fray should have at least one of their Hitches triggered regardless of side.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-sweeping">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-sweeping">
						<strong>Sweeping: </strong> Destiny
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">Destiny sounds like a simple thing, but what it often means is years of study, months of rigorous training, and at least one Crisis. This is one of those events, Gain, as appropriate<ul><li>New Proficiencies and a new Skill Annex</li><li>Increase an Annex one Tier, gaining Proficiencies as required.</li><li>A new Skill/Talent/Power Learning, Training, or Lore Descendant</li><li>A new Monolith Descendant</li></ul></aside>
				</details>
			</span>
		 <details class="t13ne-revelation-fancy"><summary><strong>Revelation</strong></summary><details class="t13ne-about"><summary><strong>About: </strong> The Story </summary><p class="t13ne-description">The information is about the current situation and plot. It might include information about one side of the Conflict, or all. It is normal when providing information to only reveal information about a single Side of the Conflict, usually the Dominant or Above sides.</p></details><details class="t13ne-info"><summary><strong>Info: </strong> Suspicions </summary><p class="t13ne-description">Suspicions vary in scope and accuracy more than any other Information. If a cop suspects some one of a crime, they are right more often than a conspiracy theorist is right about the government, but both may be wrong, or ultimately proved right. Suspicions lack evidence, and are often little more than a reaction to a person or group.</p></details><details class="t13ne-vector"><summary><strong>Vector: </strong> Advertisement </summary><p class="t13ne-description">Adverts are everywhere in our society and always conveying some information to us. But this one is different, maybe its a private ad, simply saying "Safety not guaranteed", or perhaps it's the yells of a street vendor, telling us of distant lands and the quality of their silks and spices. Then again news vendors yelling out "click-bait" headlines is as old as printing.</p></details><details class="t13ne-alternate"><summary><strong>Alternate: </strong> Power </summary><p class="t13ne-description">The information is specifically about the powers the Embodiment has, whether Annexes or political power and pacts.</p></details><details class="t13ne-detail"><summary><strong>Detail: </strong> Peculiar </summary><p class="t13ne-description">Peculiar details are odd little out-of-place touches that point to something bigger, or more extraordinary than you might first have guessed. They are the footprints that stop in the middle of the field, or that pass through (or over) a solid wall.</p></details></details>
		 <details class="t13ne-ordeal-fancy"><summary><strong>Ordeal</strong></summary><p class="t13ne-ordeal-type"><strong>Ordeal-Type </strong> — Opposed Ordeal</p><p class="t13ne-stages"><strong>Stages </strong> — 3</p><p class="t13ne-ordeal-spread"><strong>Ordeal-Spread </strong> — Railroad</p><p class="t13ne-stakes"><strong>Stakes </strong> — Low</p><p class="t13ne-test"><strong>Test </strong> — Trade (Burden)</p><p class="t13ne-obstacles"><strong>Obstacles </strong> — 1 Obstacle in a Court Stage</p><p class="t13ne-suggested-action"><strong>Suggested-Action </strong> — Purchase</p><details class="t13ne-stage"><summary><strong>Stage: </strong> Cluttered </summary><p class="t13ne-description">The route ahead is cluttered, perhaps with parked cards, litter bins. Book stacks, filing cabinets, shelves, scaffolding poles, pipes, trees or bamboo poles can also create clutter.</p></details><details class="t13ne-motional"><summary><strong>Motional: </strong> Ground Vehicle Chase </summary><p class="t13ne-description">Who is that ahead of you? You need to catch them to find out. Drop a gear and put your foot to the floor.</p></details><details class="t13ne-fight"><summary><strong>Fight: </strong> Vigil </summary><p class="t13ne-description">The fight mostly consists of watching, and waiting. Occasionally a solitary usually Vex or Chorus Extra Opponent will test your defences, very occasionally something bigger will come.<!-- t13ne-shortcode "%7B%22type%22%3A%22facetsuitaspect%22%2C%22suit%22%3A%223%22%2C%22aspect%22%3A%22Monster%22%7D"// --></p><div class="t13ne-tablewrap"><table class="t13ne-table Monster"><thead><tr><th>&nbsp;&nbsp;Facet&nbsp;&nbsp;</th><th>&nbsp;&nbsp;Monster&nbsp;&nbsp;</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td>Burden</td><td><strong>Object</strong> —The Monster is an object imbued with the semblance of life. Artificial in nature usually, the living object is usually tough and strong. An Object Monster is created with a Monster Pool equal to their Burden Score. Objects are able to multiply their Scores by 1+Monster Pool Size when appropriate to their form and function. The Object Monster may instantly Prepare cards from its Monster Pool, often as a Prepared Defence or Stunt Preparation, as appropriate for the Object's form or function.</td></tr><tr><td>Dominion</td><td><strong>Horde</strong> —The Monster is a group or swarm of lesser monsters, it might be an Army, a pack of Bulmäs, a Fortress or a Battle Moon. Horde is effectively a Pact Descendant. A Horde may pool Values to create larger rolls, however they may never create a Boon greater than their Value. Hordes may add Dominion Draw cards to their Monster Pool when the Horde increases in numbers. The Horde must Play at least one card from their Monster Pool on any action that splits the Horde (like losing an individual). E.g. two Vex Extras of Boon 10 (Value 45) that form a Horde become a single Extra of Boon 16 (Value 90), (with probably 1 card in the Monster Pool) but could never create a Boon greater than the individual's Value(45). If there were 11 Vexes, that would be Boon 44 (Value 495). 12 or One million Vexes would both give Boon 45 (Value 530) since that is the most powerful their Horde can get.</td></tr><tr><td>Fury</td><td><strong>Temperamental</strong> —The Monster is the embodiment of an Emotion, usually a negative one. Such Monsters are often capable of supernatural actions, such as telepathy, shape-shifting, or even undying. The Vampire is an embodiment of Lust, with powers of mind control, telepathy and suggestion, as well as shape-shifting abilities. The Werewolf is berserk rage, and almost indestructible unless Silver is used. The Beansidhe is the embodiment of mourning, and able to sense an oncoming death. When they experience their negative emotion the Monster may refill their Monster Pool to Fury Score cards. When appropriate to their Emotion they must add a Monster Pool card to the action and multiply the total Score by their remaining Monster Pool Reduced.</td></tr><tr><td>Rook</td><td><strong>Guardian</strong> —The Monster is a Guardian, it guards a certain territory, path, treasure or person. Guardians may add Rook Draw to their Monster Pool whenever they act as a guard, try to interfere with an attack, or are attacked. The Guardian reduces all attacks by a number of Pips equal to the current Monster Pool automatically. The Guardian will however automatically play cards from its Monster Pool to defend itself (via any Active or Passive defence method that makes sense to the Referee. From Smaug to Fafnir dragons are often Guardians, looking after a particular treasure or demanding tributes from a territory.</td></tr><tr><td>Sin</td><td><strong>Villain</strong> —The Sin Monster is an embodiment of a specific vice, taboo, or sin, often in the form of a human Villain (often with animalistic features or tendencies). They usually have Annexes and Hitches that reflect their embodied vice or sin, and are often at least partially supernatural. Villains add Sin Draw cards to their Monster Pool when they, or another (usually both) indulges in their particular vice or behaviour. Which can be as broad as "Evil" if you like. Villains may add +1 Score for each card in their Monster Pool for any action that furthers their diabolical plans. Villains automatically spend Monster Pool cards on escape actions when attacked, creating at least Sin Draw Obstacles from the Monster Pools cards. The genocidal or narcissistic maniac, of the sort British Intelligence seems to tangle with repeatedly, could be a Sin Monster. The ever-hungry cannibal spirit, the Wendigo is definitely an example, as is the Sexual-predatory Lust-embodied Vampire.</td></tr><tr><td>Wyrd</td><td><strong>Doom-weaver</strong> —The monster is a creature of the Fæ, it can draw and play Yarn cards or Wyrd Tarot cards (as appropriate). The Wyrd Monster may play cards from its Monster Pool as Wyrd Tarot or Yarn cards. The Monster may draw and add Wyrd Draw cards to its Monster Pool whenever it makes a choice, decision, or judgement. The Doom-weaver may draw and play Wyrd Draw cards as Ordeal cards whenever anyone plays Wyrd Tarot or Yarn cards. They must play a card from the Monster Pool if they have to undo a choice, decision or judgement they have made. Doom-Weavers come in many forms from the Cait-Sith, the Beansidhe, to the Faery nobles of Ironmaster.</td></tr></tbody><tfoot><tr><td>&nbsp;&nbsp;Facet&nbsp;&nbsp;</td><td>&nbsp;&nbsp;Monster&nbsp;&nbsp;</td></tr></tfoot></table></div><p></p></details></details>
		 </details>
			</div>
				<div class="t13ne-card-lea"><details class="t13ne-lea"><summary><strong>Lea</strong></summary><p class="t13ne-colour"><strong>Path Colour —</strong> Green/Turquoise Herbivore</p><p class="t13ne-bulmas"><strong>Allowed Bulmäs —</strong> Herd, Broccen, Vermis, Labyrinthine, All-Walkers</p><p class="t13ne-animal-category"><strong>Animal Category —</strong> Microfauna</p><p class="t13ne-animal-example"><strong>Example Animal —</strong> Plankton/Phytoplankton/Algae</p><p class="t13ne-notes"><strong>Notes —</strong> Microscopic animal and algal plant and bacterial paths, often overlooked.</p></details></div></div><div class="t13ne-cardwrap-jsvg" id="cardrewritebox28" data-mode="jsvg" data-wyrd="1" data-yarn="1"><img src="https://www.cjmoseley.dev.cc/wp-content/plugins/t13ne/images/cards-svg/3C.svg" class="t13ne-svgcard"><div class="t13ne-svgpips"><div class="t13ne-textpips">3<!-- write suit--><span class="t13ne-symbol"><span class="t13ne-blackcard">♣</span></span><details class="t13ne-suit"><summary></summary><p class="t13ne-name"><strong>Suit: </strong>Clubs <span class="t13ne-blackcard">♣</span></p><p><strong>Ordeal Trump: </strong><span class="t13ne-trump">Defence</span></p><p class="t13ne-desc"><strong>Description: </strong>Clubs represent the power of politics and a stoic materialism. In the Tarot the Clubs are called Staves or Wands, in some traditions they represent Intellectual Air, but in most they are Fire.</p><p class="t13ne-tarot"><strong>Tarot Meaning: </strong>Power, Fame, Politics, Ability, Money.</p><p class="t13ne-otext"><strong>Ordeal Text: </strong>Ordeal Types: Wealth, Politics, Power. Clubs act as Trump for following Actions: Bartering, Controlling, Corrupting, Emotional-intensity, Guarding, Magic, Displaying might, Politics, Self-Defence, Socializing, and Trade Negotiations. Clubs can be used to defend any type of attack.</p><p class="t13ne-parry"><strong>Parry: </strong><strong>Disarm:</strong> All the cards of the attack are immediately discarded, along with the Parry Card. The remaining cards of the Defender's Parry can now each be used to cause the Attacker to drop a Descendant (starting with the attacking weapon usually), can also cut armour straps, belts etc, or steal hats. This basically adds RT equal to the card Pips to the Annex that was used to attack. Every 5 Pips also creates a point of GRT for the Character (representing how much time the Character must spend picking up, or readying the weapon again. Court cards additionally may damage the Descendant that was disarmed, as standard for the Stakes.</p><p class="t13ne-soak"><strong>Soak: </strong><strong>Ward:</strong> The matched Attack card, and one other randomly selected Attack card are discarded.</p></details></div></div><div class="t13ne-svg-facet"><div>Sin</div></div><div class="t13ne-wound"><div><strong class="t13ne-woundname">Distract</strong><details><summary></summary><strong>Distract</strong><p class="t13ne-base-pips">Base Pips to summon: 2</p><p class="t13ne-min-pips">Minimum Pips Required: 2</p><p class="t13ne-desc">The Target is distracted by the attack.</p><details class="t13ne-roll"><summary>Rules</summary><strong>-6</strong> Score on next Roll the Character makes and then Discard.</details><details class="t13ne-ordeal"><summary>Ordeal Rules</summary>Lose a Success Level <ul><li>Draw -1 Card on next Draw and then Discard.</li><li> Or <strong>-3</strong> Pips to the next Card played and Discard.</li><li>Or <strong>+3</strong> Pips to the next Attack on the Character, then Discard.</li></ul></details><details class="t13ne-psych"><summary>Psych Wounds</summary><details
