class="t13ne-negative"><summary>Negative Psych</summary><p class="t13ne-normal"><strong>Normal</strong>Mild Emotional influence (tears in eyes, heightened heart rate, etc) until Stabilized / Discarded.</p><p class="t13ne-extreme"><strong>Extreme</strong>Must scream, swear, or otherwise express the emotion until Stabilized or Discarded.</p></details><details class="t13ne-positive"><summary>Positive Psych</summary><p class="t13ne-normal"><strong>Normal</strong>Mild Emotional Influence (Smile, Pupil Dilation, Eyebrow twitches, etc) until Stabilised / Discarded.</p><p class="t13ne-extreme"><strong>Extreme</strong>Must speak, laugh, gasp, moan, tick, or otherwise express the emotion until Stabilised or Discarded.</p></details></details></details></div></div><div class="t13ne-tarot-svg"><details><summary class="t13ne-wyrdtarot"><strong> Wyrd Tarot: </strong> Aegis</summary><p class="t13ne-normal"><strong> Normal: </strong>Discards all cards of an incoming attack.</p><p class="t13ne-crossed"><strong> Crossed: </strong>Adds +10 Penetration Pips to an Attack card in play.</p></details></div><div class="t13ne-yarn-fancy">
			<details><summary><strong>Yarn</strong> <br>The Serpent</summary>
			<span class="t13ne-description">The Serpent card represents words spoken with forked tongues, duplicity, shedding one's skin, rebirth, or abandoning the past. It is the card of liars, sinners and the untrustworthy. However, as duplicitous and sinful as the Serpent may be, it is also a card of cool, creative, rational thought and reasoning.</span>
			<span class="t13ne-significator">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-significator"><strong>Significator</strong></summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-plot"><strong>Plot: </strong> The Serpent Plot is all about deceit, lies, and sins. It may be a tale of crime, lust, or passion, or it could be a counter-intelligence operation undertaken for Monarch and country.</aside>
					<aside class="t13ne-char"><strong>Character: </strong> The Serpent Significator indicates a lying snake of a Character. They Gain Yin when they stay calm, are rational, or reasonable. They Gain Yang when they are duplicitous, misleading or creative. They Gain Chi when they lie or sin. They gain +1 Success Level to all Psyche defences.</aside>
					<aside class="t13ne-scene"><strong>Scene: </strong> The Serpent Plot is all about deceit, lies, and sins. It may be a tale of crime, lust, or passion, or it could be a counter-intelligence operation undertaken for Monarch and country.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-scene">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-scene-type">
						<strong>Scene: </strong> Ordeal
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">Ordeals test Characters in a complex way (more than a single dice roll or a Facet Test). Ordeals can range from Low Stakes Ordeals (like doing the Crossword), to High Stakes Ordeals, such as solving a crossword puzzle, while exchanging gunfire with enemies, during a car chase. Action generally, and Fights in particular, involve an Ordeal. Ordeals are usually created by playing a number of cards to define everything about the Ordeal (from the Type, Stakes, Number of Obstacles you should draw and add, to a suggested action that could be taken, or a description of a Stage).</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-conflict-embodiment">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-conflict-embodiment">
						<strong>Conflict Embodiment: </strong> Tension
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">The Conflict embodies within two Characters, but they do not come into direct Conflict. They have some relationship, that Embodies the Conflict as arguments, discussions, jokes, etc.  common to Romance and Buddy movies.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>

			<span class="t13ne-facet-embodiment">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-facet-embodiment">
						<strong>Facet Embodiment: </strong> Personality
					</summary>
					<section class="t13ne-description">The Conflict Embodies one Facet as a Character Personality. Often the most satisfying embodiment, but can lead to easy identification, and unsubtle Plots.</section>
				</details>
			</span>
			 <div class="t13ne-archetypes"><details><summary><strong>Archetype Character: </strong>Snake</summary> <details class="t13ne-Persona"><summary><strong>Persona: </strong><span class="t13ne-output">Fiend</span>
      </summary>
      <details class="t13ne-motivation"><summary><strong>Motivation: </strong></summary><span class="t13ne-description">The Fiend is motivated by selfishness, hatred, corruption, and the temptations of amoral living. They are well represented amongst criminal elements, and often seem to delight in simply breaking rules and laws.</span></details>
       <details class="t13ne-avoid"><summary><strong>Avoid: </strong></summary><span class="t13ne-description">The Fiend sees no reason to follow the rules of the rest of society, and will often deliberately break moral codes simply because they are moral codes.</span></details>
       <details class="t13ne-gain-chi"><summary><strong>Gain Chi: </strong></summary><span class="t13ne-description"><span>Gain Chi when you break a rule or law (including etiquette and unwritten rules), act “evil”, rude, or self-important, or succumb to temptations. </span><p>Ordeal gain Chi when you discard a low card to keep a higher one.</p></span></details></details>   <details class="t13ne-core"><summary><strong>Core: </strong><span class="t13ne-output">Aggressor</span></summary><span class="t13ne-description">Gain Chi when you lose your temper, shout, or argue.</span></details><details class="t13ne-hitch"><summary><strong>Hitch: </strong><span class="t13ne-output">Vulnerable</span></summary><span class="t13ne-description">Perhaps they are flammable or easily startled, they might have an allergy, or similar weakness.</span></details><p class="t13ne-description">Snakes are aggressive fiends, who use others for their own purposes, actually abuse is probably a better term. Snakes lie, and cheat not for gain or revenge, but because it is simply their nature. They have few redeeming features, but do have a Vulnerability, often an allergy or weakness.</p></details></div>
			 <span class="t13ne-hook">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-hook">
						<strong>Hook: </strong> Temptation
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">Offer a Gain to the Character, as their eventual reward.</aside>
					<details class="t13ne-hook-aspect">
						<summary><strong>Hook Aspect: </strong>I-Ching</summary>
						<section class="t13ne-description">The Hook targets the Character's I-Ching. For example: If a Character has the Cauldron (50) I-Ching, then the Hook needs to have someone hungry turn up asking for help.</section>
					</details>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-obstacle-fancy"><details class="t13ne-obstacle"><summary><strong>Obstacle:</strong>Hitch</summary><p class="t13ne-description">The way ahead is blocked by some device that will trigger a Hitch for one Character (often the PCs Love Interest). If a Character is afraid of Spiders then the way is blocked with webs.</p></details></span>
			<span class="t13ne-gain">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-gain">
						<strong>Gain </strong> 
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">Fruit of the Tree of Knowledge (A Lore / Learning Descendant)</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-fray">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-fray">
						<strong>Fray: </strong> Persuasion
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">Persuasion is a Low to Soul Stakes Ordeal where a PC tries to change another's mind (often another Side's Embodiment) or has someone try to change their minds.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-snag">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-snag">
						<strong>Snag: </strong> Skin-Shedding
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">Snakes rejuvenate by shedding their skins. And the Snag here has an embodiment doing the same. Perhaps the enemy was defeated, the PCs watched him fall, but here he is again, fresh and ready for them, or maybe half-way through the Fray one of the PCs (or more likely an Ally) reveals they were working for the enemy all along, or having won the Fray the PCs now realise that they have fought for the wrong side.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-sweeping">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-sweeping">
						<strong>Sweeping: </strong> Rebirth
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">The PCs are rejuvenated, refreshed, improved, reborn, or upgraded. Some circumstance will heal the worst of their Wounds, resolve a Hitch, or add an Alternate to a Character. The Alternate is usually uniquely suited for exactly the situation the Character finds themselves in.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
		 <details class="t13ne-revelation-fancy"><summary><strong>Revelation</strong></summary><details class="t13ne-about"><summary><strong>About: </strong> The Story </summary><p class="t13ne-description">The information is about the current situation and plot. It might include information about one side of the Conflict, or all. It is normal when providing information to only reveal information about a single Side of the Conflict, usually the Dominant or Above sides.</p></details><details class="t13ne-info"><summary><strong>Info: </strong> Warning </summary><p class="t13ne-description">Some information is just a warning. Warnings tell us things aren't safe, that some people are not to be trusted, or that now they are coming for you. Some warn us to back off, or simply that a food might contain nuts...</p></details><details class="t13ne-vector"><summary><strong>Vector: </strong> Gossip </summary><p class="t13ne-description">Gossip is as full of lies and half-truths, guesses and suspicions, as it is actual truth. Still, tap into the networks the right way, and you won't believe the things you can find out.</p></details><details class="t13ne-alternate"><summary><strong>Alternate: </strong> Sins </summary><p class="t13ne-description">The information is all negative information about a Character or thing. It may be a police rap sheet, or the tales of infamy about a ruler or villain.</p></details><details class="t13ne-detail"><summary><strong>Detail: </strong> Lack </summary><p class="t13ne-description">A lack of details can be telling. Maybe there is nothing to find out, or perhaps it is more a sign that someone's past has been sanitised.</p></details></details>
		 <details class="t13ne-ordeal-fancy"><summary><strong>Ordeal</strong></summary><p class="t13ne-ordeal-type"><strong>Ordeal-Type </strong> — Opposed Ordeal</p><p class="t13ne-stages"><strong>Stages </strong> — 3</p><p class="t13ne-ordeal-spread"><strong>Ordeal-Spread </strong> — Forking</p><p class="t13ne-stakes"><strong>Stakes </strong> — Medium</p><p class="t13ne-test"><strong>Test </strong> — Politics (Dominion)</p><p class="t13ne-obstacles"><strong>Obstacles </strong> — 1 Obstacle in a Court Stage</p><p class="t13ne-suggested-action"><strong>Suggested-Action </strong> — Discuss</p><details class="t13ne-stage"><summary><strong>Stage: </strong> Negotiate </summary><p class="t13ne-description">Sometimes there are things in the we that you have to negotiate your way past, other times there are just difficult negotiations that you have to conduct.</p></details><details class="t13ne-motional"><summary><strong>Motional: </strong> Flying Machine Chase </summary><p class="t13ne-description">They're getting away in that aeroplane / helicopter / grav-platform, get after them, or better yet, get ahead of them.</p></details><details class="t13ne-fight"><summary><strong>Fight: </strong> Third Force </summary><p class="t13ne-description">The fight is initially between two sides, often equally matched, during the third Phase a third force arrives that is at least as powerful as the least powerful force already fighting.</p></details></details>
		 </details>
			</div>
				<div class="t13ne-card-lea"><details class="t13ne-lea"><summary><strong>Lea</strong></summary><p class="t13ne-colour"><strong>Path Colour —</strong> Green/Turquoise Herbivore</p><p class="t13ne-bulmas"><strong>Allowed Bulmäs —</strong> Herd, Broccen, Vermis, Labyrinthine, All-Walkers</p><p class="t13ne-animal-category"><strong>Animal Category —</strong> Meiofauna</p><p class="t13ne-animal-example"><strong>Example Animal —</strong> Small Nematode worms / shrimps </p><p class="t13ne-notes"><strong>Notes —</strong> A lot of boring insects paths at this level</p></details></div></div><div class="t13ne-cardwrap-jsvg" id="cardrewritebox29" data-mode="jsvg" data-wyrd="1" data-yarn="1"><img src="https://www.cjmoseley.dev.cc/wp-content/plugins/t13ne/images/cards-svg/4C.svg" class="t13ne-svgcard"><div class="t13ne-svgpips"><div class="t13ne-textpips">4<!-- write suit--><span class="t13ne-symbol"><span class="t13ne-blackcard">♣</span></span><details class="t13ne-suit"><summary></summary><p class="t13ne-name"><strong>Suit: </strong>Clubs <span class="t13ne-blackcard">♣</span></p><p><strong>Ordeal Trump: </strong><span class="t13ne-trump">Defence</span></p><p class="t13ne-desc"><strong>Description: </strong>Clubs represent the power of politics and a stoic materialism. In the Tarot the Clubs are called Staves or Wands, in some traditions they represent Intellectual Air, but in most they are Fire.</p><p class="t13ne-tarot"><strong>Tarot Meaning: </strong>Power, Fame, Politics, Ability, Money.</p><p class="t13ne-otext"><strong>Ordeal Text: </strong>Ordeal Types: Wealth, Politics, Power. Clubs act as Trump for following Actions: Bartering, Controlling, Corrupting, Emotional-intensity, Guarding, Magic, Displaying might, Politics, Self-Defence, Socializing, and Trade Negotiations. Clubs can be used to defend any type of attack.</p><p class="t13ne-parry"><strong>Parry: </strong><strong>Disarm:</strong> All the cards of the attack are immediately discarded, along with the Parry Card. The remaining cards of the Defender's Parry can now each be used to cause the Attacker to drop a Descendant (starting with the attacking weapon usually), can also cut armour straps, belts etc, or steal hats. This basically adds RT equal to the card Pips to the Annex that was used to attack. Every 5 Pips also creates a point of GRT for the Character (representing how much time the Character must spend picking up, or readying the weapon again. Court cards additionally may damage the Descendant that was disarmed, as standard for the Stakes.</p><p class="t13ne-soak"><strong>Soak: </strong><strong>Ward:</strong> The matched Attack card, and one other randomly selected Attack card are discarded.</p></details></div></div><div class="t13ne-svg-facet"><div>Fury</div></div><div class="t13ne-wound"><div><strong class="t13ne-woundname">Distract</strong><details><summary></summary><strong>Distract</strong><p class="t13ne-base-pips">Base Pips to summon: 2</p><p class="t13ne-min-pips">Minimum Pips Required: 2</p><p class="t13ne-desc">The Target is distracted by the attack.</p><details class="t13ne-roll"><summary>Rules</summary><strong>-6</strong> Score on next Roll the Character makes and then Discard.</details><details class="t13ne-ordeal"><summary>Ordeal Rules</summary>Lose a Success Level <ul><li>Draw -1 Card on next Draw and then Discard.</li><li> Or <strong>-3</strong> Pips to the next Card played and Discard.</li><li>Or <strong>+3</strong> Pips to the next Attack on the Character, then Discard.</li></ul></details><details class="t13ne-psych"><summary>Psych Wounds</summary><details class="t13ne-negative"><summary>Negative Psych</summary><p class="t13ne-normal"><strong>Normal</strong>Mild Emotional influence (tears in eyes, heightened heart rate, etc) until Stabilized / Discarded.</p><p class="t13ne-extreme"><strong>Extreme</strong>Must scream, swear, or otherwise express the emotion until Stabilized or Discarded.</p></details><details class="t13ne-positive"><summary>Positive Psych</summary><p class="t13ne-normal"><strong>Normal</strong>Mild Emotional Influence (Smile, Pupil Dilation, Eyebrow twitches, etc) until Stabilised / Discarded.</p><p class="t13ne-extreme"><strong>Extreme</strong>Must speak, laugh, gasp, moan, tick, or otherwise express the emotion until Stabilised or Discarded.</p></details></details></details></div></div><div class="t13ne-tarot-svg"><details><summary class="t13ne-wyrdtarot"><strong> Wyrd Tarot: </strong> Fast Talk</summary><p class="t13ne-normal"><strong> Normal: </strong>Automatic Success on any social action (they believe the lie).</p><p class="t13ne-crossed"><strong> Crossed: </strong>Automatic Failure of any social action (they won't believe the truth).</p></details></div><div class="t13ne-yarn-fancy">
			<details><summary><strong>Yarn</strong> <br>The Waters</summary>
			<span class="t13ne-description">The Waters is a card that  can literally mean waters, in the form of a river, a lake or an ocean that is important as a location, motif, or symbol, but it can also be symbolic, standing for the darker, violent emotions, that suddenly explode from out of someone. It can be hidden, deep motifs and meanings too. In these waters unconscious acts stir unpredictable actions.</span>
			<span class="t13ne-significator">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-significator"><strong>Significator</strong></summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-plot"><strong>Plot: </strong> The Waters Plot is deep. It may be that there are a lot of sides, or that the Conflict involves deeper themes than normal. Perhaps it is an essay on the existential angst that haunts modern life, or how predestination paradoxes and prophesy counter the concept of free-will. Then again maybe the Story revolves literally around waters, waves, water-craft, and sailors.</aside>
					<aside class="t13ne-char"><strong>Character: </strong> The Waters Significator indicates a deep, philosophical, emotional Character. They Gain Yin when they act calm, thoughtful, or philosophical. They Gain Yang when they act agitated, emotional or angry. They Gain Chi when they empathise or reflect another's emotions. They gain +1 Success Level to all Psyche Attacks.</aside>
					<aside class="t13ne-scene"><strong>Scene: </strong> The Waters Plot is deep. It may be that there are a lot of sides, or that the Conflict involves deeper themes than normal. Perhaps it is an essay on the existential angst that haunts modern life, or how predestination paradoxes and prophesy counter the concept of free-will. Then again maybe the Story revolves literally around waters, waves, water-craft, and sailors.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-scene">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-scene-type">
						<strong>Scene: </strong> Gain
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">Gains give something to one of the Characters to help move the Story on. Sometimes this might be payment for a job they did, or it might be a discovery that will directly connect to the Story. This may be information. It is normal to play a separate card to define the actual Gain that is made.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-conflict-embodiment">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-conflict-embodiment">
						<strong>Conflict Embodiment: </strong> Tension
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">The Conflict embodies within two Characters, but they do not come into direct Conflict. They have some relationship, that Embodies the Conflict as arguments, discussions, jokes, etc.  common to Romance and Buddy movies.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>

			<span class="t13ne-facet-embodiment">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-facet-embodiment">
						<strong>Facet Embodiment: </strong> Core
					</summary>
					<section class="t13ne-description">The Conflict Embodies one Facet as a Character Core. This can create very satisfying embodiments that can be suitable for most Genres.</section>
				</details>
			</span>
			 <div class="t13ne-archetypes"><details><summary><strong>Archetype Character: </strong>Bully</summary> <details class="t13ne-Persona"><summary><strong>Persona: </strong><span class="t13ne-output">Beast</span>
      </summary>
      <details class="t13ne-motivation"><summary><strong>Motivation: </strong></summary><span class="t13ne-description">Beasts are simple, they seek a warm place to sleep, food, and when possible other pleasures. They rarely have greater ambitions than any animal would in a similar situation (and the Beast is most animal Character's Persona).</span></details>
       <details class="t13ne-avoid"><summary><strong>Avoid: </strong></summary><span class="t13ne-description">Complex social situations and contracts are the bane of a Beast's life (if they even understand what is going on), they'd rather not deal with things like waiting in line, filling in paperwork, or even paying people compliments. And if you think its hard for a Beast to pay someone a compliment, wait until you try explaining to them taxes that include social security, or social health systems.</span></details>
       <details class="t13ne-gain-chi"><summary><strong>Gain Chi: </strong></summary><span class="t13ne-description"><span>Gain Chi when you are honest, blunt, direct, or eat.</span><p>Ordeal gain Chi when you play a ♣ to attack</p></span></details></details>   <details class="t13ne-core"><summary><strong>Core: </strong><span class="t13ne-output">Dominator</span></summary><span class="t13ne-description">Gain Chi when you give orders, exert authority or power.</span></details><details class="t13ne-hitch"><summary><strong>Hitch: </strong><span class="t13ne-output">Fear</span></summary><span class="t13ne-description">Either suffering from (as on a phobia), or causing Fear</span></details><p class="t13ne-description">The Bully likes to get their own way, throwing orders and punches as easily and as often as each other, all bullies though are afraid of something and are driven by fury and fear.</p></details></div>
			 <span class="t13ne-hook">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-hook">
						<strong>Hook: </strong> Rising Waters
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">The story hooks the character with a slowly rising danger. This can be a literal rising water level, or a sign of worse things to come, such as being teased, before it turns to abuse and bullying. The danger is present now, but may not be taken seriously enough yet.</aside>
					<details class="t13ne-hook-aspect">
						<summary><strong>Hook Aspect: </strong>I-Ching</summary>
						<section class="t13ne-description">The Hook targets the Character's I-Ching. For example: If a Character has the Cauldron (50) I-Ching, then the Hook needs to have someone hungry turn up asking for help.</section>
					</details>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-obstacle-fancy"><details class="t13ne-obstacle"><summary><strong>Obstacle:</strong>Water</summary><p class="t13ne-description">The way is blocked by water. It might be a flash flood, a swollen river, a flooded passage, or just a huge puddle, but you will have to get wet, or clever to move on. If the Ordeal is on water then the way is blocked by earth, ice, fog, or fire as the Yarn-Teller prefers.</p></details></span>
			<span class="t13ne-gain">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-gain">
						<strong>Gain </strong> 
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">Water Location (such as a pond, pool, lake, river, shore, beach or a suitable water  vehicle)</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-fray">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-fray">
						<strong>Fray: </strong> Drowning
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">Drowning is any Ordeal where the Stakes rise rapidly during the Ordeal. Drowning might start as Low Stakes (a pleasant swim or putting a meal on credit card), but it soon escalates until the Stakes are Medium (a current starts to drag them out to sea or they have to take a pay day loan after the car breaks down, until suddenly the stakes are High or even higher (far from shore the sharks start circling or finally they made a deal with the devil to pay off the debt).</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-snag">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-snag">
						<strong>Snag: </strong> Leaks
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">After the Fray it appears that there is a leak. This could be a case of getting home to find the Embodiment's apartment flooded, or it may mean there is a mole in their organization. Either way some things are damaged or lost, or some information makes its way to the Opposed Embodiment.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-sweeping">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-sweeping">
						<strong>Sweeping: </strong> Salvage
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">You rescue someone, or something from destruction. This often is a gain of a treasure descendant, vehicle, location or powerful ally.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
		 <details class="t13ne-revelation-fancy"><summary><strong>Revelation</strong></summary><details class="t13ne-about"><summary><strong>About: </strong> The Story </summary><p class="t13ne-description">The information is about the current situation and plot. It might include information about one side of the Conflict, or all. It is normal when providing information to only reveal information about a single Side of the Conflict, usually the Dominant or Above sides.</p></details><details class="t13ne-info"><summary><strong>Info: </strong> Answers </summary><p class="t13ne-description">When we have questions, all we want is answers. Of course, an answer is not always the same as <em>the</em> answer. What are the biggest questions someone would have about the Embodiment? Now create two answers to each of those questions.</p></details><details class="t13ne-vector"><summary><strong>Vector: </strong> Rumour </summary><p class="t13ne-description">Rumour would have it that it is pretty good at spreading information. Rumour often has it wrong, but every now and then, just often enough to keep belief alive, it gets it right.</p></details><details class="t13ne-alternate"><summary><strong>Alternate: </strong> Cleverness </summary><p class="t13ne-description">The information is about how clever the EMbodiment is. Maybe they were made by a world renowned scientist, or perhaps they are dumber than a box of rocks.</p></details><details class="t13ne-detail"><summary><strong>Detail: </strong> Social </summary><p class="t13ne-description">Social details include what societies were involved in the Embodiments construction, or is a member of. It can cover details of their social circles, who their friends and rivals are, or perhaps some rumours of how lacking in charm they are.</p></details></details>
		 <details class="t13ne-ordeal-fancy"><summary><strong>Ordeal</strong></summary><p class="t13ne-ordeal-type"><strong>Ordeal-Type </strong> — Opposed Ordeal</p><p class="t13ne-stages"><strong>Stages </strong> — 3</p><p class="t13ne-ordeal-spread"><strong>Ordeal-Spread </strong> — Pyramid</p><p class="t13ne-stakes"><strong>Stakes </strong> — Medium</p><p class="t13ne-test"><strong>Test </strong> — Barter (Burden)</p><p class="t13ne-obstacles"><strong>Obstacles </strong> — 1 Obstacle in a Court Stage</p><p class="t13ne-suggested-action"><strong>Suggested-Action </strong> — Haggle</p><details class="t13ne-stage"><summary><strong>Stage: </strong> Wrangling </summary><p class="t13ne-description">The way ahead is winding and difficult to get through. There may be passing traffic or too many pedestrians, only the Ref can say for sure.</p></details><details class="t13ne-motional"><summary><strong>Motional: </strong> Street Chase </summary><p class="t13ne-description">This can be as powerful and simple as "Follow-That-Car!", or you can be the one at the wheel.</p></details><details class="t13ne-fight"><summary><strong>Fight: </strong> Siege </summary><p class="t13ne-description">One side is entrenched and well-prepared. The other side must lay siege. The attacking side will usually have a numerical advantage and may have a power advantage too. Defenders have a tactical advantage in their defences which will apply Location Bonus Cards to suitable Style Reserves .</p></details></details>
		 </details>
			</div>
				<div class="t13ne-card-lea"><details class="t13ne-lea"><summary><strong>Lea</strong></summary><p class="t13ne-colour"><strong>Path Colour —</strong> Green/Turquoise Herbivore</p><p class="t13ne-bulmas"><strong>Allowed Bulmäs —</strong> Herd, Broccen, Vermis, Labyrinthine, All-Walkers</p><p class="t13ne-animal-category"><strong>Animal Category —</strong> Mesofauna</p><p class="t13ne-animal-example"><strong>Example Animal —</strong> Greenfly / Grubs / Larvae</p><p class="t13ne-notes"><strong>Notes —</strong> Tiny Arthropods</p></details></div></div><div class="t13ne-cardwrap-jsvg" id="cardrewritebox30" data-mode="jsvg" data-wyrd="1" data-yarn="1"><img src="https://www.cjmoseley.dev.cc/wp-content/plugins/t13ne/images/cards-svg/5C.svg" class="t13ne-svgcard"><div class="t13ne-svgpips"><div class="t13ne-textpips">5<!-- write suit--><span class="t13ne-symbol"><span class="t13ne-blackcard">♣</span></span><details class="t13ne-suit"><summary></summary><p class="t13ne-name"><strong>Suit: </strong>Clubs <span class="t13ne-blackcard">♣</span></p><p><strong>Ordeal Trump: </strong><span class="t13ne-trump">Defence</span></p><p class="t13ne-desc"><strong>Description: </strong>Clubs represent the power of politics and a stoic materialism. In the Tarot the Clubs are called Staves or Wands, in some traditions they represent Intellectual Air, but in most they are Fire.</p><p class="t13ne-tarot"><strong>Tarot Meaning: </strong>Power, Fame, Politics, Ability, Money.</p><p class="t13ne-otext"><strong>Ordeal Text: </strong>Ordeal Types: Wealth, Politics, Power. Clubs act as Trump for following Actions: Bartering, Controlling, Corrupting, Emotional-intensity, Guarding, Magic, Displaying might, Politics, Self-Defence, Socializing, and Trade Negotiations. Clubs can be used to defend any type of attack.</p><p class="t13ne-parry"><strong>Parry: </strong><strong>Disarm:</strong> All the cards of the attack are immediately discarded, along with the Parry Card. The remaining cards of the Defender's Parry can now each be used to cause the Attacker to drop a Descendant (starting with the attacking weapon usually), can also cut armour straps, belts etc, or steal hats. This basically adds RT equal to the card Pips to the Annex that was used to attack. Every 5 Pips also creates a point of GRT for the Character (representing how much time the Character must spend picking up, or readying the weapon again. Court cards additionally may damage the Descendant that was disarmed, as standard for the Stakes.</p><p class="t13ne-soak"><strong>Soak: </strong><strong>Ward:</strong> The matched Attack card, and one other randomly selected Attack card are discarded.</p></details></div></div><div class="t13ne-svg-facet"><div>Rook</div></div><div class="t13ne-wound"><div><strong class="t13ne-woundname">Flesh</strong><details><summary></summary><strong>Flesh</strong><p class="t13ne-base-pips">Base Pips to summon: 5</p><p class="t13ne-min-pips">Minimum Pips Required: 5</p><p class="t13ne-desc">The Target is wounded and must stop and aknowledge that fact.</p><details class="t13ne-roll"><summary>Rules</summary>Lose one <strong>die</strong> from each pool on the next roll you make (e.g.3d6 + 2d10+5 becomes 2d6 + 1d10+5) then Discard</details><details class="t13ne-ordeal"><summary>Ordeal Rules</summary>Discard an Ordeal card from the next Full-Action or aggravate another Wound 1 Level and then Discard. </details><details class="t13ne-psych"><summary>Psych Wounds</summary><details class="t13ne-negative"><summary>Negative Psych</summary><p class="t13ne-normal"><strong>Normal</strong>Cannot approach source of negative emotion until Stabilized / Discarded</p><p class="t13ne-extreme"><strong>Extreme</strong>Paralysed (All Proficiency Dice are lost) until Stabilised / Discarded.</p></details><details class="t13ne-positive"><summary>Positive Psych</summary><p class="t13ne-normal"><strong>Normal</strong>Unwilling to leave source of positive emotion until discarded</p><p class="t13ne-extreme"><strong>Extreme</strong>Stimulated/Excited. Discard immediately, along with all GRT and RT Chips and act immediately (still with the dice or card penalties).</p></details></details></details></div></div><div class="t13ne-tarot-svg"><details><summary class="t13ne-wyrdtarot"><strong> Wyrd Tarot: </strong> Surprised</summary><p class="t13ne-normal"><strong> Normal: </strong>Target is immediately affected by GRT of 2</p><p class="t13ne-crossed"><strong> Crossed: </strong>Target may disregard current RT, GRT, Pool Size and take a Full-Action.</p></details></div><div class="t13ne-yarn-fancy">
			<details><summary><strong>Yarn</strong> <br>The Shield</summary>
			<span class="t13ne-description">The Shield card stands for those that defend us, and those who in turn defend them. The Shield is defence, armour, the thin blue line and a standing army. It can mean that something needs defending, that an attack is coming, and serves as a warning.</span>
			<span class="t13ne-significator">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-significator"><strong>Significator</strong></summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-plot"><strong>Plot: </strong> The Shield Plot seeks to defend a position. The Plot may favour a particular Plot Resolution, and will seek to actively defend that position against the Characters. Such Plots can be frustrating or exciting depending upon the situation, and the Players actions and resolve. The Shield Plot usually protects the status-quo of the world, always seeking to return the normal balance to the local universe, but it can be overcome and great change brought about through that paradigm shift.</aside>
					<aside class="t13ne-char"><strong>Character: </strong> The Shield Significator indicates a Character who acts to protect society, as well as individuals. They Gain Yin when they defend the status-quo, or a weaker Character. Gain Yang when they attack an enemy of the state, criminal or traitor to their society. They Gain Chi when they protect any group or pact which they are part of. They gain +1 Success Levels to all active defences.</aside>
					<aside class="t13ne-scene"><strong>Scene: </strong> The Shield Plot seeks to defend a position. The Plot may favour a particular Plot Resolution, and will seek to actively defend that position against the Characters. Such Plots can be frustrating or exciting depending upon the situation, and the Players actions and resolve. The Shield Plot usually protects the status-quo of the world, always seeking to return the normal balance to the local universe, but it can be overcome and great change brought about through that paradigm shift.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-scene">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-scene-type">
						<strong>Scene: </strong> Revelation
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">Revelations reveal something about the Conflict or Plot to the Characters. Typically a Revelation tells the Characters something about the Villain, or the Prize they are after. Revelations usually consist of 2-5 cards that define: What the information is about, what the actual info is, the vector the Revelation is revealed by, and per haps some Alternative Information that might be revealed (in case you can't use the Info), and a detail that is involved somehow.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-conflict-embodiment">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-conflict-embodiment">
						<strong>Conflict Embodiment: </strong> Tension
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">The Conflict embodies within two Characters, but they do not come into direct Conflict. They have some relationship, that Embodies the Conflict as arguments, discussions, jokes, etc.  common to Romance and Buddy movies.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>

			<span class="t13ne-facet-embodiment">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-facet-embodiment">
						<strong>Facet Embodiment: </strong> Core
					</summary>
					<section class="t13ne-description">The Conflict Embodies one Facet as a Character Core. This can create very satisfying embodiments that can be suitable for most Genres.</section>
				</details>
			</span>
			 <div class="t13ne-archetypes"><details><summary><strong>Archetype Character: </strong>Parent</summary> <details class="t13ne-Persona"><summary><strong>Persona: </strong><span class="t13ne-output">Guard</span>
      </summary>
      <details class="t13ne-motivation"><summary><strong>Motivation: </strong></summary><span class="t13ne-description">The Guard is driven by a desire to protect others. They may be drawn to protect certain groups, or Locations, by some spiritual need, or they might feel responsible and protect those threatened by a certain group. Then again some Guards just protect what they are paid to protect, operating entirely as a mercenary.</span></details>
       <details class="t13ne-avoid"><summary><strong>Avoid: </strong></summary><span class="t13ne-description">Guards avoid nothing in the protecting of their charges. Some will sacrifice even their own lives to protect others, but others may not even sacrifice their sleep.</span></details>
       <details class="t13ne-gain-chi"><summary><strong>Gain Chi: </strong></summary><span class="t13ne-description"><span>Gain Chi when you plan, speak, or act out of security concerns.</span><p>Ordeal gain Chi when you play a ♣ in defence.</p></span></details></details>   <details class="t13ne-core"><summary><strong>Core: </strong><span class="t13ne-output">Aggressor</span></summary><span class="t13ne-description">Gain Chi when you lose your temper, shout, or argue.</span></details><details class="t13ne-hitch"><summary><strong>Hitch: </strong><span class="t13ne-output">Devotion</span></summary><span class="t13ne-description">What some people do from duty others do from Devotion. The power of True Love can bend people to its needs again and again, whether it be Romantic, Familial or Religious in nature, this is the Hitch for those who Love.</span></details><p class="t13ne-description">The Parent is an archetype as common as the Mother, or the Father, they are seen as protectors, but family situations often cause arguments.</p></details></div>
			 <span class="t13ne-hook">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-hook">
						<strong>Hook: </strong> Tone-Setter
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">The Conflict Hooks the Character with a powerful, Tone setting Scene. The Tone should reflect the Dominant side of the Conflict, usually an under represented Facet on that side of the Conflict. This tone setting serves as a warning of what to expect later.</aside>
					<details class="t13ne-hook-aspect">
						<summary><strong>Hook Aspect: </strong>I-Ching</summary>
						<section class="t13ne-description">The Hook targets the Character's I-Ching. For example: If a Character has the Cauldron (50) I-Ching, then the Hook needs to have someone hungry turn up asking for help.</section>
					</details>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-obstacle-fancy"><details class="t13ne-obstacle"><summary><strong>Obstacle:</strong>Defences</summary><p class="t13ne-description">The way ahead is blocked by some shield, walls, fences, gates, doors, guards, or perhaps automated laser turrets. Twice the normal Difficulty to pass or disable.</p></details></span>
			<span class="t13ne-gain">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-gain">
						<strong>Gain </strong> 
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">A Defence Descendant (armour, gauntlets, shield, anti-virus software, insurance, etc)</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-fray">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-fray">
						<strong>Fray: </strong> Raise Shields
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">This is total war, and during total war you do not go into battle without putting on armour, strapping on a shield, and raising your deflector fields. This is an Extreme Stakes Opposed Ordeal, where the PCs usually defend a position, vehicle or person that's under attack.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-snag">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-snag">
						<strong>Snag: </strong> Redoubt
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">The Fray is set at least partially in a fortress, police station, dungeon, maximum security prison, airbase, or similarly well-defended place. During the Redoubt Snag every Stage has at least one Obstacle and double the number normally expected in this sort of Ordeal. Stakes will be raised one higher (and Black Twos Wild), and add in as many Vex and Chorus Extras as you think the PCs can possibly handle, and then a handful of Cast Extras on top, that will be scattered throughout the Redoubt.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-sweeping">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-sweeping">
						<strong>Sweeping: </strong> Protected
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">The PCs will be protected, usually by an Ally and/or Location from a Warp (The Ends, The Fray &amp; The Snag) that <strong><em>does</em></strong> affect the other side(s) of the Conflict.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
		 <details class="t13ne-revelation-fancy"><summary><strong>Revelation</strong></summary><details class="t13ne-about"><summary><strong>About: </strong> The Story </summary><p class="t13ne-description">The information is about the current situation and plot. It might include information about one side of the Conflict, or all. It is normal when providing information to only reveal information about a single Side of the Conflict, usually the Dominant or Above sides.</p></details><details class="t13ne-info"><summary><strong>Info: </strong> Unbelievable Truth </summary><p class="t13ne-description">The information presented is completely true, but is presented in such an unbelievable way... The assault troops of the enemy have painted their body armour with a phosphorescent paint, which makes them look like an army of skeletons, or the most powerful assassin in the guild appears to be a cute four-year old.</p></details><details class="t13ne-vector"><summary><strong>Vector: </strong> Screams </summary><p class="t13ne-description">Screams are loud cries, and piercing noises. They often pass us information about murders, assaults, and whether Darren should just leave it — because he's not worth it, those sorts of things.</p></details><details class="t13ne-alternate"><summary><strong>Alternate: </strong> Contest </summary><p class="t13ne-description">The information is about a Contest or Ordeal that the Embodiment, was or will be involved in. It might be details of what happened in the last tournament they fought in, or just the schedule for the next day's race. Occasionally the Contest may even be the Embodiment itself.</p></details><details class="t13ne-detail"><summary><strong>Detail: </strong> Ambient </summary><p class="t13ne-description">Ambient details are atmospheric touches and subtle clues about the Embodiment. They are the hints of lilly scent on the air near the vampire, the shadows deepening as the demon draws breath, or the ultra-high pitched electronic whine that only children and animals can hear, that the otherwise perfect android makes.</p></details></details>
		 <details class="t13ne-ordeal-fancy"><summary><strong>Ordeal</strong></summary><p class="t13ne-ordeal-type"><strong>Ordeal-Type </strong> — Opposed Ordeal</p><p class="t13ne-stages"><strong>Stages </strong> — 3</p><p class="t13ne-ordeal-spread"><strong>Ordeal-Spread </strong> — Forking</p><p class="t13ne-stakes"><strong>Stakes </strong> — High</p><p class="t13ne-test"><strong>Test </strong> — Emotional Intensity (Fury)</p><p class="t13ne-obstacles"><strong>Obstacles </strong> — 1 Obstacle in every Court Stage</p><p class="t13ne-suggested-action"><strong>Suggested-Action </strong> — Swim</p><details class="t13ne-stage"><summary><strong>Stage: </strong> Water </summary><p class="t13ne-description">The way ahead is flooded. It might be an ocean, sea, lake, a river, a pond, or a storm drain, but there is water ahead.</p></details><details class="t13ne-motional"><summary><strong>Motional: </strong> Water Vehicle Chase </summary><p class="t13ne-description">Roaring engines, skipping hulls and Roster Plume wakes, or whistling wind, humming lines and the slap of waves on wood, either way, they'll get away, unless you catch or kill them!</p></details><details class="t13ne-fight"><summary><strong>Fight: </strong> Minor Guardian </summary><p class="t13ne-description">Either a solitary Monster or small team (2-3) of Chorus Extras guard the way ahead. <!-- t13ne-shortcode "%7B%22type%22%3A%22facetsuitaspect%22%2C%22suit%22%3A%223%22%2C%22aspect%22%3A%22Monster%22%7D"// --></p><div class="t13ne-tablewrap"><table class="t13ne-table Monster"><thead><tr><th>&nbsp;&nbsp;Facet&nbsp;&nbsp;</th><th>&nbsp;&nbsp;Monster&nbsp;&nbsp;</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td>Burden</td><td><strong>Object</strong> —The Monster is an object imbued with the semblance of life. Artificial in nature usually, the living object is usually tough and strong. An Object Monster is created with a Monster Pool equal to their Burden Score. Objects are able to multiply their Scores by 1+Monster Pool Size when appropriate to their form and function. The Object Monster may instantly Prepare cards from its Monster Pool, often as a Prepared Defence or Stunt Preparation, as appropriate for the Object's form or function.</td></tr><tr><td>Dominion</td><td><strong>Horde</strong> —The Monster is a group or swarm of lesser monsters, it might be an Army, a pack of Bulmäs, a Fortress or a Battle Moon. Horde is effectively a Pact Descendant. A Horde may pool Values to create larger rolls, however they may never create a Boon greater than their Value. Hordes may add Dominion Draw cards to their Monster Pool when the Horde increases in numbers. The Horde must Play at least one card from their Monster Pool on any action that splits the Horde (like losing an individual). E.g. two Vex Extras of Boon 10 (Value 45) that form a Horde become a single Extra of Boon 16 (Value 90), (with probably 1 card in the Monster Pool) but could never create a Boon greater than the individual's Value(45). If there were 11 Vexes, that would be Boon 44 (Value 495). 12 or One million Vexes would both give Boon 45 (Value 530) since that is the most powerful their Horde can get.</td></tr><tr><td>Fury</td><td><strong>Temperamental</strong> —The Monster is the embodiment of an Emotion, usually a negative one. Such Monsters are often capable of supernatural actions, such as telepathy, shape-shifting, or even undying. The Vampire is an embodiment of Lust, with powers of mind control, telepathy and suggestion, as well as shape-shifting abilities. The Werewolf is berserk rage, and almost indestructible unless Silver is used. The Beansidhe is the embodiment of mourning, and able to sense an oncoming death. When they experience their negative emotion the Monster may refill their Monster Pool to Fury Score cards. When appropriate to their Emotion they must add a Monster Pool card to the action and multiply the total Score by their remaining Monster Pool Reduced.</td></tr><tr><td>Rook</td><td><strong>Guardian</strong> —The Monster is a Guardian, it guards a certain territory, path, treasure or person. Guardians may add Rook Draw to their Monster Pool whenever they act as a guard, try to interfere with an attack, or are attacked. The Guardian reduces all attacks by a number of Pips equal to the current Monster Pool automatically. The Guardian will however automatically play cards from its Monster Pool to defend itself (via any Active or Passive defence method that makes sense to the Referee. From Smaug to Fafnir dragons are often Guardians, looking after a particular treasure or demanding tributes from a territory.</td></tr><tr><td>Sin</td><td><strong>Villain</strong> —The Sin Monster is an embodiment of a specific vice, taboo, or sin, often in the form of a human Villain (often with animalistic features or tendencies). They usually have Annexes and Hitches that reflect their embodied vice or sin, and are often at least partially supernatural. Villains add Sin Draw cards to their Monster Pool when they, or another (usually both) indulges in their particular vice or behaviour. Which can be as broad as "Evil" if you like. Villains may add +1 Score for each card in their Monster Pool for any action that furthers their diabolical plans. Villains automatically spend Monster Pool cards on escape actions when attacked, creating at least Sin Draw Obstacles from the Monster Pools cards. The genocidal or narcissistic maniac, of the sort British Intelligence seems to tangle with repeatedly, could be a Sin Monster. The ever-hungry cannibal spirit, the Wendigo is definitely an example, as is the Sexual-predatory Lust-embodied Vampire.</td></tr><tr><td>Wyrd</td><td><strong>Doom-weaver</strong> —The monster is a creature of the Fæ, it can draw and play Yarn cards or Wyrd Tarot cards (as appropriate). The Wyrd Monster may play cards from its Monster Pool as Wyrd Tarot or Yarn cards. The Monster may draw and add Wyrd Draw cards to its Monster Pool whenever it makes a choice, decision, or judgement. The Doom-weaver may draw and play Wyrd Draw cards as Ordeal cards whenever anyone plays Wyrd Tarot or Yarn cards. They must play a card from the Monster Pool if they have to undo a choice, decision or judgement they have made. Doom-Weavers come in many forms from the Cait-Sith, the Beansidhe, to the Faery nobles of Ironmaster.</td></tr></tbody><tfoot><tr><td>&nbsp;&nbsp;Facet&nbsp;&nbsp;</td><td>&nbsp;&nbsp;Monster&nbsp;&nbsp;</td></tr></tfoot></table></div><p></p></details></details>
		 </details>
			</div>
				<div class="t13ne-card-lea"><details class="t13ne-lea"><summary><strong>Lea</strong></summary><p class="t13ne-colour"><strong>Path Colour —</strong> Green/Turquoise Herbivore</p><p class="t13ne-bulmas"><strong>Allowed Bulmäs —</strong> Herd, Broccen, Vermis, Labyrinthine, All-Walkers</p><p class="t13ne-animal-category"><strong>Animal Category —</strong> Macrofauna</p><p class="t13ne-animal-example"><strong>Example Animal —</strong> Voles / Hamsters / Pika </p><p class="t13ne-notes"><strong>Notes —</strong> Small (often burrowing) creatures</p></details></div></div><div class="t13ne-cardwrap-jsvg" id="cardrewritebox31" data-mode="jsvg" data-wyrd="1" data-yarn="1"><img src="https://www.cjmoseley.dev.cc/wp-content/plugins/t13ne/images/cards-svg/6C.svg" class="t13ne-svgcard"><div class="t13ne-svgpips"><div class="t13ne-textpips">6<!-- write suit--><span class="t13ne-symbol"><span class="t13ne-blackcard">♣</span></span><details class="t13ne-suit"><summary></summary><p class="t13ne-name"><strong>Suit: </strong>Clubs <span class="t13ne-blackcard">♣</span></p><p><strong>Ordeal Trump: </strong><span class="t13ne-trump">Defence</span></p><p class="t13ne-desc"><strong>Description: </strong>Clubs represent the power of politics and a stoic materialism. In the Tarot the Clubs are called Staves or Wands, in some traditions they represent Intellectual Air, but in most they are Fire.</p><p class="t13ne-tarot"><strong>Tarot Meaning: </strong>Power, Fame, Politics, Ability, Money.</p><p class="t13ne-otext"><strong>Ordeal Text: </strong>Ordeal Types: Wealth, Politics, Power. Clubs act as Trump for following Actions: Bartering, Controlling, Corrupting, Emotional-intensity, Guarding, Magic, Displaying might, Politics, Self-Defence, Socializing, and Trade Negotiations. Clubs can be used to defend any type of attack.</p><p class="t13ne-parry"><strong>Parry: </strong><strong>Disarm:</strong> All the cards of the attack are immediately discarded, along with the Parry Card. The remaining cards of the Defender's Parry can now each be used to cause the Attacker to drop a Descendant (starting with the attacking weapon usually), can also cut armour straps, belts etc, or steal hats. This basically adds RT equal to the card Pips to the Annex that was used to attack. Every 5 Pips also creates a point of GRT for the Character (representing how much time the Character must spend picking up, or readying the weapon again. Court cards additionally may damage the Descendant that was disarmed, as standard for the Stakes.</p><p class="t13ne-soak"><strong>Soak: </strong><strong>Ward:</strong> The matched Attack card, and one other randomly selected Attack card are discarded.</p></details></div></div><div class="t13ne-svg-facet"><div>Domion</div></div><div class="t13ne-wound"><div><strong class="t13ne-woundname">Flesh</strong><details><summary></summary><strong>Flesh</strong><p class="t13ne-base-pips">Base Pips to summon: 5</p><p class="t13ne-min-pips">Minimum Pips Required: 5</p><p class="t13ne-desc">The Target is wounded and must stop and aknowledge that fact.</p><details class="t13ne-roll"><summary>Rules</summary>Lose one <strong>die</strong> from each pool on the next roll you make (e.g.3d6 + 2d10+5 becomes 2d6 + 1d10+5) then Discard</details><details class="t13ne-ordeal"><summary>Ordeal Rules</summary>Discard an Ordeal card from the next Full-Action or aggravate another Wound 1 Level and then Discard. </details><details class="t13ne-psych"><summary>Psych Wounds</summary><details class="t13ne-negative"><summary>Negative Psych</summary><p class="t13ne-normal"><strong>Normal</strong>Cannot approach source of negative emotion until Stabilized / Discarded</p><p class="t13ne-extreme"><strong>Extreme</strong>Paralysed (All Proficiency Dice are lost) until Stabilised / Discarded.</p></details><details class="t13ne-positive"><summary>Positive Psych</summary><p class="t13ne-normal"><strong>Normal</strong>Unwilling to leave source of positive emotion until discarded</p><p class="t13ne-extreme"><strong>Extreme</strong>Stimulated/Excited. Discard immediately, along with all GRT and RT Chips and act immediately (still with the dice or card penalties).</p></details></details></details></div></div><div class="t13ne-tarot-svg"><details><summary class="t13ne-wyrdtarot"><strong> Wyrd Tarot: </strong> Stalemate</summary><p class="t13ne-normal"><strong> Normal: </strong>Target may automatically Stalemate any Test (Scoring exactly the Difficulty). Automatically pay a Stage / Obstacle cost in an Ordeal.</p><p class="t13ne-crossed"><strong> Crossed: </strong>Cancels a Stalemate with a random Success Level up or down. Card Draws are randomly +/- 1 Card.</p></details></div><div class="t13ne-yarn-fancy">
			<details><summary><strong>Yarn</strong> <br>The Horde</summary>
			<span class="t13ne-description">The Horde card signifies a huge crowd and the Madness of crowds. Hordes are not calmly queuing citizens. Hordes ravage, pillage and loot, they are rioting, revolutionary, reactionary things. They may be formed from a stampede, panic amongst an orderly crowd can quickly create an unthinking horde.</span>
			<span class="t13ne-significator">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-significator"><strong>Significator</strong></summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-plot"><strong>Plot: </strong> The Horde Plot is about numbers, usually big numbers, occasionally there may be literal mathematics involved, but more normally this is a tale with scores of Characters and plenty of sides. These are Epic plots usually that reflect society from top to bottom, and may span multiple kingdoms or worlds. The main weapon of the Horde Plot is the Sub-Plot, and you can expect additional Arcs and Stories galore, which each Character having at least an Arc of their own, that will interact at least once with one other Character's Story.</aside>
					<aside class="t13ne-char"><strong>Character: </strong> The Horde Significator indicates a Character who is legion. They might be a hive mind, or a linear reincarnation with multi-life recall. They Gain Yin when they focus on the bigger picture, ignore details, or act without thinking. They Gain Yang when they react strongly to something they've seen or heard, or share information with others. They Gain Chi when they work or attack with others. They gain +1 Success Levels to all actions where they can use their Horde lives together (multiple bodies or information from past lives, etc).</aside>
					<aside class="t13ne-scene"><strong>Scene: </strong> The Horde Plot is about numbers, usually big numbers, occasionally there may be literal mathematics involved, but more normally this is a tale with scores of Characters and plenty of sides. These are Epic plots usually that reflect society from top to bottom, and may span multiple kingdoms or worlds. The main weapon of the Horde Plot is the Sub-Plot, and you can expect additional Arcs and Stories galore, which each Character having at least an Arc of their own, that will interact at least once with one other Character's Story.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-scene">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-scene-type">
						<strong>Scene: </strong> Warp
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">Warps work the Conflict. They are broken into 3 parts (and require another 2 cards): <ul><li>The Ends (What the Players want, usually described as Quests ). Does not require a Yarn Card.</li><li>The Fray (what keeps the Characters from their Ends, how do they work through that Quest?). The Fray requires an extra Yarn Card.</li><li>The Snag (The complication that renders the actions of the Characters winning through the Fray moot somehow). The Snag also requires another Yarn Card.</li></ul>During Warps things tend to get worse for the Pressed side of the Conflict.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-conflict-embodiment">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-conflict-embodiment">
						<strong>Conflict Embodiment: </strong> Tension
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">The Conflict embodies within two Characters, but they do not come into direct Conflict. They have some relationship, that Embodies the Conflict as arguments, discussions, jokes, etc.  common to Romance and Buddy movies.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>

			<span class="t13ne-facet-embodiment">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-facet-embodiment">
						<strong>Facet Embodiment: </strong> Hitch
					</summary>
					<section class="t13ne-description">The Conflict Embodies one Facet as a Character Hitch. This can create quite secretive and subtle embodiments that can be very difficult to spot. Other times Hitch Embodiments can be very obvious, and in the case of Woes, glaring.</section>
				</details>
			</span>
			 <div class="t13ne-archetypes"><details><summary><strong>Archetype Character: </strong>Zombie</summary> <details class="t13ne-Persona"><summary><strong>Persona: </strong><span class="t13ne-output">Fiend</span>
      </summary>
      <details class="t13ne-motivation"><summary><strong>Motivation: </strong></summary><span class="t13ne-description">The Fiend is motivated by selfishness, hatred, corruption, and the temptations of amoral living. They are well represented amongst criminal elements, and often seem to delight in simply breaking rules and laws.</span></details>
       <details class="t13ne-avoid"><summary><strong>Avoid: </strong></summary><span class="t13ne-description">The Fiend sees no reason to follow the rules of the rest of society, and will often deliberately break moral codes simply because they are moral codes.</span></details>
       <details class="t13ne-gain-chi"><summary><strong>Gain Chi: </strong></summary><span class="t13ne-description"><span>Gain Chi when you break a rule or law (including etiquette and unwritten rules), act “evil”, rude, or self-important, or succumb to temptations. </span><p>Ordeal gain Chi when you discard a low card to keep a higher one.</p></span></details></details>   <details class="t13ne-core"><summary><strong>Core: </strong><span class="t13ne-output">Consumer</span></summary><span class="t13ne-description">Gain Chi when you consume something (food, cigarette, pills, drugs, vapes, money, patches, credits, alcohol, syringes, human hearts, bullets, souls, etc -- pick one).</span></details><details class="t13ne-hitch"><summary><strong>Hitch: </strong><span class="t13ne-output">Doomed</span></summary><span class="t13ne-description">Cursed, star-crossed, destined, fated, or just inevitably dying. This is a flexible Hitch that can cover a lot of scenarios when the gods just seem to have it in for you.</span></details><p class="t13ne-description">The Zombie can be an example of the walking dead, stumbling or running to consume the next thing. Sometimes they are even undead, mostly they are just sleepwalking through life, staring at a phone screen.</p></details></div>
			 <span class="t13ne-hook">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-hook">
						<strong>Hook: </strong> Crowd Scene
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">The story opens with a crowd scene, perhaps a sporting event, concert, theatre performance or just a busy shopping district. This scene can be Tone setting, with musical leading the way, or brutal sports, or it could be just the setting for a sudden burst of action from a Fray.</aside>
					<details class="t13ne-hook-aspect">
						<summary><strong>Hook Aspect: </strong>I-Ching</summary>
						<section class="t13ne-description">The Hook targets the Character's I-Ching. For example: If a Character has the Cauldron (50) I-Ching, then the Hook needs to have someone hungry turn up asking for help.</section>
					</details>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-obstacle-fancy"><details class="t13ne-obstacle"><summary><strong>Obstacle:</strong>Horde</summary><p class="t13ne-description">The way ahead may be blocked by a horde, army, rioting crowd, swarm of vermin, or similar danger. The Horde is a powerful Pact of Chorus or Vex Extras, the Pact should be large enough to mimic at least a Minor Guardian.</p></details></span>
			<span class="t13ne-gain">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-gain">
						<strong>Gain </strong> 
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">Membership (new Pack Descendant or Level of same)</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-fray">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-fray">
						<strong>Fray: </strong> Stampede
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">This Ordeal is usually a high Stakes every one for themselves Motional Ordeal. Stampedes are often caused by another disaster, such as earthquakes, tsunamis, mudslides, floods, fires, and terrorists. The point is huge crowds become very dangerous when everyone starts running in the same direction. Architects design buildings to minimise these risks, but people get both inventive and stupid when the pressure is on.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-snag">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-snag">
						<strong>Snag: </strong> Numbers
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">The Numbers Snag can add Numbers to the enemy, multiplying the number of Extras by at least two. This can mean additional forces during the Fray, or it can mean reinforcements afterwards. in some Frays Numbers wouldn't hurt, so follow those with a High Stakes Opposed Ordeal against a Horde of Chorus or Vex Extras.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-sweeping">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-sweeping">
						<strong>Sweeping: </strong> Party
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">The PCs and the pressed side get to party and congregate, letting their hair down together, this can be a bonding moment, or the Opposed Side Embodiment may suffer a Snag while the party is happening. </aside>
				</details>
			</span>
		 <details class="t13ne-revelation-fancy"><summary><strong>Revelation</strong></summary><details class="t13ne-about"><summary><strong>About: </strong> The Story </summary><p class="t13ne-description">The information is about the current situation and plot. It might include information about one side of the Conflict, or all. It is normal when providing information to only reveal information about a single Side of the Conflict, usually the Dominant or Above sides.</p></details><details class="t13ne-info"><summary><strong>Info: </strong> Organization </summary><p class="t13ne-description">Information about how the Embodiment is organized. Are they part of an army, a guild or group, or do they act alone? Are they part of something larger than themselves?</p></details><details class="t13ne-vector"><summary><strong>Vector: </strong> Roars </summary><p class="t13ne-description">Roars can tell us a lot, often useful things like, there is a bear in the camp, or sometimes "important" things, like a goal has been scored, or a couple has kissed. They can be an expression of rage, or simply the noise of a crowd.</p></details><details class="t13ne-alternate"><summary><strong>Alternate: </strong> Industry </summary><p class="t13ne-description">This can be knowledge about what a person does, or the industries of a Location, or it may be a statement about how hard the Embodiment will work against you.</p></details><details class="t13ne-detail"><summary><strong>Detail: </strong> Theoretical </summary><p class="t13ne-description">Theoretical details can either be detailed theories (or hypotheses) themselves, potential details, that might not exist, but people think they should, or things that are not disproven, but not proven either.</p></details></details>
		 <details class="t13ne-ordeal-fancy"><summary><strong>Ordeal</strong></summary><p class="t13ne-ordeal-type"><strong>Ordeal-Type </strong> — Opposed Ordeal</p><p class="t13ne-stages"><strong>Stages </strong> — 4</p><p class="t13ne-ordeal-spread"><strong>Ordeal-Spread </strong> — Pyramid</p><p class="t13ne-stakes"><strong>Stakes </strong> — Low</p><p class="t13ne-test"><strong>Test </strong> — Psionics (Fury)</p><p class="t13ne-obstacles"><strong>Obstacles </strong> — 1 Obstacle in every Court Stage</p><p class="t13ne-suggested-action"><strong>Suggested-Action </strong> — Shout</p><details class="t13ne-stage"><summary><strong>Stage: </strong> Mental </summary><p class="t13ne-description">It may be that the way ahead is simply something you have to think about, you may have to strategize. Then again the route may be through a Laboratory, a Library, or similar. Then again maybe the way ahead is bedlam, filled with loud noises, sudden flashes of light, or some other distractions.</p></details><details class="t13ne-motional"><summary><strong>Motional: </strong> Aerial Riding Chase </summary><p class="t13ne-description">The prisoners! They are escaping on para-wings / hover-boards / giant eagles! Quick, after them!</p></details><details class="t13ne-fight"><summary><strong>Fight: </strong> Major Guardian </summary><p class="t13ne-description">A powerful guardian stands in the way. Usually a large force (dozens) of Chorus Extras, small group of Monster Cast Extras or a Force of Nature Monster with at least 2 ♣ Monster Facets.<!-- t13ne-shortcode "%7B%22type%22%3A%22facetsuitaspect%22%2C%22suit%22%3A%223%22%2C%22aspect%22%3A%22Monster%22%7D"// --></p><div class="t13ne-tablewrap"><table class="t13ne-table Monster"><thead><tr><th>&nbsp;&nbsp;Facet&nbsp;&nbsp;</th><th>&nbsp;&nbsp;Monster&nbsp;&nbsp;</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td>Burden</td><td><strong>Object</strong> —The Monster is an object imbued with the semblance of life. Artificial in nature usually, the living object is usually tough and strong. An Object Monster is created with a Monster Pool equal to their Burden Score. Objects are able to multiply their Scores by 1+Monster Pool Size when appropriate to their form and function. The Object Monster may instantly Prepare cards from its Monster Pool, often as a Prepared Defence or Stunt Preparation, as appropriate for the Object's form or function.</td></tr><tr><td>Dominion</td><td><strong>Horde</strong> —The Monster is a group or swarm of lesser monsters, it might be an Army, a pack of Bulmäs, a Fortress or a Battle Moon. Horde is effectively a Pact Descendant. A Horde may pool Values to create larger rolls, however they may never create a Boon greater than their Value. Hordes may add Dominion Draw cards to their Monster Pool when the Horde increases in numbers. The Horde must Play at least one card from their Monster Pool on any action that splits the Horde (like losing an individual). E.g. two Vex Extras of Boon 10 (Value 45) that form a Horde become a single Extra of Boon 16 (Value 90), (with probably 1 card in the Monster Pool) but could never create a Boon greater than the individual's Value(45). If there were 11 Vexes, that would be Boon 44 (Value 495). 12 or One million Vexes would both give Boon 45 (Value 530) since that is the most powerful their Horde can get.</td></tr><tr><td>Fury</td><td><strong>Temperamental</strong> —The Monster is the embodiment of an Emotion, usually a negative one. Such Monsters are often capable of supernatural actions, such as telepathy, shape-shifting, or even undying. The Vampire is an embodiment of Lust, with powers of mind control, telepathy and suggestion, as well as shape-shifting abilities. The Werewolf is berserk rage, and almost indestructible unless Silver is used. The Beansidhe is the embodiment of mourning, and able to sense an oncoming death. When they experience their negative emotion the Monster may refill their Monster Pool to Fury Score cards. When appropriate to their Emotion they must add a Monster Pool card to the action and multiply the total Score by their remaining Monster Pool Reduced.</td></tr><tr><td>Rook</td><td><strong>Guardian</strong> —The Monster is a Guardian, it guards a certain territory, path, treasure or person. Guardians may add Rook Draw to their Monster Pool whenever they act as a guard, try to interfere with an attack, or are attacked. The Guardian reduces all attacks by a number of Pips equal to the current Monster Pool automatically. The Guardian will however automatically play cards from its Monster Pool to defend itself (via any Active or Passive defence method that makes sense to the Referee. From Smaug to Fafnir dragons are often Guardians, looking after a particular treasure or demanding tributes from a territory.</td></tr><tr><td>Sin</td><td><strong>Villain</strong> —The Sin Monster is an embodiment of a specific vice, taboo, or sin, often in the form of a human Villain (often with animalistic features or tendencies). They usually have Annexes and Hitches that reflect their embodied vice or sin, and are often at least partially supernatural. Villains add Sin Draw cards to their Monster Pool when they, or another (usually both) indulges in their particular vice or behaviour. Which can be as broad as "Evil" if you like. Villains may add +1 Score for each card in their Monster Pool for any action that furthers their diabolical plans. Villains automatically spend Monster Pool cards on escape actions when attacked, creating at least Sin Draw Obstacles from the Monster Pools cards. The genocidal or narcissistic maniac, of the sort British Intelligence seems to tangle with repeatedly, could be a Sin Monster. The ever-hungry cannibal spirit, the Wendigo is definitely an example, as is the Sexual-predatory Lust-embodied Vampire.</td></tr><tr><td>Wyrd</td><td><strong>Doom-weaver</strong> —The monster is a creature of the Fæ, it can draw and play Yarn cards or Wyrd Tarot cards (as appropriate). The Wyrd Monster may play cards from its Monster Pool as Wyrd Tarot or Yarn cards. The Monster may draw and add Wyrd Draw cards to its Monster Pool whenever it makes a choice, decision, or judgement. The Doom-weaver may draw and play Wyrd Draw cards as Ordeal cards whenever anyone plays Wyrd Tarot or Yarn cards. They must play a card from the Monster Pool if they have to undo a choice, decision or judgement they have made. Doom-Weavers come in many forms from the Cait-Sith, the Beansidhe, to the Faery nobles of Ironmaster.</td></tr></tbody><tfoot><tr><td>&nbsp;&nbsp;Facet&nbsp;&nbsp;</td><td>&nbsp;&nbsp;Monster&nbsp;&nbsp;</td></tr></tfoot></table></div><p></p></details></details>
		 </details>
			</div>
				<div class="t13ne-card-lea"><details class="t13ne-lea"><summary><strong>Lea</strong></summary><p class="t13ne-colour"><strong>Path Colour —</strong> Green/Turquoise Herbivore</p><p class="t13ne-bulmas"><strong>Allowed Bulmäs —</strong> Herd, Broccen, Vermis, Labyrinthine, All-Walkers</p><p class="t13ne-animal-category"><strong>Animal Category —</strong> Megafauna – Tiny</p><p class="t13ne-animal-example"><strong>Example Animal —</strong> Most herbivorous birds (Parrot) / Rabbit / Hare /  Iguana / Fruit-bat</p><p class="t13ne-notes"><strong>Notes —</strong> Animals smaller than 40 Kilograms</p></details></div></div><div class="t13ne-cardwrap-jsvg" id="cardrewritebox32" data-mode="jsvg" data-wyrd="1" data-yarn="1"><img src="https://www.cjmoseley.dev.cc/wp-content/plugins/t13ne/images/cards-svg/7C.svg" class="t13ne-svgcard"><div class="t13ne-svgpips"><div class="t13ne-textpips">7<!-- write suit--><span class="t13ne-symbol"><span class="t13ne-blackcard">♣</span></span><details class="t13ne-suit"><summary></summary><p class="t13ne-name"><strong>Suit: </strong>Clubs <span class="t13ne-blackcard">♣</span></p><p><strong>Ordeal Trump: </strong><span class="t13ne-trump">Defence</span></p><p class="t13ne-desc"><strong>Description: </strong>Clubs represent the power of politics and a stoic materialism. In the Tarot the Clubs are called Staves or Wands, in some traditions they represent Intellectual Air, but in most they are Fire.</p><p class="t13ne-tarot"><strong>Tarot Meaning: </strong>Power, Fame, Politics, Ability, Money.</p><p class="t13ne-otext"><strong>Ordeal Text: </strong>Ordeal Types: Wealth, Politics, Power. Clubs act as Trump for following Actions: Bartering, Controlling, Corrupting, Emotional-intensity, Guarding, Magic, Displaying might, Politics, Self-Defence, Socializing, and Trade Negotiations. Clubs can be used to defend any type of attack.</p><p class="t13ne-parry"><strong>Parry: </strong><strong>Disarm:</strong> All the cards of the attack are immediately discarded, along with the Parry Card. The remaining cards of the Defender's Parry can now each be used to cause the Attacker to drop a Descendant (starting with the attacking weapon usually), can also cut armour straps, belts etc, or steal hats. This basically adds RT equal to the card Pips to the Annex that was used to attack. Every 5 Pips also creates a point of GRT for the Character (representing how much time the Character must spend picking up, or readying the weapon again. Court cards additionally may damage the Descendant that was disarmed, as standard for the Stakes.</p><p class="t13ne-soak"><strong>Soak: </strong><strong>Ward:</strong> The matched Attack card, and one other randomly selected Attack card are discarded.</p></details></div></div><div class="t13ne-svg-facet"><div>Burden</div></div><div class="t13ne-wound"><div><strong class="t13ne-woundname">Flesh</strong><details><summary></summary><strong>Flesh</strong><p class="t13ne-base-pips">Base Pips to summon: 5</p><p class="t13ne-min-pips">Minimum Pips Required: 5</p><p class="t13ne-desc">The Target is wounded and must stop and aknowledge that fact.</p><details class="t13ne-roll"><summary>Rules</summary>Lose one <strong>die</strong> from each pool on the next roll you make (e.g.3d6 + 2d10+5 becomes 2d6 + 1d10+5) then Discard</details><details class="t13ne-ordeal"><summary>Ordeal Rules</summary>Discard an Ordeal card from the next Full-Action or aggravate another Wound 1 Level and then Discard. </details><details class="t13ne-psych"><summary>Psych Wounds</summary><details class="t13ne-negative"><summary>Negative Psych</summary><p class="t13ne-normal"><strong>Normal</strong>Cannot approach source of negative emotion until Stabilized / Discarded</p><p class="t13ne-extreme"><strong>Extreme</strong>Paralysed (All Proficiency Dice are lost) until Stabilised / Discarded.</p></details><details class="t13ne-positive"><summary>Positive Psych</summary><p class="t13ne-normal"><strong>Normal</strong>Unwilling to leave source of positive emotion until discarded</p><p class="t13ne-extreme"><strong>Extreme</strong>Stimulated/Excited. Discard immediately, along with all GRT and RT Chips and act immediately (still with the dice or card penalties).</p></details></details></details></div></div><div class="t13ne-tarot-svg"><details><summary class="t13ne-wyrdtarot"><strong> Wyrd Tarot: </strong> Failure</summary><p class="t13ne-normal"><strong> Normal: </strong>Target rolls a Complete Failure or Draws minimum.</p><p class="t13ne-crossed"><strong> Crossed: </strong>+2 Success Levels to one roll, or Ordeal Card.</p></details></div><div class="t13ne-yarn-fancy">
			<details><summary><strong>Yarn</strong> <br>The Crystal</summary>
			<span class="t13ne-description">The Crystal represents both riches and the fragility of finery. Riches are easily bestowed by the card, but easily taken away again too. The Crystal can represent a change of circumstances, clarity of purpose, or signify a flaw or weakness that is hidden, but very important.</span>
			<span class="t13ne-significator">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-significator"><strong>Significator</strong></summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-plot"><strong>Plot: </strong> The Crystal Plot is about changing circumstances and fortunes. It may appear like a Rags-To-Riches story, but there is more emphasis on realising that money is essentially worthless, and that human connections are more important than money.</aside>
					<aside class="t13ne-char"><strong>Character: </strong> The Crystal Significator indicates a Character who so rich that money has no meaning to them. They Gain Yin when they listen to another talk. They Gain Yang when they give advice, aid or time to another. They Gain Chi when they give Chi or Descendants to other Characters. They gain +1 Success Level on all Purchase or summoning Tests and Ordeal actions.</aside>
					<aside class="t13ne-scene"><strong>Scene: </strong> The Crystal Plot is about changing circumstances and fortunes. It may appear like a Rags-To-Riches story, but there is more emphasis on realising that money is essentially worthless, and that human connections are more important than money.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-scene">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-scene-type">
						<strong>Scene: </strong> Weft
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">Wefts work the Conflict through 3 Parts: <ul><li>First by the Characters Recoiling from the Snag (They emotionally respond to the previous event) which does not require a Yarn card</li><li>Secondly Sweeping (where the Characters recover from what happened) which does require another Yarn Card</li><li>Finally, Picking (Where the Characters will have a choice how the Story will proceed) which does not require Yarn Cards, but can be represented by more cards during card play.</li></ul>During Wefts the Pressed side makes a little headway, or gets a little breather.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-conflict-embodiment">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-conflict-embodiment">
