						<strong>Conflict Embodiment: </strong> Tension
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">The Conflict embodies within two Characters, but they do not come into direct Conflict. They have some relationship, that Embodies the Conflict as arguments, discussions, jokes, etc.  common to Romance and Buddy movies.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>

			<span class="t13ne-facet-embodiment">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-facet-embodiment">
						<strong>Facet Embodiment: </strong> Hitch
					</summary>
					<section class="t13ne-description">The Conflict Embodies one Facet as a Character Hitch. This can create quite secretive and subtle embodiments that can be very difficult to spot. Other times Hitch Embodiments can be very obvious, and in the case of Woes, glaring.</section>
				</details>
			</span>
			 <div class="t13ne-archetypes"><details><summary><strong>Archetype Character: </strong>Thief</summary> <details class="t13ne-Persona"><summary><strong>Persona: </strong><span class="t13ne-output">Reaper</span>
      </summary>
      <details class="t13ne-motivation"><summary><strong>Motivation: </strong></summary><span class="t13ne-description">Reapers are materialists, they may be collectors, trying to complete their collection, or just want more than anyone else. Fundamentally their motivation is one of greed.</span></details>
       <details class="t13ne-avoid"><summary><strong>Avoid: </strong></summary><span class="t13ne-description">Reapers do not like to give away anything, without receiving something in compensation, or acknowledging the debt.</span></details>
       <details class="t13ne-gain-chi"><summary><strong>Gain Chi: </strong></summary><span class="t13ne-description"><span>Gain Chi when you make, take, purchase or accept something, or ask for more.</span><p>Ordeal gain Chi when you draw a ♣.</p></span></details></details>   <details class="t13ne-core"><summary><strong>Core: </strong><span class="t13ne-output">Master</span></summary><span class="t13ne-description">The character has one chosen environment in which they automatically gain one level of success.</span></details><details class="t13ne-hitch"><summary><strong>Hitch: </strong><span class="t13ne-output">Persecuted</span></summary><span class="t13ne-description">Some people are abused or controlled by bullies, or society, others are persecuted by the gods themselves. Either way the Persecuted Hitch is how you model this.</span></details><p class="t13ne-description">Thieves see nothing wrong with taking something shiny of someone else. They usually have an environment that they prefer to operate in or on (highways, crowds, banks, homes, museums, etc), and all those wanted posters would make anyone feel persecuted.</p></details></div>
			 <span class="t13ne-hook">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-hook">
						<strong>Hook: </strong> Payment
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">The Story hooks the by offering to pay them. It could be that this plot is part of their normal job, the next case, or delivery, or it might be that they are specifically offered money to look into (or perhaps turn a blind eye to) something.</aside>
					<details class="t13ne-hook-aspect">
						<summary><strong>Hook Aspect: </strong>I-Ching</summary>
						<section class="t13ne-description">The Hook targets the Character's I-Ching. For example: If a Character has the Cauldron (50) I-Ching, then the Hook needs to have someone hungry turn up asking for help.</section>
					</details>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-obstacle-fancy"><details class="t13ne-obstacle"><summary><strong>Obstacle:</strong>Window</summary><p class="t13ne-description">The way ahead is blocked by a window, it may be broken, which will turn it from an Obstacle to a Trap, the trap must then be disarmed. It is possible to break the window and pass the trap simultaneously with a Stunt or Trick.</p></details></span>
			<span class="t13ne-gain">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-gain">
						<strong>Gain </strong> 
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">Treasure Descendant</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-fray">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-fray">
						<strong>Fray: </strong> Crystal Ball
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">The Fray is an attempt to spy upon the enemy, or predict the future somehow, this may involve a trip to a fortune-teller, or running simulations, or use of Wyrd or Yarn abilities. Regardless of method, the fray consists of trying to create a Chronolith of a suitable size to fix a point in the future, or observe the past, or the enemy's current position. This is normally done with a Solitary or Opposed Ordeal of Paradoxical Stakes.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-snag">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-snag">
						<strong>Snag: </strong> Flawed
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">The Flawed Snag can express a few different ways depending upon narrative requirements. The Snag can trigger the PCs Flaws during the Fray (or following), or it can attempt to create a new Flaw. Alternatively, the Snag can indicate that the PC's plan is flawed, and that the result of the Fray will not be what they expected, or wanted.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-sweeping">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-sweeping">
						<strong>Sweeping: </strong> Crystal Clear
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">This Sweeping grants the PCs a moment of clarity. The Referee or Yarn-Teller should use direct exposition to explain the situation properly to the PC. This can be handled as the PC making this realisation themselves, and a good Yarn-Teller can sometimes arrange this, but if the attempt fails the Yarn-Teller or Ref should be prepared to explain what's really going on. Sometimes this sort of exposition can be handled via Villain monologues, or confessions, but they are not compatible with every genre.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
		 <details class="t13ne-revelation-fancy"><summary><strong>Revelation</strong></summary><details class="t13ne-about"><summary><strong>About: </strong> The Story </summary><p class="t13ne-description">The information is about the current situation and plot. It might include information about one side of the Conflict, or all. It is normal when providing information to only reveal information about a single Side of the Conflict, usually the Dominant or Above sides.</p></details><details class="t13ne-info"><summary><strong>Info: </strong> Properties </summary><p class="t13ne-description">Information about Properties can be as simple as a Location's address, the buildings and treasures that the Embodiment owns, or it can be a list of known properties of the Embodiment or one of its abilities.</p></details><details class="t13ne-vector"><summary><strong>Vector: </strong> Wounds </summary><p class="t13ne-description">Wounds can carry information, even if it is the wounded messenger that carries them. Even the wounds on a corpse can tell us things about the height, handedness, strength and weight of the attacker, as well as information about the weapon used.</p></details><details class="t13ne-alternate"><summary><strong>Alternate: </strong> Advantages </summary><p class="t13ne-description">Every Embodiment has some advantages over the other side. This is practically a list of those advantages, or at least one of them.</p></details><details class="t13ne-detail"><summary><strong>Detail: </strong> Suspicious </summary><p class="t13ne-description">Some details are suspicious, they draw attention to themselves. If you know what to look for. They are the gun loaded with blanks found at the scene of a murder, or the single specialised orthopaedic footprint found outside the museum heist.</p></details></details>
		 <details class="t13ne-ordeal-fancy"><summary><strong>Ordeal</strong></summary><p class="t13ne-ordeal-type"><strong>Ordeal-Type </strong> — Opposed Ordeal</p><p class="t13ne-stages"><strong>Stages </strong> — 4</p><p class="t13ne-ordeal-spread"><strong>Ordeal-Spread </strong> — Block</p><p class="t13ne-stakes"><strong>Stakes </strong> — Medium</p><p class="t13ne-test"><strong>Test </strong> — Self-defence (Rook)</p><p class="t13ne-obstacles"><strong>Obstacles </strong> — 1 Obstacle in every Court Stage</p><p class="t13ne-suggested-action"><strong>Suggested-Action </strong> — Parry</p><details class="t13ne-stage"><summary><strong>Stage: </strong> Defences </summary><p class="t13ne-description">The way ahead is through, or around, or on defences of some sort. These might be as simple as a hedge or chain-link fence, or be a gatehouse, or even a fortified citadels walls.</p></details><details class="t13ne-motional"><summary><strong>Motional: </strong> Aquatic Riding Chase </summary><p class="t13ne-description">The lizard men are getting away on a giant Alligator, you'll have to chase them, perhaps on those jet-skis?</p></details><details class="t13ne-fight"><summary><strong>Fight: </strong> Greater Guardian </summary><p class="t13ne-description">The way ahead is defended by a Kaiju or Force-of-Nature Monsters, or dozens or hundreds of well armed Cast Extras.<!-- t13ne-shortcode "%7B%22type%22%3A%22facetsuitaspect%22%2C%22suit%22%3A%223%22%2C%22aspect%22%3A%22Monster%22%7D"// --></p><div class="t13ne-tablewrap"><table class="t13ne-table Monster"><thead><tr><th>&nbsp;&nbsp;Facet&nbsp;&nbsp;</th><th>&nbsp;&nbsp;Monster&nbsp;&nbsp;</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td>Burden</td><td><strong>Object</strong> —The Monster is an object imbued with the semblance of life. Artificial in nature usually, the living object is usually tough and strong. An Object Monster is created with a Monster Pool equal to their Burden Score. Objects are able to multiply their Scores by 1+Monster Pool Size when appropriate to their form and function. The Object Monster may instantly Prepare cards from its Monster Pool, often as a Prepared Defence or Stunt Preparation, as appropriate for the Object's form or function.</td></tr><tr><td>Dominion</td><td><strong>Horde</strong> —The Monster is a group or swarm of lesser monsters, it might be an Army, a pack of Bulmäs, a Fortress or a Battle Moon. Horde is effectively a Pact Descendant. A Horde may pool Values to create larger rolls, however they may never create a Boon greater than their Value. Hordes may add Dominion Draw cards to their Monster Pool when the Horde increases in numbers. The Horde must Play at least one card from their Monster Pool on any action that splits the Horde (like losing an individual). E.g. two Vex Extras of Boon 10 (Value 45) that form a Horde become a single Extra of Boon 16 (Value 90), (with probably 1 card in the Monster Pool) but could never create a Boon greater than the individual's Value(45). If there were 11 Vexes, that would be Boon 44 (Value 495). 12 or One million Vexes would both give Boon 45 (Value 530) since that is the most powerful their Horde can get.</td></tr><tr><td>Fury</td><td><strong>Temperamental</strong> —The Monster is the embodiment of an Emotion, usually a negative one. Such Monsters are often capable of supernatural actions, such as telepathy, shape-shifting, or even undying. The Vampire is an embodiment of Lust, with powers of mind control, telepathy and suggestion, as well as shape-shifting abilities. The Werewolf is berserk rage, and almost indestructible unless Silver is used. The Beansidhe is the embodiment of mourning, and able to sense an oncoming death. When they experience their negative emotion the Monster may refill their Monster Pool to Fury Score cards. When appropriate to their Emotion they must add a Monster Pool card to the action and multiply the total Score by their remaining Monster Pool Reduced.</td></tr><tr><td>Rook</td><td><strong>Guardian</strong> —The Monster is a Guardian, it guards a certain territory, path, treasure or person. Guardians may add Rook Draw to their Monster Pool whenever they act as a guard, try to interfere with an attack, or are attacked. The Guardian reduces all attacks by a number of Pips equal to the current Monster Pool automatically. The Guardian will however automatically play cards from its Monster Pool to defend itself (via any Active or Passive defence method that makes sense to the Referee. From Smaug to Fafnir dragons are often Guardians, looking after a particular treasure or demanding tributes from a territory.</td></tr><tr><td>Sin</td><td><strong>Villain</strong> —The Sin Monster is an embodiment of a specific vice, taboo, or sin, often in the form of a human Villain (often with animalistic features or tendencies). They usually have Annexes and Hitches that reflect their embodied vice or sin, and are often at least partially supernatural. Villains add Sin Draw cards to their Monster Pool when they, or another (usually both) indulges in their particular vice or behaviour. Which can be as broad as "Evil" if you like. Villains may add +1 Score for each card in their Monster Pool for any action that furthers their diabolical plans. Villains automatically spend Monster Pool cards on escape actions when attacked, creating at least Sin Draw Obstacles from the Monster Pools cards. The genocidal or narcissistic maniac, of the sort British Intelligence seems to tangle with repeatedly, could be a Sin Monster. The ever-hungry cannibal spirit, the Wendigo is definitely an example, as is the Sexual-predatory Lust-embodied Vampire.</td></tr><tr><td>Wyrd</td><td><strong>Doom-weaver</strong> —The monster is a creature of the Fæ, it can draw and play Yarn cards or Wyrd Tarot cards (as appropriate). The Wyrd Monster may play cards from its Monster Pool as Wyrd Tarot or Yarn cards. The Monster may draw and add Wyrd Draw cards to its Monster Pool whenever it makes a choice, decision, or judgement. The Doom-weaver may draw and play Wyrd Draw cards as Ordeal cards whenever anyone plays Wyrd Tarot or Yarn cards. They must play a card from the Monster Pool if they have to undo a choice, decision or judgement they have made. Doom-Weavers come in many forms from the Cait-Sith, the Beansidhe, to the Faery nobles of Ironmaster.</td></tr></tbody><tfoot><tr><td>&nbsp;&nbsp;Facet&nbsp;&nbsp;</td><td>&nbsp;&nbsp;Monster&nbsp;&nbsp;</td></tr></tfoot></table></div><p></p></details></details>
		 </details>
			</div>
				<div class="t13ne-card-lea"><details class="t13ne-lea"><summary><strong>Lea</strong></summary><p class="t13ne-colour"><strong>Path Colour —</strong> Green/Turquoise Herbivore</p><p class="t13ne-bulmas"><strong>Allowed Bulmäs —</strong> Herd, Broccen, Vermis, Labyrinthine, All-Walkers</p><p class="t13ne-animal-category"><strong>Animal Category —</strong> Megafauna – Small</p><p class="t13ne-animal-example"><strong>Example Animal —</strong> Capybara  / Sheep / Goats / Small Antelope </p><p class="t13ne-notes"><strong>Notes —</strong> Small domesticated animals are the most common paths in human occupied lands.</p></details></div></div><div class="t13ne-cardwrap-jsvg" id="cardrewritebox33" data-mode="jsvg" data-wyrd="1" data-yarn="1"><img src="https://www.cjmoseley.dev.cc/wp-content/plugins/t13ne/images/cards-svg/8C.svg" class="t13ne-svgcard"><div class="t13ne-svgpips"><div class="t13ne-textpips">8<!-- write suit--><span class="t13ne-symbol"><span class="t13ne-blackcard">♣</span></span><details class="t13ne-suit"><summary></summary><p class="t13ne-name"><strong>Suit: </strong>Clubs <span class="t13ne-blackcard">♣</span></p><p><strong>Ordeal Trump: </strong><span class="t13ne-trump">Defence</span></p><p class="t13ne-desc"><strong>Description: </strong>Clubs represent the power of politics and a stoic materialism. In the Tarot the Clubs are called Staves or Wands, in some traditions they represent Intellectual Air, but in most they are Fire.</p><p class="t13ne-tarot"><strong>Tarot Meaning: </strong>Power, Fame, Politics, Ability, Money.</p><p class="t13ne-otext"><strong>Ordeal Text: </strong>Ordeal Types: Wealth, Politics, Power. Clubs act as Trump for following Actions: Bartering, Controlling, Corrupting, Emotional-intensity, Guarding, Magic, Displaying might, Politics, Self-Defence, Socializing, and Trade Negotiations. Clubs can be used to defend any type of attack.</p><p class="t13ne-parry"><strong>Parry: </strong><strong>Disarm:</strong> All the cards of the attack are immediately discarded, along with the Parry Card. The remaining cards of the Defender's Parry can now each be used to cause the Attacker to drop a Descendant (starting with the attacking weapon usually), can also cut armour straps, belts etc, or steal hats. This basically adds RT equal to the card Pips to the Annex that was used to attack. Every 5 Pips also creates a point of GRT for the Character (representing how much time the Character must spend picking up, or readying the weapon again. Court cards additionally may damage the Descendant that was disarmed, as standard for the Stakes.</p><p class="t13ne-soak"><strong>Soak: </strong><strong>Ward:</strong> The matched Attack card, and one other randomly selected Attack card are discarded.</p></details></div></div><div class="t13ne-svg-facet"><div>Burden</div></div><div class="t13ne-wound"><div><strong class="t13ne-woundname">Maim</strong><details><summary></summary><strong>Maim</strong><p class="t13ne-base-pips">Base Pips to summon: 8</p><p class="t13ne-min-pips">Minimum Pips Required: 8</p><p class="t13ne-desc">The Target is injured enough for it to affect their abilities.</p><details class="t13ne-roll"><summary>Rules</summary>Lose 1 Proficiency Slot from affected Annex (including Personality Proficiencies) and recalculate. If all Proficiencies are Maimed, the Annex is Crippled.</details><details class="t13ne-ordeal"><summary>Ordeal Rules</summary>Maimed Annexes lose Proficiency Slots too, but may lose access to Nimbeds affected or take a -1 Card Draw Penalty until Healed / Recalculated. If all Proficiencies are Maimed, the Annex is Crippled.</details><details class="t13ne-psych"><summary>Psych Wounds</summary><details class="t13ne-negative"><summary>Negative Psych</summary><p class="t13ne-normal"><strong>Normal</strong>Proficiency lost from Personality Profs. Must try to flee source of negative emotion until Stabilised or Discarded.</p><p class="t13ne-extreme"><strong>Extreme</strong>Proficiency lost from Personality Profs. All Proficiency Dice are reduced one dice size (1d6 becomes 1d4) until Stabilised.</p></details><details class="t13ne-positive"><summary>Positive Psych</summary><p class="t13ne-normal"><strong>Normal</strong>Gain 1 Proficiency (permanently) and must approach / follow source of positive emotion until Stabilised.</p><p class="t13ne-extreme"><strong>Extreme</strong>Gain 2 Proficiencies (permanently), plus must drop to their knees (or simlar) to express the emotion until Stabilised.</p></details></details></details></div></div><div class="t13ne-tarot-svg"><details><summary class="t13ne-wyrdtarot"><strong> Wyrd Tarot: </strong> Psst</summary><p class="t13ne-normal"><strong> Normal: </strong>Add a card from your Hand to the Target's Hand and Draw 2 cards.</p><p class="t13ne-crossed"><strong> Crossed: </strong>Take a random card from the Target's Hand and add it to your own. They may draw a replacement.</p></details></div><div class="t13ne-yarn-fancy">
			<details><summary><strong>Yarn</strong> <br>The Scythe</summary>
			<span class="t13ne-description">The Scythe card represents an honest day's work, usually manual labour, often field or yard work (throughout human history) in a hot harvest sun, but it can represent any work with tools, labour, gathering or harvest activities. The Scythe can also represent danger at work, and of course has a long association with both Death and Time.</span>
			<span class="t13ne-significator">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-significator"><strong>Significator</strong></summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-plot"><strong>Plot: </strong> The Scythe Plot is about everyday life and death. Death is a hum-drum event, it is constantly occurring all around us, this Plot shows us that, but often not through violence or pestilence. The Scythe is a Plot of small, everyday dramas, soap opera, ordinary lives stuff, but death is always a part of that Tapestry. Old People, the ill, the careless, the unlucky, the Scythe may take a swing at them all, and keeps swinging until someone dies.</aside>
					<aside class="t13ne-char"><strong>Character: </strong> The Scythe Significator indicates a Character that works hard and expects rewards. They Gain Yin when they complain, grumble, or moan. They Gain Yang when they do work. They Gain Chi when they rest, or are paid after work. They gain +1 Success Levels to any task when they complain about it.</aside>
					<aside class="t13ne-scene"><strong>Scene: </strong> The Scythe Plot is about everyday life and death. Death is a hum-drum event, it is constantly occurring all around us, this Plot shows us that, but often not through violence or pestilence. The Scythe is a Plot of small, everyday dramas, soap opera, ordinary lives stuff, but death is always a part of that Tapestry. Old People, the ill, the careless, the unlucky, the Scythe may take a swing at them all, and keeps swinging until someone dies.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-scene">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-scene-type">
						<strong>Scene: </strong> Warp
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">Warps work the Conflict. They are broken into 3 parts (and require another 2 cards): <ul><li>The Ends (What the Players want, usually described as Quests ). Does not require a Yarn Card.</li><li>The Fray (what keeps the Characters from their Ends, how do they work through that Quest?). The Fray requires an extra Yarn Card.</li><li>The Snag (The complication that renders the actions of the Characters winning through the Fray moot somehow). The Snag also requires another Yarn Card.</li></ul>During Warps things tend to get worse for the Pressed side of the Conflict.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-conflict-embodiment">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-conflict-embodiment">
						<strong>Conflict Embodiment: </strong> Tension
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">The Conflict embodies within two Characters, but they do not come into direct Conflict. They have some relationship, that Embodies the Conflict as arguments, discussions, jokes, etc.  common to Romance and Buddy movies.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>

			<span class="t13ne-facet-embodiment">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-facet-embodiment">
						<strong>Facet Embodiment: </strong> Annex
					</summary>
					<section class="t13ne-description">The Conflict Embodies one Facet within an Annex, this could be a Subtle Nimbed or Umbral, or be as obvious as being Root, Channel and at least one Umbral. The Annex may be within Descendants, or Characters.</section>
				</details>
			</span>
			 <div class="t13ne-archetypes"><details><summary><strong>Archetype Character: </strong>Farmhand</summary> <details class="t13ne-Persona"><summary><strong>Persona: </strong><span class="t13ne-output">Beast</span>
      </summary>
      <details class="t13ne-motivation"><summary><strong>Motivation: </strong></summary><span class="t13ne-description">Beasts are simple, they seek a warm place to sleep, food, and when possible other pleasures. They rarely have greater ambitions than any animal would in a similar situation (and the Beast is most animal Character's Persona).</span></details>
       <details class="t13ne-avoid"><summary><strong>Avoid: </strong></summary><span class="t13ne-description">Complex social situations and contracts are the bane of a Beast's life (if they even understand what is going on), they'd rather not deal with things like waiting in line, filling in paperwork, or even paying people compliments. And if you think its hard for a Beast to pay someone a compliment, wait until you try explaining to them taxes that include social security, or social health systems.</span></details>
       <details class="t13ne-gain-chi"><summary><strong>Gain Chi: </strong></summary><span class="t13ne-description"><span>Gain Chi when you are honest, blunt, direct, or eat.</span><p>Ordeal gain Chi when you play a ♣ to attack</p></span></details></details>   <details class="t13ne-core"><summary><strong>Core: </strong><span class="t13ne-output">Wielder</span></summary><span class="t13ne-description">When the character is holding/wearing or using a specific descendant/trophy/monolith/artefact add a success level.</span></details><details class="t13ne-hitch"><summary><strong>Hitch: </strong><span class="t13ne-output">Scarred</span></summary><span class="t13ne-description">Time heals all wounds it is said, but anyone who carries a prominent facial scar knows that while the pain may fade, the scars are always present. Scars don't have to be physical or visible to affect a character forever, they just need to worth a Hitch.</span></details><p class="t13ne-description">The Farmhand is a simple labourer who happens to be an expert with their preferred tool, whether it be a scythe, a rope, a tractor, or moisture-vaporator</p></details></div>
			 <span class="t13ne-hook">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-hook">
						<strong>Hook: </strong> Reap What Was Sowed
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">The Story opens with a call back to something that happened in a previous Story. This story could be a spin-off from the original, or this could just be a reuse of a location, descendant or character.</aside>
					<details class="t13ne-hook-aspect">
						<summary><strong>Hook Aspect: </strong>I-Ching</summary>
						<section class="t13ne-description">The Hook targets the Character's I-Ching. For example: If a Character has the Cauldron (50) I-Ching, then the Hook needs to have someone hungry turn up asking for help.</section>
					</details>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-obstacle-fancy"><details class="t13ne-obstacle"><summary><strong>Obstacle:</strong>Work</summary><p class="t13ne-description">The Obstacle requires work to be done to pass it. This might be pulling a rope, turning a wheel, drawing water, shovelling coal, splitting wood, that primes a mechanism, lowers a bridge, or opens a way.</p></details></span>
			<span class="t13ne-gain">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-gain">
						<strong>Gain </strong> 
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">Personalised Tool (Trophy/Monolith)</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-fray">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-fray">
						<strong>Fray: </strong> Labour
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">Hard Work and Labour is usually an Extended Ordeal, there is not often opposition, but there may be a requirement for multiple people to work together, or at a pace. The Stakes are usually Low or Medium, but higher Stakes Labour exist (miners, lumberjacks and crab fisherman spring to mind as high or extreme Stakes labour). The point is this work is hard, sweaty, exhausting and will push the PCs to exhaustion.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-snag">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-snag">
						<strong>Snag: </strong> Scythes, Pitchforks and Torches
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">When Peasants revolt they have a tendency to grab anything that they can use as a weapon, considering their day to day life involves mowing grass with scythes, lifting the mowed grass with pitchforks and so on they get quite well trained with this tools. This Snag means that whomever is involved in the Fray they are more heavily armed (and better equipped and trained) than you might have imagined. </aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-sweeping">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-sweeping">
						<strong>Sweeping: </strong> Reaping
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">The PCs will make a major Gain (such as a harvest) if they succeed in a Test or Ordeal (such as Harvesting).</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
		 <details class="t13ne-revelation-fancy"><summary><strong>Revelation</strong></summary><details class="t13ne-about"><summary><strong>About: </strong> The Story </summary><p class="t13ne-description">The information is about the current situation and plot. It might include information about one  side of the Conflict, or all. It is normal when providing information to only reveal information about a single Side of the Conflict, usually the Dominant or Above sides.</p></details><details class="t13ne-info"><summary><strong>Info: </strong> How Much? </summary><p class="t13ne-description">Information that tells us something about amounts, volumes, or weights. It could be as simple as a receipt, or a tax return, but reveals something more interesting than you'd think.</p></details><details class="t13ne-vector"><summary><strong>Vector: </strong> Bullet Holes </summary><p class="t13ne-description">Bullet holes peppering a wall tell us about the gunfight, if you know how to read them. Sometimes the bullets themselves can reveal information, even beyond the calibre and make of weapon that fired them. Especially if the bad guy used them to leave letters in the plasterboard, perhaps spelling "DETH", such a message might be a damning indictment of the education system, or point to a specific group or killer.</p></details><details class="t13ne-alternate"><summary><strong>Alternate: </strong> Strategy </summary><p class="t13ne-description">The information reveals something about the Embodiment's strategy. Perhaps they are a heavy bruiser, that deals and takes damage in equal measure, but is honourable and forthright, or maybe they are known for avoiding combat personally, but have a large personal guard. It may not reveal their plan, but it could reveal what they are planning.</p></details><details class="t13ne-detail"><summary><strong>Detail: </strong> Glorious </summary><p class="t13ne-description">Glorious details may be a military record, or signs of opulence. Or they could just be exquisite craftsmanship that demonstrates the Descendant is well made.</p></details></details>
		 <details class="t13ne-ordeal-fancy"><summary><strong>Ordeal</strong></summary><p class="t13ne-ordeal-type"><strong>Ordeal-Type </strong> — Opposed Ordeal</p><p class="t13ne-stages"><strong>Stages </strong> — 5</p><p class="t13ne-ordeal-spread"><strong>Ordeal-Spread </strong> — Forking</p><p class="t13ne-stakes"><strong>Stakes </strong> — High</p><p class="t13ne-test"><strong>Test </strong> — Magic (Wyrd)</p><p class="t13ne-obstacles"><strong>Obstacles </strong> — 1 Obstacle in every Stage</p><p class="t13ne-suggested-action"><strong>Suggested-Action </strong> — Weave</p><details class="t13ne-stage"><summary><strong>Stage: </strong> Web </summary><p class="t13ne-description">The way ahead is some sort of webbing, net, or weave, maybe the internet, or perhaps a suspension bridge, or maybe a confluence of roads, threads or wires. Or perhaps the way ahead is just heavy with old cobwebs.</p></details><details class="t13ne-motional"><summary><strong>Motional: </strong> Ground Riding Chase </summary><p class="t13ne-description">They're just ahead, you can hear the pounding hoof-beats on the dark forest road. You pursue pushing your horse harder, until you can see their cloaks flapping in the wind. One turns in the saddle, there is a flash of fire that reveals his face, masked beneath his Tricorn hat, and then the crack-whistle of a musket shot.</p></details><details class="t13ne-fight"><summary><strong>Fight: </strong> Capture </summary><p class="t13ne-description">The enemy needs to be captured alive (and conscious ideally). Usually this enemy is a solitary Monster (Cast Extra) with 2 Monster Facets .<!-- t13ne-shortcode "%7B%22type%22%3A%22facetsuitaspect%22%2C%22suit%22%3A%223%22%2C%22aspect%22%3A%22Monster%22%7D"// --></p><div class="t13ne-tablewrap"><table class="t13ne-table Monster"><thead><tr><th>&nbsp;&nbsp;Facet&nbsp;&nbsp;</th><th>&nbsp;&nbsp;Monster&nbsp;&nbsp;</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td>Burden</td><td><strong>Object</strong> —The Monster is an object imbued with the semblance of life. Artificial in nature usually, the living object is usually tough and strong. An Object Monster is created with a Monster Pool equal to their Burden Score. Objects are able to multiply their Scores by 1+Monster Pool Size when appropriate to their form and function. The Object Monster may instantly Prepare cards from its Monster Pool, often as a Prepared Defence or Stunt Preparation, as appropriate for the Object's form or function.</td></tr><tr><td>Dominion</td><td><strong>Horde</strong> —The Monster is a group or swarm of lesser monsters, it might be an Army, a pack of Bulmäs, a Fortress or a Battle Moon. Horde is effectively a Pact Descendant. A Horde may pool Values to create larger rolls, however they may never create a Boon greater than their Value. Hordes may add Dominion Draw cards to their Monster Pool when the Horde increases in numbers. The Horde must Play at least one card from their Monster Pool on any action that splits the Horde (like losing an individual). E.g. two Vex Extras of Boon 10 (Value 45) that form a Horde become a single Extra of Boon 16 (Value 90), (with probably 1 card in the Monster Pool) but could never create a Boon greater than the individual's Value(45). If there were 11 Vexes, that would be Boon 44 (Value 495). 12 or One million Vexes would both give Boon 45 (Value 530) since that is the most powerful their Horde can get.</td></tr><tr><td>Fury</td><td><strong>Temperamental</strong> —The Monster is the embodiment of an Emotion, usually a negative one. Such Monsters are often capable of supernatural actions, such as telepathy, shape-shifting, or even undying. The Vampire is an embodiment of Lust, with powers of mind control, telepathy and suggestion, as well as shape-shifting abilities. The Werewolf is berserk rage, and almost indestructible unless Silver is used. The Beansidhe is the embodiment of mourning, and able to sense an oncoming death. When they experience their negative emotion the Monster may refill their Monster Pool to Fury Score cards. When appropriate to their Emotion they must add a Monster Pool card to the action and multiply the total Score by their remaining Monster Pool Reduced.</td></tr><tr><td>Rook</td><td><strong>Guardian</strong> —The Monster is a Guardian, it guards a certain territory, path, treasure or person. Guardians may add Rook Draw to their Monster Pool whenever they act as a guard, try to interfere with an attack, or are attacked. The Guardian reduces all attacks by a number of Pips equal to the current Monster Pool automatically. The Guardian will however automatically play cards from its Monster Pool to defend itself (via any Active or Passive defence method that makes sense to the Referee. From Smaug to Fafnir dragons are often Guardians, looking after a particular treasure or demanding tributes from a territory.</td></tr><tr><td>Sin</td><td><strong>Villain</strong> —The Sin Monster is an embodiment of a specific vice, taboo, or sin, often in the form of a human Villain (often with animalistic features or tendencies). They usually have Annexes and Hitches that reflect their embodied vice or sin, and are often at least partially supernatural. Villains add Sin Draw cards to their Monster Pool when they, or another (usually both) indulges in their particular vice or behaviour. Which can be as broad as "Evil" if you like. Villains may add +1 Score for each card in their Monster Pool for any action that furthers their diabolical plans. Villains automatically spend Monster Pool cards on escape actions when attacked, creating at least Sin Draw Obstacles from the Monster Pools cards. The genocidal or narcissistic maniac, of the sort British Intelligence seems to tangle with repeatedly, could be a Sin Monster. The ever-hungry cannibal spirit, the Wendigo is definitely an example, as is the Sexual-predatory Lust-embodied Vampire.</td></tr><tr><td>Wyrd</td><td><strong>Doom-weaver</strong> —The monster is a creature of the Fæ, it can draw and play Yarn cards or Wyrd Tarot cards (as appropriate). The Wyrd Monster may play cards from its Monster Pool as Wyrd Tarot or Yarn cards. The Monster may draw and add Wyrd Draw cards to its Monster Pool whenever it makes a choice, decision, or judgement. The Doom-weaver may draw and play Wyrd Draw cards as Ordeal cards whenever anyone plays Wyrd Tarot or Yarn cards. They must play a card from the Monster Pool if they have to undo a choice, decision or judgement they have made. Doom-Weavers come in many forms from the Cait-Sith, the Beansidhe, to the Faery nobles of Ironmaster.</td></tr></tbody><tfoot><tr><td>&nbsp;&nbsp;Facet&nbsp;&nbsp;</td><td>&nbsp;&nbsp;Monster&nbsp;&nbsp;</td></tr></tfoot></table></div><p></p></details></details>
		 </details>
			</div>
				<div class="t13ne-card-lea"><details class="t13ne-lea"><summary><strong>Lea</strong></summary><p class="t13ne-colour"><strong>Path Colour —</strong> Green/Turquoise Herbivore</p><p class="t13ne-bulmas"><strong>Allowed Bulmäs —</strong> Herd, Broccen, Vermis, Labyrinthine, All-Walkers</p><p class="t13ne-animal-category"><strong>Animal Category —</strong> Megafauna – Medium</p><p class="t13ne-animal-example"><strong>Example Animal —</strong> Cattle / Deer / Large Antelope / Horse</p><p class="t13ne-notes"><strong>Notes —</strong> These are the most commonly walked paths amongst the Herd. The Paths often blend numerous animals together into super-herds that are common in Africa.</p></details></div></div><div class="t13ne-cardwrap-jsvg" id="cardrewritebox34" data-mode="jsvg" data-wyrd="1" data-yarn="1"><img src="https://www.cjmoseley.dev.cc/wp-content/plugins/t13ne/images/cards-svg/9C.svg" class="t13ne-svgcard"><div class="t13ne-svgpips"><div class="t13ne-textpips">9<!-- write suit--><span class="t13ne-symbol"><span class="t13ne-blackcard">♣</span></span><details class="t13ne-suit"><summary></summary><p class="t13ne-name"><strong>Suit: </strong>Clubs <span class="t13ne-blackcard">♣</span></p><p><strong>Ordeal Trump: </strong><span class="t13ne-trump">Defence</span></p><p class="t13ne-desc"><strong>Description: </strong>Clubs represent the power of politics and a stoic materialism. In the Tarot the Clubs are called Staves or Wands, in some traditions they represent Intellectual Air, but in most they are Fire.</p><p class="t13ne-tarot"><strong>Tarot Meaning: </strong>Power, Fame, Politics, Ability, Money.</p><p class="t13ne-otext"><strong>Ordeal Text: </strong>Ordeal Types: Wealth, Politics, Power. Clubs act as Trump for following Actions: Bartering, Controlling, Corrupting, Emotional-intensity, Guarding, Magic, Displaying might, Politics, Self-Defence, Socializing, and Trade Negotiations. Clubs can be used to defend any type of attack.</p><p class="t13ne-parry"><strong>Parry: </strong><strong>Disarm:</strong> All the cards of the attack are immediately discarded, along with the Parry Card. The remaining cards of the Defender's Parry can now each be used to cause the Attacker to drop a Descendant (starting with the attacking weapon usually), can also cut armour straps, belts etc, or steal hats. This basically adds RT equal to the card Pips to the Annex that was used to attack. Every 5 Pips also creates a point of GRT for the Character (representing how much time the Character must spend picking up, or readying the weapon again. Court cards additionally may damage the Descendant that was disarmed, as standard for the Stakes.</p><p class="t13ne-soak"><strong>Soak: </strong><strong>Ward:</strong> The matched Attack card, and one other randomly selected Attack card are discarded.</p></details></div></div><div class="t13ne-svg-facet"><div>Dominion</div></div><div class="t13ne-wound"><div><strong class="t13ne-woundname">Maim</strong><details><summary></summary><strong>Maim</strong><p class="t13ne-base-pips">Base Pips to summon: 8</p><p class="t13ne-min-pips">Minimum Pips Required: 8</p><p class="t13ne-desc">The Target is injured enough for it to affect their abilities.</p><details class="t13ne-roll"><summary>Rules</summary>Lose 1 Proficiency Slot from affected Annex (including Personality Proficiencies) and recalculate. If all Proficiencies are Maimed, the Annex is Crippled.</details><details class="t13ne-ordeal"><summary>Ordeal Rules</summary>Maimed Annexes lose Proficiency Slots too, but may lose access to Nimbeds affected or take a -1 Card Draw Penalty until Healed / Recalculated. If all Proficiencies are Maimed, the Annex is Crippled.</details><details class="t13ne-psych"><summary>Psych Wounds</summary><details class="t13ne-negative"><summary>Negative Psych</summary><p class="t13ne-normal"><strong>Normal</strong>Proficiency lost from Personality Profs. Must try to flee source of negative emotion until Stabilised or Discarded.</p><p class="t13ne-extreme"><strong>Extreme</strong>Proficiency lost from Personality Profs. All Proficiency Dice are reduced one dice size (1d6 becomes 1d4) until Stabilised.</p></details><details class="t13ne-positive"><summary>Positive Psych</summary><p class="t13ne-normal"><strong>Normal</strong>Gain 1 Proficiency (permanently) and must approach / follow source of positive emotion until Stabilised.</p><p class="t13ne-extreme"><strong>Extreme</strong>Gain 2 Proficiencies (permanently), plus must drop to their knees (or simlar) to express the emotion until Stabilised.</p></details></details></details></div></div><div class="t13ne-tarot-svg"><details><summary class="t13ne-wyrdtarot"><strong> Wyrd Tarot: </strong> Shake Off</summary><p class="t13ne-normal"><strong> Normal: </strong>Target discards <strong>all</strong> Distract Wounds</p><p class="t13ne-crossed"><strong> Crossed: </strong>Target takes 1d4 Unsoakable Distract Wounds.</p></details></div><div class="t13ne-yarn-fancy">
			<details><summary><strong>Yarn</strong> <br>The Crown</summary>
			<span class="t13ne-description">The Crown is the card of Leadership. It stands for Leaders, Commanders, Monarchy and Politicians. Uneasy lies the head that wears the Crown though, and those in power have additional worries, responsibilities and other troublesome Hitches. The Crown can serve as a warning for Leaders to look within, to act for those they lead, not themselves or ruin may follow. But it can also warn followers to watch those they choose to follow.</span>
			<span class="t13ne-significator">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-significator"><strong>Significator</strong></summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-plot"><strong>Plot: </strong> The Crown Plot is usually a Political Thriller, these are stories of alliances, enmities, betrayals and treaties. There's a lot going on and usually a lot at stake. The fates of countries and worlds are decided by Crown Plots.</aside>
					<aside class="t13ne-char"><strong>Character: </strong> The Crown Significator indicates an absolute monarch, at least within their territory. They Gain Yin when they speak to their followers, act emotional, or listen to others. They Gain Yang when they behave aloof, or polite, use logic, or amuse themselves. They Gain Chi when they act for the benefit of their followers, country or people. They gain +1 Success Levels to all Psyche Defences.</aside>
					<aside class="t13ne-scene"><strong>Scene: </strong> The Crown Plot is usually a Political Thriller, these are stories of alliances, enmities, betrayals and treaties. There's a lot going on and usually a lot at stake. The fates of countries and worlds are decided by Crown Plots.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-scene">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-scene-type">
						<strong>Scene: </strong> Weft
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">Wefts work the Conflict through 3 Parts: <ul><li>First by the Characters Recoiling from the Snag (They emotionally respond to the previous event) which does not require a Yarn card</li><li>Secondly Sweeping (where the Characters recover from what happened) which does require another Yarn Card</li><li>Finally, Picking (Where the Characters will have a choice how the Story will proceed) which does not require Yarn Cards, but can be represented by more cards during card play.</li></ul>During Wefts the Pressed side makes a little headway, or gets a little breather.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-conflict-embodiment">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-conflict-embodiment">
						<strong>Conflict Embodiment: </strong> Tension
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">The Conflict embodies within two Characters, but they do not come into direct Conflict. They have some relationship, that Embodies the Conflict as arguments, discussions, jokes, etc.  common to Romance and Buddy movies.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>

			<span class="t13ne-facet-embodiment">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-facet-embodiment">
						<strong>Facet Embodiment: </strong> Tone
					</summary>
					<section class="t13ne-description">The Conflict Embodies one Facet as the Tone of the Scene, Act or Story. This can be an easy way to embody some Facets, but can be restrictive and may not suit your style. Use with care.</section>
				</details>
			</span>
			 <div class="t13ne-archetypes"><details><summary><strong>Archetype Character: </strong>Diplomat</summary> <details class="t13ne-Persona"><summary><strong>Persona: </strong><span class="t13ne-output">Judge</span>
      </summary>
      <details class="t13ne-motivation"><summary><strong>Motivation: </strong></summary><span class="t13ne-description">A Judge is driven by a strong sense of fairness and justice. A Judge may feel that some people should be rewarded, for their deeds, or less commonly a Judge may punish those who they feel deserve it. Judges may be legal professional, or more often is just a member of society who has strong opinions about how others should behave.</span></details>
       <details class="t13ne-avoid"><summary><strong>Avoid: </strong></summary><span class="t13ne-description">A Judge will always try to avoid being judged unfair. They aren't keen on being judged themselves, but have an almost unnatural need to be seen as fair, or at least consistent, in their dealings. Most Judges will also avoid hypocrisy, if they can, as they see it as an extension of unfairness.</span></details>
       <details class="t13ne-gain-chi"><summary><strong>Gain Chi: </strong></summary><span class="t13ne-description"><span>Gain Chi when you ask questions, pass judgement on someone, or something, or when you examine evidence.</span><p>Ordeal gain Chi when you set a Trap or Snare or Prepare a ♣</p></span></details></details>   <details class="t13ne-core"><summary><strong>Core: </strong><span class="t13ne-output">Greeter</span></summary><span class="t13ne-description">Gain Chi when they speak to a stranger or group of strangers.</span></details><details class="t13ne-hitch"><summary><strong>Hitch: </strong><span class="t13ne-output">Fatigued</span></summary><span class="t13ne-description">This hitch covers everything from laziness to sickness, some people and things just seem tired or useless most of the time.</span></details><p class="t13ne-description">The Diplomat is a mediator, they're job is working out treaties and problems.</p></details></div>
			 <span class="t13ne-hook">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-hook">
						<strong>Hook: </strong> Commanded
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">The Hook of this Story is simply that the PC is ordered to follow the Plot (investigate the mystery, secure the asset, overthrow the government, etc) by their boss, monarch, superior officer or spouse.</aside>
					<details class="t13ne-hook-aspect">
						<summary><strong>Hook Aspect: </strong>I-Ching</summary>
						<section class="t13ne-description">The Hook targets the Character's I-Ching. For example: If a Character has the Cauldron (50) I-Ching, then the Hook needs to have someone hungry turn up asking for help.</section>
					</details>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-obstacle-fancy"><details class="t13ne-obstacle"><summary><strong>Obstacle:</strong>Orders</summary><p class="t13ne-description">The way ahead is blocked by official orders, such as "No High Speed Pursuits In The Financial District". Failure to comply (and not avoid the Obstacle) will result in repercussions.</p></details></span>
			<span class="t13ne-gain">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-gain">
						<strong>Gain </strong> 
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">Trophy / Monolith Treasure Descendant</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-fray">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-fray">
						<strong>Fray: </strong> Society
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">Sometimes we all need a leg up, and friends are the people to do that. Making and keeping friends can be Ordeals in their own right, especially when one is talking about joining clubs, guilds and fraternity houses. Fortunately, they are usually low Stakes, but Opposed by large numbers of people at the same time.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-snag">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-snag">
						<strong>Snag: </strong> Responsibilities
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">The Crown demands responsibility from the PCs. Something about the Fray is the PCs personal responsibility. Perhaps they caused some collateral damage and hence are directly responsible for fixing the damage, or is it a case of some old decision they made has just come back to bite them.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-sweeping">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-sweeping">
						<strong>Sweeping: </strong> Taxes
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">The Taxes of the Crown might indicate the PCs collecting taxes from their subjects, or they might be getting a rebate from taxes they have already paid. Either way, they'll gain Chi, or a Treasure Descendant.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
		 <details class="t13ne-revelation-fancy"><summary><strong>Revelation</strong></summary><details class="t13ne-about"><summary><strong>About: </strong> The Story </summary><p class="t13ne-description">The information is about the current situation and plot. It might include information about one side of the Conflict, or all. It is normal when providing information to only reveal information about a single Side of the Conflict, usually the Dominant or Above sides.</p></details><details class="t13ne-info"><summary><strong>Info: </strong> Official </summary><p class="t13ne-description">Official Information can be information about someone's rank or class within a society, or it can be the information that governments have at their disposal.</p></details><details class="t13ne-vector"><summary><strong>Vector: </strong> Political </summary><p class="t13ne-description">Politics is always about messages, and so political campaigns can carry information about Embodiments (obviously if those Embodiments are politicians, or are voted non-political positions).</p></details><details class="t13ne-alternate"><summary><strong>Alternate: </strong> Accomplishment </summary><p class="t13ne-description">The information concerns the Embodiment's greatest, or most impressive accomplishment. Do they have the highest tower in the country, or did they kill the most Goblins in the battle of Fellmeer?</p></details><details class="t13ne-detail"><summary><strong>Detail: </strong> Geopolitical </summary><p class="t13ne-description">The details concern the geopolitics of the situation or Characters. Where are their allegiances, how far away are their banner-men, where is the armoury inside the fortress walls? Those sorts of questions can be answered.</p></details></details>
		 <details class="t13ne-ordeal-fancy"><summary><strong>Ordeal</strong></summary><p class="t13ne-ordeal-type"><strong>Ordeal-Type </strong> — Opposed Ordeal</p><p class="t13ne-stages"><strong>Stages </strong> — 5</p><p class="t13ne-ordeal-spread"><strong>Ordeal-Spread </strong> — Pyramid</p><p class="t13ne-stakes"><strong>Stakes </strong> — Low</p><p class="t13ne-test"><strong>Test </strong> — Corrupt (Sin)</p><p class="t13ne-obstacles"><strong>Obstacles </strong> — 1 Obstacle in every Stage</p><p class="t13ne-suggested-action"><strong>Suggested-Action </strong> — Attack</p><details class="t13ne-stage"><summary><strong>Stage: </strong> Squeeze </summary><p class="t13ne-description">A squeeze can be a tight space, or something a little more romantic, it can even be an illegal operation. Here the chances are it is a very narrow gap, too small for a normal vehicle or person to pass, without some clever thinking.</p></details><details class="t13ne-motional"><summary><strong>Motional: </strong> Dangerous Riding Chase </summary><p class="t13ne-description">The enemy cavalry and the Baron's standard, break. Retreating past their own lines. Ignoring the fusillade of cannon, and the still unbroken archers on the right flank, you wheel your horses to follow.</p></details><details class="t13ne-fight"><summary><strong>Fight: </strong> Protect </summary><p class="t13ne-description">A Force is attacking that will attempt to kill one of the Party's Allies. Usually this force is around the PCs in power level and numbers, but may have lower or higher powered reserves arriving in following Rounds. The Party must protect the Ally, usually for ethical or narrative reasons.</p></details></details>
		 </details>
			</div>
				<div class="t13ne-card-lea"><details class="t13ne-lea"><summary><strong>Lea</strong></summary><p class="t13ne-colour"><strong>Path Colour —</strong> Green/Turquoise Herbivore</p><p class="t13ne-bulmas"><strong>Allowed Bulmäs —</strong> Herd, Broccen, Vermis, Labyrinthine, All-Walkers</p><p class="t13ne-animal-category"><strong>Animal Category —</strong> Megafauna – Large</p><p class="t13ne-animal-example"><strong>Example Animal —</strong> Wildebeest / Bison / Moose / Auroch / Giraffe / Rhino</p><p class="t13ne-notes"><strong>Notes —</strong> The largest of the common paths that can still be walked today.</p></details></div></div><div class="t13ne-cardwrap-jsvg" id="cardrewritebox35" data-mode="jsvg" data-wyrd="1" data-yarn="1"><img src="https://www.cjmoseley.dev.cc/wp-content/plugins/t13ne/images/cards-svg/10C.svg" class="t13ne-svgcard"><div class="t13ne-svgpips"><div class="t13ne-textpips">10<!-- write suit--><span class="t13ne-symbol"><span class="t13ne-blackcard">♣</span></span><details class="t13ne-suit"><summary></summary><p class="t13ne-name"><strong>Suit: </strong>Clubs <span class="t13ne-blackcard">♣</span></p><p><strong>Ordeal Trump: </strong><span class="t13ne-trump">Defence</span></p><p class="t13ne-desc"><strong>Description: </strong>Clubs represent the power of politics and a stoic materialism. In the Tarot the Clubs are called Staves or Wands, in some traditions they represent Intellectual Air, but in most they are Fire.</p><p class="t13ne-tarot"><strong>Tarot Meaning: </strong>Power, Fame, Politics, Ability, Money.</p><p class="t13ne-otext"><strong>Ordeal Text: </strong>Ordeal Types: Wealth, Politics, Power. Clubs act as Trump for following Actions: Bartering, Controlling, Corrupting, Emotional-intensity, Guarding, Magic, Displaying might, Politics, Self-Defence, Socializing, and Trade Negotiations. Clubs can be used to defend any type of attack.</p><p class="t13ne-parry"><strong>Parry: </strong><strong>Disarm:</strong> All the cards of the attack are immediately discarded, along with the Parry Card. The remaining cards of the Defender's Parry can now each be used to cause the Attacker to drop a Descendant (starting with the attacking weapon usually), can also cut armour straps, belts etc, or steal hats. This basically adds RT equal to the card Pips to the Annex that was used to attack. Every 5 Pips also creates a point of GRT for the Character (representing how much time the Character must spend picking up, or readying the weapon again. Court cards additionally may damage the Descendant that was disarmed, as standard for the Stakes.</p><p class="t13ne-soak"><strong>Soak: </strong><strong>Ward:</strong> The matched Attack card, and one other randomly selected Attack card are discarded.</p></details></div></div><div class="t13ne-svg-facet"><div>Rook</div></div><div class="t13ne-wound"><div><strong class="t13ne-woundname">Cripple</strong><details><summary></summary><strong>Cripple</strong><p class="t13ne-base-pips">Base Pips to summon: 12</p><p class="t13ne-min-pips">Minimum Pips Required: 10</p><p class="t13ne-desc">The Target is seriously wounded, losing access to whole abilities. This can even result in Coma.</p><details class="t13ne-roll"><summary>Rules</summary>Lose 1 Annex Slot (usually the largest one involved in the Roll) until Healed. Once healed Proficiencies may be repurchased for 1 Chi each. Then the Annex will regenerate at Phoenix / Incarna Score Boons per Day. It may be purchased back for the normal cost (Boon in Chi + 2/5/8/12) instantly (or altered and purchased back as a new Annex, if narratively appropriate).</details><details class="t13ne-ordeal"><summary>Ordeal Rules</summary>Crippled Annexes cannot be used as long as the Wound is in play. If applied to a Personality Annex (or a Descendant Master Annex) then the Alternate is unconscious (or the Descendant is lost or useless). They may not use that Alternate (or Descendant) until the Wound is healed (or found / fixed).</details><details class="t13ne-psych"><summary>Psych Wounds</summary><details class="t13ne-negative"><summary>Negative Psych</summary><p class="t13ne-normal"><strong>Normal</strong>Automatically affects the Personality Annex. No Proficiencies or Annexes are lost, but Character loses consciousness at sight of the source until healed.</p><p class="t13ne-extreme"><strong>Extreme</strong>Automatically affects the Personality Annex. Loses consciousness until Healed (not just when the source is present).</p></details><details class="t13ne-positive"><summary>Positive Psych</summary><p class="t13ne-normal"><strong>Normal</strong>Consciousness is interrupted for a few moments (1 Phase). May Gain a Skill Annex/Descendant or 3 Proficiencies.</p><p class="t13ne-extreme"><strong>Extreme</strong>Target loses consciousness (or at least responsiveness) until Stabilised. Gains a new Skill Annex/Descendant.</p></details></details></details></div></div><div class="t13ne-tarot-svg"><details><summary class="t13ne-wyrdtarot"><strong> Wyrd Tarot: </strong> Relieve Wound</summary><p class="t13ne-normal"><strong> Normal: </strong>Remove the Target's largest Wound.</p><p class="t13ne-crossed"><strong> Crossed: </strong>Draw and Apply one Unsoakable Wound to the Target.</p></details></div><div class="t13ne-yarn-fancy">
			<details><summary><strong>Yarn</strong> <br>The Guardian</summary>
			<span class="t13ne-description">The Guardian card represents the people who protect us. From Parents and Teachers to friends and even social systems such as City Watch or Police. The Guardian will always look out for us, helping us to avoid the worst situations and injuries, as long as we heed their warnings, and they are trustworthy.</span>
			<span class="t13ne-significator">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-significator"><strong>Significator</strong></summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-plot"><strong>Plot: </strong> The Guardian Plot is often a plot about families. These maybe coming of age tales (and often are), but a Guardian and Ward relationship will be important to this Plot. Guardian Plots can also fall into Police procedural lines, and Superhero adventures, where protecting others is an important plot point.</aside>
					<aside class="t13ne-char"><strong>Character: </strong> The Guardian Significator indicates a protector, who guides as well as guards. They Gain Yin when they watch or listen to those they would protect, or comfort those who are injured. They Gain Yang when they offer advice or guide others. They Gain Chi when they act to protect or shield another. They gain +1 Success Level when they act to assist one of their wards.</aside>
					<aside class="t13ne-scene"><strong>Scene: </strong> The Guardian Plot is often a plot about families. These maybe coming of age tales (and often are), but a Guardian and Ward relationship will be important to this Plot. Guardian Plots can also fall into Police procedural lines, and Superhero adventures, where protecting others is an important plot point.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-scene">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-scene-type">
						<strong>Scene: </strong> Finale
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">The Finale is a final Ordeal (or Quest or Warp), a great battle, or Revelation. This is where the Conflict comes to a head. The Stakes will be higher (High or Extreme) and there will terrible consequences for failure.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-conflict-embodiment">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-conflict-embodiment">
						<strong>Conflict Embodiment: </strong> Tension
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">The Conflict embodies within two Characters, but they do not come into direct Conflict. They have some relationship, that Embodies the Conflict as arguments, discussions, jokes, etc.  common to Romance and Buddy movies.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>

			<span class="t13ne-facet-embodiment">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-facet-embodiment">
						<strong>Facet Embodiment: </strong> Proficiency
					</summary>
					<section class="t13ne-description">The Conflict Embodies one Facet as a single Proficiency from that Facet. Characters or Descendants that have the Proficiency may be Hooked to that side. This is a very subtle Embodiment when handled gently.</section>
				</details>
			</span>
			 <div class="t13ne-archetypes"><details><summary><strong>Archetype Character: </strong>Guard</summary> <details class="t13ne-Persona"><summary><strong>Persona: </strong><span class="t13ne-output">Guard</span>
      </summary>
      <details class="t13ne-motivation"><summary><strong>Motivation: </strong></summary><span class="t13ne-description">The Guard is driven by a desire to protect others. They may be drawn to protect certain groups, or Locations, by some spiritual need, or they might feel responsible and protect those threatened by a certain group. Then again some Guards just protect what they are paid to protect, operating entirely as a mercenary.</span></details>
       <details class="t13ne-avoid"><summary><strong>Avoid: </strong></summary><span class="t13ne-description">Guards avoid nothing in the protecting of their charges. Some will sacrifice even their own lives to protect others, but others may not even sacrifice their sleep.</span></details>
       <details class="t13ne-gain-chi"><summary><strong>Gain Chi: </strong></summary><span class="t13ne-description"><span>Gain Chi when you plan, speak, or act out of security concerns.</span><p>Ordeal gain Chi when you play a ♣ in defence.</p></span></details></details>   <details class="t13ne-core"><summary><strong>Core: </strong><span class="t13ne-output">Wielder</span></summary><span class="t13ne-description">When the character is holding/wearing or using a specific descendant/trophy/monolith/artefact add a success level.</span></details><details class="t13ne-hitch"><summary><strong>Hitch: </strong><span class="t13ne-output">Lament</span></summary><span class="t13ne-description">When something has been lost, or forgotten, or you never had what everyone else has. It is the Hitch of Orphans and Widows, of Lost Loves and Haunting Memories</span></details><p class="t13ne-description">Guards, whether body, security, military, prison, royal or city guards are all essentially the same.</p></details></div>
			 <span class="t13ne-hook">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-hook">
						<strong>Hook: </strong> Help!
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">The Hook is a direct call to help from someone (usually at least a Cast Extra) such as an Ally or Friend.</aside>
					<details class="t13ne-hook-aspect">
						<summary><strong>Hook Aspect: </strong>I-Ching</summary>
						<section class="t13ne-description">The Hook targets the Character's I-Ching. For example: If a Character has the Cauldron (50) I-Ching, then the Hook needs to have someone hungry turn up asking for help.</section>
					</details>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-obstacle-fancy"><details class="t13ne-obstacle"><summary><strong>Obstacle:</strong>Guardian</summary><p class="t13ne-description">The way ahead is defended by a powerful Major Guardian, it might be your date's Father, a Dragon, or Asterion, the Minotaur who guards the centre of Caester. They are often more powerful than the whole party of PCs and must be persuaded, beguiled, impressed, misled, bribed, fought, knocked out, killed, or vaporised, before they will let any of you past.</p></details></span>
			<span class="t13ne-gain">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-gain">
						<strong>Gain </strong> 
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">A Cast Extra Ally with Power Descendant Armour</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-fray">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-fray">
						<strong>Fray: </strong> Protect and Serve
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">The Fray is trying to keep people safe in a dangerous situation. Maybe the PCs are cops during a riot, or perhaps there is a natural disaster and very one has to pitch in, then again maybe someone the PCs have a Duty or Devotion to is at risk.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-snag">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-snag">
						<strong>Snag: </strong> Untrustworthy Guardian
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">Whether its a bad cop or a demonic schoolteacher, an untrustworthy guardian is a complication that can make things bad for everyone involved.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-sweeping">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-sweeping">
						<strong>Sweeping: </strong> Safe House
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">The Pressed Embodiment finds a safe house. A Location where they can regroup and heal, that is unknown to, or otherwise protected from, the other sides.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
		 <details class="t13ne-revelation-fancy"><summary><strong>Revelation</strong></summary><details class="t13ne-about"><summary><strong>About: </strong> The Story </summary><p class="t13ne-description">The information is about the current situation and plot. It might include information about either side of the Conflict, or both. It is normal when providing information to only reveal information about a single Side of the Conflict, usually the Dominant or Above sides.</p></details><details class="t13ne-info"><summary><strong>Info: </strong> Safety </summary><p class="t13ne-description">This could tell you information about someone's safety, where do they feel safe, who with and why? Or it could as easily be instructions on how to train dragons without being broiled or a simple warning to avoid the forest.</p></details><details class="t13ne-vector"><summary><strong>Vector: </strong> Walls </summary><p class="t13ne-description">Walls and barriers can tell us things about buildings and people, but they can also be a source of graffiti and bill posters that spread word of bands, club nights, impending temporal schisms, and so on.</p></details><details class="t13ne-alternate"><summary><strong>Alternate: </strong> Infamy </summary><p class="t13ne-description">The information concerns the most debased and terrible things the Embodiment has ever been accused of. <em title="maybe all">Some</em> of them may even be true.</p></details><details class="t13ne-detail"><summary><strong>Detail: </strong> Flaw </summary><p class="t13ne-description">Everyone and and everything have their flaws, in T13 they often have at least one Hitched of the Flaw Tier, but this might be a flaw in their story, or a flaw in the prosecution's case.</p></details></details>
		 <details class="t13ne-ordeal-fancy"><summary><strong>Ordeal</strong></summary><p class="t13ne-ordeal-type"><strong>Ordeal-Type </strong> — Opposed Ordeal</p><p class="t13ne-stages"><strong>Stages </strong> — 5</p><p class="t13ne-ordeal-spread"><strong>Ordeal-Spread </strong> — Block</p><p class="t13ne-stakes"><strong>Stakes </strong> — Medium</p><p class="t13ne-test"><strong>Test </strong> — Tempt (Sin)</p><p class="t13ne-obstacles"><strong>Obstacles </strong> — 1 Obstacle in every Non-Court Stage, 2 in every Court Stage</p><p class="t13ne-suggested-action"><strong>Suggested-Action </strong> — Flirt</p><details class="t13ne-stage"><summary><strong>Stage: </strong> Skid-row </summary><p class="t13ne-description">Red-light districts are the home of sin, down and outs litter the streets (to say nothing of the litter). There's plenty going on at all times of the day or night, and you can't generally drive through at speed because of the crawling cars and occasional police presence.</p></details><details class="t13ne-motional"><summary><strong>Motional: </strong> Foot Chase </summary><p class="t13ne-description">Each breath burns your lungs, but you can still see them ahead, as they cross the bridge, and head down to the platform and waiting train below. Ignoring the pain, you push on, you have to make it to that train.</p></details><details class="t13ne-fight"><summary><strong>Fight: </strong> Targeted Assault </summary><p class="t13ne-description">A force around the Party's size and power-level attacks. The attackers focus their attack on one Party member (usually the most Pressed or Hooked), trying to inflict maximum damage for one Round, after which they retreat, fighting a retreating battle. Also called a Drive-By.</p></details></details>
		 </details>
			</div>
				<div class="t13ne-card-lea"><details class="t13ne-lea"><summary><strong>Lea</strong></summary><p class="t13ne-colour"><strong>Path Colour —</strong> Green/Turquoise Herbivore</p><p class="t13ne-bulmas"><strong>Allowed Bulmäs —</strong> Herd, Broccen, Vermis, Labyrinthine, All-Walkers</p><p class="t13ne-animal-category"><strong>Animal Category —</strong> Megafauna – Largest</p><p class="t13ne-animal-example"><strong>Example Animal —</strong> Hippopotamus / Elephant / Sauropod / Mammoth </p><p class="t13ne-notes"><strong>Notes —</strong> These uncommon paths are only really walkable in Africa and India. Elsewhere on Earth they are more likely to connect back to the Palaeolithic (or earlier), becoming buried beneath more modern paths.</p></details></div></div><div class="t13ne-cardwrap-jsvg" id="cardrewritebox36" data-mode="jsvg" data-wyrd="1" data-yarn="1"><img src="https://www.cjmoseley.dev.cc/wp-content/plugins/t13ne/images/cards-svg/JC.svg" class="t13ne-svgcard"><div class="t13ne-svgpips"><div class="t13ne-textpips">11<!-- write suit--><span class="t13ne-symbol"><span class="t13ne-blackcard">♣</span></span><details class="t13ne-suit"><summary></summary><p class="t13ne-name"><strong>Suit: </strong>Clubs <span class="t13ne-blackcard">♣</span></p><p><strong>Ordeal Trump: </strong><span class="t13ne-trump">Defence</span></p><p class="t13ne-desc"><strong>Description: </strong>Clubs represent the power of politics and a stoic materialism. In the Tarot the Clubs are called Staves or Wands, in some traditions they represent Intellectual Air, but in most they are Fire.</p><p class="t13ne-tarot"><strong>Tarot Meaning: </strong>Power, Fame, Politics, Ability, Money.</p><p class="t13ne-otext"><strong>Ordeal Text: </strong>Ordeal Types: Wealth, Politics, Power. Clubs act as Trump for following Actions: Bartering, Controlling, Corrupting, Emotional-intensity, Guarding, Magic, Displaying might, Politics, Self-Defence, Socializing, and Trade Negotiations. Clubs can be used to defend any type of attack.</p><p class="t13ne-parry"><strong>Parry: </strong><strong>Disarm:</strong> All the cards of the attack are immediately discarded, along with the Parry Card. The remaining cards of the Defender's Parry can now each be used to cause the Attacker to drop a Descendant (starting with the attacking weapon usually), can also cut armour straps, belts etc, or steal hats. This basically adds RT equal to the card Pips to the Annex that was used to attack. Every 5 Pips also creates a point of GRT for the Character (representing how much time the Character must spend picking up, or readying the weapon again. Court cards additionally may damage the Descendant that was disarmed, as standard for the Stakes.</p><p class="t13ne-soak"><strong>Soak: </strong><strong>Ward:</strong> The matched Attack card, and one other randomly selected Attack card are discarded.</p></details></div></div><div class="t13ne-svg-facet"><div>Fury</div></div><div class="t13ne-wound"><div><strong class="t13ne-woundname">Cripple</strong><details><summary></summary><strong>Cripple</strong><p class="t13ne-base-pips">Base Pips to summon: 12</p><p class="t13ne-min-pips">Minimum Pips Required: 10</p><p class="t13ne-desc">The Target is seriously wounded, losing access to whole abilities. This can even result in Coma.</p><details class="t13ne-roll"><summary>Rules</summary>Lose 1 Annex Slot (usually the largest one involved in the Roll) until Healed. Once healed Proficiencies may be repurchased for 1 Chi each. Then the Annex will regenerate at Phoenix / Incarna Score Boons per Day. It may be purchased back for the normal cost (Boon in Chi + 2/5/8/12) instantly (or altered and purchased back as a new Annex, if narratively appropriate).</details><details class="t13ne-ordeal"><summary>Ordeal Rules</summary>Crippled Annexes cannot be used as long as the Wound is in play. If applied to a Personality Annex (or a Descendant Master Annex) then the Alternate is unconscious (or the Descendant is lost or useless). They may not use that Alternate (or Descendant) until the Wound is healed (or found / fixed).</details><details class="t13ne-psych"><summary>Psych Wounds</summary><details class="t13ne-negative"><summary>Negative Psych</summary><p class="t13ne-normal"><strong>Normal</strong>Automatically affects the Personality Annex. No Proficiencies or Annexes are lost, but Character loses consciousness at sight of the source until healed.</p><p class="t13ne-extreme"><strong>Extreme</strong>Automatically affects the Personality Annex. Loses consciousness until Healed (not just when the source is present).</p></details><details class="t13ne-positive"><summary>Positive Psych</summary><p class="t13ne-normal"><strong>Normal</strong>Consciousness is interrupted for a few moments (1 Phase). May Gain a Skill Annex/Descendant or 3 Proficiencies.</p><p class="t13ne-extreme"><strong>Extreme</strong>Target loses consciousness (or at least responsiveness) until Stabilised. Gains a new Skill Annex/Descendant.</p></details></details></details></div></div><div class="t13ne-tarot-svg"><details><summary class="t13ne-wyrdtarot"><strong> Wyrd Tarot: </strong> Charm</summary><p class="t13ne-normal"><strong> Normal: </strong>Target gains +2  Success Levels on any Social actions while atop the pile. Additionally adds a "Charm" Reserve for Target that they may "Prepare" Ordeal cards to for any social action.</p><p class="t13ne-crossed"><strong> Crossed: </strong>Target takes -2 Success Levels on any Social actions while atop the pile. Additionally automatically closes and discards any "Charm" Reserve.</p></details></div><div class="t13ne-yarn-fancy">
			<details><summary><strong>Yarn</strong> <br>The Tempest</summary>
			<span class="t13ne-description">The Tempest is the card of violent disturbance. People are scared, afraid, and otherwise perturbed by the violence of this situation, be it of the weather or a Character. Tempests can be tumultuous events of almost any sort, storms, wars, shipwrecks, political upheavals, coups, etc. It is a period of destruction, where the old is torn down, not necessarily to make way for the new.</span>
			<span class="t13ne-significator">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-significator"><strong>Significator</strong></summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-plot"><strong>Plot: </strong> The Tempest Plot is about destruction and tearing down the old world. Huge changes and shifts in social norms are indicated by the Tempest. It is the Plot of disaster movies, apocalypses and wars.</aside>
					<aside class="t13ne-char"><strong>Character: </strong> The Tempest Significator indicates a Character who is violent and unpredictable. They Gain Yin when they are attacked. They Gain Yang when they attack. They Gain Chi when they act randomly or react violently. They also gain +1 Success Level on every attack.</aside>
					<aside class="t13ne-scene"><strong>Scene: </strong> The Tempest Plot is about destruction and tearing down the old world. Huge changes and shifts in social norms are indicated by the Tempest. It is the Plot of disaster movies, apocalypses and wars.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-scene">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-scene-type">
						<strong>Scene: </strong> Gain
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">Gains give something to one of the Characters to help move the Story on. Sometimes this might be payment for a job they did, or it might be a discovery that will directly connect to the Story. This may be information. It is normal to play a separate card to define the actual Gain that is made.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-conflict-embodiment">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-conflict-embodiment">
						<strong>Conflict Embodiment: </strong> Tension
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">The Conflict embodies within two Characters, but they do not come into direct Conflict. They have some relationship, that Embodies the Conflict as arguments, discussions, jokes, etc.  common to Romance and Buddy movies.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>

			<span class="t13ne-facet-embodiment">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-facet-embodiment">
						<strong>Facet Embodiment: </strong> Hitch
					</summary>
					<section class="t13ne-description">The Conflict Embodies one Facet as a Character Hitch. This can create quite secretive and subtle embodiments that can be very difficult to spot. Other times Hitch Embodiments can be very obvious, and in the case of Woes, glaring.</section>
				</details>
			</span>
			 <div class="t13ne-archetypes"><details><summary><strong>Archetype Character: </strong>Prince</summary> <details class="t13ne-Persona"><summary><strong>Persona: </strong><span class="t13ne-output">Leader</span>
      </summary>
      <details class="t13ne-motivation"><summary><strong>Motivation: </strong></summary><span class="t13ne-description">A Leader seeks to be followed, they may have ambitions to rise in rank and command, or they may just be happy with a small band of followers. </span></details>
       <details class="t13ne-avoid"><summary><strong>Avoid: </strong></summary><span class="t13ne-description">Leaders worry about losing the respect of their followers, and so tend to avoid whatever behaviour they believe might do that.</span></details>
