       <details class="t13ne-gain-chi"><summary><strong>Gain Chi: </strong></summary><span class="t13ne-description"><span>Gain Chi when your orders are followed, you make suggestions, ask questions, or you succeed alone.</span><p>Ordeal gain Chi when you play any card on, or for, an ally.</p></span></details></details>   <details class="t13ne-core"><summary><strong>Core: </strong><span class="t13ne-output">Aggressor</span></summary><span class="t13ne-description">Gain Chi when you lose your temper, shout, or argue.</span></details><details class="t13ne-hitch"><summary><strong>Hitch: </strong><span class="t13ne-output">Doubt</span></summary><span class="t13ne-description">Doubt is the opposite of belief and yet walks hand in hand with it. Mistrust and Suspicion are related, and these Hitches affect a lot of people. Think about whether you trust your politicians, police or your friends.</span></details><p class="t13ne-description">A Prince is a noble ruler of an area, often brutal and inexperienced, they are usually defined by their doubts and how they deal with them.</p></details></div>
			 <span class="t13ne-hook">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-hook">
						<strong>Hook: </strong> It Was A Dark And Stormy Night...
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">The Story opens with inclement weather, this may reflect the Conflict in some strange way, or simply be a bad night to be out. Into this night is injected a scene of violence, perhaps a murder or assault. It might be the PCs who are attacked, or more likely the dying stranger at their door.</aside>
					<details class="t13ne-hook-aspect">
						<summary><strong>Hook Aspect: </strong>I-Ching</summary>
						<section class="t13ne-description">The Hook targets the Character's I-Ching. For example: If a Character has the Cauldron (50) I-Ching, then the Hook needs to have someone hungry turn up asking for help.</section>
					</details>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-obstacle-fancy"><details class="t13ne-obstacle"><summary><strong>Obstacle:</strong>Weather</summary><p class="t13ne-description">It seems the Weather is not on your side and is hampering your progress, winds are against you, rain, hail, snow, even the sun, whatever really. The Weather is a (Vex / Chorus / Cast) Extra, depending on the Ref's mood and the Narrative (if you have been told about the storm rolling in for hours don't expect the a Vex).</p></details></span>
			<span class="t13ne-gain">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-gain">
						<strong>Gain </strong> 
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">Monolith / Chronolith or Artefact</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-fray">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-fray">
						<strong>Fray: </strong> Tempest
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">A Tempest is a storm or similar huge event (Terrorist attack, aerial bombardment, war front, tsunami, volcano, etc) that uses 1-12 Force-Of-Nature Extras, or go for a Kaiju in extreme circumstances, to create an Extreme Stakes Motional Ordeal. These events are destructive to Locations / Stages which can result in paths being closed or creating new obstacles. Ideally, the PCs should have their own goals, entirely separate to the Tempest that they are trying to complete against this backdrop of destruction.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-snag">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-snag">
						<strong>Snag: </strong> Alarming
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">Ideally, one of the Embodiment's Allies, or a random passer-by, is disturbed by the actions, or sight of, of the PCs. This is especially common in bestial or ghostly Characters who didn't mean to scare the little girl... Alternatively, the Fray will be complicated by an alarm being triggered, usually by the PCs actions, this can include, Police Alarms, Fire Alarms, Security Alerts, Lock downs, or screams.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-sweeping">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-sweeping">
						<strong>Sweeping: </strong> False Completion
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">This Sweeping causes the PCs to think that their struggle is over, that they have won early. But this is just the Eye of the Storm, the storm will return. This is occasionally some attempt by the other sides to trick or flush out the PCs, but this should be used rarely and with care, more normally it is a chance to recover and rest, and perhaps a little preparation before the storm returns.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
		 <details class="t13ne-revelation-fancy"><summary><strong>Revelation</strong></summary><details class="t13ne-about"><summary><strong>About: </strong> The Story </summary><p class="t13ne-description">The information is about the current situation and plot. It might include information about either side of the Conflict, or both. It is normal when providing information to only reveal information about a single Side of the Conflict, usually the Dominant or Above sides.</p></details><details class="t13ne-info"><summary><strong>Info: </strong> Reasons </summary><p class="t13ne-description">Things always happen for reasons, there are reasons the people are starving, and crime is rampant, sometimes the right reasons might have been identified, but usually not. This is especially true of the reasons people are angry...</p></details><details class="t13ne-vector"><summary><strong>Vector: </strong> Synchronicities </summary><p class="t13ne-description">First codified by Jung, these are coincidences with apparent meaning. The meaning may be the information that is passed, or it may just be part of one of the coincidences, by synchronicity.</p></details><details class="t13ne-alternate"><summary><strong>Alternate: </strong> Obstacles </summary><p class="t13ne-description">The information is either about the Obstacles that stand in an Ordeal, or may be encoded in Obstacles in the next (or last) Ordeal. When they realise they were meant to count the number of spears in the pit trap. Oh, the look on their faces...</p></details><details class="t13ne-detail"><summary><strong>Detail: </strong> Date </summary><p class="t13ne-description">The detail is an important date, it might be coming soon, or perhaps the anniversary of an important event is coming up.</p></details></details>
		 <details class="t13ne-ordeal-fancy"><summary><strong>Ordeal</strong></summary><p class="t13ne-ordeal-type"><strong>Ordeal-Type </strong> — Opposed Ordeal</p><p class="t13ne-stages"><strong>Stages </strong> — 6</p><p class="t13ne-ordeal-spread"><strong>Ordeal-Spread </strong> — Map-Grid</p><p class="t13ne-stakes"><strong>Stakes </strong> — Blended Low-Medium</p><p class="t13ne-test"><strong>Test </strong> — Guard (Rook)</p><p class="t13ne-obstacles"><strong>Obstacles </strong> — 1 Obstacle in every Non-Court Stage, 2 in every Court Stage</p><p class="t13ne-suggested-action"><strong>Suggested-Action </strong> — Soak</p><details class="t13ne-stage"><summary><strong>Stage: </strong> Jump Stunt </summary><p class="t13ne-description">Jump over, past, or through an Obstacle. There's always at least one Obstacle that must be jumped over or through in this Stage.</p></details><details class="t13ne-motional"><summary><strong>Motional: </strong> Swimming Chase </summary><p class="t13ne-description">They swim, you swim after them.</p></details><details class="t13ne-fight"><summary><strong>Fight: </strong> Knight </summary><p class="t13ne-description">The opponent is a solitary Monster, usually well armoured (passive defences) and with strong attacks. Should be at least as powerful as any Two PCs. At least 2 ♣ Monster Facets<!-- t13ne-shortcode "%7B%22type%22%3A%22facetsuitaspect%22%2C%22suit%22%3A%223%22%2C%22aspect%22%3A%22Monster%22%7D"// --></p><div class="t13ne-tablewrap"><table class="t13ne-table Monster"><thead><tr><th>&nbsp;&nbsp;Facet&nbsp;&nbsp;</th><th>&nbsp;&nbsp;Monster&nbsp;&nbsp;</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td>Burden</td><td><strong>Object</strong> —The Monster is an object imbued with the semblance of life. Artificial in nature usually, the living object is usually tough and strong. An Object Monster is created with a Monster Pool equal to their Burden Score. Objects are able to multiply their Scores by 1+Monster Pool Size when appropriate to their form and function. The Object Monster may instantly Prepare cards from its Monster Pool, often as a Prepared Defence or Stunt Preparation, as appropriate for the Object's form or function.</td></tr><tr><td>Dominion</td><td><strong>Horde</strong> —The Monster is a group or swarm of lesser monsters, it might be an Army, a pack of Bulmäs, a Fortress or a Battle Moon. Horde is effectively a Pact Descendant. A Horde may pool Values to create larger rolls, however they may never create a Boon greater than their Value. Hordes may add Dominion Draw cards to their Monster Pool when the Horde increases in numbers. The Horde must Play at least one card from their Monster Pool on any action that splits the Horde (like losing an individual). E.g. two Vex Extras of Boon 10 (Value 45) that form a Horde become a single Extra of Boon 16 (Value 90), (with probably 1 card in the Monster Pool) but could never create a Boon greater than the individual's Value(45). If there were 11 Vexes, that would be Boon 44 (Value 495). 12 or One million Vexes would both give Boon 45 (Value 530) since that is the most powerful their Horde can get.</td></tr><tr><td>Fury</td><td><strong>Temperamental</strong> —The Monster is the embodiment of an Emotion, usually a negative one. Such Monsters are often capable of supernatural actions, such as telepathy, shape-shifting, or even undying. The Vampire is an embodiment of Lust, with powers of mind control, telepathy and suggestion, as well as shape-shifting abilities. The Werewolf is berserk rage, and almost indestructible unless Silver is used. The Beansidhe is the embodiment of mourning, and able to sense an oncoming death. When they experience their negative emotion the Monster may refill their Monster Pool to Fury Score cards. When appropriate to their Emotion they must add a Monster Pool card to the action and multiply the total Score by their remaining Monster Pool Reduced.</td></tr><tr><td>Rook</td><td><strong>Guardian</strong> —The Monster is a Guardian, it guards a certain territory, path, treasure or person. Guardians may add Rook Draw to their Monster Pool whenever they act as a guard, try to interfere with an attack, or are attacked. The Guardian reduces all attacks by a number of Pips equal to the current Monster Pool automatically. The Guardian will however automatically play cards from its Monster Pool to defend itself (via any Active or Passive defence method that makes sense to the Referee. From Smaug to Fafnir dragons are often Guardians, looking after a particular treasure or demanding tributes from a territory.</td></tr><tr><td>Sin</td><td><strong>Villain</strong> —The Sin Monster is an embodiment of a specific vice, taboo, or sin, often in the form of a human Villain (often with animalistic features or tendencies). They usually have Annexes and Hitches that reflect their embodied vice or sin, and are often at least partially supernatural. Villains add Sin Draw cards to their Monster Pool when they, or another (usually both) indulges in their particular vice or behaviour. Which can be as broad as "Evil" if you like. Villains may add +1 Score for each card in their Monster Pool for any action that furthers their diabolical plans. Villains automatically spend Monster Pool cards on escape actions when attacked, creating at least Sin Draw Obstacles from the Monster Pools cards. The genocidal or narcissistic maniac, of the sort British Intelligence seems to tangle with repeatedly, could be a Sin Monster. The ever-hungry cannibal spirit, the Wendigo is definitely an example, as is the Sexual-predatory Lust-embodied Vampire.</td></tr><tr><td>Wyrd</td><td><strong>Doom-weaver</strong> —The monster is a creature of the Fæ, it can draw and play Yarn cards or Wyrd Tarot cards (as appropriate). The Wyrd Monster may play cards from its Monster Pool as Wyrd Tarot or Yarn cards. The Monster may draw and add Wyrd Draw cards to its Monster Pool whenever it makes a choice, decision, or judgement. The Doom-weaver may draw and play Wyrd Draw cards as Ordeal cards whenever anyone plays Wyrd Tarot or Yarn cards. They must play a card from the Monster Pool if they have to undo a choice, decision or judgement they have made. Doom-Weavers come in many forms from the Cait-Sith, the Beansidhe, to the Faery nobles of Ironmaster.</td></tr></tbody><tfoot><tr><td>&nbsp;&nbsp;Facet&nbsp;&nbsp;</td><td>&nbsp;&nbsp;Monster&nbsp;&nbsp;</td></tr></tfoot></table></div><p></p></details></details>
		 </details>
			</div>
				<div class="t13ne-card-lea"><details class="t13ne-lea"><summary><strong>Lea</strong></summary><p class="t13ne-colour"><strong>Path Colour —</strong> Green/Turquoise Herbivore</p><p class="t13ne-bulmas"><strong>Allowed Bulmäs —</strong> Herd, Broccen, Vermis, Labyrinthine, All-Walkers</p><p class="t13ne-animal-category"><strong>Animal Category —</strong> Man-paths/Broccen Territory</p><p class="t13ne-animal-example"><strong>Example Animal —</strong> Human / Groonians / Eelafin / Eusaivean / Mer-person / Bigfoot / Giant / Goblin, etc</p><p class="t13ne-notes"><strong>Notes —</strong> Broccen may use Jack’s to dig new tunnels in the Lea, creating species to fill niches, in this case specific Herbivores may be created. Jacks may also be used to create Chimerical changes, blending parts of different animals together, each Jack adds another place the Bulmäs can play Lea cards.</p></details></div></div><div class="t13ne-cardwrap-jsvg" id="cardrewritebox37" data-mode="jsvg" data-wyrd="1" data-yarn="1"><img src="https://www.cjmoseley.dev.cc/wp-content/plugins/t13ne/images/cards-svg/QC.svg" class="t13ne-svgcard"><div class="t13ne-svgpips"><div class="t13ne-textpips">12<!-- write suit--><span class="t13ne-symbol"><span class="t13ne-blackcard">♣</span></span><details class="t13ne-suit"><summary></summary><p class="t13ne-name"><strong>Suit: </strong>Clubs <span class="t13ne-blackcard">♣</span></p><p><strong>Ordeal Trump: </strong><span class="t13ne-trump">Defence</span></p><p class="t13ne-desc"><strong>Description: </strong>Clubs represent the power of politics and a stoic materialism. In the Tarot the Clubs are called Staves or Wands, in some traditions they represent Intellectual Air, but in most they are Fire.</p><p class="t13ne-tarot"><strong>Tarot Meaning: </strong>Power, Fame, Politics, Ability, Money.</p><p class="t13ne-otext"><strong>Ordeal Text: </strong>Ordeal Types: Wealth, Politics, Power. Clubs act as Trump for following Actions: Bartering, Controlling, Corrupting, Emotional-intensity, Guarding, Magic, Displaying might, Politics, Self-Defence, Socializing, and Trade Negotiations. Clubs can be used to defend any type of attack.</p><p class="t13ne-parry"><strong>Parry: </strong><strong>Disarm:</strong> All the cards of the attack are immediately discarded, along with the Parry Card. The remaining cards of the Defender's Parry can now each be used to cause the Attacker to drop a Descendant (starting with the attacking weapon usually), can also cut armour straps, belts etc, or steal hats. This basically adds RT equal to the card Pips to the Annex that was used to attack. Every 5 Pips also creates a point of GRT for the Character (representing how much time the Character must spend picking up, or readying the weapon again. Court cards additionally may damage the Descendant that was disarmed, as standard for the Stakes.</p><p class="t13ne-soak"><strong>Soak: </strong><strong>Ward:</strong> The matched Attack card, and one other randomly selected Attack card are discarded.</p></details></div></div><div class="t13ne-svg-facet"><div>Sin</div></div><div class="t13ne-wound"><div><strong class="t13ne-woundname">Mortal</strong><details><summary></summary><strong>Mortal</strong><p class="t13ne-base-pips">Base Pips to summon: 16</p><p class="t13ne-min-pips">Minimum Pips Required: 12</p><p class="t13ne-desc">The Target is now dying from the wound.</p><details class="t13ne-roll"><summary>Rules</summary>Create a "Dying / Broken / Burning" quirk Hitch that slowly grows +1 Hitch Boon per Miasma Boon as Chi Limit Duration) until healed. If no Hitch can be increased by the Mortal Wound then decrease an appropriate Facet (Hitch, Incarna, Persona or Core usually) by a Boon equal to the Wounded Character’s Miasma Score. I-Ching, Personality, Proficiency slots and Annexes affected should be recalculated. If the Hitch becomes a Flaw the Wound will automatically upgrade to a Carnage Wound.</details><details class="t13ne-ordeal"><summary>Ordeal Rules</summary>Treat as though the Personality Annex or Master Annex has been Crippled, until the end of the Round. Then add a new "Dying / Broken / Burning" quirk Hitch with a Boon of Miasma Score (to a Max of Half the Incarna Boon). Or increase a quirk Hitch by 1 Boon at the end of each Round.</details><details class="t13ne-psych"><summary>Psych Wounds</summary><details class="t13ne-negative"><summary>Negative Psych</summary><p class="t13ne-normal"><strong>Normal</strong>As Normal but does not create a "Dying" Hitch, but instead a Scars (Mental/Emotional) / Fear / Fatigued / Lament / Taboo / Offensive / Doubt,  etc Hitch.  Plus Consciousness is usually lost until Stabilised.</p><p class="t13ne-extreme"><strong>Extreme</strong>Creates any normal Hitch type. At Yarn-Teller's discretion Mortal Wounds can also alter the Persona, or Core Facet choice (Ref arbitrates). Plus Consciousness is usually lost until healed.</p></details><details class="t13ne-positive"><summary>Positive Psych</summary><p class="t13ne-normal"><strong>Normal</strong>As Normal but creates a Scars (Mental/Emotional) / Duty / Dependance / Paranoia / Devotion / Madness / Vague, etc quirk Hitch.  Plus Consciousness is usually lost until Stabilised. May Gain a new Talent Annex / Descendant or Resolve a quirk Hitch.</p><p class="t13ne-extreme"><strong>Extreme</strong>May create any Flaw Hitch. At Yarn-Teller's discretion Mortal Wounds can also alter the wounded character’s Persona, or Core Facet choices. Consciousness is usually lost until healed. Gains a new Talent Annex / Descendant or Resolves a quirk Handicap.</p></details></details></details></div></div><div class="t13ne-tarot-svg"><details><summary class="t13ne-wyrdtarot"><strong> Wyrd Tarot: </strong> Plan</summary><p class="t13ne-normal"><strong> Normal: </strong>+1 Success Level to everyone following the Plan while atop pile. Additionally creates a "Plan" Reserve for each participant that they may "Prepare" Ordeal cards to, that may be used for any Planned action.</p><p class="t13ne-crossed"><strong> Crossed: </strong>+2 Success Levels when working alone while atop pile.</p></details></div><div class="t13ne-yarn-fancy">
			<details><summary><strong>Yarn</strong> <br>The Inn</summary>
			<span class="t13ne-description">The Inn card represents the den of iniquity, impropriety, sins and vices that every society has. The Inn may simply be the place that exotic outsiders stay in town, but more often it is the place of shady deals, criminal and immoral activities and recreational intoxicants. It offers satisfaction, comfort, protection, anonymity, and a certain lack of consequences to those who visit, with hints of adventure, rebelliousness, risk, and even danger for those that seek it.</span>
			<span class="t13ne-significator">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-significator"><strong>Significator</strong></summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-plot"><strong>Plot: </strong> The Inn Plot is about sins, dirty secrets and adult situations. There is always an air of adventure to the Inn, a temptation that can be a part of coming of age plots, and such like, but is more normally a part of a deeper awakening.</aside>
					<aside class="t13ne-char"><strong>Character: </strong> The Inn Significator indicates a Character who revels in the joys of life. They Gain Yin when they take pleasure in something. They Gain Yang when they tempt others. They Gain Chi when they enjoy something socially. They also gain +1 Success Level on all passive defences.</aside>
					<aside class="t13ne-scene"><strong>Scene: </strong> The Inn Plot is about sins, dirty secrets and adult situations. There is always an air of adventure to the Inn, a temptation that can be a part of coming of age plots, and such like, but is more normally a part of a deeper awakening.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-scene">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-scene-type">
						<strong>Scene: </strong> Revelation
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">Revelations reveal something about the Conflict or Plot to the Characters. Typically a Revelation tells the Characters something about the Villain, or the Prize they are after. Revelations usually consist of 2-5 cards that define: What the information is about, what the actual info is, the vector the Revelation is revealed by, and per haps some Alternative Information that might be revealed (in case you can't use the Info), and a detail that is involved somehow.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-conflict-embodiment">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-conflict-embodiment">
						<strong>Conflict Embodiment: </strong> Tension
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">The Conflict embodies within two Characters, but they do not come into direct Conflict. They have some relationship, that Embodies the Conflict as arguments, discussions, jokes, etc.  common to Romance and Buddy movies.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>

			<span class="t13ne-facet-embodiment">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-facet-embodiment">
						<strong>Facet Embodiment: </strong> Resolved Hitch
					</summary>
					<section class="t13ne-description">The Conflict Embodies one Facet as a Character's Resolved Hitch. As with all Character Embodiments, these can be obvious under the right circumstances.</section>
				</details>
			</span>
			 <div class="t13ne-archetypes"><details><summary><strong>Archetype Character: </strong>Bar-tender</summary> <details class="t13ne-Persona"><summary><strong>Persona: </strong><span class="t13ne-output">Vandal</span>
      </summary>
      <details class="t13ne-motivation"><summary><strong>Motivation: </strong></summary><span class="t13ne-description">Vandals (or Trolls) like nothing more than inciting other people's anger and outrage, except perhaps the beautiful music of smashing glass and crashing noises. Vandals are driven by a serious desire to smash, destroy, degrade and insult, and enjoy watching it happen as much as doing it in most cases.</span></details>
       <details class="t13ne-avoid"><summary><strong>Avoid: </strong></summary><span class="t13ne-description">Apologies are the bane of the Vandal's existence. Not only are they no fun, but they effectively undo all the outrage that the Vandal has caused, and they never enjoy that.</span></details>
       <details class="t13ne-gain-chi"><summary><strong>Gain Chi: </strong></summary><span class="t13ne-description"><span>Gain Chi whenever you see or hear someone, or something being attacked, insulted, mocked, or degraded in some way, even if you are the one doing it.</span><p>Ordeal gain Chi when anyone plays any card that successfully Wounds anyone, or anything, except you and your Descendants.</p></span></details></details>   <details class="t13ne-core"><summary><strong>Core: </strong><span class="t13ne-output">Greeter</span></summary><span class="t13ne-description">Gain Chi when they speak to a stranger or group of strangers.</span></details><details class="t13ne-hitch"><summary><strong>Hitch: </strong><span class="t13ne-output">Confined</span></summary><span class="t13ne-description">When imprisoned, bound, held, confined, locked up, unable to cross running water, you can handle all that with a Key Hitch.</span></details><p class="t13ne-description">The Bar-tender is a common archetype, you'll find one behind most bars. They enjoy meeting people and don't mind hearing another's problems and offering them beverages. May sometimes cross over with Priests and Cleric types, who also practice listening to problems and offering beverages.</p></details></div>
			 <span class="t13ne-hook">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-hook">
						<strong>Hook: </strong> The Stranger Arrives
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">The Story opens with a stranger arriving in town. This person may be a Rival or an Embodiment, but more normally is some unknown Ally who brings the PCs some warning or message about a threat beyond the town.</aside>
					<details class="t13ne-hook-aspect">
						<summary><strong>Hook Aspect: </strong>I-Ching</summary>
						<section class="t13ne-description">The Hook targets the Character's I-Ching. For example: If a Character has the Cauldron (50) I-Ching, then the Hook needs to have someone hungry turn up asking for help.</section>
					</details>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-obstacle-fancy"><details class="t13ne-obstacle"><summary><strong>Obstacle:</strong>Temptation</summary><p class="t13ne-description">The Obstacle is a temptation, traditionally this is represented by a Pleasure Dome filled with loads of semi-naked, sexually-alluring people, but temptations vary, drink (a bar), gambling (casino), drugs (opium den / crack-house), money or valuables lying around, or even a video game may also qualify.</p></details></span>
			<span class="t13ne-gain">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-gain">
						<strong>Gain </strong> 
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">Dark Territory (a Twist riddled Location)</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-fray">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-fray">
						<strong>Fray: </strong> Social Drinking
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">The Fray involves a complex social Ordeal, usually of Medium Stakes, although higher and lower Stakes versions are possible. There will be drinks, usually alcoholic, which may be replaced or augmented by other recreational drugs. This Fray is social and allows Characters to alter their social status with other Characters, earning respect, friendship or enmity as appropriate.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-snag">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-snag">
						<strong>Snag: </strong> Illegal or Immoral
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">Something about the situation is either Illegal or Immoral, ideally it should be a choice between immoral or illegal (such as keeping slaves being immoral, but freeing slaves being illegal).</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-sweeping">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-sweeping">
						<strong>Sweeping: </strong> Party!
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">The PCs may have cause to celebrate from recent events, more likely they want to distract themselves from recent events with a celebration. There will be drinks, there will be music or games, and there is usually food (often cake). It may be a Character's birthday, or a similar anniversary, or a feast or festival.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
		 <details class="t13ne-revelation-fancy"><summary><strong>Revelation</strong></summary><details class="t13ne-about"><summary><strong>About: </strong> The Story </summary><p class="t13ne-description">The information is about the current situation and plot. It might include information about either side of the Conflict, or both. It is normal when providing information to only reveal information about a single Side of the Conflict, usually the Dominant or Above sides.</p></details><details class="t13ne-info"><summary><strong>Info: </strong> Pleasures </summary><p class="t13ne-description">Information about what an Embodiment likes to do with their time is always useful. Sometimes we can learn more from someone's Vices than from their words.</p></details><details class="t13ne-vector"><summary><strong>Vector: </strong> Tapestry </summary><p class="t13ne-description">The information is present upon a tapestry, poster, billboard, or perhaps is read from Wyrd's Tapestry directly for some, or by Doom-reading from cards in play (the bad guy has a Wyrd Tarot Hand?! He must be at least a Mercari).</p></details><details class="t13ne-alternate"><summary><strong>Alternate: </strong> Sympathetic </summary><p class="t13ne-description">The information covers the reasons the bad guy is doing what he is doing and it turns out that, yeah, he might be doing the right thing after all. Maybe he is leading an army invading the country because his twin sister has been kidnapped by the king, or perhaps the reason he butchers the desert snake tribes <em>that way</em> is because he was raised by a nest of serpent people hiding in the capital city, after they slaughtered his family in front of him.</p></details><details class="t13ne-detail"><summary><strong>Detail: </strong> Mistake </summary><p class="t13ne-description">A Mistake detail could be a misspelled word, or an auto-corrected text, or it could be a deliberate mistake. So are the outlaws you're tracking inside the barn and forgot to post a rear guard, or are they well aware you are out here and have deliberately left a way in?</p></details></details>
		 <details class="t13ne-ordeal-fancy"><summary><strong>Ordeal</strong></summary><p class="t13ne-ordeal-type"><strong>Ordeal-Type </strong> — Opposed Ordeal</p><p class="t13ne-stages"><strong>Stages </strong> — 6</p><p class="t13ne-ordeal-spread"><strong>Ordeal-Spread </strong> — Map-Hex</p><p class="t13ne-stakes"><strong>Stakes </strong> — Blended Medium-High</p><p class="t13ne-test"><strong>Test </strong> — Law (Wyrd)</p><p class="t13ne-obstacles"><strong>Obstacles </strong> — 2 Obstacles in every Non-Court Stage, 3 in every Court Stage</p><p class="t13ne-suggested-action"><strong>Suggested-Action </strong> — Dodge</p><details class="t13ne-stage"><summary><strong>Stage: </strong> Quick Stunt </summary><p class="t13ne-description">A number of Obstacles, A trap/or other Obstacles, an unforeseen expense</p></details><details class="t13ne-motional"><summary><strong>Motional: </strong> Winged Chase </summary><p class="t13ne-description">He blows you a kiss, and steps back from the edge of the building, dropping from sight, before he unfurls his wings and slips into a long glide across the rooftops. You hurl your grappling hook, and swing to chase.</p></details><details class="t13ne-fight"><summary><strong>Fight: </strong> Bad Boy / Girl </summary><p class="t13ne-description">The fight is against a solitary opponent who is around the level of the strongest PC. Usually the opponent will use subterfuge and asymmetrical warfare to isolate and neutralise the PCs. Usually a Monster with at least one ♣ Monster Facet. <!-- t13ne-shortcode "%7B%22type%22%3A%22facetsuitaspect%22%2C%22suit%22%3A%223%22%2C%22aspect%22%3A%22Monster%22%7D"// --></p><div class="t13ne-tablewrap"><table class="t13ne-table Monster"><thead><tr><th>&nbsp;&nbsp;Facet&nbsp;&nbsp;</th><th>&nbsp;&nbsp;Monster&nbsp;&nbsp;</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td>Burden</td><td><strong>Object</strong> —The Monster is an object imbued with the semblance of life. Artificial in nature usually, the living object is usually tough and strong. An Object Monster is created with a Monster Pool equal to their Burden Score. Objects are able to multiply their Scores by 1+Monster Pool Size when appropriate to their form and function. The Object Monster may instantly Prepare cards from its Monster Pool, often as a Prepared Defence or Stunt Preparation, as appropriate for the Object's form or function.</td></tr><tr><td>Dominion</td><td><strong>Horde</strong> —The Monster is a group or swarm of lesser monsters, it might be an Army, a pack of Bulmäs, a Fortress or a Battle Moon. Horde is effectively a Pact Descendant. A Horde may pool Values to create larger rolls, however they may never create a Boon greater than their Value. Hordes may add Dominion Draw cards to their Monster Pool when the Horde increases in numbers. The Horde must Play at least one card from their Monster Pool on any action that splits the Horde (like losing an individual). E.g. two Vex Extras of Boon 10 (Value 45) that form a Horde become a single Extra of Boon 16 (Value 90), (with probably 1 card in the Monster Pool) but could never create a Boon greater than the individual's Value(45). If there were 11 Vexes, that would be Boon 44 (Value 495). 12 or One million Vexes would both give Boon 45 (Value 530) since that is the most powerful their Horde can get.</td></tr><tr><td>Fury</td><td><strong>Temperamental</strong> —The Monster is the embodiment of an Emotion, usually a negative one. Such Monsters are often capable of supernatural actions, such as telepathy, shape-shifting, or even undying. The Vampire is an embodiment of Lust, with powers of mind control, telepathy and suggestion, as well as shape-shifting abilities. The Werewolf is berserk rage, and almost indestructible unless Silver is used. The Beansidhe is the embodiment of mourning, and able to sense an oncoming death. When they experience their negative emotion the Monster may refill their Monster Pool to Fury Score cards. When appropriate to their Emotion they must add a Monster Pool card to the action and multiply the total Score by their remaining Monster Pool Reduced.</td></tr><tr><td>Rook</td><td><strong>Guardian</strong> —The Monster is a Guardian, it guards a certain territory, path, treasure or person. Guardians may add Rook Draw to their Monster Pool whenever they act as a guard, try to interfere with an attack, or are attacked. The Guardian reduces all attacks by a number of Pips equal to the current Monster Pool automatically. The Guardian will however automatically play cards from its Monster Pool to defend itself (via any Active or Passive defence method that makes sense to the Referee. From Smaug to Fafnir dragons are often Guardians, looking after a particular treasure or demanding tributes from a territory.</td></tr><tr><td>Sin</td><td><strong>Villain</strong> —The Sin Monster is an embodiment of a specific vice, taboo, or sin, often in the form of a human Villain (often with animalistic features or tendencies). They usually have Annexes and Hitches that reflect their embodied vice or sin, and are often at least partially supernatural. Villains add Sin Draw cards to their Monster Pool when they, or another (usually both) indulges in their particular vice or behaviour. Which can be as broad as "Evil" if you like. Villains may add +1 Score for each card in their Monster Pool for any action that furthers their diabolical plans. Villains automatically spend Monster Pool cards on escape actions when attacked, creating at least Sin Draw Obstacles from the Monster Pools cards. The genocidal or narcissistic maniac, of the sort British Intelligence seems to tangle with repeatedly, could be a Sin Monster. The ever-hungry cannibal spirit, the Wendigo is definitely an example, as is the Sexual-predatory Lust-embodied Vampire.</td></tr><tr><td>Wyrd</td><td><strong>Doom-weaver</strong> —The monster is a creature of the Fæ, it can draw and play Yarn cards or Wyrd Tarot cards (as appropriate). The Wyrd Monster may play cards from its Monster Pool as Wyrd Tarot or Yarn cards. The Monster may draw and add Wyrd Draw cards to its Monster Pool whenever it makes a choice, decision, or judgement. The Doom-weaver may draw and play Wyrd Draw cards as Ordeal cards whenever anyone plays Wyrd Tarot or Yarn cards. They must play a card from the Monster Pool if they have to undo a choice, decision or judgement they have made. Doom-Weavers come in many forms from the Cait-Sith, the Beansidhe, to the Faery nobles of Ironmaster.</td></tr></tbody><tfoot><tr><td>&nbsp;&nbsp;Facet&nbsp;&nbsp;</td><td>&nbsp;&nbsp;Monster&nbsp;&nbsp;</td></tr></tfoot></table></div><p></p></details></details>
		 </details>
			</div>
				<div class="t13ne-card-lea"><details class="t13ne-lea"><summary><strong>Lea</strong></summary><p class="t13ne-colour"><strong>Path Colour —</strong> Green/Turquoise Herbivore</p><p class="t13ne-bulmas"><strong>Allowed Bulmäs —</strong> Herd, Broccen, Vermis, Labyrinthine, All-Walkers</p><p class="t13ne-animal-category"><strong>Animal Category —</strong> Black paths and Migratory</p><p class="t13ne-animal-example"><strong>Example Animal —</strong> Native Form / Dragon / Swarm</p><p class="t13ne-notes"><strong>Notes —</strong> These paths open and close naturally through the Lea from time to time, they are easily walked by all Bulmäs, but Vermis and All-Walkers may walk them in Swarm or Dragon configurations.</p></details></div></div><div class="t13ne-cardwrap-jsvg" id="cardrewritebox38" data-mode="jsvg" data-wyrd="1" data-yarn="1"><img src="https://www.cjmoseley.dev.cc/wp-content/plugins/t13ne/images/cards-svg/KC.svg" class="t13ne-svgcard"><div class="t13ne-svgpips"><div class="t13ne-textpips">13<!-- write suit--><span class="t13ne-symbol"><span class="t13ne-blackcard">♣</span></span><details class="t13ne-suit"><summary></summary><p class="t13ne-name"><strong>Suit: </strong>Clubs <span class="t13ne-blackcard">♣</span></p><p><strong>Ordeal Trump: </strong><span class="t13ne-trump">Defence</span></p><p class="t13ne-desc"><strong>Description: </strong>Clubs represent the power of politics and a stoic materialism. In the Tarot the Clubs are called Staves or Wands, in some traditions they represent Intellectual Air, but in most they are Fire.</p><p class="t13ne-tarot"><strong>Tarot Meaning: </strong>Power, Fame, Politics, Ability, Money.</p><p class="t13ne-otext"><strong>Ordeal Text: </strong>Ordeal Types: Wealth, Politics, Power. Clubs act as Trump for following Actions: Bartering, Controlling, Corrupting, Emotional-intensity, Guarding, Magic, Displaying might, Politics, Self-Defence, Socializing, and Trade Negotiations. Clubs can be used to defend any type of attack.</p><p class="t13ne-parry"><strong>Parry: </strong><strong>Disarm:</strong> All the cards of the attack are immediately discarded, along with the Parry Card. The remaining cards of the Defender's Parry can now each be used to cause the Attacker to drop a Descendant (starting with the attacking weapon usually), can also cut armour straps, belts etc, or steal hats. This basically adds RT equal to the card Pips to the Annex that was used to attack. Every 5 Pips also creates a point of GRT for the Character (representing how much time the Character must spend picking up, or readying the weapon again. Court cards additionally may damage the Descendant that was disarmed, as standard for the Stakes.</p><p class="t13ne-soak"><strong>Soak: </strong><strong>Ward:</strong> The matched Attack card, and one other randomly selected Attack card are discarded.</p></details></div></div><div class="t13ne-svg-facet"><div>Wyrd</div></div><div class="t13ne-wound"><div><strong class="t13ne-woundname">Mortal</strong><details><summary></summary><strong>Mortal</strong><p class="t13ne-base-pips">Base Pips to summon: 16</p><p class="t13ne-min-pips">Minimum Pips Required: 12</p><p class="t13ne-desc">The Target is now dying from the wound.</p><details class="t13ne-roll"><summary>Rules</summary>Create a "Dying / Broken / Burning" quirk Hitch that slowly grows +1 Hitch Boon per Miasma Boon as Chi Limit Duration) until healed. If no Hitch can be increased by the Mortal Wound then decrease an appropriate Facet (Hitch, Incarna, Persona or Core usually) by a Boon equal to the Wounded Character’s Miasma Score. I-Ching, Personality, Proficiency slots and Annexes affected should be recalculated. If the Hitch becomes a Flaw the Wound will automatically upgrade to a Carnage Wound.</details><details class="t13ne-ordeal"><summary>Ordeal Rules</summary>Treat as though the Personality Annex or Master Annex has been Crippled, until the end of the Round. Then add a new "Dying / Broken / Burning" quirk Hitch with a Boon of Miasma Score (to a Max of Half the Incarna Boon). Or increase a quirk Hitch by 1 Boon at the end of each Round.</details><details class="t13ne-psych"><summary>Psych Wounds</summary><details class="t13ne-negative"><summary>Negative Psych</summary><p class="t13ne-normal"><strong>Normal</strong>As Normal but does not create a "Dying" Hitch, but instead a Scars (Mental/Emotional) / Fear / Fatigued / Lament / Taboo / Offensive / Doubt,  etc Hitch.  Plus Consciousness is usually lost until Stabilised.</p><p class="t13ne-extreme"><strong>Extreme</strong>Creates any normal Hitch type. At Yarn-Teller's discretion Mortal Wounds can also alter the Persona, or Core Facet choice (Ref arbitrates). Plus Consciousness is usually lost until healed.</p></details><details class="t13ne-positive"><summary>Positive Psych</summary><p class="t13ne-normal"><strong>Normal</strong>As Normal but creates a Scars (Mental/Emotional) / Duty / Dependance / Paranoia / Devotion / Madness / Vague, etc quirk Hitch.  Plus Consciousness is usually lost until Stabilised. May Gain a new Talent Annex / Descendant or Resolve a quirk Hitch.</p><p class="t13ne-extreme"><strong>Extreme</strong>May create any Flaw Hitch. At Yarn-Teller's discretion Mortal Wounds can also alter the wounded character’s Persona, or Core Facet choices. Consciousness is usually lost until healed. Gains a new Talent Annex / Descendant or Resolves a quirk Handicap.</p></details></details></details></div></div><div class="t13ne-tarot-svg"><details><summary class="t13ne-wyrdtarot"><strong> Wyrd Tarot: </strong> Mage</summary><p class="t13ne-normal"><strong> Normal: </strong>Magic On. Target has +2 Success Levels to all Magical Attacks, Defences, etc. while atop Pile. Additionally creates a "Magic" Reserve for the Target.</p><p class="t13ne-crossed"><strong> Crossed: </strong>Magic Off. Target has -2 Success Levels to all Magical Attacks, Defences, etc. while atop Pile. Additionally discards all "Magic" Reserves.</p></details></div><div class="t13ne-yarn-fancy">
			<details><summary><strong>Yarn</strong> <br>The Faery Noble</summary>
			<span class="t13ne-description">The Faery Noble card stands for the Lords of the Fae. It represents power, of an unearthly, paradoxical, fateful, magical, cruel or capricious nature. It can be a sign of erratic, irrational, or even insane seeming behaviour, but says there's a reason beneath. The Faery Noble can be involved in important historical events, but you are just as likely to find one engaged in something mundane (at least from their point of view) such as painting the dawn red to warn shepherds of rain. Then again sometimes the card represents a Leader or Mentor who is "(Fairy-)Touched(-in-the-head)". Here again, the irrationality may be signs of genius, prophecy, or insanity, or a mixture.</span>
			<span class="t13ne-significator">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-significator"><strong>Significator</strong></summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-plot"><strong>Plot: </strong> The Faery Noble Plot is a tale of imagination and fantasy. Any fantasy so fantastic that it may be a dream is suitably close to the Faery Noble. Madness and magic are common themes and you can expect reality warping Yarn-Telling as a common thread.</aside>
					<aside class="t13ne-char"><strong>Character: </strong> The Faery Noble Significator indicates a powerful, capricious Character. They Gain Yin by changing their mind. They Gain Yang by using their Annexes. They Gain Chi by utilizing deeper structures than logic or linear time, which can appear as acting crazy to normal people. They gain +1 Success Levels when Stakes are Paradoxical.</aside>
					<aside class="t13ne-scene"><strong>Scene: </strong> The Faery Noble Plot is a tale of imagination and fantasy. Any fantasy so fantastic that it may be a dream is suitably close to the Faery Noble. Madness and magic are common themes and you can expect reality warping Yarn-Telling as a common thread.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-scene">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-scene-type">
						<strong>Scene: </strong> Completion
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">How the Story Completes and Resolves. depending on how the Characters do the Plot may be destroyed, demoted, or promoted.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-conflict-embodiment">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-conflict-embodiment">
						<strong>Conflict Embodiment: </strong> Tension
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">The Conflict embodies within two Characters, but they do not come into direct Conflict. They have some relationship, that Embodies the Conflict as arguments, discussions, jokes, etc.  common to Romance and Buddy movies.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>

			<span class="t13ne-facet-embodiment">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-facet-embodiment">
						<strong>Facet Embodiment: </strong> Monster
					</summary>
					<section class="t13ne-description">The Conflict Embodies one Facet as a Character Monster Facet.This Monster might be a Dragon, an Orc Horde, or just a human Villain. Monsters can be great fun, but are rarely subtle. Where possible when using Monster embodiments, the Plot may want to keep the exact nature of the Monster a secret for as long as it can.</section>
				</details>
			</span>
			 <div class="t13ne-archetypes"><details><summary><strong>Archetype Character: </strong>King</summary> <details class="t13ne-Persona"><summary><strong>Persona: </strong><span class="t13ne-output">Leader</span>
      </summary>
      <details class="t13ne-motivation"><summary><strong>Motivation: </strong></summary><span class="t13ne-description">A Leader seeks to be followed, they may have ambitions to rise in rank and command, or they may just be happy with a small band of followers. </span></details>
       <details class="t13ne-avoid"><summary><strong>Avoid: </strong></summary><span class="t13ne-description">Leaders worry about losing the respect of their followers, and so tend to avoid whatever behaviour they believe might do that.</span></details>
       <details class="t13ne-gain-chi"><summary><strong>Gain Chi: </strong></summary><span class="t13ne-description"><span>Gain Chi when your orders are followed, you make suggestions, ask questions, or you succeed alone.</span><p>Ordeal gain Chi when you play any card on, or for, an ally.</p></span></details></details>   <details class="t13ne-core"><summary><strong>Core: </strong><span class="t13ne-output">Master</span></summary><span class="t13ne-description">The character has one chosen environment in which they automatically gain one level of success.</span></details><details class="t13ne-hitch"><summary><strong>Hitch: </strong><span class="t13ne-output">Duty</span></summary><span class="t13ne-description">Whether its a duty to family, the law, or king and Country, some people are just driven by something greater than themselves.</span></details><p class="t13ne-description">Kings are Leaders who feel a Duty to protect their subjects not just lead them.</p></details></div>
			 <span class="t13ne-hook">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-hook">
						<strong>Hook: </strong> Danger
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">The Hook just consists of some enormous danger, it isn't looming, it isn't coming, it is right here, right now.</aside>
					<details class="t13ne-hook-aspect">
						<summary><strong>Hook Aspect: </strong>I-Ching</summary>
						<section class="t13ne-description">The Hook targets the Character's I-Ching. For example: If a Character has the Cauldron (50) I-Ching, then the Hook needs to have someone hungry turn up asking for help.</section>
					</details>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-obstacle-fancy"><details class="t13ne-obstacle"><summary><strong>Obstacle:</strong>Doom</summary><p class="t13ne-description">The way is blocked by the forces of history somehow. Perhaps some Faery Noble has been tasked to keep that route free as part of Wyrd's Tapestry for an hour, enter it, and the full forces of a powerful Yarn-Teller will be raised against you. It may not always be instant death... Although I'm not sure if that's meant to be reassuring, or terrifying.</p></details></span>
			<span class="t13ne-gain">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-gain">
						<strong>Gain </strong> 
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">Extremely Powerful Yarn-Teller Ally / Artefact Power Descendant</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-fray">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-fray">
						<strong>Fray: </strong> Magic
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">Maybe there's a Ward, a Runic barrier, an Alchemical Seal, or some dark and sinister Psibertek involved, but magic is in the Fray, and the PCs better have a mage on their side. Magic can come in a lot of different flavours, spells and cantrips, glamours and illusions, necromancy, scrying, summoning, invocations and evocations. This is usually a Low to Paradoxical Opposed, Co-operative or occasionally Solitary Ordeal.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-snag">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-snag">
						<strong>Snag: </strong> Changeable
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">The Fray is complicated by a changing or changeable situation. Perhaps a race is occurring in a tidal area, or perhaps the Villain is capricious and changes their plan during the middle of the Fray. Maybe the PCs goals should change mid-way through the Fray, as having promised to win, they are made to promise to lose or vice versa.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-sweeping">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-sweeping">
						<strong>Sweeping: </strong> Heirophant
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">The PCs meet a Heirophant or Prophetess Mentor Character who can explain everything, and usually has some special ability the Character's will require. This is a combined Gain Ally/Training/Lore Descendant and Revelation. Think Obi-Wan or the Oracle.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
		 <details class="t13ne-revelation-fancy"><summary><strong>Revelation</strong></summary><details class="t13ne-about"><summary><strong>About: </strong> The Story </summary><p class="t13ne-description">The information is about the current situation and plot. It might include information about either side of the Conflict, or both. It is normal when providing information to only reveal information about a single Side of the Conflict, usually the Dominant or Above sides.</p></details><details class="t13ne-info"><summary><strong>Info: </strong> Doom </summary><p class="t13ne-description">Sometimes information can include Dooms, Mercari can get reads on other people's fate from time to time, and Prophets can do that for whole people or regions, or time-travellers may believe that they know exactly what is going to happen ahead of time. Of course, they aren't always right. Sometimes its enough to know that the bad guy will get it in the very end, other times it isn't.</p></details><details class="t13ne-vector"><summary><strong>Vector: </strong> Hunch </summary><p class="t13ne-description">Intuition can be more than wild guesses. Sometimes subtle clues are not noticed by the conscious mind, but they tease at the subconscious, sometimes these clues emerge in dreams, but other times they present as gut instincts, or intuitions. For some Character's these hunches can be more valuable than another man's word, at least until they are proved wrong.</p></details><details class="t13ne-alternate"><summary><strong>Alternate: </strong> TMI </summary><p class="t13ne-description">Sometimes the information we get is Too Much Information, this might be that there are months of transcripts to work through, or that you now have detailed records of lavatory activities. There will be important information in there, if you can sift through the stuff you'd rather not know.</p></details><details class="t13ne-detail"><summary><strong>Detail: </strong> Danger </summary><p class="t13ne-description">The detail is about some danger or hazard to do with the Embodiment. Perhaps the Death-Ray pistol everyone is hunting for is actually not completed and if shaken when fired can short-circuit, or perhaps the fact that the warlord just purchased a number of Kallidian Moat Vipers should make the party rethink the "We can just swim that" plan.</p></details></details>
		 <details class="t13ne-ordeal-fancy"><summary><strong>Ordeal</strong></summary><p class="t13ne-ordeal-type"><strong>Ordeal-Type </strong> — Opposed Ordeal</p><p class="t13ne-stages"><strong>Stages </strong> — 6</p><p class="t13ne-ordeal-spread"><strong>Ordeal-Spread </strong> — Map-Geography</p><p class="t13ne-stakes"><strong>Stakes </strong> — Blended Low-Medium-High</p><p class="t13ne-test"><strong>Test </strong> — Power (Wyrd-Dominion)</p><p class="t13ne-obstacles"><strong>Obstacles </strong> — 2 Obstacles in every Non-Court Stage, 3 in every Court Stage</p><p class="t13ne-suggested-action"><strong>Suggested-Action </strong> — Choose</p><details class="t13ne-stage"><summary><strong>Stage: </strong> Intersection Stunt </summary><p class="t13ne-description">A choice of three directions. A negotiation or a demonstration of power</p></details><details class="t13ne-motional"><summary><strong>Motional: </strong> Dangerous Foot Chase </summary><p class="t13ne-description">We're halting pursuit, Captain. Sir, yes I understand, but... Sir... Yessir... Okay, you heard the Captain, follow them into the minefield.</p></details><details class="t13ne-fight"><summary><strong>Fight: </strong> Boss Fight </summary><p class="t13ne-description">The Fight is a major battle against a very powerful Monster. Sometimes a Force of Nature Monster with at least 2 ♣ Monster Facets, or even a Kaiju.<!-- t13ne-shortcode "%7B%22type%22%3A%22facetsuitaspect%22%2C%22suit%22%3A%223%22%2C%22aspect%22%3A%22Monster%22%7D"// --></p><div class="t13ne-tablewrap"><table class="t13ne-table Monster"><thead><tr><th>&nbsp;&nbsp;Facet&nbsp;&nbsp;</th><th>&nbsp;&nbsp;Monster&nbsp;&nbsp;</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td>Burden</td><td><strong>Object</strong> —The Monster is an object imbued with the semblance of life. Artificial in nature usually, the living object is usually tough and strong. An Object Monster is created with a Monster Pool equal to their Burden Score. Objects are able to multiply their Scores by 1+Monster Pool Size when appropriate to their form and function. The Object Monster may instantly Prepare cards from its Monster Pool, often as a Prepared Defence or Stunt Preparation, as appropriate for the Object's form or function.</td></tr><tr><td>Dominion</td><td><strong>Horde</strong> —The Monster is a group or swarm of lesser monsters, it might be an Army, a pack of Bulmäs, a Fortress or a Battle Moon. Horde is effectively a Pact Descendant. A Horde may pool Values to create larger rolls, however they may never create a Boon greater than their Value. Hordes may add Dominion Draw cards to their Monster Pool when the Horde increases in numbers. The Horde must Play at least one card from their Monster Pool on any action that splits the Horde (like losing an individual). E.g. two Vex Extras of Boon 10 (Value 45) that form a Horde become a single Extra of Boon 16 (Value 90), (with probably 1 card in the Monster Pool) but could never create a Boon greater than the individual's Value(45). If there were 11 Vexes, that would be Boon 44 (Value 495). 12 or One million Vexes would both give Boon 45 (Value 530) since that is the most powerful their Horde can get.</td></tr><tr><td>Fury</td><td><strong>Temperamental</strong> —The Monster is the embodiment of an Emotion, usually a negative one. Such Monsters are often capable of supernatural actions, such as telepathy, shape-shifting, or even undying. The Vampire is an embodiment of Lust, with powers of mind control, telepathy and suggestion, as well as shape-shifting abilities. The Werewolf is berserk rage, and almost indestructible unless Silver is used. The Beansidhe is the embodiment of mourning, and able to sense an oncoming death. When they experience their negative emotion the Monster may refill their Monster Pool to Fury Score cards. When appropriate to their Emotion they must add a Monster Pool card to the action and multiply the total Score by their remaining Monster Pool Reduced.</td></tr><tr><td>Rook</td><td><strong>Guardian</strong> —The Monster is a Guardian, it guards a certain territory, path, treasure or person. Guardians may add Rook Draw to their Monster Pool whenever they act as a guard, try to interfere with an attack, or are attacked. The Guardian reduces all attacks by a number of Pips equal to the current Monster Pool automatically. The Guardian will however automatically play cards from its Monster Pool to defend itself (via any Active or Passive defence method that makes sense to the Referee. From Smaug to Fafnir dragons are often Guardians, looking after a particular treasure or demanding tributes from a territory.</td></tr><tr><td>Sin</td><td><strong>Villain</strong> —The Sin Monster is an embodiment of a specific vice, taboo, or sin, often in the form of a human Villain (often with animalistic features or tendencies). They usually have Annexes and Hitches that reflect their embodied vice or sin, and are often at least partially supernatural. Villains add Sin Draw cards to their Monster Pool when they, or another (usually both) indulges in their particular vice or behaviour. Which can be as broad as "Evil" if you like. Villains may add +1 Score for each card in their Monster Pool for any action that furthers their diabolical plans. Villains automatically spend Monster Pool cards on escape actions when attacked, creating at least Sin Draw Obstacles from the Monster Pools cards. The genocidal or narcissistic maniac, of the sort British Intelligence seems to tangle with repeatedly, could be a Sin Monster. The ever-hungry cannibal spirit, the Wendigo is definitely an example, as is the Sexual-predatory Lust-embodied Vampire.</td></tr><tr><td>Wyrd</td><td><strong>Doom-weaver</strong> —The monster is a creature of the Fæ, it can draw and play Yarn cards or Wyrd Tarot cards (as appropriate). The Wyrd Monster may play cards from its Monster Pool as Wyrd Tarot or Yarn cards. The Monster may draw and add Wyrd Draw cards to its Monster Pool whenever it makes a choice, decision, or judgement. The Doom-weaver may draw and play Wyrd Draw cards as Ordeal cards whenever anyone plays Wyrd Tarot or Yarn cards. They must play a card from the Monster Pool if they have to undo a choice, decision or judgement they have made. Doom-Weavers come in many forms from the Cait-Sith, the Beansidhe, to the Faery nobles of Ironmaster.</td></tr></tbody><tfoot><tr><td>&nbsp;&nbsp;Facet&nbsp;&nbsp;</td><td>&nbsp;&nbsp;Monster&nbsp;&nbsp;</td></tr></tfoot></table></div><p></p></details></details>
		 </details>
			</div>
				<div class="t13ne-card-lea"><details class="t13ne-lea"><summary><strong>Lea</strong></summary><p class="t13ne-colour"><strong>Path Colour —</strong> Green/Turquoise Herbivore</p><p class="t13ne-bulmas"><strong>Allowed Bulmäs —</strong> Herd, Broccen, Vermis, Labyrinthine, All-Walkers</p><p class="t13ne-animal-category"><strong>Animal Category —</strong> Labyrinth</p><p class="t13ne-animal-example"><strong>Example Animal —</strong> Labrys patterned Minotaur</p><p class="t13ne-notes"><strong>Notes —</strong> Kings represent paths in the Labyrinth, and Cæster in the centre as well. A normal Bulmäs that tries to walk the Labyrinth would be lost, and would then be recruited into the Labyrinth. The Labyrinth can connect any point in space and time to any other, allowing Bulmäs to consciously time-travel through the Lea.  A King can allow a Bulmäs to create or reload a Chronolith or side-step depending on the power-level of the Bulmäs.</p></details></div></div><div class="t13ne-cardwrap-jsvg" id="cardrewritebox39" data-mode="jsvg" data-wyrd="1" data-yarn="1"><img src="https://www.cjmoseley.dev.cc/wp-content/plugins/t13ne/images/cards-svg/AS.svg" class="t13ne-svgcard"><div class="t13ne-svgpips"><div class="t13ne-textpips">14<!-- write suit--><span class="t13ne-symbol"><span class="t13ne-blackcard">♠</span></span><details class="t13ne-suit"><summary></summary><p class="t13ne-name"><strong>Suit: </strong>Spades <span class="t13ne-blackcard">♠</span></p><p><strong>Ordeal Trump: </strong><span class="t13ne-trump">Attack</span></p><p class="t13ne-desc"><strong>Description: </strong>The Spades Suit is the suit of suffering, said to be good for the soul, but miserable for a Monday. In the tarot, the Suit is replaced with Swords, which (depending on the tradition) may represent either damaging Fire, or more commonly the cutting intellect of Air.</p><p class="t13ne-tarot"><strong>Tarot Meaning: </strong>Misfortune, Suffering, Loss, Mourning, Treachery, Betrayal, War, Lawsuit, Conflict.</p><p class="t13ne-otext"><strong>Ordeal Text: </strong>Ordeal Types: Confrontation (e.g. Warfare, Lawsuit), Perception (Spot Hidden). Spades act as Trumps for the Following Action Types: Attacking, Balance, Climbing, Contagion, Desecration, Destruction, Feats of Strength, Lock-picking, Navigation, Perception (Search/Spot), Staying power, and Stoicism. Spades can be used for any type of attack.</p><p class="t13ne-parry"><strong>Parry: </strong><strong>Redirect-Attack:</strong> The damage from the original attack (less the Parried Card) is redirected by the Defender to the Attacker, their Descendants or their Allies.</p><p class="t13ne-soak"><strong>Soak: </strong><strong>Counter-Attack:</strong> The matched Attack card is reflected back at the Attacker, or their Descendant, as Narratively appropriate. </p></details></div></div><div class="t13ne-svg-facet"><div>Gossamer, Inertia, Key, Miasma, Trial, Yonder</div></div><div class="t13ne-wound"><div><strong class="t13ne-woundname">Carnage</strong><details><summary></summary><strong>Carnage</strong><p class="t13ne-base-pips">Base Pips to summon: 21</p><p class="t13ne-min-pips">Minimum Pips Required: 14</p><p class="t13ne-desc">The Target should be dead, all exploded or turned to stone, or something. Sometimes though, they keep on going... for a while. And just occasionally, they get better.</p><details class="t13ne-roll"><summary>Rules</summary>Create a "Dead/Destroyed" Flaw Hitch, or increase the Boon of any Flaw Hitch by 1 Boon per Miasma Boon treated as Yarn Limit Duration outside of Ordeals) until healed. May also alter the target’s Incarna Facet choice (turning to stone, etc). Alternatively Decrease Character Scale by 1 or remove a Character's access to the Wounded Alternate.</details><details class="t13ne-ordeal"><summary>Ordeal Rules</summary>Immediately Treat Personality / Master Descendant as Wounded. At the end of the Round either create a new "Dead / Destroyed" Flaw Hitch (Half the Incarna Boon + Miasma Score) or increase a Flaw Hitch by Miasma Score. Alternatively Decrease Scale by 1, or alter Incarna / Persona / Core choices. If the Hitch Boon rises above Incarna Boon the Wound increases to Carnage+</details><details class="t13ne-psych"><summary>Psych Wounds</summary><details class="t13ne-negative"><summary>Negative Psych</summary><p class="t13ne-normal"><strong>Normal</strong>Can create a Flaw Hitch of any Type, as the Ref decides. Consciousness is always lost until the wound is Stabilised. Note may remove a Character's access to the Wounded Alternate.</p><p class="t13ne-extreme"><strong>Extreme</strong>Creates Maximised Flaw Hitches of any type but favours... Fear, Madness, Vague (Amnesia), Paranoia, Loss of Sense (e.g. Hysterical Blindness) or Taboo Hitches. Consciousness is always lost until Healed and may remove a Character's access to the Wounded Alternate. Can create a new Split Alternate.</p></details><details class="t13ne-positive"><summary>Positive Psych</summary><p class="t13ne-normal"><strong>Normal</strong>May Gain a new Power Annex / Descendant. Can Create a Flaw Hitch of any type, Consciousness is always lost until Stabilised, and access to the Alternate may be lost.</p><p class="t13ne-extreme"><strong>Extreme</strong> May create or Resolve Flaw Hitches of any type but favours... Duty, Dependance, Madness, Vague (Amnesia), Paranoia, Loss of Sense (e.g. Hysterical Blindness) or Taboo Hitches. Consciousness is always lost until Healed. Either creates a new Alternate, Intergrates an Alternate one Step toward Coalesce, or Gain a new Power Annex / Descendant.</p></details></details></details></div></div><div class="t13ne-tarot-svg"><details><summary class="t13ne-wyrdtarot"><strong> Wyrd Tarot: </strong> Death</summary><p class="t13ne-normal"><strong> Normal: </strong>All Attacks by Target add +21 Pips of Penetration to the whole attack while atop Pile. All Hitches created by or on the Target are "Dying", "Dead" or "Destroyed" </p><p class="t13ne-crossed"><strong> Crossed: </strong>Automatically adds +21 Pips to any Defence. All Hitches created by or on the Target cannot be "Dying", "Dead" or "Destroyed".</p></details></div><div class="t13ne-yarn-fancy">
			<details><summary><strong>Yarn</strong> <br>The Threshold</summary>
			<span class="t13ne-description">The Threshold Card represents doorways, portals and liminal spaces. It is the edge of something else, a place where one goes from one place to another, often with different rules. It is a card of paradigm shifts, of new places. Great changes, events and dangers may lie ahead, and it is not too late to turn back, but then fresh understandings will not be gained, and no new discoveries will be made.</span>
			<span class="t13ne-significator">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-significator"><strong>Significator</strong></summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-plot"><strong>Plot: </strong> The Threshold Plot is often one of invasion. Of course, who the invaders are, and how they invade varies from Plot to Plot. Threshold Plots can be about stepping into another world (Portal Fantasies), exploring strange new lands, or space invaders.</aside>
					<aside class="t13ne-char"><strong>Character: </strong> The Threshold Significator indicates a Character who is between two worlds, perhaps on the cusp between one thing and another. They Gain Yin when they act confused, tired, or shy. They Gain Yang when they act like a know-it-all, or act arrogant or confident. They Gain Chi when they cross a Threshold, enter a new space, or notice the time (usually by dusk or dawn). They gain +1 Success Levels to all actions in liminal spaces and times (at dusk, midnight, new years, doorways, crossroads etc)</aside>
					<aside class="t13ne-scene"><strong>Scene: </strong> The Threshold Plot is often one of invasion. Of course, who the invaders are, and how they invade varies from Plot to Plot. Threshold Plots can be about stepping into another world (Portal Fantasies), exploring strange new lands, or space invaders.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-scene">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-scene-type">
						<strong>Scene: </strong> Hook
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">Hooks hook a character into a Conflict. A Hook offers a Character (or Group) a reason to get involved in the Story. This lets the Plot use the Conflict Facet Boon/Value of the Character to help build their Conflict Annex.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-conflict-embodiment">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-conflict-embodiment">
						<strong>Conflict Embodiment: </strong> Rational
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">The Conflict embodies each side of the Conflict into Characters who are in direct Conflict.  This is the classic Heroes verses Bad Guys plot.This may follow a period of Tension, or not. Often the Rational Embodiment creates a Monster.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>

			<span class="t13ne-facet-embodiment">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-facet-embodiment">
						<strong>Facet Embodiment: </strong> Incarna
					</summary>
					<section class="t13ne-description">The Conflict Embodies one Facet as a Character or Descendant Incarna. Often the easiest Embodiment to create Fantastical Characters, but not often suitable to all Genres and Conflicts. Also this tends to to make for a very obvious Conflict.</section>
				</details>
			</span>
			 <div class="t13ne-archetypes"><details><summary><strong>Archetype Character: </strong>Hero</summary> <details class="t13ne-Persona"><summary><strong>Persona: </strong><span class="t13ne-output">Hero</span>
      </summary>
      <details class="t13ne-motivation"><summary><strong>Motivation: </strong></summary><span class="t13ne-description">Heroes are extraordinary individuals who are noted for their great acts of courage, and enormous achievements. Heroes often act selflessly to protect others in harms way.</span></details>
       <details class="t13ne-avoid"><summary><strong>Avoid: </strong></summary><span class="t13ne-description">Heroes tend to avoid situations where they show weakness to others.</span></details>
       <details class="t13ne-gain-chi"><summary><strong>Gain Chi: </strong></summary><span class="t13ne-description"><span>Gain Chi when you risk something, help another, volunteer, or are praised for your actions. </span><p>Ordeal gain Chi when attacked.</p></span></details></details>   <details class="t13ne-core"><summary><strong>Core: </strong><span class="t13ne-output">Poser</span></summary><span class="t13ne-description">Gain Chi whenever you recite a catchphrase, cliche, or strike a dramatic pose.</span></details><details class="t13ne-hitch"><summary><strong>Hitch: </strong><span class="t13ne-output">Duty</span></summary><span class="t13ne-description">Whether its a duty to family, the law, or king and Country, some people are just driven by something greater than themselves.</span></details><p class="t13ne-description">The Hero is always the first to volunteer, and the last to quit, they are the star of many an 80's action movie.</p></details></div>
			 <span class="t13ne-hook">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-hook">
						<strong>Hook: </strong> Kick in the door
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">The Story opens with action, often a Fight, it could be the PCs who kick in a door, or an enemy may burst in.</aside>
					<details class="t13ne-hook-aspect">
						<summary><strong>Hook Aspect: </strong>Hitches</summary>
						<section class="t13ne-description">The Hook targets the Character's Hitches, the bigger the better.</section>
					</details>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-obstacle-fancy"><details class="t13ne-obstacle"><summary><strong>Obstacle:</strong>Portal</summary><p class="t13ne-description">The way is blocked by what may be just a closed, locked, barred, or trapped door, airlock, gateway or it could be a portal to another realm. Portals have a tendency to be a bit final, especially when you open them when you don't know where they go.</p></details></span>
			<span class="t13ne-gain">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-gain">
						<strong>Gain </strong> 
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">Ref Special: e.g. (Immortality)</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-fray">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-fray">
						<strong>Fray: </strong> The Edge of the World
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">This is as bad as a Threshold can get, it is the liminal space between the known and the unknown. It might be the edge of a map, the ocean over the horizon, the edge of space, or the mouth of a cave. Usually such portals and thresholds will have some sort of guardian that must be defeated, sneaked or riddled past with an Ordeal.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-snag">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-snag">
						<strong>Snag: </strong> The End of the Day
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">Sometimes the Snag is due to the time which adds some extra complications to the situation. Perhaps the faltering dusk light makes it harder to be spotted, or the first light of dawn may be what shows your silhouettes to the enemy. Then again the night of first frosts, first snows, or the first day of the thaw are all good complications too).</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
			<span class="t13ne-sweeping">
				<details>
					<summary class="t13ne-sweeping">
						<strong>Sweeping: </strong> On the Threshold
					</summary>
					<aside class="t13ne-description">A quiet moment, almost an aside from the plot, that allows the PCs a breather before they push on. It usually occurs before some major event, while opening a door, or entering or exiting a space, and is often marked by one of the Plot's Motifs. They may have fallen in a dark hole, but upon climbing out, the PCs see a butterfly.</aside>
				</details>
			</span>
		 <details class="t13ne-revelation-fancy"><summary><strong>Revelation</strong></summary><details class="t13ne-about"><summary><strong>About: </strong> Dominant </summary><p class="t13ne-description">The information revealed is about the Dominant Conflict Embodiment. Sometimes they are Characters, but almost as often they are Locations, Quests, Ordeals, or similar. Where this is true the Revelation should reveal details about future Quests the Pressed Characters must complete, or places they should visit. The Revelation can be little more than a call-back or foreshadowing if handled correct.</p></details><details class="t13ne-info"><summary><strong>Info: </strong> Inside Knowledge </summary><p class="t13ne-description">Information that comes from sources close to the Embodiment (or even from the Embodiment themselves). Inside knowledge is always detailed, and specific, but can be coloured by bias, or simply be misinformation.</p></details><details class="t13ne-vector"><summary><strong>Vector: </strong> Aura </summary><p class="t13ne-description">Auras are for some people a spiritual or emotional energy field that they see, or they may just be another word for a fragrance.</p></details><details class="t13ne-alternate"><summary><strong>Alternate: </strong> Sacrifices </summary><p class="t13ne-description">The information may be about the Sacrifices the Embodiment has made, or Sacrifices that will have to be made. These Sacrifices might be great and grand, or just toll for the tunnel, or it could just be information about a Sacrifice Umbral.</p></details><details class="t13ne-detail"><summary><strong>Detail: </strong> Target </summary><p class="t13ne-description">A target detail is simply that, where, what, or whom the target of the Embodiment is.</p></details></details>
		 <details class="t13ne-ordeal-fancy"><summary><strong>Ordeal</strong></summary><p class="t13ne-ordeal-type"><strong>Ordeal-Type </strong> — Opposed Ordeal</p><p class="t13ne-stages"><strong>Stages </strong> — 9</p><p class="t13ne-ordeal-spread"><strong>Ordeal-Spread </strong> — Map-Geography</p><p class="t13ne-stakes"><strong>Stakes </strong> — Blended Medium-High-Extreme</p><p class="t13ne-test"><strong>Test </strong> — Strength (Gossamer)</p><p class="t13ne-obstacles"><strong>Obstacles </strong> — 3 Obstacles per Stage</p><p class="t13ne-suggested-action"><strong>Suggested-Action </strong> — Push</p><details class="t13ne-stage"><summary><strong>Stage: </strong> Danger Stunt </summary><p class="t13ne-description">Maybe the buildings are collapsing, or an earthquake is happening. You know the sort of thing, jets of flame leap from the ground, the floor bucks and shakes, power cables spark and fall. Danger is all around.</p></details><details class="t13ne-motional"><summary><strong>Motional: </strong> Dangerous Vehicular Flee </summary><p class="t13ne-description">The smoke and flames are thick, but you can still see the headlights behind you. They aren't giving up...</p></details><details class="t13ne-fight"><summary><strong>Fight: </strong> Shock Attack </summary><p class="t13ne-description">This is a special Extreme Stakes Ambush, where the Ambushers actually rapidly assault (rather than being hidden). Shock Attackers have +1 Success Level to all rolls and cards during the opening Phase. Although they are rarely more than a Cast Extra, they may be Vex Monsters. With 1 Monster Facet <!-- t13ne-shortcode "%7B%22type%22%3A%22facetsuitaspect%22%2C%22suit%22%3A%224%22%2C%22aspect%22%3A%22Monster%22%7D"// --></p><div class="t13ne-tablewrap"><table class="t13ne-table Monster"><thead><tr><th>&nbsp;&nbsp;Facet&nbsp;&nbsp;</th><th>&nbsp;&nbsp;Monster&nbsp;&nbsp;</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td>Gossamer</td><td><strong>Wiry</strong> —The Monster is immensely physically powerful, although often its form may not reveal it. It often demonstrates supernatural power as well as strength and toughness. A small, strong Goblin, a robot that looks like a little girl, a cybernetically enhanced rat, or even a killer doll are all examples of a Gossamer Monster. The Wiry Monster can refill Gossamer Score cards to its Monster Pool whenever it is underestimated. A Wiry monster may add their current Monster Pool size to any Score or Pips to do with their Monstrous strength or toughness. The Monster must spend at least one card from their Monster Pool when they physically attack.</td></tr><tr><td>Inertia</td><td><strong>Unstoppable</strong> —The Monster is inexorable, it does not stop and cannot be killed by any normal means. They start play with a Monster Pool of Inertia Score cards. Unstoppable Monsters can ignore a number of Wounds equal to the number of cards in their Monster Pool. Wounds stacked beyond the Monster Pool affect them as normal. Inexorable Monsters can also ignore all Hitches with a Boon less than their Inertia Score while they have cards in their Monster Pool. Whenever an Unstoppable Monster is actually damaged it must spend cards from its Monster Pool while remaining motionless for Inertia Score as Chi Extend Duration to discard all Wounds less than Mortal in nature, and Stabilise all Mortal + Wounds (which may then be ignored if the Unstoppable has enough cards remaining in its Monster Pool). Examples of the Inertial Monster type include the Hungry Dead (Zombies) and most robotic assassins (especially ones from the future).</td></tr><tr><td>Key</td><td><strong>Hunter</strong> —The Monster is a hunter or tracker, it should favour harrying attacks and hit and run tactics. Typically such a Monster will vanish or flee if it is wounded, only to reappear when healed. The hunter may be fooled by false trails, but never for long. Hunters may add Key Draw cards to their Monster Pool whenever they spot a track, clue, or similar, of the subject of their hunt. Hunters receive a bonus equal to the number of cards in the Monster Pool to any tracking, attacking or hunting actions if they also spend a card from the Monster Pool on the action. Hunters are usually immune to Carnage and Mortal Wounds until the Zenith of the story (such wounds are taken as Crippling Wounds instead, but at a cost of one card from the Monster Pool).
